  sample_request_entitlement varchar [note: 'Maximum number of product sample requests the customer is entitled to per calendar year under this service profile. Supports the technical sales model where samples are provided for application evaluation and qualification.']
  sap_sold_to_party varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA SD sold-to party number for the customer associated with this service profile. Used to link technical service entitlements to order management, pricing, and delivery processes in SAP.']
  sds_distribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Chemical Mfg is responsible for proactively distributing Safety Data Sheets (SDS) to this customer as part of the service profile. When True, updated SDS documents are automatically distributed upon revision.']
  service_site_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer site location where technical services are delivered. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (e.g., REACH for EU, TSCA for USA) and regional TSR assignment.']
  service_site_location varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the specific customer plant, facility, or site location to which this technical service profile applies. A customer may have multiple sites with different service profiles.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the level of technical service and application support contracted or agreed upon with the customer. \'Standard\' provides basic technical support; \'Premium\' includes dedicated resources and faster response; \'Strategic\' includes joint development, pilot plant access, and executive engagement. Drives resource allocation and SLA commitments.']
  technical_training_entitlement varchar [note: 'Number of technical training sessions (webinars, workshops, or on-site training) the customer is entitled to per year under this service profile. Covers product application training, safety handling, and regulatory updates.']

  Note: 'Technical service and application support profile for a customer account, capturing the level of technical assistance contracted or agreed upon. Stores assigned technical service representative (TSR), application engineer, service tier (standard, premium, strategic), approved product applications, lab support entitlement, on-site visit frequency, formulation assistance flag, pilot plant access flag, joint development agreement reference, and technical complaint escalation path. Supports the technical sales model common in specialty chemical businesses where deep application engineering differentiates suppliers.'
}

Table customer.qualification {
  qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for qualification']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the B2B customer account for whom this product qualification applies. Links to the customer master record in Salesforce CRM and SAP SD.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal product master record (SKU/material) that has been qualified for supply to this customer site.']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer plant, facility, or site location where the qualified product is approved for use. Qualifications are site-specific in chemical supply to automotive, pharma, and electronics sectors.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Qualification owner employee tracks responsibility for product approvals, required for qualification audit and renewal processes.']
  apl_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the qualification record, typically assigned by the customer or jointly by the supplier and customer. Used in customer correspondence, COA references, and audit trails.']
  application_use_case varchar [note: 'The specific end-use application or process for which the chemical product has been qualified at the customer site (e.g., automotive coating adhesion, semiconductor etch bath, pharmaceutical excipient, agricultural formulation carrier).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer product qualification. \'approved\' = fully qualified for commercial supply; \'conditionally_approved\' = approved with restrictions or deviations; \'pending_review\' = under evaluation; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted pending investigation; \'revoked\' = permanently withdrawn; \'expired\' = past expiry date without renewal.']
  approved_by_customer_contact varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the customer representative (e.g., quality engineer, procurement manager) who formally approved the qualification. Business-confidential as it identifies a specific customer decision-maker.']
  approved_by_internal_user varchar [note: 'The name or employee ID of the internal Chemical Mfg representative (e.g., technical sales, quality manager) who managed and approved the qualification from the supplier side.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'The CAS Registry Number of the primary chemical substance in the qualified product. Used for regulatory cross-referencing under REACH, TSCA, and customer EHS systems.']
  change_notification_lead_days varchar [note: 'The minimum number of calendar days advance notice required to be given to the customer before implementing any change to the qualified product. Populated only when change_notification_required_flag is True.']
  change_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires formal notification (e.g., PCN - Product Change Notification) before any change to the qualified product formulation, process, or raw material source. Critical in automotive and electronics supply chains.']
  coa_template_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the COA template configured for this customer-product qualification. Defines which quality parameters and test results are reported on the COA accompanying each shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this qualification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crm_qualification_code varchar [note: 'The qualification record identifier in Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer-facing qualification activities. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRM.']
  customer_part_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s own internal part number or material code assigned to this qualified chemical product. Used for cross-referencing in purchase orders, COA requests, and EDI transactions.']
  customer_spec_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s internal specification document number that defines the quality requirements for this qualified product. Referenced on COA and COC documents during shipment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this qualification record is binding and commercial supply may commence. May differ from qualification_date if there is a ramp-up or transition period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this qualification is contractually binding for commercial supply. Null for open-ended qualifications. Used in supply chain planning and order management validation.']
  exclusive_supply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Chemical Mfg is the sole qualified supplier for this product at this customer site. True = exclusive supply arrangement; False = customer has multiple qualified suppliers.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer qualification expires and must be renewed or re-qualified. Null indicates an open-ended qualification with no defined expiry. Critical for supply continuity planning.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'The end-market industry segment of the customer for which this qualification applies. Drives regulatory requirements, COA content, and qualification rigor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|electronics|pharmaceutical|agriculture|construction|consumer_goods|industrial|aerospace|food_beverage|coatings — promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this qualification record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lims_qualification_reference varchar [note: 'The qualification record identifier in LabWare LIMS, used to cross-reference lab trial data, test results, and analytical reports that supported the qualification decision.']
  max_approved_volume_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum annual or periodic supply volume in kilograms approved under this qualification. Volumes exceeding this threshold require customer notification or re-qualification.']
  min_approved_volume_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum annual or periodic supply volume in kilograms approved under this qualification. Volumes below this threshold may require re-qualification or customer notification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional qualification conditions, restrictions, or contextual information not captured in structured fields. Examples: special handling requirements, packaging restrictions, labelling requirements specific to this customer.']
  product_grade varchar [note: 'The specific chemical grade or quality tier of the product that has been qualified (e.g., Technical Grade, Reagent Grade, Electronic Grade, Pharmaceutical Grade, Food Grade). Critical differentiator in chemical supply.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'The date on which the product qualification was formally approved and granted by the customer. This is the effective start date for commercial supply under this qualification.']
  qualification_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the product was qualified for use at the customer site. \'lab_trial\' = bench-scale testing at customer or supplier lab; \'plant_trial\' = full-scale production trial at customer facility; \'third_party_cert\' = qualification via third-party certification body; \'desk_review\' = documentation-only review; \'customer_audit\' = qualification via customer site audit; \'equivalency_study\' = qualification via equivalency to an already-approved product.']
  qualification_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the geographic or organizational scope of the qualification. \'site_specific\' = applies only to the named customer site; \'global_account\' = applies across all sites of the customer account; \'regional\' = applies to a defined regional cluster of sites; \'product_family\' = applies to a family of related products.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'The European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) REACH registration number for the qualified substance. Required for supply into EU markets. Format: 01-XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX-XX-XXXX.']
  regulatory_compliance_standard varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory or quality standard under which this qualification was conducted (e.g., IATF 16949 for automotive, ICH Q7 for pharmaceutical API, IPC-7711 for electronics, ISO 9001 for general industrial). Drives audit and documentation requirements.']
  renewal_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days before the expiry date that the renewal process must be initiated to ensure supply continuity. Used to trigger renewal workflow alerts in the quality management system.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification requires periodic renewal before the expiry date. True = renewal process must be initiated; False = qualification is perpetual or renewal is not contractually required.']
  sds_issue_date date [note: 'The issue date of the Safety Data Sheet version referenced in this qualification. Used to verify that the customer was provided with current GHS-compliant hazard information at time of qualification.']
  sds_version varchar [note: 'The version number of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) that was current at the time of qualification. Ensures traceability between the qualification and the hazard communication documentation provided to the customer.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'The Stock Keeping Unit code identifying the specific product variant approved for supply. Aligns with SAP material number and customer-facing product catalogue.']
  spec_deviation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any customer-approved deviations from the standard product specification included in this qualification. Populated only when spec_deviation_flag is True.']
  spec_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification includes customer-approved deviations from the standard product specification. True = one or more approved deviations exist; False = qualification is to standard specification.']
  volume_period varchar [note: 'The time period to which the approved volume range (min/max) applies. Defines whether volume limits are annual, quarterly, monthly, or per-order constraints.']

  Note: 'Customer-approved chemical products and grades that have been qualified for use at a specific customer site or application. Captures customer account, site, approved SKU/product code, grade, application use case, qualification date, qualification method (lab trial, plant trial, third-party cert), approval status, expiry date, approved volume range, and any customer-specific specification deviations. Critical in automotive, pharma, and electronics chemical supply where customer qualification is mandatory before commercial supply.'
}

Table customer.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer interaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the interaction data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (account) involved in this interaction. Links to the customer master data product.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service case or complaint record linked to this interaction, if applicable. Supports complaint resolution tracking and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflows.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Supports support interaction logs tying each customer interaction to the specific chemical product discussed, required for service quality metrics.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer contact person who participated in this interaction. Links to the customer contact data product.']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer site or plant location where the interaction occurred or is relevant to. Supports site-level account management and technical service tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee (account manager or sales representative) who owns the customer account and is responsible for this interaction.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales opportunity linked to this interaction, if applicable. Enables tracking of interactions that advance a specific sales pursuit.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Interaction Log per Order ties customer/technical interactions to a specific sales order, enabling SLA measurement and order‑level support metrics.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Enables Interaction‑Project Mapping report, tying customer interactions (meetings, calls) to specific R&D projects for account planning and ROI analysis.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the interaction was conducted. Supports omnichannel analytics and customer engagement reporting.']
  coa_request_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested a Certificate of Analysis (COA) during this interaction. Supports quality document fulfillment tracking and customer quality management.']
  complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction involves or originated from a customer complaint. Triggers linkage to the complaint/case management workflow and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this interaction record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  crm_activity_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from Salesforce CRM activity record. Used to trace the interaction back to the system of record for reconciliation and lineage.']
  customer_participant_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer-side contacts who participated in the interaction. Indicates the breadth of customer engagement and stakeholder involvement.']
  customer_participants varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer contact names or identifiers who participated in the interaction. Confidential business relationship data.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Numeric satisfaction rating provided by the customer or assessed by the account manager following the interaction, typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Supports Key Performance Indicator (KPI) tracking for customer relationship health.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total elapsed time of the interaction in minutes. Used to assess engagement depth, resource utilization, and account planning efficiency.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which the required follow-up action must be completed. Used for task management and account manager accountability tracking.']
  follow_up_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific follow-up actions committed to during the interaction. Captures commitments made to the customer for accountability and continuity.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up action or subsequent interaction is required as a result of this interaction. Drives task creation and account planning workflows.']
  interaction_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer interaction occurred. Used for day-level scheduling, account planning, and reporting.']
  interaction_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the interaction content, discussion points, and context. Captures technical topics discussed, products referenced, and any regulatory or compliance matters raised.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interaction record. Indicates whether the interaction was completed as planned, cancelled, or requires rescheduling.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the customer interaction commenced. Supports time-zone-aware analytics and SLA measurement.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature or purpose of the customer interaction. Distinguishes between sales-driven, technical service, complaint handling, and digital engagement touchpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_call|technical_visit|complaint_call|email_exchange|webinar|trade_show|portal_login|video_conference — promote to reference product]']
  internal_participant_count varchar [note: 'Number of internal Chemical Mfg employees who participated in the interaction. Used for resource utilization and engagement intensity analysis.']
  internal_participants varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of internal employee names or identifiers who participated in the interaction (e.g., account manager, technical service representative, application chemist). Supports multi-participant interaction tracking.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the interaction took place (e.g., customer plant site, trade show venue, video conference URL, Chemical Mfg office). Relevant for in-person and on-site technical visits.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this interaction record was last updated in the system. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  next_interaction_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next planned customer interaction as agreed during this touchpoint. Supports account planning cadence and proactive customer engagement management.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The result or disposition of the interaction as assessed by the internal participant. Supports win/loss analysis, customer satisfaction tracking, and account health scoring.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the interaction, reflecting urgency of follow-up or strategic importance of the customer engagement.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The Chemical Mfg product line or portfolio segment discussed during the interaction (e.g., performance materials, agricultural solutions, specialty chemicals). Supports product-level engagement analytics.']
  regulatory_topic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction involved discussion of regulatory or compliance topics such as REACH, TSCA, GHS, SDS requirements, or EPA/OSHA obligations. Supports regulatory engagement tracking and compliance reporting.']
  sales_territory_code varchar [note: 'The sales territory code associated with this interaction, derived from the account owner\'s territory assignment. Supports territory-level performance reporting and account planning.']
  sample_request_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested a product sample during this interaction. Triggers linkage to the sample request fulfillment process in the sales domain.']
  sds_request_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) during this interaction. Supports regulatory document fulfillment tracking per GHS and OSHA HazCom requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system from which this interaction record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration governance.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief title or subject line summarizing the topic of the interaction. Mirrors the Salesforce CRM activity subject field and supports keyword search and categorization.']
  technical_topic varchar [note: 'The primary technical subject matter discussed during the interaction (e.g., formulation support, application development, regulatory compliance, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) review, Certificate of Analysis (COA) query). Supports technical service analytics.']

  Note: 'Log of all customer-facing interactions and touchpoints including sales calls, technical visits, complaint calls, email exchanges, webinar attendance, trade show meetings, and portal logins. Captures interaction type, channel (phone, email, in-person, video, portal), date and time, duration, participants (internal and customer contacts), subject, outcome, follow-up actions, and linked opportunity or case. Sourced from Salesforce CRM activity records. Supports customer relationship management and account planning.'
}

Table customer.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for status_history']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account whose status changed. Links this history record to the master customer account entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user or system account that initiated the status change. Used for audit trail and accountability tracking in credit department compliance reviews.']
  reversed_history_status_history_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original account_status_history_id that this record reverses or corrects. Null when is_reversal is False. Enables complete audit chain reconstruction for compliance inquiries.']
  approval_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the approval workflow record or authorization document that sanctioned this status change. Required for credit department audit compliance and segregation of duties verification.']
  approval_reference_number varchar [note: 'External or human-readable reference number of the approval document, workflow ticket, or authorization memo that sanctioned this status change. Complements the approval_reference_id for audit trail documentation and cross-system traceability.']
  billing_block_code varchar [note: 'The SAP billing block code applied to or removed from the customer account as part of this status change. Null when no billing block change occurred. Supports accounts receivable and revenue recognition impact analysis.']
  blocked_order_count varchar [note: 'The number of open sales orders that were blocked or released as a direct result of this status change. Captured as a snapshot at transition time to quantify the operational impact of credit hold and suspension events.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a CAPA record when the status change was triggered by a quality or compliance corrective action outcome. Links customer account standing changes to the quality management system for traceability.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the status transition (e.g., CREDIT_REVIEW, INSOLVENCY, REACTIVATION, REGULATORY_ACTION, MA_ACTIVITY, PAYMENT_DEFAULT, CUSTOMER_REQUEST). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREDIT_REVIEW|INSOLVENCY|REACTIVATION|REGULATORY_ACTION|MA_ACTIVITY|PAYMENT_DEFAULT|CUSTOMER_REQUEST|CAPA_OUTCOME — promote to reference product]']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the status change beyond the standardized reason code. Captures nuanced circumstances such as specific regulatory action details, M&A transaction references, or credit review findings.']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the status change was recorded in the system. Serves as the EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this event log record. Distinct from effective_date which represents the business effective date.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text commentary entered by the initiating user or approver providing supplementary context for the status change. May include credit analyst notes, legal counsel observations, or customer service remarks.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code associated with this status change record. Identifies the legal entity within the chemical manufacturing enterprise for which the customer account status was changed. Supports multi-entity financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status history record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail completeness, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  credit_exposure_at_change decimal(18,2) [note: 'The customer\'s total outstanding credit exposure (open orders + open invoices + deliveries) in the base currency at the time of this status change. Provides context for credit hold and suspension decisions.']
  credit_limit_at_change decimal(18,2) [note: 'The customer\'s approved credit limit in the base currency at the time of this status change. Captured as a snapshot to support credit department audit compliance and historical credit exposure analysis.']
  credit_risk_category varchar [note: 'The credit risk classification assigned to the customer at the time of this status change. Captured as a snapshot to support credit portfolio risk analysis and audit trail for credit hold decisions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit limit and credit exposure amounts captured in this status history record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  days_in_previous_status varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days the customer account remained in the previous status before this transition. Captured at the time of transition for customer lifecycle analytics and credit risk reporting. Not a calculated metric — stored as a business fact at transition time.']
  delivery_block_code varchar [note: 'The SAP delivery block code applied to or removed from the customer account as part of this status change (e.g., 01 for credit block). Null when no delivery block change occurred. Supports supply chain impact analysis.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business date on which the new status became operationally effective. Used for customer lifecycle analytics, credit reporting, and regulatory inquiry responses. Distinct from the record creation timestamp.']
  end_date date [note: 'The business date on which this status record was superseded by a subsequent status change. Null if this is the current active status record. Enables point-in-time status reconstruction for audit and analytics.']
  insolvency_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Court case number, bankruptcy filing reference, or insolvency proceeding identifier when the status change was triggered by an insolvency filing. Null for non-insolvency transitions. Critical for accounts receivable risk management.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status change record represents a reversal or correction of a previously recorded status transition. True when the record corrects an erroneous prior status change. Used for audit trail integrity and credit department compliance.']
  ma_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the merger, acquisition, divestiture, or corporate restructuring event that triggered this status change. Null for non-M&A transitions. Supports customer hierarchy realignment and account consolidation tracking.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The customer account status assigned as a result of this transition event. Captures the \'to\' state in the status lifecycle transition pair (e.g., credit_hold in a transition from active→credit_hold).']
  notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer was formally notified of the status change. Null when notification_sent is False. Used to track compliance with contractual notification obligations and credit department procedures.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal notification was sent to the customer regarding this status change. Relevant for credit hold and suspension events where customer communication is required per contractual or regulatory obligations.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The SAP payment terms code (e.g., NT30, NT60, ZB14) applicable to the customer at the time of this status change. Captures any payment terms adjustment associated with the status transition such as tightening terms on credit hold.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The customer account status immediately before this transition event. Captures the \'from\' state in the status lifecycle transition pair (e.g., active in a transition from active→credit_hold).']
  reach_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status change was triggered by or is associated with a REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) compliance issue with the customer. Supports ECHA regulatory reporting and EHS compliance tracking.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The governing body or regulatory authority whose action triggered this status change. Applicable when triggering_business_event is \'regulatory_action\'. Supports regulatory inquiry responses and EHS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPA|OSHA|ECHA|FDA|DOT|TSCA|ACC|NFPA|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'External regulatory case number, enforcement action identifier, or compliance filing reference associated with this status change when triggered by a regulatory action (e.g., EPA enforcement, TSCA violation, OSHA citation). Null for non-regulatory transitions.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date by which this status should be reviewed for potential change (e.g., credit hold review date, suspension expiry review). Supports proactive credit management and customer lifecycle workflows.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'The SAP sales organization code under which this status change was processed. Relevant for multi-org chemical manufacturing enterprises where customer account status may vary by sales organization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this status change event. Identifies whether the change was initiated in SAP S/4HANA credit management, Salesforce CRM, manual entry, or another integrated system.']
  source_system_change_reference varchar [note: 'The native change document number or record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP change document number, Salesforce record ID). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  transition_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the direction and nature of the status change. Distinguishes upgrades (e.g., prospect→active, credit_hold→active) from downgrades (e.g., active→credit_hold, active→suspended) and closures for lifecycle analytics.']
  triggering_business_event varchar [note: 'Classification of the specific business event that triggered this status transition. Distinguishes between credit review outcomes, M&A activity, insolvency filings, regulatory actions, and other lifecycle events for customer analytics and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_review|ma_activity|insolvency_filing|regulatory_action|payment_default|customer_request|periodic_review|reactivation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status history record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks corrections, reversals, or enrichment updates applied after initial ingestion.']

  Note: 'Audit trail of customer account lifecycle status transitions, capturing every change in account standing over time. Records transitions such as prospect→active, active→credit hold, credit hold→suspended, suspended→inactive, and reactivation events. Stores previous status, new status, effective date, change reason code, initiating user, approval reference, triggering business event (credit review outcome, M&A activity, insolvency filing, regulatory action), and reversal flag. Required for credit department audit compliance, customer lifecycle analytics, and regulatory inquiries into account standing history.'
}

Table customer.customer_lead {
  customer_lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer lead record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the customer_lead data product. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead object.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated or influenced this lead. Used for campaign ROI measurement, marketing attribution, and budget allocation decisions. References the Salesforce Campaign object.']
  converted_account_id varchar [note: 'Salesforce Account ID of the customer account created upon lead conversion. Populated only when lead_status = \'converted\'. Enables traceability from lead origin to active customer account for pipeline attribution and cohort analysis.']
  converted_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Salesforce Opportunity ID created upon lead conversion. Populated only when lead_status = \'converted\'. Links the lead origin to the downstream commercial deal for end-to-end pipeline attribution.']
  primary_customer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Salesforce User ID of the sales representative currently responsible for working this lead. Used for workload balancing, performance reporting, and commission attribution.']
  annual_revenue_band varchar [note: 'Estimated annual revenue band of the prospective customer\'s organization (e.g., \'<$10M\', \'$10M–$50M\', \'$50M–$250M\', \'>$250M\'). Used for strategic account tiering and prioritization. Sourced from third-party firmographic data or sales rep estimation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: <$10M|$10M-$50M|$50M-$250M|$250M-$1B|>$1B — promote to reference product]']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the prospective customer\'s primary operating location. Used for geographic clustering analysis and logistics proximity assessment for potential supply chain planning.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the prospective customer organization. Represents the business entity being targeted for chemical product sales. Used for account matching and deduplication prior to conversion.']
  company_size_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the prospective customer\'s organization size. \'small\' = <50 employees, \'medium\' = 50–499, \'large\' = 500–4999, \'enterprise\' = 5000+. Used for segmentation, pricing strategy, and sales motion selection (transactional vs. strategic).']
  contact_first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the primary contact person at the prospective customer organization. Captured from trade show registrations, web inquiries, referrals, or cold outreach.']
  contact_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the primary contact person at the prospective customer organization. Combined with contact_first_name to form the full contact identity.']
  contact_title varchar [note: 'Professional job title or role of the primary contact at the prospective customer (e.g., Procurement Manager, R&D Director, Plant Engineer). Informs sales approach and technical engagement strategy.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date on which the lead was converted into a formal customer account and/or sales opportunity in the CRM. Marks the end of the lead lifecycle and the beginning of the account/opportunity lifecycle. Used to calculate lead-to-conversion cycle time KPI.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the country of the prospective customer\'s primary operating location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Used for geographic segmentation, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and export compliance screening.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer lead record was first created in the source Salesforce CRM system. Represents the moment the lead entered the pipeline. Used for lead age calculation, SLA tracking, and pipeline velocity reporting.']
  crm_lead_code varchar [note: 'Native Salesforce CRM Lead record identifier (18-character Salesforce ID). Used for traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the lead was disqualified from the pipeline (e.g., \'no budget\', \'competitor locked in\', \'wrong product fit\', \'regulatory barrier\', \'no decision maker access\'). Populated only when lead_status = \'disqualified\'. Used for pipeline quality analysis and process improvement.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the lead contact. Used for digital outreach, campaign tracking, and CRM communication logging. Subject to GDPR and CCPA consent requirements.']
  estimated_annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual revenue potential from this lead expressed in US dollars. Derived from volume estimates and indicative pricing. Used for pipeline valuation, sales target tracking, and territory planning. Distinct from volume as it reflects commercial value.']
  estimated_annual_volume_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Prospective annual purchase volume of chemical products expressed in kilograms. Used to prioritize lead qualification effort, assess strategic value, and forecast pipeline contribution. Captured during initial qualification conversation or estimated from industry benchmarks.']
  export_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the prospective customer or the chemical products of interest are subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, or dual-use chemical restrictions). When True, requires screening against denied party lists before conversion and shipment.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The end-market industry sector of the prospective customer (e.g., Automotive, Construction, Electronics, Consumer Goods, Agriculture, Pharmaceuticals, Coatings). Used for market segmentation, product-to-market alignment, and territory planning. Aligns with NAICS/SIC classification.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'The originating channel or method through which the lead was acquired. Used for marketing attribution, ROI analysis, and sales pipeline source reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade_show|referral|web_inquiry|cold_outreach|distributor|conference|digital_campaign|inbound_call — promote to reference product]']
  lead_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of the lead in the sales qualification lifecycle. \'new\' = recently captured, \'working\' = active outreach in progress, \'qualified\' = meets criteria for conversion, \'converted\' = promoted to formal account/opportunity, \'disqualified\' = does not meet criteria, \'recycled\' = re-entered pipeline after prior disqualification. Drives CRM workflow automation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer lead record in the source Salesforce CRM system. Used for incremental data ingestion, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  next_follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next sales outreach or follow-up action with this lead. Used by sales representatives to manage their activity pipeline and ensure timely engagement. Drives CRM task and reminder generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing qualitative context about the lead, including conversation summaries, specific application requirements, competitive intelligence, or special handling instructions. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead Description field.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number of the lead contact. Used for direct sales outreach and qualification calls.']
  product_interest varchar [note: 'The chemical product line, product family, or specific chemical compound the lead has expressed interest in (e.g., specialty polymers, performance coatings, agricultural intermediates, solvents). Drives assignment to the appropriate technical sales specialist.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the lead was formally assessed and determined to meet the minimum criteria for advancement in the sales pipeline (e.g., confirmed budget, authority, need, and timeline — BANT qualification). Marks transition from \'new\' or \'working\' to \'qualified\' status.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Sales representative\'s qualitative assessment of the lead\'s near-term conversion likelihood. \'hot\' = high urgency and fit, \'warm\' = moderate interest, \'cold\' = low engagement or long-horizon. Used to prioritize outreach cadence.']
  referral_source_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual, company, or partner who referred this lead. Populated when lead_source = \'referral\'. Used for referral program tracking and partner relationship management.']
  regulatory_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead involves products or applications subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny (e.g., REACH-restricted substances, TSCA new chemical notifications, EPA hazardous waste regulations, export-controlled chemicals). When True, triggers compliance review before conversion.']
  sales_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic or market-based sales territory to which this lead is assigned. Used for territory performance reporting, quota allocation, and rep assignment routing.']
  sample_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead contact has requested a product sample for laboratory evaluation or formulation testing. Sample requests indicate active technical qualification and are a key pipeline progression signal in chemical B2B sales.']
  score varchar [note: 'Numeric score assigned by the CRM marketing automation engine reflecting the lead\'s engagement level and fit with ideal customer profile. Typically ranges 0–100. Inputs include email opens, web visits, content downloads, and firmographic match. Used to trigger automated nurture workflows.']
  sds_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead contact has requested a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for a chemical product of interest. SDS requests are a strong buying signal in chemical sales and indicate technical evaluation stage. Aligns with GHS SDS documentation requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this lead record was sourced. For the customer_lead product, this is always \'salesforce_crm\'. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the prospective customer\'s primary operating location. Used for regional sales analysis, territory assignment, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping (e.g., state-level EPA or OSHA requirements).']
  technical_inquiry_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead has submitted a technical inquiry requiring specialist response (e.g., formulation compatibility, regulatory compliance documentation, application engineering support). Triggers routing to technical sales or application development team.']
  trade_show_name varchar [note: 'Name of the trade show, industry conference, or event at which the lead was captured (e.g., \'ChemShow 2024\', \'ACHEMA\', \'ACS National Meeting\'). Populated when lead_source = \'trade_show\' or \'conference\'. Used for event ROI analysis.']

  Note: 'Prospective customer leads and new business development targets in the chemical sales pipeline, representing potential customer identities before they become formal accounts. Captures lead source (trade show, referral, web inquiry, cold outreach), company name, contact name, industry vertical, chemical product interest, estimated annual volume potential, lead status (new, qualified, converted, disqualified), assigned sales rep, qualification date, and conversion to account date. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead object. Owned by the customer domain because a lead represents a prospective customer identity — distinct from a sales opportunity which tracks commercial deal progression.'
}

Table customer.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the customer service case or support ticket. Primary key for the case data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that initiated or is associated with this case.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical product involved in this case, particularly relevant for quality complaints, technical inquiries, and documentation requests (SDS, COA, COC).']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer contact person who submitted or is the primary point of contact for this case.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal customer service representative or technical service engineer assigned to resolve this case.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Invoice Dispute Case Management requires linking each case to the specific invoice it concerns, enabling traceability for financial reconciliation and regulatory reporting.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order Issue Management report links each service case to the originating sales order for root‑cause analysis and compliance tracking.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Allows linking support cases to related R&D projects, essential for issue escalation and regulatory audit trails.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'The internal manufacturing batch reference number associated with the product involved in the case. Distinct from lot_number (which is the customer-facing identifier); batch_reference links to the production batch record in SAP PP and MES systems.']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) record created in SAP QM in response to a quality complaint case. Links the customer case to the formal quality improvement workflow. Populated only when a CAPA has been initiated.']
  case_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the customer issue, request, or complaint as reported by the customer. For quality complaints, includes the customer\'s account of the product defect, observed symptoms, and business impact.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable case reference number used in all customer communications and cross-system references. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Number field.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the case lifecycle. Open: newly created, awaiting assignment. In Progress: actively being worked. Pending Customer: awaiting customer response or information. Resolved: solution provided, awaiting confirmation. Closed: fully completed and confirmed.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer case by business function. Technical Support covers formulation and application assistance; Order Issue covers shipment discrepancies and billing disputes; SDS (Safety Data Sheet) Request and COA (Certificate of Analysis) Request cover documentation fulfillment; Regulatory Inquiry covers REACH, TSCA, GHS compliance questions; Quality Complaint covers OOS (Out of Specification) product issues.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the customer submitted or initiated the case. Supports channel analytics and resource allocation for customer service operations.']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date on which the case was fully closed after customer confirmation of resolution. Distinct from resolved_date; a case may be resolved but remain open pending customer acknowledgment.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with the product lot involved in the case. Used for quality complaints to cross-reference the analytical results at time of shipment against the customer\'s reported issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the case record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and is used for SLA calculation baseline.']
  credit_note_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial credit note has been issued to the customer as part of the resolution of a quality complaint or order discrepancy case. When True, a corresponding credit memo exists in SAP FI/CO.']
  credit_note_number varchar [note: 'The SAP document number of the credit note issued to the customer for a quality complaint or order discrepancy resolution. Populated only when credit_note_issued is True. Enables financial reconciliation between the case and the accounting entry.']
  crm_case_code varchar [note: 'The native case identifier from Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer cases. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system.']
  customer_satisfaction_outcome varchar [note: 'Categorical outcome of the customer satisfaction assessment following case closure. Derived from the customer\'s qualitative feedback or survey response. Complements the numeric customer_satisfaction_rating for segmentation and reporting.']
  customer_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric customer satisfaction score collected after case resolution, typically on a 1-5 scale (1=Very Dissatisfied, 5=Very Satisfied). Sourced from post-resolution customer survey. Used as a KPI (Key Performance Indicator) for customer service quality.']
  eight_d_report_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the 8D (Eight Disciplines) problem-solving report generated for a quality complaint case. The 8D methodology is the standard structured problem-solving workflow for quality complaints in chemical manufacturing. Populated when an 8D report has been formally initiated.']
  escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case has been formally escalated beyond the initially assigned service representative, either due to SLA breach, severity, or customer request. Escalated cases trigger management notification workflows.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which the case was formally escalated. Populated only when escalated is True. Used to measure escalation response time and identify systemic SLA failure patterns.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason the case was escalated. SLA Breach: resolution deadline exceeded. Customer Request: customer demanded escalation. Safety Concern: potential EHS (Environment Health and Safety) implication. Regulatory Risk: potential regulatory non-compliance. Management Directive: escalated by management decision.']
  lims_investigation_reference varchar [note: 'The investigation record identifier in LabWare LIMS linked to this case for OOS (Out of Specification) product investigations. Populated when an OOS investigation has been initiated in the LIMS system in response to a quality complaint.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing lot or batch lot number of the chemical product involved in a quality complaint or technical inquiry. Enables traceability back to the production batch for root cause investigation. Populated primarily for quality_complaint and technical_support case types.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the case record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quality complaint involves a product that has been confirmed as Out of Specification (OOS) against the product\'s Certificate of Analysis (COA) or specification sheet. When True, triggers mandatory OOS investigation workflow in LabWare LIMS.']
  opened_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer case was officially opened and entered into the system. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) calculation and aging analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the case, driving SLA (Service Level Agreement) response and resolution targets. Critical priority is reserved for safety-related quality complaints or regulatory non-compliance issues.']
  rci_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Root Cause Investigation (RCI) triggered by a quality complaint. Tracks whether the investigation into the product defect or OOS (Out of Specification) condition has been initiated, is ongoing, or has been completed. Applicable only to quality_complaint case type.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory framework or standard that is the subject of a regulatory inquiry case. Identifies whether the customer\'s inquiry relates to REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals), TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act), GHS (Globally Harmonized System), or other applicable regulation. Populated for regulatory_inquiry case type.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the actions taken and solution provided to resolve the customer case. For quality complaints, includes findings from the RCI (Root Cause Investigation) and corrective actions applied.']
  resolved_date date [note: 'The date on which the case was marked as resolved, meaning the solution or response was provided to the customer. Used to calculate actual resolution time against SLA targets.']
  sds_document_number varchar [note: 'The document control number of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) provided or referenced in response to an SDS request case. Enables traceability of which SDS version was fulfilled. Applicable to sds_request case type.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the issue impact on the customer\'s operations or safety. Distinct from priority (which drives internal response urgency), severity reflects the actual business or safety impact of the reported issue. Critical severity triggers mandatory CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) and RCI (Root Cause Investigation) workflows.']
  sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case resolution exceeded the SLA due date, constituting an SLA breach. Used for service quality reporting, customer penalty tracking, and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  sla_due_date date [note: 'The contractual or policy-defined deadline by which the case must be resolved, based on case type, priority, and applicable customer SLA agreement. Drives escalation workflows when breached.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this case record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief, descriptive title summarizing the customer issue or request. Used as the primary display label in case management queues and customer communications.']

  Note: 'Customer service cases and support tickets covering the full spectrum of customer issues: technical assistance requests, order discrepancies, documentation requests (SDS, COA, COC), regulatory inquiries, and quality/OOS product complaints. This is the single source of truth for all customer-initiated issues and complaints. Captures case number, case type (technical support, order issue, SDS request, COA request, regulatory inquiry, quality complaint), priority, severity, status (open, in progress, resolved, closed), assigned service rep, SLA due date, resolution description, customer satisfaction rating. For quality complaints: product involved, lot number, batch reference, COA reference, complaint description, root cause investigation (RCI) status, CAPA reference, severity classification, credit note issued flag, and customer satisfaction outcome. Integrates with LabWare LIMS for OOS investigation, SAP QM for CAPA tracking, and Salesforce CRM Case object as system of record. Supports 8D problem-solving workflow for quality complaints.'
}

Table customer.account_document {
  account_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer account document record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account to which this document belongs. Links the document to the customer master record.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Document creation audit requires linking to the employee who authored the account document for compliance and traceability.']
  superseded_by_document_account_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer account_document record that replaces this document when it has been superseded by a revised version. Enables document version chain traceability for audit and compliance purposes. Null if this is the current active version.']
  acknowledged_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the customer representative who acknowledged the document. Captured for audit trail and product liability defense purposes. Particularly important for SDS acknowledgments under OSHA HazCom.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer formally acknowledged receipt or acceptance of the document. Required for SDS acknowledgment audit trails under OSHA HazCom compliance. Null if acknowledgment has not yet been received.']
  acknowledgment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer acknowledged receipt of the document, particularly for SDS/MSDS acknowledgments. Electronic acknowledgment via digital signature, signed physical copy, customer portal confirmation, email confirmation, or fax. Null or not_applicable for documents that do not require acknowledgment.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the internal authority who approved this document record for use in customer account management. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails and quality management system documentation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the document record was formally approved by the designated internal authority. Provides precise audit trail for compliance inspections and product liability defense.']
  coa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document specifies or mandates that a Certificate of Analysis (COA) must accompany product shipments to this customer. Drives order fulfillment and quality documentation workflows.']
  coc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document specifies or mandates that a Certificate of Compliance (COC) must accompany product shipments to this customer. Supports quality assurance and regulatory compliance for customer deliveries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this document record was first created in the system. Provides the foundational audit trail entry for the document lifecycle.']
  customer_labeling_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of customer-specific labeling requirements captured in this document, such as proprietary label formats, language requirements, GHS pictogram specifications, or private-label branding instructions. Relevant for product packaging and distribution compliance.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the document for reporting and analytics. Enables segmentation across regulatory compliance, commercial agreements, quality certifications, safety documentation, trade compliance, and customer-specific requirements.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or reference number assigned to the document, such as a certificate number, agreement reference, or license number issued by the issuing authority or internal document management system.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document. Active documents are valid and in force. Expired documents have passed their expiry date. Pending renewal documents are approaching expiry and renewal is in progress. Superseded documents have been replaced by a newer version. Revoked documents have been invalidated by the issuing authority.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the document as it appears on the official record, such as \'ISO 9001 Quality Management Certificate\', \'SDS Acknowledgment Agreement\', or \'Customer EHS Audit Report\'.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document by its business purpose. Drives workflow routing, retention rules, and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: signed_agreement|qualification_certificate|iso_certification|ehs_audit_report|import_export_license|sds_acknowledgment|labeling_requirement|coa_requirement|reach_declaration|other — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the document becomes legally or operationally binding and valid. For certifications, this is the issuance date. For agreements, this is the contract start date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the document ceases to be valid or requires renewal. Null for open-ended documents with no defined expiration. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance alerts.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Format of the stored document file. PDF is the standard for regulatory submissions and signed agreements. Used to determine rendering and archival requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|JPG|PNG|XML|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_reference varchar [note: 'Storage path, URL, or document management system reference pointing to the actual document file (e.g., SharePoint path, S3 URI, Salesforce ContentDocument ID). Enables retrieval of the source document for audit and inspection purposes.']
  ghs_revision varchar [note: 'The revision number of the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals (GHS) standard under which the associated SDS was prepared (e.g., \'Rev 7\', \'Rev 8\', \'Rev 9\'). Required to confirm SDS currency and compliance with current GHS requirements.']
  import_export_license_number varchar [note: 'Official license number assigned by the relevant trade authority for import or export of controlled chemicals. Populated when document_type is import_export_license. Required for trade compliance and customs clearance documentation.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal comments, context, or instructions related to this document record. Not shared externally. Used by account managers, compliance teams, and customer service representatives to capture relevant context.']
  iso_standard_code varchar [note: 'The specific ISO standard number referenced by this document (e.g., \'ISO 9001:2015\', \'ISO 14001:2015\', \'ISO 45001:2018\'). Populated for ISO certification documents. Null for non-ISO documents.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, regulatory body, or certification body that issued or authenticated the document (e.g., \'Bureau Veritas\', \'TÜV Rheinland\', \'EPA\', \'ECHA\', \'U.S. Customs and Border Protection\'). Critical for validating document authenticity.']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country in which the document was issued. Used for trade compliance, import/export license validation, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the import/export license covers import activities, export activities, or both. Populated when document_type is import_export_license. Null or not_applicable for non-license documents.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this document record was last updated in the system. Supports change tracking, audit trail completeness, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'ECHA-assigned REACH registration number associated with this document when the document relates to REACH compliance declarations or substance registration. Format follows ECHA numbering convention (e.g., \'01-XXXXXXXXXX-XX-XXXX\').']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The regulatory jurisdiction or geographic scope under which the document is valid (e.g., \'USA\', \'EU\', \'APAC\', \'Global\'). Determines which regulatory framework governs the document\'s requirements and enforcement.']
  renewal_reminder_date date [note: 'Date on which an automated or manual renewal reminder should be triggered, typically set a defined number of days before the expiry date. Supports proactive compliance management and prevents lapses in required documentation.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document requires periodic renewal before the expiry date. True for time-limited certifications, licenses, and agreements. Drives automated renewal reminder workflows and compliance monitoring.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was last reviewed internally for validity, completeness, and continued compliance. Supports periodic document review cycles required under ISO 9001 and regulatory frameworks.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the internal staff member who reviewed and validated this document for completeness and compliance. Part of the document control audit trail.']
  sds_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document record represents a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) acknowledgment from the customer. True when the document type is an SDS/MSDS receipt acknowledgment required under OSHA HazCom.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this document record originated. Salesforce CRM is the primary system of record for customer account documents. Supports data lineage and integration traceability.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The primary key or unique identifier of this document record in the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce ContentDocument ID, SAP document number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing.']
  tsca_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document serves as evidence of TSCA compliance for the customer account. True when the document is a TSCA certification, import certification, or compliance declaration under the Toxic Substances Control Act.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier of the document as assigned by the issuing authority or internal document control system (e.g., \'1.0\', \'Rev B\', \'2024-v3\'). Critical for document control and audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Repository of customer account-level documents and acknowledgments including signed agreements, customer qualification certificates, ISO certifications, EHS audit reports, import/export licenses, customer-specific labeling requirements, and Safety Data Sheet (SDS/MSDS) acknowledgment records. Captures document type, document title, file reference, version, effective date, expiry date, issuing authority, document status, acknowledgment method (for SDS: electronic, signed, portal), GHS revision, and regulatory jurisdiction. Provides audit trail for regulatory inspections, product liability defense, and GHS/OSHA HazCom compliance.'
}

Table customer.supply_contract {
  supply_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the supply_contract association']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the raw material master']
  agreed_price decimal [note: 'Unit price agreed for the material in this contract']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the supply contract expires']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the supply contract becomes effective']
  volume_kg decimal [note: 'Total volume of material agreed for the contract (kilograms)']

  Note: 'Represents a contractual agreement between a customer account and a raw material, capturing the terms of supply such as start/end dates, price, and volume. Each record links one account to one material and stores attributes that belong to the contract itself.. Existence Justification: Customer accounts negotiate and manage supply contracts for raw materials. Each contract links one account to one material and records start/end dates, agreed price, and volume, allowing many accounts to contract for many materials and vice‑versa.'
}

Table production.manufacturing_order {
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for manufacturing_order']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for order fulfillment: each manufacturing order must be linked to the customer account that placed the order, enabling production scheduling, invoicing, and customer‑specific reporting.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the Bill of Materials defining the raw materials, intermediates, and components required for this production order.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this order to a multi-batch production campaign. Used for extended runs of the same product to optimize changeover and yield.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Production order execution needs product master to fetch specifications, SDS, and regulatory compliance for the material being produced.']
  control_recipe_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the control recipe instance downloaded to the DCS (Honeywell Experion, Siemens PCS 7) for automated execution of process phases and unit procedures.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Production Cost Allocation Report: each manufacturing order must be charged to a cost center for monthly cost accounting.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.demand_forecast. Business justification: Order release uses the demand forecast that generated the manufacturing order; linking enables the Order Release report.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the production planner or system user who created the production order.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula. Business justification: Production Order Specification Report requires the exact formula to be produced; planners assign a formula to each manufacturing order.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Manufacturing Order Planning uses material_master for specs, hazard data, and costing; linking provides single source of truth for each order.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Margin tracking: each manufacturing order references the sales price list used for the customer order to align production cost with pricing.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Production planning ties each manufacturing order to the purchase order that supplies its raw materials, enabling material availability and cost tracking.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: PRODUCTION: Production orders are generated from approved sales quotes; linking enables traceability, cost accounting, and revenue recognition.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Required for new‑product launch reporting linking each manufacturing order to its originating R&D project for cost allocation and regulatory traceability.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production routing or master recipe defining the sequence of operations, process steps, and control parameters.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Required for Production Order Material Allocation Report linking each order to the allocated stock position.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the primary work center (production line, reactor, blending unit) assigned to execute this order.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred for this production order, settled from material consumption, labor confirmations, and activity allocations.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when production execution completed, including final operation confirmation and goods receipt posting.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of finished product manufactured and confirmed via goods receipt. Used for yield calculation and variance analysis.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when production execution began, typically captured from MES or DCS system when the first operation started.']
  actual_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated yield percentage achieved in this production run: (actual output / theoretical output) * 100. Key performance indicator for process efficiency.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Unique lot or batch number assigned to the output of this production order. Critical for traceability, COA issuance, and regulatory compliance.']
  cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned total cost for this production order, including raw materials, labor, utilities, and overhead. Used for cost accounting and variance analysis.']
  cost_variance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that actual cost exceeded planned cost by a threshold percentage, triggering variance investigation and root cause analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production order record was first created in the ERP system, marking the beginning of the order lifecycle.']
  dcs_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Batch identifier assigned by the DCS (Honeywell Experion, Siemens PCS 7) for automated process control and SCADA data historian linkage.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that one or more process deviations, out-of-specification events, or quality exceptions occurred during production execution.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the quality inspection lot created for this production order output, linking to LIMS test results and Certificate of Analysis.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the production order, supporting change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the production order record, capturing any changes to quantities, dates, or status.']
  master_recipe_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master recipe or Process Instruction (PI) sheet governing the synthesis, reaction conditions, and quality parameters for this product.']
  mes_integration_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier used for integration with MES systems. Links ERP production order to MES work order for real-time execution tracking.']
  moc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for any Management of Change request associated with this production order, documenting process deviations or recipe modifications.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the production order, externally visible and used across systems. Typically follows plant-specific numbering conventions.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production order: created (planned but not released), released (authorized for execution), started (production begun), in_progress (active manufacturing), confirmed (goods receipt posted), technically_complete (production finished, pending final settlement), closed (financially settled), cancelled (order voided). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|started|in_progress|confirmed|technically_complete|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the manufacturing process mode: batch (discrete lots), continuous (steady-state flow), semi-continuous (hybrid), campaign (extended multi-batch run), rework (reprocessing off-spec material), or trial (R&D pilot run).']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target quantity of finished product to be manufactured, as specified in the production order. Expressed in the base unit of measure.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the manufacturing plant or production facility where the order is executed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Scheduling priority ranking for the production order. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by MES and DCS systems for sequencing and resource allocation.']
  process_order_type varchar [note: 'Specific classification of the order purpose: standard (normal production), rework (reprocessing), trial (R&D pilot), validation (process qualification), cleaning (CIP campaign).']
  production_version varchar [note: 'Four-digit version number linking the specific BOM and routing combination used for this order. Enables traceability of recipe changes over time.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicator that finished product must undergo quality control inspection and COA issuance before release to inventory or shipment.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'Planned date for production order completion, including all manufacturing operations and goods receipt confirmation.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for production order to begin execution, as determined by production scheduling and capacity planning.']
  settlement_rule varchar [note: 'Configuration defining how production order costs are settled to inventory, cost centers, or profitability segments upon order closure.']
