[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process|material|equipment|documentation|environmental|safety|quality|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hold_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a quality or production hold was placed on the affected batch, if applicable.']
  hold_released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was released and the batch was cleared for further processing or shipment.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current hold status of the affected batch or material: no_hold (no restriction), quality_hold (QA hold pending investigation), production_hold (manufacturing stopped), released (hold lifted after investigation).']
  immediate_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate corrective actions taken at the time of deviation detection to contain the issue.']
  investigation_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the root cause investigation was completed and documented.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this deviation record was last updated or modified.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number of the affected material, used for traceability and genealogy.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this deviation requires a formal Management of Change (MOC) process to modify procedures, equipment, or processes.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this deviation resulted in an out-of-specification (OOS) quality result.']
  oot_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this deviation represents an out-of-trend (OOT) condition in process or quality data.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit operation sequence number within the production order routing where the deviation occurred.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the manufacturing plant where the deviation occurred.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Description of the preventive actions taken to eliminate potential causes of similar deviations in the future.']
  production_deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the deviation event, including what was observed and the circumstances.']
  production_deviation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deviation: open (newly reported), under_investigation (root cause analysis in progress), pending_approval (awaiting management review), closed (resolved and approved), cancelled (determined not a deviation).']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the deviation was reported to the relevant regulatory authority, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this deviation must be reported to regulatory authorities (FDA, EPA, OSHA, etc.) based on severity and impact.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deviation was formally reported and logged in the system.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the deviation after investigation, typically determined through RCI (Root Cause Investigation) methods.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause: human_error, equipment_failure, material_defect, process_design, environmental, procedural, or unknown (if investigation incomplete). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: human_error|equipment_failure|material_defect|process_design|environmental|procedural|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the deviation: critical (immediate product/safety impact), major (significant quality impact), minor (low impact, correctable).']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific work center or production unit where the deviation occurred.']

  Note: 'Records deviations, non-conformances, OOS events, and production holds that occur during manufacturing. Captures deviation type, severity, affected batch, hold status, root cause, and resolution actions.'
}

Table production.process_change_record {
  process_change_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Management of Change (MOC) record. Primary key for process change tracking and PSM compliance.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person who approved the change.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: MOC (Management of Change) tracks regulatory status of affected chemicals; FK to CAS registry provides authoritative data.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Process change records must link to the affected chemical product to assess impact on SDS and regulatory filings.']
  management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'FK to ehs.management_of_change.management_of_change_id — Production process changes must link to master MOC for regulatory traceability. Without this, cannot generate a complete MOC audit trail across all affected domains.']
  primary_process_initiator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person who initiated the change request.']
  process_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.process_order. Business justification: Associate process change records with the process order they modify; enables tracking of changes per order.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred for implementing the change, captured after completion.']
  actual_implementation_date date [note: 'Actual date when the change was implemented in the production environment.']
  affected_equipment_tag varchar [note: 'Equipment tag number or identifier for the specific equipment affected by the change, referencing P&ID documentation.']
  affected_process_unit varchar [note: 'Identification of the process unit, production line, or manufacturing area impacted by the change.']
  affected_sop_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SOP document that requires revision as a result of this change.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the MOC received final approval to proceed with implementation.']
  approval_workflow_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the multi-level approval workflow (e.g., supervisor review, engineering review, safety review, management approval).']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who provided final approval for the MOC.']
  change_category varchar [note: 'Duration classification of the change: temporary (time-limited trial), permanent (long-term modification), or emergency (immediate safety-driven change).']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of change being requested: process (reaction parameters, operating conditions), equipment (hardware modifications), material (raw material substitutions), procedure (SOP updates), facility (infrastructure changes), or software (control system updates).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MOC record was first created in the system.']
  document_references varchar [note: 'List of supporting documents associated with the MOC, including P&IDs, SOPs, risk assessments, training materials, and approval forms.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of implementing the change, including materials, labor, downtime, and training expenses.']
  hazop_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the HAZOP study conducted to evaluate the change.']
  implementation_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the implementation process, any deviations from the plan, and observations during execution.']
  initiator_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area of the change initiator (e.g., Production, Engineering, Quality, R&D).']
  initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who initiated the MOC request.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business and technical justification for the change, including expected benefits, cost savings, safety improvements, or regulatory compliance drivers.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MOC record was last updated or modified.']
  moc_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the MOC record. Externally-known unique number used for tracking and referencing the change across systems and documentation.']
  pha_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the associated PHA or HAZOP study conducted to evaluate the change.']
  planned_implementation_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for implementing the approved change in the production environment.']
  post_implementation_review_date date [note: 'Date when the post-implementation review was conducted to assess the effectiveness and safety of the change.']
  post_implementation_review_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings from the post-implementation review, including any corrective actions or lessons learned.']
  post_implementation_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-implementation review is required to verify the change achieved its objectives and did not introduce new hazards.']
  process_change_record_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the proposed change, including technical specifications, scope, and rationale for the modification.']
  process_change_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MOC record in the approval and implementation workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|implementation|completed|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency that was notified (e.g., EPA, OSHA, ECHA, FDA, state environmental agency).']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory notification was submitted to the appropriate governing body.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory agencies (EPA, OSHA, ECHA, FDA) must be notified of this change due to compliance requirements.']
  risk_assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal risk assessment (PHA, HAZOP, or other methodology) is required for this change.']
  risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the risk assessment findings, including identified hazards, risk levels, and mitigation measures.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the change based on the risk assessment (low, medium, high, critical).']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the nature of the change for quick identification and reporting.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all required training for affected personnel was completed.']
  training_description varchar [note: 'Description of the training content and scope provided to personnel affected by the change.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operator or staff training is required before or after implementing the change.']

  Note: 'Management of Change (MOC) record tracking approved changes to production processes, equipment configurations, raw material substitutions, or operating procedures. Captures MOC number, change type (process, equipment, material, procedure, facility), change description, initiator, affected process/equipment, risk assessment summary, PHA/HAZOP reference, approval workflow status, implementation date, post-implementation review date, training requirements, and regulatory notification flag. Required for PSM compliance (OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119) and process safety management.'
}

Table production.dcs_event_log {
  dcs_event_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each DCS/SCADA event log entry. Primary key for the event log. Inferred role: EVENT_LOG.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the operator who acknowledged the event, performed the action, or authored the shift entry. Links to workforce/employee master data. Required for audit trail and accountability per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing order or production batch active at the time of the event. Links event to specific production run for lot genealogy and batch record traceability. Maps to SAP PP Production Order.']
  research_process_simulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_process_simulation. Business justification: Connects DCS events to the HYSYS simulation model used for process design, enabling validation of control actions against the simulated baseline.']
  acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event (typically an alarm or interlock) has been acknowledged by an operator. True if acknowledged, False if unacknowledged. Critical for alarm management and operator response tracking.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event was acknowledged by an operator. Null if not yet acknowledged. Used to calculate alarm response time and operator performance metrics. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  alarm_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Configured alarm threshold value that triggered the alarm for alarm-type events. Null for non-alarm events. Used for alarm rationalization and setpoint optimization.']
  alarm_state varchar [note: 'Current state of the alarm for alarm-type events. Active: alarm condition present. Cleared: alarm condition resolved. Suppressed: alarm temporarily disabled. Shelved: alarm acknowledged but not yet cleared. Null for non-alarm events.']
  batches_completed varchar [note: 'Number of production batches completed during the shift for shift summary events. Null for non-shift-summary events. Key performance indicator for shift productivity.']
  batches_in_progress varchar [note: 'Number of production batches in progress at shift end for shift summary events. Null for non-shift-summary events. Critical for shift handover continuity.']
  control_mode_new varchar [note: 'New control mode after a mode_change event. Null for non-mode-change events. Tracks transitions between automatic, manual, cascade, remote, and local control modes.']
  control_mode_previous varchar [note: 'Previous control mode before a mode_change event. Null for non-mode-change events. Tracks transitions between automatic, manual, cascade, remote, and local control modes.']
  dcs_node_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DCS/SCADA node or controller that generated the event. Maps to specific Honeywell Experion or Siemens PCS 7 controller instances within the distributed control architecture.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the event in seconds. For alarms: time from activation to clearance. For interlocks: trip duration. For mode changes: time in transient state. Null for instantaneous events.']
  environmental_readings varchar [note: 'Summary of environmental monitoring readings during the shift (e.g., emissions, VOC levels, wastewater parameters). Null if not recorded. Supports EPA and environmental compliance reporting.']
  equipment_issues varchar [note: 'Free-text description of equipment problems, malfunctions, or maintenance needs identified during the shift. Null if no issues. Used for shift handover and maintenance planning.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the event. For alarms: alarm message text. For operator actions: action description. For shift handovers: handover notes and instructions. For shift summaries: comprehensive shift narrative.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the event occurred in the DCS/SCADA system. Critical for chronological event sequencing, alarm analysis, and incident investigation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event type. Discriminates between alarms, interlocks, operator actions, shift documentation, and system state changes. Critical for event filtering, alarm rationalization, and safety analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alarm|interlock_trip|operator_override|setpoint_change|mode_change|shift_handover|shift_summary|shift_observation|system_fault|process_deviation|safety_event — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  handover_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions and action items for the incoming shift. Null for non-handover events. Ensures operational continuity and clear communication between shifts.']
  interlock_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the safety interlock that tripped for interlock_trip events. Null for non-interlock events. Links to interlock logic documentation and safety instrumented systems (SIS).']
  material_shortages varchar [note: 'Free-text description of raw material shortages or supply issues encountered during the shift. Null if none. Used for shift handover and supply chain escalation.']
  operator_count varchar [note: 'Number of operators on duty during the shift for shift-related events. Null for non-shift events. Used for staffing analysis and workload assessment.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the event. Critical and high priority events require immediate operator response. Used for alarm rationalization and operator workload analysis per ISA-18.2 guidelines.']
  process_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual process measurement value at the time of the event (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow rate, pH). Null if not applicable. Used for alarm analysis and process deviation investigation.']
  process_value_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the process value (e.g., degC, bar, pH, kg/h, L/min, ppm). Null if process_value is null. Critical for engineering analysis.']
  quality_holds varchar [note: 'Free-text description of batches or materials placed on quality hold during the shift. Null if none. Critical for QA/QC tracking and batch disposition.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event log record was created in the lakehouse silver layer. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the event occurred in the DCS). Used for data lineage and ETL audit. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event log record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  safety_observations varchar [note: 'Free-text description of safety observations, near-misses, or EHS concerns noted during the shift. Null if none. Critical for Process Safety Management and continuous improvement.']
  setpoint_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for setpoint values (e.g., degC, bar, pH, kg/h, L/min). Null for non-setpoint-change events. Critical for engineering analysis and process control.']
  setpoint_value_new decimal(18,4) [note: 'New setpoint value after a setpoint_change event. Null for non-setpoint-change events. Used for audit trail and process optimization analysis.']
  setpoint_value_previous decimal(18,4) [note: 'Previous setpoint value before a setpoint_change event. Null for non-setpoint-change events. Used for audit trail and process optimization analysis.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift for shift-related events (shift_handover, shift_summary, shift_observation). Null for non-shift events. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Type of shift for shift-related events. Identifies day, afternoon (swing), night, weekend, or holiday shifts. Null for non-shift events. Used for shift handover continuity and staffing analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source DCS/SCADA system that generated the event. Distinguishes between Honeywell Experion DCS, Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7, Aveva PI System, and other SCADA platforms.']
  supervisor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shift supervisor on duty for shift-related events. Null for non-shift events. Provides accountability and escalation contact for shift operations.']
  tag_reference varchar [note: 'Unique process tag identifier from the DCS/SCADA system representing the specific instrument, sensor, valve, or control point associated with this event. Used for traceability to P&ID diagrams and process control loops.']

  Note: 'Unified operational event log, shift record, and DCS/SCADA audit trail from production control systems (Honeywell Experion, Siemens PCS 7) capturing all production floor events, operator shift documentation, and handover records. Records alarms, interlocks, operator actions, system state changes, setpoint modifications, mode changes, shift handover entries, and shift summary narratives. Captures event timestamp, DCS node ID, tag ID, event type (alarm, interlock_trip, operator_override, setpoint_change, mode_change, shift_handover, shift_summary, shift_observation), event description, priority level, acknowledged flag, operator ID, duration, associated manufacturing order, shift date, shift type (day/afternoon/night), supervisor name, operator count, batches completed, batches in-progress, equipment issues, safety observations, environmental readings, material shortages, quality holds, and handover instructions. The single source of truth for production floor operational continuity, shift-to-shift communication, process safety analysis, alarm rationalization, incident investigation, and regulatory audit trail.'
}

Table production.mes_execution_log {
  mes_execution_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MES execution log record. Primary key for the execution log entry capturing a single step or phase execution within a production batch or process order.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production batch or lot for which this execution step was performed. Enables lot genealogy and traceability.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production equipment or reactor unit on which this operation was performed. Links execution to physical asset.']
  primary_mes_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator or production worker who executed or confirmed this operation step. Critical for accountability and electronic signature compliance.']
  process_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent process order or production order under which this execution step was performed. Links the execution log to the overall manufacturing order.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this execution step requires supervisory or quality approval before the batch can proceed. True indicates approval workflow is required.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when supervisory or quality approval was granted for this execution step. Part of the approval audit trail.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or notes entered by the operator or supervisor regarding this execution step. Provides supplementary context for the batch record.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source from which the execution data was captured (manual operator entry, automatic DCS feed, SCADA system, PLC, LIMS import). Critical for data integrity assessment.']
  dcs_tag_name varchar [note: 'DCS or SCADA tag identifier from which the parameter value was read. Links MES execution data to real-time process control system (Honeywell Experion, Siemens PCS 7).']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the execution step in minutes. Calculated from execution start and end timestamps. Used for cycle time analysis and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation.']
  electronic_signature varchar [note: 'Encrypted electronic signature of the operator confirming execution of the step. Required for GMP and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of exception or deviation that occurred (e.g., OOS, equipment malfunction, material shortage). Used for exception categorization and trending.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exception or deviation that occurred during execution. Provides context for investigation and root cause analysis.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether an exception or deviation occurred during this execution step. True indicates an abnormal condition requiring investigation.']
  execution_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the execution of this recipe phase or operation step was completed or terminated.']
  execution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the execution of this recipe phase or operation step was initiated by the operator or system.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the execution step. Tracks the lifecycle state of the operation from initiation through completion or exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: started|in_progress|completed|paused|aborted|exception|confirmed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  instruction_text varchar [note: 'The full text of the work instruction or Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) step displayed to the operator during execution. Captures what the operator was instructed to do.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower acceptable limit for the process parameter. Values below this threshold trigger Out of Specification (OOS) alerts.']
  material_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the raw material or intermediate consumed or produced during this execution step. Enables material traceability and genealogy.']
  material_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material charged, consumed, or produced during this execution step. Used for Bill of Materials (BOM) reconciliation and yield calculation.']
  material_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the material quantity (e.g., kg, L, gal, lbs). Essential for accurate material accounting.']
  mes_system_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the MES software system that recorded this execution log entry. Important for audit trail and system validation.']
  mes_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier generated by the MES system for this execution event. Used for audit trail and system-level traceability.']
  operation_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order number of this operation within the process order or batch. Defines the execution order of steps.']
  operator_response_text varchar [note: 'Free-text response or confirmation entered by the operator in response to the instruction. May include observations, comments, or verification statements.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the process parameter recorded during this execution step (e.g., Temperature, Pressure, pH, Agitation Speed, Flow Rate). Identifies what was measured or controlled.']
  parameter_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the parameter value (e.g., Celsius, PSI, pH units, RPM, L/min). Essential for correct interpretation of parameter values.']
  parameter_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual numeric value of the process parameter entered or recorded during execution. Represents the measured or set value for the parameter.']
  recipe_phase_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific recipe phase or operation step executed (e.g., CHARGE, HEAT, REACT, COOL, DISCHARGE). Corresponds to the master recipe definition.']
  recipe_phase_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the recipe phase or operation step executed. Provides business context for the phase code.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this execution log record was first created in the MES system. Part of the electronic batch record audit trail.']
  record_locked_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this execution log record is locked and cannot be modified. True indicates the record is finalized and part of the permanent batch record.']
  record_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this execution log record was last modified. Tracks changes to the electronic batch record for audit purposes.']
  signature_meaning varchar [note: 'The meaning or intent of the electronic signature (e.g., performed, reviewed, approved, verified). Defines what the signature certifies.']
  signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the electronic signature was applied by the operator. Part of the electronic signature audit trail.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target or setpoint value for the process parameter as defined in the master recipe. Used for comparison against actual parameter value.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper acceptable limit for the process parameter. Values above this threshold trigger Out of Specification (OOS) alerts.']

  Note: 'Manufacturing Execution System (MES) log capturing step-by-step execution records for production operations. Records MES transaction ID, process order reference, recipe phase executed, execution start/end timestamps, operator ID, equipment ID, actual parameter values entered, electronic signature details, instruction text displayed, operator response/confirmation, exception flags, and MES system version. Provides the complete electronic batch record audit trail required for GMP compliance and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.'
}

Table production.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for campaign']
  campaign_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production planner responsible for campaign planning and scheduling.']
  campaign_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production supervisor responsible for campaign execution oversight.']
  formula_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials used for raw material planning and consumption tracking across the campaign.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.plant']
  pm_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_plan. Business justification: Campaign planning includes the associated preventive maintenance plan for equipment, essential for the Production‑Maintenance Alignment Dashboard.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.product_family']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production routing or process flow used for all batches in the campaign. Defines the sequence of operations and work centers.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Primary work center or production line assigned to execute this campaign. References the work center master data.']
  actual_batch_count varchar [note: 'Number of production batches actually executed within this campaign. Updated as batches are completed.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred during campaign execution. Captured from production orders and settled to cost centers.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the last batch of the campaign was completed and equipment was released. Captured from MES or DCS systems.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total actual production quantity achieved across all batches in the campaign. Updated as batches are completed and yield is confirmed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the first batch of the campaign was started. Captured from MES or DCS systems.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the production campaign, typically indicating the product family or synthesis route being manufactured.']
  campaign_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the campaign, externally visible and used in production planning and scheduling systems. Typically follows plant-specific numbering conventions.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production campaign. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|scheduled|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled|aborted — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign based on equipment utilization strategy. Dedicated campaigns run a single product exclusively; shared campaigns alternate between products in the same family; multi-product campaigns produce multiple SKUs; trial campaigns are for R&D validation.']
  changeover_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total time in hours required for equipment changeover and setup at the start of the campaign. Includes line clearance, equipment configuration, and pre-production checks.']
  cip_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total time in hours allocated for CIP operations during the campaign. Used for OEE calculations and scheduling.']
  cip_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Cleaning schedule strategy for the campaign. Between-batches requires CIP after each batch; end-of-campaign performs CIP only at campaign completion; as-needed is based on contamination risk assessment.']
  cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost for the campaign including raw materials, labor, utilities, and overhead. Used for financial planning and variance analysis.']
  cost_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between estimated and actual cost, calculated as ((actual_cost - cost_estimate) / cost_estimate) * 100. Key financial performance indicator.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the campaign record. Audit trail field.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any deviations from standard operating procedures occurred during the campaign. Triggers quality review and CAPA processes.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the campaign record. Audit trail field.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was last updated. Audit trail field.']
  master_recipe_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master recipe or process specification used for all batches in this campaign. Ensures consistency across campaign execution.']
  mes_campaign_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from the MES system used for integration and traceability. Links to MES execution logs and real-time process data.']
  moc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any Management of Change documentation associated with this campaign. Required when process parameters, equipment, or materials differ from standard operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the campaign, including special instructions, lessons learned, or operational observations.']
  oee_actual_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual OEE percentage achieved during the campaign, calculated from availability, performance, and quality metrics. Key KPI for production efficiency.']
  oee_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE percentage for the campaign, representing planned equipment utilization efficiency. Used for performance benchmarking.']
  planned_batch_count varchar [note: 'Number of production batches planned for this campaign at the time of campaign creation.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total planned production quantity for the campaign across all batches. Expressed in the campaign unit of measure.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the campaign for scheduling and resource allocation. Critical campaigns receive preferential treatment in capacity planning.']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign output is under quality hold pending final release by QA. Prevents shipment until cleared.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the campaign. Used for production scheduling and delivery commitments.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the campaign. Used for production scheduling and capacity planning.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for campaign quantities. Typically mass (KG, MT, LB, TON) or volume (L, GAL) depending on product characteristics.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall yield percentage for the campaign, calculated as (actual_quantity / planned_quantity) * 100. Key performance indicator for campaign efficiency.']

  Note: 'Manages multi-batch production campaigns where the same product or product family is manufactured in consecutive runs to maximize equipment utilization and minimize changeover. Captures campaign ID, campaign name, product family, campaign type (dedicated, shared), planned batch count, actual batch count, campaign start date, campaign end date, work center assignment, total planned quantity, total actual quantity, campaign yield, CIP (Clean-In-Place) schedule between batches, and campaign status. Supports campaign planning, scheduling optimization, and OEE reporting.'
}

Table production.cip_record {
  cip_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CIP execution record. Primary key for the CIP record entity.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reactor, vessel, pipeline, or processing equipment that was cleaned. Links to the equipment master data.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Functional location identifier where the CIP operation was performed. Provides hierarchical plant structure context.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order that preceded this CIP cycle. Links the CIP record to the manufacturing order for lot genealogy and traceability.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.plant']
  previous_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: CIP scheduling needs the previous product\'s raw‑material details to validate cleaning effectiveness.']
  primary_cip_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who executed or supervised the CIP cycle. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center (production line or area) where the CIP-cleaned equipment is located. Links to work center master data.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the CIP cycle was completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the CIP cycle began. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cip_cycle_number varchar [note: 'Sequential cycle number for this equipment. Tracks the number of CIP cycles performed on the equipment for maintenance and lifecycle analysis.']
  cip_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code to the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or master recipe used for this CIP execution. Defines the cleaning protocol, agent, temperature, duration, and acceptance criteria.']
  cip_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the CIP execution: in_progress (cleaning underway), completed (successfully finished), failed (did not meet acceptance criteria), aborted (manually stopped), pending_review (awaiting QA approval).']
  cip_type varchar [note: 'Type of CIP execution: manual (operator-driven), automatic (fully automated via DCS/PLC), or semi-automatic (operator-initiated, system-controlled).']
  cleaning_agent_code varchar [note: 'Material code of the cleaning agent or detergent used in the CIP process (e.g., caustic soda, acid wash, sanitizer). Links to material master.']
  cleaning_agent_concentration_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Concentration of the cleaning agent in parts per million (PPM) used during the CIP cycle. Critical for ensuring effective cleaning without residue.']
  cleaning_agent_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the cleaning agent used (e.g., Sodium Hydroxide Solution, Nitric Acid 10%, Hydrogen Peroxide Sanitizer).']
  cleaning_duration_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total duration of the CIP cleaning cycle in minutes, from start of cleaning agent circulation to completion of final rinse.']
  cleaning_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the CIP cleaning cycle was executed. Temperature is critical for cleaning efficacy and must meet SOP specifications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this CIP record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  dcs_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Batch identifier from the DCS (Honeywell Experion or Siemens PCS 7) that executed the automated CIP cycle. Links to real-time process historian data.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any deviations from the standard CIP procedure, including root cause and corrective actions taken.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether any deviations from the CIP procedure occurred during execution. True triggers a deviation investigation workflow.']
  flow_rate_liters_per_minute decimal(10,2) [note: 'Flow rate of cleaning solution in liters per minute during the CIP cycle. Adequate flow rate ensures complete coverage and effective cleaning.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the CIP execution meets GMP requirements. True means compliant; False triggers corrective action.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this CIP record. Captured for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this CIP record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mes_integration_reference varchar [note: 'Integration identifier linking this CIP record to the MES system for production scheduling, lot genealogy, and traceability.']
  next_product_code varchar [note: 'Material code of the chemical product scheduled to be manufactured in the equipment after this CIP cycle. Used to validate cleaning protocol adequacy for product changeover.']
  next_production_clearance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the equipment has been cleared for the next production run. True means equipment is approved for use; False means additional cleaning or inspection is required.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the operator who executed or supervised the CIP cycle. Captured for audit trail and accountability.']
  pass_fail_result varchar [note: 'Final pass/fail result of the CIP execution based on acceptance criteria (rinse water conductivity, temperature profile, duration). Conditional_pass indicates minor deviations requiring management review.']
  qa_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments from the QA reviewer regarding the CIP execution, any deviations observed, or additional actions required.']
  qa_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QA reviewer approved or rejected the CIP record. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the CIP execution with respect to GMP, FDA, and food-contact regulations. Compliant means all criteria met; non_compliant triggers investigation; pending_review awaits final determination.']
  rinse_water_conductivity_us_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of the final rinse water in microsiemens per centimeter (µS/cm). Low conductivity indicates effective removal of cleaning agent residues. Typical acceptance threshold is below 20 µS/cm.']
  scada_integration_reference varchar [note: 'Integration identifier linking this CIP record to SCADA system logs and real-time process data captured during the cleaning cycle.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the CIP cycle was scheduled to begin. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Clean-In-Place (CIP) execution record documenting the cleaning and sanitization of reactors, vessels, pipelines, and processing equipment between production runs. Captures CIP record ID, equipment ID, CIP procedure reference, cleaning agent used, cleaning agent concentration (PPM), rinse water conductivity, temperature profile, duration, flow rate, operator ID, pass/fail result, next production clearance flag, and regulatory compliance status. Critical for preventing cross-contamination between chemical products and maintaining GMP/food-contact compliance.'
}

Table production.equipment_effectiveness {
  equipment_effectiveness_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for equipment_effectiveness']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Downtime Cost Reporting: downtime impact is charged to a cost center for financial loss analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment or asset that experienced downtime. Links to equipment master data in SAP PM.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the functional location in the plant hierarchy where the downtime occurred.']
  maintenance_notification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance notification that triggered investigation or repair. Links to SAP PM notification for issue tracking.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order created to address the downtime event. Links to SAP PM work order for repair tracking and cost capture.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order that was interrupted by this downtime event. Links to manufacturing order for impact analysis.']
  primary_equipment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who reported or was on duty during the downtime event. Used for training needs analysis and accountability.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which the downtime event occurred. Used for shift-level OEE analysis and performance comparison.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: REQUIRED: Maintenance KPI reports need the equipment vendor to assess service contract performance and downtime root causes.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center where the downtime event occurred. Links to the production work center master data.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the product being manufactured when the downtime occurred. Supports lot genealogy and traceability.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event requires formal CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) investigation and documentation per quality management system.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context about the downtime event not captured in structured fields.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this downtime event record. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this downtime event record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  dcs_alarm_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DCS or SCADA alarm that triggered or was associated with the downtime event. Links to process control system alarm history.']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the downtime event type. Used for OEE availability loss categorization and root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical_failure|electrical_failure|process_upset|material_shortage|changeover|planned_maintenance|utility_failure|operator_shortage|quality_issue|safety_incident — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  downtime_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the downtime event in minutes. Calculated as the difference between end and start timestamps. Used directly in OEE availability calculation.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the equipment was restored to operational status and production resumed.']
  downtime_event_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by MES or DCS system.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the downtime event began. Captured from DCS/SCADA system or manually recorded by operator.']
  downtime_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. Tracks progression from detection through resolution and closure.']
  downtime_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory or specific failure mode within the downtime category. Provides granular classification for maintenance and continuous improvement analysis.']
  environmental_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event resulted in an environmental release or incident requiring EPA or regulatory reporting.']
  ideal_cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Theoretical minimum time to produce one unit of product under ideal conditions. Used for OEE performance calculation.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this downtime event record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this downtime event record was last updated. Tracks record change history for audit and compliance.']
  mes_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the MES system that recorded the downtime event. Supports integration and data lineage.']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Management of Change record if the downtime was related to a recent process or equipment modification.']
  mtbf_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event should be counted as a failure event in MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) calculation for reliability analysis.']
  mttr_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event should be included in MTTR (Mean Time To Repair) calculation for maintainability analysis.']
  oee_availability_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event should be included in the OEE availability component calculation (True) or excluded (False).']
  oee_performance_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event impacted the OEE performance component (speed losses) in addition to availability.']
  oee_quality_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event resulted in quality losses or defects that impact the OEE quality component.']
  planned_downtime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime was scheduled and planned (True) or unplanned/unscheduled (False). Critical for distinguishing availability losses in OEE calculation.']
  planned_production_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned production time for the period in which the downtime occurred. Used as the denominator in OEE availability calculation.']
  production_impact_batches_delayed varchar [note: 'Number of production batches delayed or rescheduled as a result of this downtime event. Measures schedule adherence impact.']
  production_impact_quantity_lost decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of product that could not be produced due to the downtime event, measured in the product\'s base unit of measure. Used for throughput loss calculation.']
  production_impact_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity lost (e.g., kg, liters, tons, units). Aligns with material master UOM.']
  recovery_action_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the type of recovery action performed (e.g., repair, replacement, adjustment, restart).']
  recovery_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions taken to restore equipment to operational status and resume production.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the root cause category from the plant\'s failure mode taxonomy. Enables systematic analysis and trending.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause identified through RCI (Root Cause Investigation) or failure analysis. Supports CAPA and continuous improvement.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event was associated with a safety incident requiring EHS reporting or investigation.']

  Note: 'Records unplanned and planned downtime events for production equipment and work centers, serving as the source data for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations. Captures downtime event ID, work center, equipment ID, downtime start/end datetime, duration minutes, downtime category (mechanical failure, electrical failure, process upset, material shortage, changeover, planned maintenance, utility failure, operator shortage), root cause description, maintenance work order reference, production impact (batches delayed, quantity lost), recovery action, shift reference, planned production time, ideal cycle time, and availability/performance/quality component flags. Feeds OEE calculations (availability × performance × quality), TPM programs, maintenance prioritization, continuous improvement initiatives, and production KPI dashboards.'
}

Table production.shift_log {
  shift_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shift log record. Primary key for the shift log entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supervisor who managed the shift.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific work center or production area covered by this shift log.']
  batches_completed varchar [note: 'Number of production batches successfully completed during the shift.']
  batches_in_progress varchar [note: 'Number of production batches that were in-progress at the end of the shift.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created the shift log record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift log record was first created in the system.']
  dcs_system_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the Distributed Control System during the shift.']
  environmental_readings varchar [note: 'Summary of environmental monitoring readings such as temperature, humidity, air quality, or emissions captured during the shift.']
  equipment_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes of unplanned equipment downtime during the shift.']
  equipment_issues_noted varchar [note: 'Description of any equipment malfunctions, breakdowns, or performance issues observed during the shift.']
  handover_instructions varchar [note: 'Instructions and guidance provided to the next shift supervisor and operators for operational continuity.']
  key_events_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting significant events, observations, and activities that occurred during the shift.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the shift log record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift log record was last updated.']
  material_shortages_flagged varchar [note: 'Description of any raw material or component shortages identified during the shift that impacted production.']
  moc_activities varchar [note: 'Documentation of any Management of Change activities or temporary process modifications implemented during the shift.']
  operator_count varchar [note: 'Total number of operators working during the shift.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the shift occurred.']
  process_deviations_noted varchar [note: 'Description of any deviations from standard operating procedures or master recipes observed during the shift.']
  production_area varchar [note: 'Name or designation of the production area or manufacturing zone covered during the shift.']
  production_orders_active varchar [note: 'Count of production orders that were active or in-progress during the shift.']
  production_output_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of product manufactured during the shift.']
  production_output_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the production output quantity (e.g., kg, liters, tons, gallons).']
  quality_holds_noted varchar [note: 'Documentation of any quality holds, Out of Specification (OOS) events, or quality concerns raised during the shift.']
  quality_issues_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality-related issues or deviations identified during the shift.']
  safety_incidents_count varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents or near-miss events recorded during the shift.']
  safety_observations varchar [note: 'Documentation of safety-related observations, near-misses, or incidents noted during the shift.']
  scada_alarms_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SCADA system alarms triggered during the shift.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shift occurred. Principal business event timestamp for the shift log.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift officially ended.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift officially began.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift log record.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift period (day, afternoon, night, swing, graveyard, or rotating shift).']
  supervisor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the production supervisor responsible for the shift.']
  training_conducted varchar [note: 'Description of any operator training, safety briefings, or skill development activities conducted during the shift.']
  utility_consumption_notes varchar [note: 'Observations regarding utility consumption (electricity, steam, water, compressed air) during the shift.']
  visitor_contractor_activity varchar [note: 'Documentation of any visitor or contractor activities on-site during the shift.']
  waste_generation_notes varchar [note: 'Documentation of waste generation, disposal activities, or environmental compliance observations during the shift.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Notable weather conditions during the shift that may have impacted operations (e.g., extreme heat, cold, storms).']

  Note: 'Operational shift log maintained by production supervisors and operators documenting key events, observations, handover notes, and production status during each shift. Captures shift log ID, shift date, shift type (day, afternoon, night), work center or production area, supervisor name, operator count, production orders active, batches completed, batches in-progress, key events narrative, equipment issues noted, safety observations, environmental readings, material shortages flagged, quality holds noted, and handover instructions for next shift. Provides operational continuity and audit trail.'
}

Table production.production_procurement_plan {
  production_procurement_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the procurement_plan association']
  production_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production campaign']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the procurement plan (e.g., Pending, Approved, Rejected)']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract governing this procurement']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected lead time in days for the vendor to deliver the material']
  planned_procurement_quantity decimal [note: 'Quantity of material planned to be procured for the campaign from this vendor']
  price_per_unit decimal [note: 'Agreed price per unit of material for this campaign‑vendor pair']
  total_planned_value decimal [note: 'Total monetary value of the planned procurement (quantity * price)']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual and logistical agreement between a production campaign and a vendor. Each record links one campaign to one vendor and stores procurement‑specific details that belong to the relationship itself.. Existence Justification: A production campaign may source raw materials from multiple vendors, and a vendor can supply to many different campaigns. The procurement team actively creates and maintains a procurement plan for each campaign‑vendor pairing, capturing quantities, pricing, lead times, and contract details. This plan is a managed business entity that is updated throughout the campaign lifecycle.'
}

Table production.process_unit {
  process_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for process_unit']
  parent_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on process_unit (parent_process_unit_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.plant']
  capacity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Designed production capacity of the unit.']
  capacity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the capacity field.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the unit passed all acceptance tests and entered service.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the unit.']
  control_system varchar [note: 'Distributed control system that governs the unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the master record was first created.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the unit was permanently taken out of service, if applicable.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the unit\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  emission_factor_kg_per_hour decimal(10,3) [note: 'Typical emissions generated by the unit per hour of operation.']
  energy_consumption_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Average energy consumption of the unit per operating hour.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Primary hazard class associated with the unit.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the unit was installed and became operational.']
  integration_point varchar [note: 'Primary integration technology used for data acquisition.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit is considered critical for production continuity.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which preventive maintenance was performed.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location or plant where the unit resides.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between routine maintenance activities.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that built the process unit.']
  max_pressure_bar decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum safe operating pressure for the unit.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum safe operating temperature for the unit.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model designation assigned by the manufacturer.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the process unit used in reports and dashboards.']
  operating_mode varchar [note: 'Mode in which the unit runs.']
  operating_shift varchar [note: 'Typical shift schedule for the unit.']
  process_unit_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping or line to which the unit belongs.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification based on industry standards.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the unit.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the unit.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'External code or tag assigned to the unit by engineering standards.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the unit describing its primary function in the plant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the master record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for process_unit. Referenced by process_unit_id.'
}

Table production.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plant']
  parent_plant_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on plant (parent_plant_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the plant location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).']
  capacity_tons_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Design‑rated mass production capacity of the plant.']
  capacity_volume_m3_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Design‑rated volumetric production capacity of the plant.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the plant\'s physical location.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the plant by the organization.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the plant location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the plant record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing additional context about the plant.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for emergency coordination.']
  emergency_contact_number varchar [note: 'Phone number to call for plant emergency response.']
  emissions_co2_tons_per_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Reported greenhouse‑gas emissions for the plant per year.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit number authorizing emissions and waste handling.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the latest inspection activity.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the plant processes or stores regulated hazardous substances.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the latest safety or regulatory inspection.']
  latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the plant for mapping and GIS analysis.']
  longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the plant for mapping and GIS analysis.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Approach used for maintaining plant equipment.']
  manager_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone for the plant manager.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the plant manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person responsible for plant operations.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the plant used in reports and user interfaces.']
  number_of_shifts varchar [note: 'Count of distinct production shifts run each calendar day.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of primary production equipment units at the plant.']
  operational_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the plant was decommissioned or closed; null if still active.']
  operational_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the plant first became operational.']
  permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the environmental permit must be renewed.']
  plant_area_sq_meters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Physical size of the plant site.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code for the plant\'s address.']
  primary_energy_source varchar [note: 'Main energy source powering the plant\'s processes.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Internal safety classification based on OSHA/ISO 45001 audits.']
  shift_duration_hours varchar [note: 'Standard duration of a production shift.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the plant resides.']
  status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status indicating whether the plant is operating, under maintenance, or retired.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the plant\'s local time.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plant based on its primary function.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the plant record.']
  waste_treatment_capacity_tons_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of waste the plant can treat daily.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plant. Referenced by plant_id.'
}

Table production.resource {
  resource_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for resource']
  parent_resource_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on resource (parent_resource_id)']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the asset based on criticality.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the capacity value.']
  capacity_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Primary quantitative capacity of the resource (e.g., volume, weight).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the resource.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Capital cost incurred to acquire the resource.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the resource record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amount.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the resource was retired or removed from service (if applicable).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the resource.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the resource was installed or commissioned.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the most recent maintenance was performed.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical location or site of the resource.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between routine maintenance activities.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the resource.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the resource.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the upcoming maintenance.']
  operating_pressure_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical operating pressure in bar units.']
  operating_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical operating temperature range in degrees Celsius.']
  resource_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or catalogue number for the resource.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Category that classifies the kind of resource.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification based on internal risk assessment.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the resource.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the resource.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the resource record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty expires.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the resource record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for resource. Referenced by resource_id.'
}

Table production.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for routing']
  superseded_routing_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on routing (superseded_routing_id)']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary work center associated with the routing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the routing.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the routing was approved.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent revision of the routing.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique business code for the routing.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the routing.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing record was created.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the routing.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the routing purpose and scope.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the routing is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the routing becomes effective.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Typical energy usage for the routing per batch.']
  estimated_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard expected cycle time for the routing.']
  estimated_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical yield expected when following this routing.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates if this routing is the default for its product family.']
  material_consumption_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average material consumption per unit output.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the routing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the routing.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Three-letter code of the plant where routing is used.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority order when multiple routings apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision count for changes to the routing.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Category of routing based on process nature.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety risk rating for the routing.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the routing record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the routing record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the routing definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the routing record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for routing. Referenced by routing_id.'
}

Table production.bill_of_materials {
  bill_of_materials_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bill_of_materials']
  parent_bom_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent BOM in a hierarchical BOM structure (e.g., for sub‑assemblies).']
  superseded_bill_of_materials_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bill_of_materials (superseded_bill_of_materials_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the BOM within engineering change control.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOM received final approval.']
  bom_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the BOM in production and planning processes.']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM indicating whether it represents a finished assembly, a kit, raw material collection, or a sub‑assembly.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the most recent change to the BOM.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) applicable to the BOM (e.g., EPA, OSHA, REACH, GHS).']
  component_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct component lines defined for this BOM.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the standard_cost field.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOM record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the BOM purpose, scope, and any special handling notes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the BOM becomes valid for use in production planning.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the BOM is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the BOM contains any regulated hazardous substances.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this BOM is the default version used for the associated product.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of calendar days required to procure and assemble the BOM.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the BOM, typically the product or assembly name.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments about the BOM.']
  quality_check_required boolean [note: 'True if a quality inspection is mandatory for the finished product derived from this BOM.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision to the BOM.']
  routing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manufacturing routing must be executed for this BOM.']
  safety_data_sheet_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the latest Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with hazardous components.']
  scrap_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of material loss during production of the BOM.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of the BOM based on component costs and engineering estimates.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOM (e.g., active for production, draft for engineering).']
  total_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all components expressed in the base unit of measure for the BOM.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Combined volume of all components in cubic meters, supporting storage planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Combined weight of all components in kilograms, used for logistics and handling.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the total_quantity field (e.g., kilograms, pieces).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the BOM record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOM record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number incremented each time the BOM is revised.']
  yield_factor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected yield efficiency for the BOM, expressed as a percentage of usable output.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the BOM record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for bill_of_materials. Referenced by bom_id.'
}

Table production.control_recipe {
  control_recipe_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for control_recipe']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the catalyst used in this recipe.']
  template_control_recipe_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on control_recipe (template_control_recipe_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the recipe.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipe was formally approved for production.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent change to the recipe.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the recipe.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipe record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the recipe purpose, scope, and key characteristics.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the recipe is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the recipe is valid for production.']
  equipment_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of equipment (e.g., reactor, distillation column) that the recipe applies to.']
  flow_rate_lph decimal(8,2) [note: 'Desired liquid flow rate in liters per hour.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recipe is the default for its product line.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent production run that used this recipe.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the recipe.']
  ph_setpoint decimal(4,2) [note: 'Target pH value for the reaction environment.']
  pressure_setpoint_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target pressure in bar for the process step.']
  recipe_code varchar [note: 'External code or catalogue number that uniquely identifies the recipe across systems.']
  recipe_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control recipe used by operators and engineers.']
  recipe_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recipe based on production mode.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Internal revision counter for change management.']
  safety_class varchar [note: 'Safety classification based on hazard potential.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recipe.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired product yield expressed as a percentage for this recipe.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature in degrees Celsius for the process step defined by the recipe.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recipe record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the recipe has been executed.']
  version_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment describing the changes in this version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the recipe definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for control_recipe. Referenced by control_recipe_id.'
}

Table production.phase {
