  height_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the packaging container in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting, stacking calculations, and transportation planning.']
  iata_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IATA dangerous goods air transport labels are required on this packaging configuration.']
  imdg_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IMDG Code maritime transport labels are required on this packaging configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this packaging configuration record was last modified or updated.']
  length_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of rectangular packaging (IBCs, totes, boxes) in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.']
  material_of_construction varchar [note: 'Material used to construct the packaging container. Options include HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene), steel, stainless steel, fiber, aluminum, and lined containers.']
  nominal_capacity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Nominal or rated capacity of the packaging container, expressed in the unit specified by capacity_unit_of_measure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional information about the packaging configuration, including special handling instructions, compatibility notes, or regulatory remarks.']
  outer_diameter_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Outer diameter of cylindrical packaging (drums, cylinders, pails) in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting and space planning.']
  packaging_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the packaging configuration, used in SAP EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) and order fulfillment systems.']
  packaging_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packaging configuration. Active configurations are available for order fulfillment; inactive/obsolete are phased out; pending approval are under review; restricted require special authorization.']
  packaging_group varchar [note: 'UN Packaging Group classification indicating the degree of danger: I (high danger), II (medium danger), III (low danger). Determines packaging performance requirements.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging container used for the chemical product. Includes drum, IBC (Intermediate Bulk Container), bulk tanker, bag, cylinder, pail, tote, flexitank, and isotank. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drum|ibc|bulk_tanker|bag|cylinder|pail|tote|flexitank|isotank — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pallet_type varchar [note: 'Type of pallet used for this packaging configuration (e.g., EUR pallet, standard 48x40, block pallet, chemical-resistant pallet).']
  pharmaceutical_grade_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging configuration meets pharmaceutical-grade standards for API (Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient) and pharmaceutical chemical packaging.']
  preferred_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the preferred or default packaging configuration for the associated product SKU (Stock Keeping Unit).']
  region_restriction varchar [note: 'Geographic regions where this packaging configuration is approved or restricted for use, based on local regulatory requirements (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, EUR, CHN). Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.']
  returnable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging configuration is returnable (e.g., returnable drums, IBCs, cylinders) requiring deposit tracking and reverse logistics.']
  stack_height_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallet layers that can be safely stacked vertically in warehouse storage, considering packaging strength and product stability.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the empty packaging container in kilograms, excluding product. Used for net weight calculations and transportation compliance.']
  un_packaging_code varchar [note: 'United Nations packaging certification code indicating the packaging meets UN performance standards for hazardous materials transport. Examples: 4G (fiberboard box), 1A1 (steel drum), 1H1 (plastic drum), 31HA1 (IBC composite).']
  units_per_pallet varchar [note: 'Number of packaging units (drums, IBCs, bags, etc.) that can be loaded on a single pallet for warehouse storage and transportation.']
  width_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of rectangular packaging (IBCs, totes, boxes) in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.']

  Note: 'Packaging configuration master defining all approved packaging formats for chemical product SKUs. Captures packaging type (drum, IBC, bulk tanker, bag, cylinder, pail, tote, flexitank, isotank), material of construction (HDPE, steel, stainless steel, fiber, aluminum, lined), nominal capacity, fill weight, fill volume, tare weight, closure type, UN packaging certification code (4G, 1A1, 1H1, 31HA1), packaging group (I, II, III), label requirements (GHS, DOT, IATA), pallet configuration (units per pallet, pallet type, stack height), and packaging status. Supports DOT/IATA/IMDG hazmat packaging compliance, warehouse slotting in SAP EWM, and customer order fulfillment.'
}

Table product.composition {
  composition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product composition record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the finished chemical product to which this composition belongs.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Required for BOM: links each product composition entry to the specific raw material master record, enabling formulation, cost, and regulatory reporting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the component.']
  component_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the component received regulatory approval.']
  component_cas_number varchar [note: 'Standard CAS number uniquely identifying the chemical component.']
  component_concentration_range_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concentration is expressed as a range.']
  component_concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the component concentration.']
  component_concentration_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric concentration of the component.']
  component_ec_number varchar [note: 'EC number identifying the component within European regulatory lists.']
  component_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the component is considered expired.']
  component_function varchar [note: 'Role of the component within the product formulation.']
  component_hazard_class varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard class applicable to the component.']
  component_hazard_statement varchar [note: 'Standard hazard statement describing the risks of the component.']
  component_iupac_name varchar [note: 'Systematic IUPAC name of the component.']
  component_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number assigned to the component by the supplier.']
  component_physical_state varchar [note: 'Physical state of the component at room temperature.']
  component_precautionary_statement varchar [note: 'Standard precautionary statement for safe handling of the component.']
  component_purity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Purity of the component expressed as a percentage.']
  component_purity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the component purity value.']
  component_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Absolute quantity of the component used in the formulation.']
  component_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the component quantity.']
  component_regulatory_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory registration or listing number for the component.']
  component_regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the component under applicable chemical regulations.']
  component_trade_name varchar [note: 'Commercial trade name used for the component.']
  composition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the composition record.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component information is protected as confidential business information.']
  disclosure_threshold varchar [note: 'Regulatory threshold that triggers mandatory disclosure of the component.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the composition became effective for the product.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the composition was retired or superseded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes about the composition.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the composition record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the composition record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the composition data.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the composition record.']

  Note: 'Chemical composition disclosure record for a product, capturing the ingredient-level breakdown required for SDS Section 3 (Composition/Information on Ingredients) and REACH substance identification. Stores component CAS number, IUPAC name, trade name, EC number, concentration (exact or range), concentration unit, function in mixture (active ingredient, solvent, stabilizer, surfactant, filler), classification of component, disclosure threshold (OSHA, REACH, SARA 313), and confidentiality flag (CBI — Confidential Business Information). Supports SDS authoring, REACH mixture notification, and TSCA reporting.'
}

Table product.cross_reference {
  cross_reference_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each product cross‑reference record.']
  primary_cross_chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary chemical product for which the cross‑reference is defined.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the person/department that approved the cross‑reference.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the cross‑reference was formally approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cross‑reference record was first created in the system.']
  cross_reference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cross‑reference record.']
  customer_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if the customer must approve the substitution before execution.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer must be notified when this substitution is applied.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the cross‑reference becomes valid for operational use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the cross‑reference is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  equivalence_type varchar [note: 'Degree of similarity between the primary product and the referenced product.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Code assigned by an external party (competitor, distributor, or legacy system) that maps to the referenced product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the cross‑reference.']
  quality_hold_substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'True if substitution is permitted when the primary product is on a quality hold.']
  reference_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or team that owns and maintains the cross‑reference record.']
  reference_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the origin or nature of the reference (e.g., internal, competitor, legacy).']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance classification of the referenced product relative to the primary product.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the cross‑reference originated (e.g., SAP, CRM).']
  stock_out_substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'True if substitution may be used automatically when primary product is out of stock.']
  substitution_condition varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of conditions under which the substitution may be applied (e.g., stock‑out, quality hold).']
  substitution_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the substitution relationship works both ways or only from primary to referenced product.']
  substitution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of substitution based on business policy (direct, conditional, or emergency).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the cross‑reference record.']

  Note: 'Cross-reference and substitution master record — the single source of truth (SSOT) for all product equivalence mappings, approved substitutions, and external reference linkages. Links a chemical product to equivalent products, approved substitutes (direct, conditional, emergency), competitor products, legacy product codes, customer-specific part numbers, and distributor catalog numbers. Captures reference type, external reference code, reference owner, equivalence type, substitution type, substitution conditions (stock-out, quality hold, customer approval required), substitution direction (bidirectional or one-way), approval authority, approval date, customer notification requirement, validity dates, and status. Supports customer order management, SAP SD availability check substitution logic, product substitution workflows during fulfillment, competitive intelligence, and supply chain continuity planning. This entity consolidates all substitution rules previously tracked separately — there is no other substitution entity in this domain.'
}

Table product.substitution {
  substitution_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the product substitution rule record.']
  substitution_chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product that may be substituted.']
  substitution_substitute_product_chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product that can replace the original product.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the person/committee that approved the substitution rule.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the substitution rule was formally approved.']
  condition_customer_approval_required boolean [note: 'True when the customer must explicitly approve the substitution.']
  condition_quality_hold boolean [note: 'True when substitution is permitted only if the original product is under a quality hold.']
  condition_stock_out boolean [note: 'True when substitution is allowed only if the original product is out of stock.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'True when the customer must be notified of the substitution before fulfillment.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether substitution can occur both ways or only from original to substitute.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the substitution rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the substitution rule is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or special instructions related to the substitution.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the substitution rule was created (e.g., cost reduction, supply risk).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the substitution record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the substitution record.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the substitution record (e.g., SAP SD, custom MDM).']
  substitution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the substitution rule.']
  substitution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of substitution logic: direct (one-to-one), conditional (based on criteria), or emergency (unplanned).']

  Note: 'Approved product substitution rule record defining when and how one chemical product or SKU may be substituted for another during order fulfillment or production. Captures substitution type (direct, conditional, emergency), substitute product reference, substitution direction (bidirectional or one-way), approval authority, approval date, conditions for substitution (stock-out, quality hold, customer approval required), customer notification requirement, and substitution status. Supports SAP SD availability check substitution logic and supply chain continuity planning.'
}

Table product.reach_dossier {
  reach_dossier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each REACH registration dossier record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Each REACH dossier pertains to a specific chemical product; linking enables traceability and reporting. No existing reverse FK, so direction dossier → product is appropriate.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the chemical substance assigned by the CAS registry.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes on compliance actions, exemptions, or special conditions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dossier record was first created in the system.']
  csr_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference or file identifier for the associated Chemical Safety Report document.']
  csr_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a full Chemical Safety Report is required for this substance.']
  dossier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the REACH dossier.']
  dossier_update_date date [note: 'Most recent date the dossier information was updated.']
  ec_number varchar [note: 'European Community identifier for the substance.']
  echa_submission_date date [note: 'Date the dossier was submitted to the ECHA portal.']
  echa_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code for the submission package in the ECHA REACH-IT portal.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the REACH registration expires or must be renewed.']
  exposure_scenarios_count varchar [note: 'Total number of exposure scenarios defined within the dossier.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class assigned to the substance.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category of the chemical product.']
  registrant_role varchar [note: 'Role of the registrant in the REACH dossier (lead registrant, member, or coordinator).']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date the REACH registration was initially submitted to ECHA.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official REACH registration number assigned by ECHA.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory status of the substance across applicable frameworks.']
  risk_assessment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a risk assessment has been completed for the dossier.']
  sief_membership varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the dossier is part of a Substance Information Exchange Forum (SIEF).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the dossier record (e.g., Intelex EHS).']
  substance_iupac_name varchar [note: 'Systematic chemical name of the substance as defined by IUPAC.']
  tonnage_band varchar [note: 'Annual tonnage range for which the substance is registered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dossier record.']

  Note: 'REACH registration dossier record — the single source of truth (SSOT) for substance registration under EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, including the full Chemical Safety Report (CSR) and all exposure scenarios. Captures REACH registration number, substance identity (IUPAC name, CAS, EC number), registrant role (lead registrant, member), SIEF membership, tonnage band, registration date, dossier update date, ECHA submission reference, CSR requirement flag, and dossier status. Contains all exposure scenarios as child records: scenario title, use descriptor codes (SU, PC, PROC, AC, ERC), contributing scenario name, operational conditions (temperature, duration, frequency, amount used), risk management measures (PPE, ventilation, containment), DNEL values, PNEC values, risk characterization ratios (RCR), and scenario validity dates. Supports extended SDS (eSDS) generation for downstream users. Managed in Intelex EHS and submitted to ECHA REACH-IT. This entity is the sole owner of exposure scenario data — there is no separate exposure scenario entity.'
}

Table product.exposure_scenario {
  exposure_scenario_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the exposure scenario record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical product to which this exposure scenario applies.']
  amount_used_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of the chemical used in the scenario, expressed in kilograms.']
  article_category_code varchar [note: 'Code for the article form (e.g., solid, liquid) of the chemical in the scenario.']
  contributing_scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of any parent or related scenario that contributes to this exposure scenario.']
  dnel_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the DNEL (e.g., mg/m³).']
  dnel_value decimal(12,6) [note: 'Quantitative DNEL value derived for the scenario.']
  environmental_release_category_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the type of environmental release considered in the scenario.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  operational_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of time the exposure condition is applied, measured in minutes.']
  operational_frequency_per_day varchar [note: 'Number of times the exposure condition occurs each day.']
  operational_temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the chemical is used or processed, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  pnec_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the PNEC (e.g., µg/L).']
  pnec_value decimal(12,6) [note: 'PNEC value used for environmental risk assessment.']
  process_category_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the manufacturing or usage process category for the scenario.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the product category of the chemical under REACH PC classification.']
  rcr_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Ratio of exposure to the DNEL/PNEC, indicating risk level.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scenario record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scenario record.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) applicable to the scenario.']
  risk_management_measure_containment varchar [note: 'Physical containment measures (e.g., fume hood) needed for the scenario.']
  risk_management_measure_ppe varchar [note: 'Description of required PPE for safe handling under this scenario.']
  risk_management_measure_ventilation varchar [note: 'Ventilation controls required to mitigate exposure.']
  scenario_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the scenario in regulatory documents and internal systems.']
  scenario_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the scenario is considered valid.']
  scenario_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the scenario is no longer valid.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title describing the exposure scenario.']
  scenario_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scenario.']
  sector_of_use_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the industrial sector where the chemical is used (REACH SU classification).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP QM, Intelex EHS).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the scenario record.']

  Note: 'Chemical Safety Report (CSR) exposure scenario record defining the conditions of safe use for a chemical substance or mixture under REACH. Captures exposure scenario title, use descriptor codes (sector of use SU, product category PC, process category PROC, article category AC, environmental release category ERC), contributing scenario name, operational conditions (OC: temperature, duration, frequency, amount used), risk management measures (RMM: PPE, ventilation, containment), derived no-effect level (DNEL), predicted no-effect concentration (PNEC), risk characterization ratio (RCR), and scenario validity date. Appended to extended SDS (eSDS) for downstream users.'
}

Table product.product_order {
  product_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  asset_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset_change_request. Business justification: Change‑management process ties equipment change requests to the sales order that triggered the modification, ensuring regulatory traceability.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order Entry captures the buying contact who placed the order, supporting sales attribution, commission calculations, and contact‑order reporting.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a blanket or framework contract, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Cost Allocation Report: each sales order must be charged to an internal cost center for accurate expense tracking and financial reporting.']
  distributor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.distributor. Business justification: Links orders placed through a distributor; required for distributor performance and rebate calculations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee responsible for the sale.']
  forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.forecast_version. Business justification: Order Planning Audit uses the forecast version that drove demand planning for the sales order, required for regulatory reporting and demand accuracy analysis.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Project Accounting process requires associating a sales order with an internal order to track project‑related costs and budgets.']
  mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.mrp_run. Business justification: MRP Run linkage records which MRP execution generated the plan for a sales order, needed for traceability and performance KPI reporting.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Tracks Opportunity win to Order creation for pipeline‑to‑revenue analysis.']
  patent_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.patent_filing. Business justification: Patent Royalty Tracking requires linking each order to the patent filing that protects the sold chemical for royalty accounting.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Ensures each order references the price agreement applied, needed for pricing compliance audit.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: REQUIRED: Orders for key accounts use a specific price list; storing price_list_id enables contract‑price compliance reporting.']
  primary_formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula. Business justification: Required for Order master reporting: the primary formula defining the product family is stored on the sales order to support profitability analysis and regulatory traceability.']
  product_order_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the address used for invoicing.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Needed for Profitability Analysis: linking orders to profit centers enables revenue attribution and margin reporting per business unit.']
  proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_proposal. Business justification: REQUIRED: Special pricing proposals are attached to orders; linking enables proposal acceptance tracking and profitability review.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Required for Quote‑to‑Order conversion report; orders are created from approved sales quotes.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Product Development Traceability Report links sales orders to originating R&D project for regulatory compliance and cost allocation.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Assigns each order to a sales territory for regional sales reporting and quota tracking.']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the attached Safety Data Sheet or regulatory compliance document.']
  shipping_address_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination address for delivery.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.sop_cycle. Business justification: SOP Cycle assignment links a sales order to the Sales & Operations Planning cycle used for its demand‑supply balancing, required for cycle performance metrics.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: WBS Tracking report links each order to a Work Breakdown Structure element for detailed project cost control.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the transaction currency.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the order.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the product is sold (e.g., wholesale, retail).']
  division varchar [note: 'Business division responsible for the product line.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert foreign currency to company reporting currency.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Responsibility for freight costs.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total order value before taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains regulated hazardous substances.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True when any line items are pending due to stock shortage.']
  is_split boolean [note: 'True when the order is fulfilled in multiple shipments.']
  line_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct line items on the order.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External order number visible to customers and used in communications.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'System or channel through which the order was created.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the order was entered into the system.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order based on business process and fulfillment rules.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Credit terms agreed with the customer (e.g., NET30).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant assigned to fulfill the order.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level used for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer expects the order to be delivered.']
  sales_org varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the sale.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region assigned to the sales organization.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation selected for delivery.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Customer‑provided notes for handling, packaging, or compliance.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status, e.g., reason for cancellation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the order.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all line items in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all line items in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order record.']

  Note: 'Core master record for a customer sales order capturing the full order header: customer account, order type (standard, rush, blanket, consignment, return), requested delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, total order value, currency, plant assignment, sales organization, distribution channel, and division. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for all customer order commitments in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Links to quotation (source), blanket order (if call-off), and downstream confirmations, deliveries, and status events. Maps to SAP SD transaction VA01/VA02.'
}

Table product.line_item {
  line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for line_item']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Needed for Regulatory Reporting to map each ordered chemical to its CAS registry entry for hazard classification and REACH compliance.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Required for order fulfillment and regulatory compliance reports: each line item must reference the master chemical product to derive pricing, hazard classification, and traceability.']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Regulatory Hazard Reporting requires associating sold items with the compound registry entry (CAS) for SDS generation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Detailed Costing report allocates line‑item costs to specific cost centers for granular expense analysis.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales order entry audit requires recording which employee created each line item for commission and compliance reporting.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: MES production scheduling assigns each order line to specific equipment for batch manufacturing, required for traceability and capacity planning.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Formulation Release Management links each sold line item to the approved experimental formulation used to produce the chemical.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Required for Order‑to‑Production planning: each sales order line must be tied to the exact formula version that will be manufactured, enabling batch scheduling and compliance reporting.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Production planning links each order line to the functional location (plant/area) where it will be produced, essential for allocation and compliance reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: GL Posting process: each line item must map to a GL account for revenue recognition and financial statement generation.']
  lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record. Business justification: Supports Traceability & Recall Management by linking each order line to the specific lot record of the shipped material.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Required for Order Fulfillment to retrieve material specifications, hazard data, and compliance info for each line item.']
  opportunity_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity_product. Business justification: Links each order line to the opportunity product that generated it, supporting win‑loss and forecasting reports.']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planned_order. Business justification: Order line fulfillment tracking links each sales order line to the MRP‑generated planned production order that will satisfy it, essential for production scheduling and order status reporting.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: REQUIRED: Each order line must reference the exact price‑list item applied, supporting price audit and margin analysis.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales order to which this line belongs.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Needed for procurement allocation process to map each sales order line to the PO that supplies the material.']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Maps order line items to their originating quote lines for margin and variance analysis.']
  special_handling_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.handling_instruction. Business justification: Line items reference handling instructions; moving to a FK normalizes data and removes the free-text column.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Supports supplier allocation per line for sourcing decisions and vendor performance tracking.']
  batch_determination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether batch allocation is required for this line (True) or not (False).']
  coa_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if a COA must be attached to the shipment for regulatory or customer reasons.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the supply chain after availability check.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price fields.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Discount value applied to this line item.']
  ehs_hazard_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS hazard statement associated with the material.']
  ehs_precautionary_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS precautionary statement for safe handling.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product batch is no longer sellable.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class for the product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  is_gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the product is manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practice standards.']
  last_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the line record.']
  line_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment entered by order entry personnel.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the order, starting at 1.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line item.']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net price multiplied by confirmed quantity before tax.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the produced batch.']
  material_gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account used for revenue recognition of this material.']
  material_sds_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic Safety Data Sheet for the material.']
  moq_enforced_flag boolean [note: 'True when the line must meet the customer\'s minimum order quantity requirement.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net price per unit before taxes and discounts, in the order currency.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity requested by the customer, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  partial_delivery_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the line can be split across multiple deliveries.']
  plant varchar [note: 'SAP plant code where the product will be produced or shipped from.']
  pricing_condition varchar [note: 'Code representing the pricing condition applied (e.g., discount, surcharge).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line item meets all applicable chemical regulations (REACH, TSCA, EPA).']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer requested for delivery of this line item.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the product remains usable from the production date.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage location identifier for inventory allocation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to this line item.']
  temperature_control_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product must be stored or transported at a controlled temperature.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the ordered quantity (kilograms, liters, metric tons, drums).']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a customer sales order representing a specific chemical product SKU, grade, or formulation being ordered. Captures line number, material number (SAP), product description, ordered quantity, unit of measure (kg, L, MT, drums), confirmed quantity, net price, pricing conditions, requested delivery date per line, plant, storage location, batch determination flag, COA/COC requirement flag, and special handling instructions (e.g., hazmat class, temperature-controlled). Supports MOQ enforcement and partial delivery rules.'
}

Table product.order_confirmation {
  order_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the order confirmation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Confirmation approval must be signed off by an authorized employee to satisfy internal controls and compliance.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sales order that this confirmation relates to.']
  special_handling_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.handling_instruction. Business justification: Order confirmations also store handling instructions; linking to handling_instruction standardizes handling guidance across confirmations.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Allocated production batch number for the confirmed product.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier responsible for delivery.']
  coa_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis is attached to the confirmation.']
  coc_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Compliance is attached to the confirmation.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'External confirmation number issued to the customer for this order confirmation.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the confirmation was issued to the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the confirmation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  delivery_date_confirmed date [note: 'Delivery date confirmed by the supplier.']
  delivery_date_requested date [note: 'Delivery date originally requested by the customer.']
  dot_classification varchar [note: 'U.S. Department of Transportation classification for hazardous material transport. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1|class_2|class_3|class_4|class_5|class_6|class_7|class_8|class_9 — promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the product lot, if applicable.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterm governing the shipment responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  ghs_hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification for the product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  is_deviation boolean [note: 'True if the confirmation deviates from the original customer request (e.g., quantity, packaging).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the batch of product supplied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information or remarks.']
  order_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the order confirmation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms for the order.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant that will supply the confirmed order.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price agreed for the confirmed quantity.']
  quantity_confirmed decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of product confirmed for delivery.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance status with applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, TSCA).']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method selected for transporting the confirmed order.']
  tax_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Applicable tax amount for the confirmed order.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the order confirmation.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount before discounts, taxes, and adjustments.']
  total_net_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net amount payable after discounts and taxes.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the shipment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the confirmed quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the confirmation record.']

  Note: 'Official order acknowledgment document sent to the customer confirming acceptance of a sales order. Captures confirmation number, confirmed order quantities, confirmed delivery dates, confirmed pricing, plant of supply, batch allocation status, COA/COC commitment, special handling confirmations (GHS hazard class, DOT classification), and any deviations from the original customer request. Serves as the contractual commitment record between Chemical Mfg and the customer.'
}

Table product.order_delivery_schedule {
  order_delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery schedule line.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product being scheduled for delivery.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Needed for the Lot Traceability report; each delivery schedule line must reference the specific production lot to satisfy GHS/REACH reporting.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this schedule line belongs.']
  research_trial_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.trial_batch. Business justification: Pilot Production Scheduling links delivery schedules to the trial batch used for pilot runs, enabling traceability.']
  scheduled_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Production planning assigns a scheduler; the employee ID is needed for workload balancing and audit of schedule changes.']
  shipment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics route planned for this delivery.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Delivery scheduling needs to know the exact stock position (warehouse location) from which inventory will be drawn for JIT shipping.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number linked to the scheduled product.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier responsible for the delivery.']
  carrier_scac varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code identifying the transportation carrier.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity that has been confirmed for delivery by the customer or logistics partner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule line record was created.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the allowed delivery time window as agreed with the customer.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the allowed delivery time window as agreed with the customer.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms of freight payment (e.g., prepaid, collect, third party).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheduled product is classified as hazardous.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'GHS classification code for the hazardous material (e.g., 3, 6, 8).']
  humidity_requirement_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowed humidity range for the product during transport.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Standard trade term governing delivery responsibilities.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'True if the schedule line is part of a JIT delivery program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any special instructions or comments related to the delivery schedule.']
  order_delivery_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery schedule line.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for the delivery (e.g., drum, tote, bulk).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority of the delivery schedule line.']
  regulatory_compliance_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating specific regulatory compliance requirements for the product (e.g., REACH, TSCA).']
  schedule_line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the schedule line within the order.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the delivery.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact end time of the scheduled delivery window.']
  scheduled_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity originally planned for this delivery schedule line.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact start time of the scheduled delivery window.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Incoterm defining the point of responsibility transfer between seller and buyer.']
  temperature_requirement_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum or minimum temperature condition required for the product during transport.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport selected for the delivery (e.g., truck, rail, ship, air, pipeline).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule line.']

  Note: 'Planned delivery schedule associated with a sales order or blanket order, defining agreed shipment windows, delivery quantities per period, and scheduling agreement call-offs. Captures schedule line dates, confirmed quantities, transportation planning point, shipping condition, route, and delivery priority. Supports just-in-time (JIT) delivery programs for automotive and industrial customers. Maps to SAP SD scheduling agreements (VA31) and delivery schedule lines.'
}

Table product.outbound_delivery {
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the outbound delivery record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics carrier assigned to the delivery.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order that triggered this delivery.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or party receiving the shipment.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Outbound delivery picking references a specific stock position; linking supports picking optimization and audit of shipped inventory.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the delivery was actually completed.']
  batch_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of production batch numbers assigned to the shipped material.']
  coa_attachment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a COA document is attached to the delivery.']
  coc_attachment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a COC document is attached to the delivery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the outbound delivery record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight cost.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'External delivery document number assigned by SAP EWM/SD.']
  delivery_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the delivery.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outbound delivery.']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery based on service level or special handling.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the agreed delivery time window.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the agreed delivery time window.']
  dot_hazard_class varchar [note: 'Department of Transportation hazard class code for the material.']
  freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of transportation for the delivery.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterm‑style freight payment terms.']
  goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods issue posting occurred, marking the physical shipment.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the delivery contains hazardous chemicals.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing the delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DAP|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  packing_instructions varchar [note: 'Special packing requirements or instructions for the delivery.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer‑requested delivery date.']
  ship_to_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the ship‑to party.']
  shipping_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the ship‑to location.']
  shipping_instruction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed shipping instruction document.']
  shipping_point_code varchar [note: 'Code of the plant or warehouse location from which the goods are shipped.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates if the delivery requires temperature control.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature requirement for temperature‑controlled shipments.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature requirement for temperature‑controlled shipments.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all items in the delivery, expressed in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the delivery, expressed in kilograms.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the delivery.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations identification number for hazardous material, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the outbound delivery record.']

  Note: 'Outbound delivery document created in SAP EWM/SD to execute the physical shipment of chemical products against a sales order. Captures delivery number, delivery date, ship-to party, shipping point, total weight, total volume, hazardous material indicator, UN number, DOT hazard class, packing instructions, batch numbers assigned, COA/COC attachment status, and goods issue posting date. Integrates with Oracle Transportation Management for carrier assignment and freight execution.'
}

Table product.status_event {
  status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for status_event']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this status event belongs.']
  triggering_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Event logs must capture which employee triggered a status change for audit and root‑cause analysis.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date on which the milestone was actually completed.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or instructions associated with the status change.']
  event_source_reference varchar [note: 'Logical name of the system or process that generated the event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the status change event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the status event, e.g., order_created, credit_check_passed, production_scheduled, goods_issued, delivered, invoiced, cancelled.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the event was recorded by a user; false if generated automatically by a system.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Order status after this event was applied.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Target date that was originally scheduled for this milestone.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Order status before this event was applied.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the event record.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Business party or role accountable for the event execution.']
  triggering_system varchar [note: 'Name of the automated system or service that caused the status transition.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the actual date deviated from the planned date; positive indicates delay.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log capturing every status transition and fulfillment milestone in the sales order lifecycle from creation through delivery, invoicing, and closure. Records event timestamp, previous status, new status, milestone type (order created, order confirmed, credit check passed, production scheduled, batch released, quality released, goods issued, picked up, in transit, customs cleared, delivered, POD received, invoiced), planned date, actual date, variance in days, triggering user or system, event source (SAP SD, OTM, MES, WMS, manual), responsible party, and event notes. Supports full order lifecycle traceability, SLA monitoring, on-time delivery (OTD) KPI calculation, proactive customer communication, customer inquiry resolution, and regulatory audit trail for controlled substance shipment tracking.'
}

Table product.product_hold {
  product_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hold']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  placed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory holds require identifying the employee who placed the hold for accountability and reporting.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this hold is applied.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount associated with a credit or financial hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the hold amount.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the hold, used in communications and reporting.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of the hold, indicating the underlying business reason.']
  is_regulatory_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold satisfies all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was initially placed on the order.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the hold for escalation handling.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details about why the hold was placed.']
  release_authorization varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that authorized the hold release.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was released or cleared.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Notes documenting actions taken to resolve or release the hold.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hold record.']

  Note: 'Record of a hold placed on a sales order preventing further processing or shipment. Captures hold ID, hold type (credit hold, quality hold, regulatory hold, export compliance hold, customer dispute hold), hold reason code, hold placed by, hold placed timestamp, hold release timestamp, release authorization, and resolution notes. Supports credit management integration with SAP FI/CO and quality hold integration with QM module. Tracks order blockage duration for SLA and customer service reporting.'
}

Table product.returns_order {
  returns_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying the return order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who initiated the return.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Returns are processed per lot to manage quality quarantine and regulatory compliance; linking enables the Returns Quality Inspection workflow.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Return processing is performed by a specific employee; tracking supports credit memo audit and quality investigations.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the original sales order linked to this return.']
  actual_return_date date [note: 'Date the returned goods were actually received.']
  coa_attached boolean [note: 'True if a Certificate of Analysis (COA) is attached to the return record.']
  coc_attached boolean [note: 'True if a Certificate of Compliance (COC) is attached to the return record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return order record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  credit_memo_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return qualifies for a credit memo to the customer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code for monetary values (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  disposal_instruction varchar [note: 'Business instruction for how the returned product should be handled after receipt.']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the returned goods are expected to arrive at the plant.']
  inspection_result_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the outcome of the inspection.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection performed on the returned product.']
  logistics_provider varchar [note: 'Name or code of the carrier responsible for transporting the return.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Refund amount after tax and any applicable deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or instructions related to the return.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or warehouse receiving the returned goods.']
  quality_issue_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any quality problems discovered.']
  quality_issue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a quality issue was identified during inspection.']
  refund_amount_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the return before taxes and deductions.']
  return_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the returned product.']
  return_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return order was created or submitted by the customer.']
  return_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the return order by the source system.']
  return_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of product being returned, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the product is being returned.']
  returns_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the return order.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the return shipment.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the refund.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the return shipment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the return quantity (e.g., kg, L, pcs).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this return order record.']

  Note: 'Customer return order (RMA) capturing the authorization and processing of product returns from customers. Captures return order number, original sales order reference, return reason code (OOS, damaged, wrong product, excess inventory, quality complaint), return quantity, return condition, plant receiving the return, credit memo eligibility, disposal instructions (restock, destroy, rework), and return logistics details. Maps to SAP SD Returns Order (VA01 with order type RE) and reverse logistics workflow.'
}

Table product.handling_instruction {
  handling_instruction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for handling_instruction']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Special handling instructions are authored by a responsible employee; audit trail is required for safety and regulatory reviews.']
  applicable_cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service number identifying the chemical substance.']
  applicable_product_code varchar [note: 'Product code (SKU) to which this handling instruction applies.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating the geographic region where the instruction applies.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents (COA, COC) are attached.']
  carrier_restriction varchar [note: 'Allowed transportation modes for the product.']
  compliance_document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory document (e.g., SOP, MSDS) that mandates this instruction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the instruction record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the instruction is no longer applicable (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the instruction is valid for use.']
  emergency_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for emergency communications.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or department to contact in an emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for emergency communications.']
  handling_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary charge associated with the special handling.']
  handling_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the handling fee.']
  handling_instruction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the instruction.']
  hazard_code varchar [note: 'UN identification number for the hazardous material.']
  hazardous_material_class varchar [note: 'Hazard class of the material (limited to primary six classes; additional classes may be added via reference table).']
  humidity_max_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest allowable relative humidity level.']
  humidity_min_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest allowable relative humidity level.']
  inert_atmosphere_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an inert gas environment is mandatory.']
  instruction_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the instruction in ERP and logistics systems.']
  instruction_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the handling instruction.']
  instruction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the instruction indicating the primary handling requirement.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the instruction must be followed for the product.']
  labeling_requirement varchar [note: 'Labeling details such as hazard symbols, temperature icons, or carrier marks.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the instruction.']
  packaging_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific packaging needed (e.g., double‑walled, UN‑rated container).']
  pressure_max_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Highest permissible pressure for the product.']
  pressure_min_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lowest permissible pressure for the product.']
  pressure_vessel_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transport must use a pressure‑rated vessel.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework that drives the handling requirement.']
  segregation_requirement varchar [note: 'Instructions to keep the product separate from incompatible chemicals.']
  special_equipment_needed varchar [note: 'Equipment required for handling (e.g., forklift, crane, ventilated pallet).']
  storage_location_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific storage location constraints (e.g., refrigerated warehouse, ventilated area).']
  temperature_control_method varchar [note: 'Method used to maintain temperature (e.g., refrigerated, heated, ambient).']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest permissible temperature for storage or transport.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest permissible temperature for storage or transport.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the instruction.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the instruction for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Customer-specific or product-specific handling instructions for chemical products requiring non-standard treatment during order fulfillment. Captures instruction type (temperature-controlled storage/transport, segregation from incompatible materials, inert atmosphere, pressure vessel requirements), applicable product codes, effective date range, packaging requirements, labeling requirements, carrier restrictions, and regulatory basis (DOT 49 CFR, IATA DGR, IMDG Code). Ensures safe and compliant handling from warehouse pick through final delivery.'
}

Table product.fulfillment_milestone {
  fulfillment_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fulfillment_milestone']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this milestone belongs.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Milestone ownership is assigned to a specific employee; needed for performance tracking and exception handling.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date on which the milestone was actually completed.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch associated with the milestone, when applicable.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier handling the shipment.']
  compliance_document_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required regulatory documents (e.g., SDS, COA) are attached to the milestone record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance for cross‑border shipments.']
  delivery_window_end date [note: 'End date of the expected delivery window for the order.']
  delivery_window_start date [note: 'Start date of the expected delivery window for the order.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is considered critical for on‑time delivery (True) or not (False).']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the plant, warehouse, or logistics hub where the milestone occurs.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the milestone indicating whether it is pending, completed, delayed, or cancelled.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the fulfillment step (e.g., order confirmed, production scheduled, batch released, goods issued, in transit, delivered).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special handling instructions related to the milestone.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Date originally scheduled for the milestone to be achieved.']
  proof_of_delivery_received boolean [note: 'True when a signed proof of delivery (POD) has been captured for the shipment.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Internal team, department, or external partner accountable for executing the milestone.']
  shipping_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment related to the milestone.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the milestone record (e.g., SAP Sales & Distribution, Oracle Transportation Management, Manufacturing Execution System, Warehouse Management System).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in days between planned_date and actual_date (positive = delay, negative = early).']

  Note: 'Tracks key fulfillment milestones for a sales order from confirmation through final delivery, providing end-to-end order visibility for customer service and supply chain teams. Captures milestone type (order confirmed, production scheduled, batch released, goods issued, in transit, customs cleared, delivered, POD received), planned date, actual date, variance in days, responsible party, and milestone source system (SAP SD, OTM, MES). Enables on-time delivery (OTD) KPI calculation and proactive customer communication.'
}

Table product.proof_of_delivery {
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the proof of delivery record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who received the shipment.']
  delivery_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle used for the delivery (e.g., trailer number).']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order associated with this delivery.']
  receiving_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: POD process records the internal employee who received the shipment, required for traceability and liability.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier associated with the shipped product.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier that performed the delivery.']
  carrier_scac varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code identifying the carrier.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the goods upon receipt.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the POD record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the signed amount.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Physical address or site where the goods were delivered.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment was physically received by the customer.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing any discrepancies observed.']
  humidity_percent double [note: 'Relative humidity measured at the delivery site at receipt.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery fulfillment cycle is closed.']
  is_invoice_released boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POD triggered invoice release in SAP SD.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the chemical product delivered.']
  pod_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the POD document (paper or electronic).']
  pod_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the POD (e.g., electronic signature, paper scan, EDI 214).']
  pod_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the POD document.']
  pod_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the POD document was recorded in the system.']
  proof_of_delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the POD record.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the signed quantity.']
  receiving_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for the delivery.']
  receiving_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the receiving contact.']
  signed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the goods as confirmed by the POD.']
  signed_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of product confirmed as received by the customer.']
  temperature_celsius double [note: 'Temperature recorded at the delivery site at receipt.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the POD record.']

  Note: 'Proof of Delivery (POD) record confirming that a chemical shipment has been received by the customer at the agreed delivery point. Captures POD document number, delivery number, actual delivery date and time, receiving contact name, delivery location, signed quantity, condition on receipt (intact, damaged, short), discrepancy notes, POD method (electronic signature, paper scan, EDI 214), and POD receipt timestamp. Triggers invoice release in SAP SD and closes the order fulfillment cycle.'
}

Table product.certification {
  certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product certification record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product this certification applies to.']
  renewed_certification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on certification (renewed_certification_id)']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit related to the certification.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique number assigned by the certifying body.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of certification held by the product.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the certification ceases to be effective, if different from expiry.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the certification becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certification expires.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the certification was issued.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory authority or jurisdiction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the certification.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number associated with the certification.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or regulatory jurisdiction for the certification.']
  renewal_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewals.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification requires renewal.']
  scope_of_certification varchar [note: 'Description of the product scope covered by the certification.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the certification data originated.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the certification document.']

  Note: 'Product certification master record tracking all quality, safety, and regulatory certifications held by a chemical product or grade. Captures certification type (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, food-grade/FCC, pharmaceutical-grade/USP/EP, kosher, halal, NSF/ANSI 60, FDA FCN, organic, ISCC PLUS), certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, scope of certification, audit date, renewal requirements, and certification status (active, expired, suspended, withdrawn). Supports customer qualification questionnaires, food-contact compliance, pharma supply chain audits, and sustainability certifications (ISCC, RSB). Critical for Chemical Mfg customers in food, pharma, and personal care sectors who require certified supply chain documentation.'
}

Table product.order_amendment {
  order_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order amendment record.']
  primary_order_chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product line before substitution.']
  primary_order_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who entered the amendment request.']
  primary_order_site_id varchar [note: 'Address identifier for shipment before the amendment.']
