  adjusted_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Value applied to the instrument after calibration to bring it within tolerance.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tag or barcode used for inventory tracking.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the calibration certificate issued after a successful calibration.']
  calibration_history_count varchar [note: 'Total number of calibration events recorded for this instrument.']
  calibration_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded during calibration.']
  calibration_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the calibration event.']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Reference material or standard used during calibration.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the instrument\'s calibration lifecycle.']
  calibration_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the calibration event (routine, after repair, or verification).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the instrument master record was first created.']
  gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument meets Good Manufacturing Practice requirements.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the instrument was first installed and made operational.']
  instrument_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label of the instrument as used in the lab.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of analytical instrument (e.g., HPLC, GC, ICP‑MS, UV‑Vis, Titrator, pH Meter).']
  iso17025_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with the international standard for testing and calibration laboratories.']
  lab_instrument_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the instrument.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration event.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent preventive maintenance was performed.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location or plant code where the instrument is installed.']
  maintenance_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval in days between routine maintenance activities.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactured the instrument.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the next calibration must be performed.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance.']
  part11_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with electronic records and signatures regulations.']
  performed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the technician who performed the calibration.']
  post_calibration_reading decimal(12,4) [note: 'Instrument reading recorded after calibration adjustments.']
  pre_calibration_reading decimal(12,4) [note: 'Instrument reading recorded before calibration adjustments.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date the most recent qualification activity was completed.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification stage of the instrument (Installation Qualification, Operational Qualification, Performance Qualification, or not qualified).']
  qualified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/department that performed the qualification.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory compliance status of the instrument.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the instrument hardware.']
  tolerance decimal(12,4) [note: 'Acceptable deviation range for the calibration reading.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the instrument record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for laboratory analytical instruments and their complete calibration history used in quality testing. Captures instrument identity (ID, name, type: HPLC, GC, ICP-MS, UV-Vis, titrator, pH meter, viscometer), manufacturer, model, serial number, location, qualification status (IQ/OQ/PQ), and full calibration event records including calibration event ID, calibration type (routine, after-repair, verification), calibration standard used, calibration date, performed by, calibration result (pass, fail, adjusted), pre-calibration reading, post-calibration reading, tolerance, next due date, calibration certificate number, and calibration status (in-calibration, overdue, out-of-service). Single source of truth for instrument master data and all calibration lifecycle events. Supports GMP instrument qualification, 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, and ISO/IEC 17025 audit trails.'
}

Table quality.instrument_calibration {
  instrument_calibration_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each calibration event.']
  lab_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the laboratory instrument that was calibrated.']
  performed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the qualified personnel who executed the calibration.']
  adjusted_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Value applied to the instrument to bring it within tolerance, if a correction was needed.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date on which the calibration activity was performed.']
  calibration_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or plant where the calibration was performed.']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Technique used for calibration (e.g., gravimetric, electronic, photometric).']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Name or code of the reference standard used for calibration (e.g., NIST SRM).']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calibration record.']
  calibration_type varchar [note: 'Category of calibration performed (e.g., routine, after repair, verification).']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the calibration certificate was formally issued.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the calibration certificate issued after a successful calibration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration record was initially created in the system.']
  gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the calibration activity complies with Good Manufacturing Practice standards.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the pre- and post-calibration readings (e.g., mg/L, ppm).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration of the instrument.']
  post_calibration_reading decimal(18,6) [note: 'Instrument reading recorded after calibration adjustment.']
  pre_calibration_reading decimal(18,6) [note: 'Instrument reading recorded before calibration adjustment.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calibration meets applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., 21 CFR Part 11, ISO/IEC 17025).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations recorded by the calibrator.']
  result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the calibration activity indicating whether the instrument passed, failed, or required adjustment.']
  standard_version varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier of the calibration standard.']
  tolerance decimal(10,4) [note: 'Acceptable deviation range for the instrument reading, expressed in the same unit as the readings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the calibration record.']

  Note: 'Calibration event record for a laboratory instrument capturing each calibration activity performed to ensure measurement accuracy and GMP compliance. Records calibration event ID, instrument reference, calibration type (routine, after-repair, verification), calibration standard used, calibration date, performed by, calibration result (pass, fail, adjusted), pre-calibration reading, post-calibration reading, tolerance, next due date, and calibration certificate number. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 and ISO/IEC 17025 audit trails.'
}

Table quality.stability_study {
  stability_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for stability_study']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or formulation under study.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Stability testing cost allocation: each stability study is budgeted and expensed against a cost center.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Stability studies run in controlled chambers (equipment); linking ensures equipment calibration and compliance tracking.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Stability studies assess shelf‑life of a particular formulation version, requiring direct linkage for compliance reporting.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Stability studies are assigned to a specific production order batch; required for shelf‑life documentation.']
  primary_stability_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality analyst responsible for the study.']
  production_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_plan. Business justification: Production Planning uses stability study outcomes to set shelf‑life limits and batch size constraints for the associated production plan.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when the final time‑point measurement was recorded.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality approval status of the study.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the study.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the study was approved.']
  batch_lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot number used for the study sample.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes or observations about the study.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the study record was created in the system.']
  degradation_trend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a degradation trend was observed across time‑points.']
  labware_lims_study_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the study record in LabWare LIMS.']
  light_exposure varchar [note: 'Description of light exposure condition for the study.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if any measurement exceeded specification limits.']
  oot_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if any measurement was out of trend criteria.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Target date for study completion as per protocol.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory guideline or standard governing the study (e.g., ICH Q1A, EPA).']
  shelf_life_estimate_months varchar [note: 'Estimated shelf life in months based on study results.']
  shelf_life_units varchar [note: 'Units for the shelf life estimate.']
  stability_study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stability study.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the stability study commenced.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target relative humidity percentage for the study condition.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the study condition.']
  study_number varchar [note: 'External identifier for the stability study as assigned by the quality department.']
  study_protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the study protocol document.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Category of stability testing performed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the study record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number for audit tracking of the study record.']

  Note: 'Stability study master record and complete time-point result data managing long-term, accelerated, and stress stability testing programs for chemical products and formulations. Captures study number, study type (long-term, accelerated, stress, photostability), product/formulation reference, storage conditions (temperature, humidity, light), study protocol, testing intervals, regulatory reference (ICH Q1A, EPA), study status, and all individual time-point measurement results including stability result ID, time point (T0, T3M, T6M, T12M, T24M), storage condition, characteristic tested, measured value, specification limit, pass/fail status, degradation trend flag, OOT flag, analyst, test date, and LabWare LIMS result reference. Single source of truth for stability study design and all associated measurement data. Supports shelf-life determination, product registration dossiers, and regulatory stability report generation.'
}

Table quality.stability_result {
  stability_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the stability result record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch_record. Business justification: Stability result must be tied to the exact batch record to correlate test conditions with production data.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory analyst who performed or reviewed the test.']
  lab_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory instrument used for the test.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Stability results are generated for a stored lot; association with inventory lot is required for shelf‑life tracking.']
  sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical sample taken for analysis.']
  stability_study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stability study to which this result belongs.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number of the product being tested.']
  characteristic_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the analytical characteristic tested (e.g., potency, impurity).']
  characteristic_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the characteristic tested.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Analyst or system comments related to the result.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stability result record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originally captured the result.']
  degradation_trend_flag boolean [note: 'True if the result shows a statistically significant degradation trend over time.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product is considered expired for regulatory purposes.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the time of testing.']
  labware_result_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the raw result record stored in LabWare LIMS.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the storage location where the sample was kept.']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric result of the analytical measurement.']
  measurement_method_code varchar [note: 'Code of the analytical method used for the measurement.']
  measurement_method_description varchar [note: 'Full description of the analytical method.']
  oot_flag boolean [note: 'True if the result deviates from the expected trend (Out‑of‑Trend).']
  pass_fail_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value meets the specification (pass) or not (fail).']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the product under stability study.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the result complies with applicable regulatory specifications.']
  sample_weight decimal(12,4) [note: 'Weight of the sample used for the test.']
  sample_weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample weight (e.g., g, mg).']
  specification_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Acceptable limit defined in the product specification for this characteristic.']
  stability_result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the result record.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Condition under which the sample was stored during the stability interval.']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature during the test, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stability test was executed.']
  time_point varchar [note: 'Scheduled time point of the stability measurement (e.g., T0, T3M, T6M, T12M, T24M).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the measured value (e.g., mg/L, % w/w).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the stability result record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the result record for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Individual test result record from a stability study time point measurement. Captures stability result ID, study reference, time point (T0, T3M, T6M, T12M, T24M), storage condition, characteristic tested, measured value, specification limit, pass/fail status, degradation trend flag, OOT flag, analyst, test date, and LabWare LIMS result reference. Feeds shelf-life calculations and regulatory stability reports for product registration dossiers.'
}

Table quality.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Audit cost allocation: internal audit expenses are posted to the cost center responsible for the audited process.']
  lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Lead auditor is a designated employee; linking enables audit assignment tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Material‑focused compliance audits require linking the audit record to the specific Material Master under review.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the audit actually concluded.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the audit actually began.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the audit, used for tracking and reference.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit, e.g., ISO 9001, GMP, Process Safety, etc.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit record.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on its origin or purpose.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the audit record was formally closed.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the audit record.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the person or group responsible for implementing corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department or functional area audited.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the audit report or supporting documents.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the audit (date level).']
  external_auditor varchar [note: 'Name of the external auditor or audit firm, if applicable.']
  findings_major varchar [note: 'Count of major non‑conformance findings.']
  findings_minor varchar [note: 'Count of minor non‑conformance findings.']
  findings_observation varchar [note: 'Count of observation‑type findings.']
  findings_ofi varchar [note: 'Count of OFI‑type findings.']
  findings_total varchar [note: 'Total number of findings recorded for the audit.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the audit record was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  lead_auditor varchar [note: 'Name of the primary auditor responsible for the audit.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next review of the audit record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by auditors.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the audit after evaluation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the audit took place.']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulation or clause referenced by the audit.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority associated with the audit, if applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPA|OSHA|ECHA|ISO|FDA|TSCA|DOT|NFPA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the root cause for findings.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the root cause identified.']
  standard varchar [note: 'Standard or regulation against which the audit is performed.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the audit (date level).']
  team varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of auditors participating in the audit.']
  type_detail varchar [note: 'Additional detail describing the audit type, e.g., supplier certification, customer compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of verification for the corrective action.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the audit record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Quality audit record managing internal and external quality system audits and all associated findings for ISO 9001, GMP, customer, and regulatory compliance. Captures audit header (audit number, type: internal/external/supplier/customer/regulatory, scope, standard, lead auditor, audit team, dates, plant/department, outcome) and individual finding detail records (finding ID, finding type: major NC, minor NC, observation, OFI; finding description, clause reference, affected process, root cause category, assigned owner, response due date, response submitted date, response description, verification status, closure date). Tracks number of findings by severity, audit outcome (pass, conditional pass, fail), follow-up due dates, and CAPA linkages for major non-conformances. Single source of truth for audit program management, finding tracking, and continuous improvement evidence.'
}

Table quality.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each audit finding.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit that generated this finding.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated CAPA record, if one exists.']
  evidence_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the document or file that provides evidence for the finding.']
  primary_audit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team assigned to address the finding.']
  tertiary_audit_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who performed the latest update.']
  affected_process varchar [note: 'Name or code of the process, unit, or operation impacted by the finding.']
  audit_finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the audit observation or non‑conformance.']
  audit_finding_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the finding originated from an internal audit or an external party.']
  audit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit finding.']
  clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the ISO 9001 or GMP clause that the finding relates to.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was formally closed after verification.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the finding represents a regulatory non‑compliance.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'True if the finding mandates a corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the audit finding record was first created.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number of the finding within the audit.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the finding: major non‑conformance, minor non‑conformance, observation, or opportunity for improvement.']
  regulatory_clause varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory requirement (e.g., EPA, OSHA, REACH) cited by the finding.']
  response_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the corrective or preventive action proposed.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which a corrective response must be submitted.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which the corrective response was actually submitted.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) calculated from severity, likelihood, and exposure.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the underlying cause of the finding.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating assigned to the finding based on impact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the finding record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the response: pending, verified, or rejected.']

  Note: 'Individual finding record from a quality audit capturing a specific non-conformance, observation, or opportunity for improvement identified during an audit. Records finding ID, audit reference, finding type (major NC, minor NC, observation, OFI), finding description, clause reference (ISO 9001 clause, GMP section), affected process, root cause category, assigned owner, response due date, response submitted date, response description, verification status, and closure date. Drives CAPA creation for major non-conformances.'
}

Table quality.reference_standard {
  reference_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reference standard record.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique CAS registry number identifying the chemical substance.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certificate of analysis issued for the standard.']
  concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of the standard (e.g., mol/L) when applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reference standard record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the reference standard is no longer considered valid for use.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class applicable to the standard.']
  is_gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the standard meets Good Manufacturing Practice requirements.']
  is_iso17025_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO/IEC 17025 laboratory accreditation standards.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier assigned by the supplier for traceability.']
  purity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Purity of the standard expressed as a percentage.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the reference standard was received into inventory.']
  reference_standard_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or catalogue code assigned to the reference standard.']
  reference_standard_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the reference standard as used in laboratory documentation.']
  reference_standard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reference standard.']
  reference_standard_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the standard (e.g., primary, secondary, working, certified reference material).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the standard satisfies all applicable regulatory requirements (EPA, OSHA, REACH, etc.).']
  reorder_level varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold that triggers a replenishment request.']
  safety_considerations varchar [note: 'Notes on handling, PPE, and safety precautions for the standard.']
  stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Current on‑hand quantity of the reference standard in the designated unit of measure.']
  stock_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the stock quantity (e.g., kg, L).']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage environment for the standard (e.g., refrigerated, ambient).']
  supplier varchar [note: 'Name of the external supplier or manufacturer of the reference standard.']
  traceability_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the standard\'s traceability chain (e.g., NIST, ECHA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reference standard record.']

  Note: 'Master record for certified reference standards and reagents used in laboratory testing and instrument calibration. Captures standard ID, standard name, CAS number, standard type (primary, secondary, working, certified reference material), supplier, lot number, purity/concentration, certificate number, receipt date, expiry date, storage condition, current stock quantity, reorder level, and traceability chain (NIST, ECHA, pharmacopoeia). Ensures measurement traceability per ISO/IEC 17025 and GMP requirements.'
}

Table quality.evidence_document {
  evidence_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for evidence_document']
  superseded_evidence_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on evidence_document (superseded_evidence_document_id)']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the document was formally approved.']
  archive_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was archived.']
  archived boolean [note: 'True if the document has been moved to archive storage.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who originally created the document.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 checksum used to verify file integrity.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Organizational classification of the document\'s sensitivity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the document record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context or summary of the document content.']
  document_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the evidence document.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned unique code or number for the document, used in quality records and audits.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the evidence document, such as Certificate of Analysis (COA) or Test Report.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the document becomes effective for its intended use.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the document is no longer valid or must be reviewed.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'Logical storage location or URI of the document in the lakehouse.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the stored document file in bytes.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document contains confidential information.']
  mime_type varchar [note: 'Internet media type of the document file (e.g., application/pdf).']
  related_process varchar [note: 'Quality or manufacturing process to which the document is linked (e.g., Batch Release).']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the document must be retained according to policy.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the most recent review of the document.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the document (e.g., LabWare LIMS).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document within the quality system.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of user‑defined tags for ad‑hoc categorization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the document record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the document, incremented on each revision.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for evidence_document. Referenced by evidence_document_id.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the template record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for coa_template. Referenced by coa_template_id.'
