Table quality.spc_chart {
  spc_chart_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for spc_chart']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or group responsible for the chart.']
  parent_spc_chart_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on spc_chart (parent_spc_chart_id)']
  chart_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the chart\'s focus area.']
  chart_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the chart for quick lookup.']
  chart_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the SPC chart used in reports and dashboards.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Statistical method used for the chart (e.g., X‑Bar, R, S, P, NP, C).']
  chart_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the chart definition, supporting change management.']
  control_limit_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit for the chart.']
  control_limit_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit for the chart.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the chart record was first created in the system.']
  data_points_count varchar [note: 'Total number of data points currently stored for the chart.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the data feeding the chart (e.g., LabWare LIMS, process sensor).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the chart purpose, scope, and application.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the chart becomes active for monitoring.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the chart is retired or no longer used (null if open‑ended).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often new data points are collected for the chart.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the chart was most recently refreshed with new data.']
  measurement_parameter varchar [note: 'Name of the process variable or quality attribute being monitored (e.g., viscosity, impurity %).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the chart.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of individual observations collected for each subgroup.']
  spec_limit_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable specification limit for the parameter.']
  spec_limit_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable specification limit for the parameter.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the chart definition.']
  subgroup_size varchar [note: 'Number of units in each subgroup used for control‑limit calculations.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired nominal value or set point for the measured parameter.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit for the measured parameter (e.g., kg, ppm, °C).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the chart record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for spc_chart. Referenced by chart_id.'
}

Table formulation.formula {
  formula_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the master formula record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory audit requires the employee who approved the formula to be recorded for compliance and traceability.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Safety and regulatory submissions need the full CAS registry record for each formula; linking provides hazard classification and compliance data.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost Allocation Report requires each formula to be charged to a cost center for accurate COGS reporting.']
  primary_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Required for Sourcing Strategy Report: each formula designates a primary vendor for key raw material, enabling procurement planning and regulatory compliance.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability dashboards require each formula to be linked to a profit center for margin analysis.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Needed for Product Development Lifecycle Report linking final formula to originating R&D project for traceability.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated Safety Data Sheet record.']
  active_ingredient_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the primary active ingredient in the formulation.']
  blend_ratio_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of component blend ratios.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service registry number identifying the primary substance.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the formula.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formula record was first created.']
  default_process_parameter varchar [note: 'Key process parameter (e.g., temperature, pH) that is standard for this formula.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the formula becomes effective for production.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the formula is no longer valid (nullable).']
  formula_code varchar [note: 'External code or identifier used across systems to reference the formulation.']
  formula_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the chemical formulation.']
  formula_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the formula.']
  formulation_category varchar [note: 'Category describing the manufacturing approach of the formula.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard classification for the formulation.']
  hazard_statement varchar [note: 'Standardized hazard statement(s) associated with the formulation.']
  ip_ownership_flag boolean [note: 'True if the formulation is proprietary and protected by IP.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formula was last reviewed for compliance or performance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the formula.']
  precautionary_statement varchar [note: 'Standardized precautionary statement(s) for safe handling.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Higher‑level product family or line to which the formula belongs.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory regime governing the formulation (e.g., GHS, REACH).']
  standard_batch_size_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Typical batch size for which the formula is defined, expressed in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the formula record.']
  version_control_method varchar [note: 'Method used to control formula version changes.']
  version_count varchar [note: 'Total number of version records associated with this master formula.']

  Note: 'Master record for a chemical formulation or blend recipe. Represents the authoritative, version-independent identity of a chemical product\'s composition including formula code, product family, regulatory classification, IP ownership flag, and lifecycle state (active, discontinued, archived). This is the SSOT for all formulation identities within the enterprise. Child formula_version records carry version-specific composition details, process parameters, and specifications.'
}

Table formulation.formula_version {
  formula_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each formula version record.']
  approved_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Vendor Assignment in Version Control: a formula version records the vendor responsible for supplying the versioned product, used in change management and cost tracking.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical product that this formula version produces.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Version‑specific budgeting and variance analysis need a cost center link for each formula version.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Version control logs require the employee who created each formula version for regulatory change‑management.']
  management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the linked Management of Change record governing this revision.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Version‑level profit tracking supports detailed product line profitability reporting.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual/committee that approved the formula version.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the formula version received formal approval.']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bill of materials associated with the formula version.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique Chemical Abstracts Service registry number for the primary chemical(s) in the formula.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Reason or business justification for the formula revision.']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to produce a batch using this formula version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the formula version record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost estimate.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for the formulation (e.g., R&D, Production).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this formula version becomes effective for production.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the formula version is no longer valid for use.']
  formula_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the formula (e.g., internal catalog or recipe code).']
  formula_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the chemical formulation.']
  formulation_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or responsible department for the formula.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class assigned to the formulation.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this version is the active version for the formula.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the formula with applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, TSCA).']
  target_batch_size decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned batch size for which the formula version is intended, expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for the target batch size (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the latest modification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the formula version record.']
  version_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of changes, special notes, or usage guidance for this version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the formula revision.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the formula version.']

  Note: 'Version-controlled record of a specific formula revision including its Bill of Materials header data. Tracks version number, effective date, expiry date, version status (draft, approved, obsolete), change rationale, approval authority, MOC linkage, target batch size, BOM type (R&D, pilot, commercial), and unit of measure basis. Serves as the grouping entity for recipe_line_items (BOM components) and recipe_steps (process instructions). Enables full audit trail of formula evolution from initial R&D through commercial scale-up and subsequent reformulations.'
}

Table formulation.formula_bom {
  formula_bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the formula BOM record.']
  primary_formula_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who created the BOM record.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SDS document governing the component\'s safe handling.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred supplier for the component.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the BOM was formally approved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number associated with the component.']
  bom_code varchar [note: 'Business code or identifier assigned to the formula BOM.']
  bom_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the formula BOM.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Management of Change (MOC) record governing this BOM version.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Category indicating the type of formula (e.g., final product, intermediate, raw material).']
  component_cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service registry number uniquely identifying the component substance.']
  component_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the component material.']
  component_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Nominal quantity of the component required for the BOM.']
  component_sequence varchar [note: 'Line order of the component within the BOM.']
  component_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the component quantity (e.g., kg, L, mol).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the BOM record was first created in the system.']
  critical_ingredient boolean [note: 'True if the component is designated as critical for product performance or regulatory compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the BOM becomes effective for production.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the environmental impact of the BOM (e.g., based on VOC, HAP content).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the BOM is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended BOMs).']
  formula_bom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOM.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard classification for the component.']
  hazardous_ingredient_flag boolean [note: 'True if the component is classified as hazardous under EPA regulations.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the component batch.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the BOM.']
  process_stage varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the formula (research, scale‑up, or commercial production).']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the component under applicable regulations.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date the BOM was released for production use.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision of the BOM.']
  substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component may be substituted with an approved alternative.']
  tolerance_max decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable quantity for the component (upper bound).']
  tolerance_min decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable quantity for the component (lower bound).']
  total_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate nominal weight of all components in the BOM, expressed in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the BOM record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the BOM, supporting change control and revision tracking.']

  Note: 'Bill of Materials for a chemical formulation, listing all raw material inputs, intermediates, and additives required to produce a specified batch quantity. Captures BOM item sequence, component CAS number, nominal quantity, unit of measure, tolerance range (min/max), substitution flag, and critical ingredient designation. Supports both R&D and commercial production BOMs.'
}

Table formulation.recipe_line_item {
  recipe_line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for recipe_line_item']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Component Traceability Report links each BOM component to master compound registry for safety, compliance, and regulatory reporting.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent formula version (recipe header).']
  primary_recipe_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the component supplier.']
  reach_dossier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the REACH registration dossier for the component.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Safety Data Sheet for the component.']
  active_ingredient_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component is an active ingredient in the final product.']
  addition_sequence varchar [note: 'Order in which the component is added during the manufacturing process.']
  analytical_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory method used to verify component concentration (e.g., HPLC, GC).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier associated with the component supply.']
  blend_ratio_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of the component expressed as a percentage of the total blend.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Management of Change (MOC) reference number for any modification to the component specification.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the component within the formulation.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the target concentration (weight/weight, volume/volume, parts per million).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line item record was created.']
  critical_ingredient_flag boolean [note: 'Marks components whose deviation could impact product safety or performance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the component specification is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the component specification is no longer valid (nullable).']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification codes for the component.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates if this component is the default entry for the formula version.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Logical deletion flag for soft‑deleting the line item without physical removal.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the line item (e.g., draft, released).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the component within the formula.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier for traceability.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the component concentration or ratio.']
  measurement_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which analytical measurements are performed.']
  mixing_order varchar [note: 'Timing of component addition relative to mixing steps.']
  nominal_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'Base amount of the component specified for the formula.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the component.']
  ratio_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used for the blend ratio (weight, volume, or molar).']
  recipe_line_item_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the component within the formula.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the component.']
  substitution_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the component can be substituted with an approved alternative.']
  target_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Desired concentration of the component in the final product.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for the nominal quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line item.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the component concentration or ratio.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the line item for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Line-item component within a formula version\'s Bill of Materials. Represents a single raw material, intermediate, solvent, catalyst, or additive with its blend ratio, concentration targets, and SPC control limits. Stores component identifier (CAS number), nominal quantity, unit of measure, blend ratio (% w/w, % v/v, mol%), upper/lower control limits, target concentration, measurement basis (weight, volume, molar), analytical verification method, addition sequence, mixing order, active ingredient flag, substitution eligibility, and critical ingredient designation. SSOT for what goes into a formula, in what proportion, and within what tolerance — consolidating all ratio, concentration, and component-level specification data.'
}

Table formulation.blend_ratio {
  blend_ratio_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each blend ratio record.']
  primary_blend_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical component used in the blend.']
  analytical_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory method used to verify the component concentration.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blend ratio was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the blend ratio.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier when the blend ratio is applied to a production batch.']
  blend_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the blend across systems.']
  blend_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the blend formulation.']
  blend_ratio_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blend ratio record.']
  blend_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blend (e.g., pilot, commercial, experimental, research).']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining why the blend ratio was changed.']
  component_cas_number varchar [note: 'Standard CAS registry number for the component.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the concentration values.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blend ratio record was created.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the blend ratio data (e.g., SAP, Aspen HYSYS).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the blend ratio becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the blend ratio remains valid (null for open‑ended).']
  ehs_review_status varchar [note: 'Environmental Health & Safety review outcome for the blend.']
  ghs_classification_code varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification code for the blend.']
  ip_protection_status varchar [note: 'IP status of the blend (patented, trade secret, or none).']
  is_proprietary boolean [note: 'True if the blend formulation is proprietary.']
  last_quality_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last quality control verification.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier associated with the blend application.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(12,6) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit for the component concentration.']
  measurement_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the concentration is measured (weight, volume, or molar).']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was recorded.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(5,2) [note: 'Uncertainty (±%) associated with the analytical measurement.']
  nominal_concentration decimal(12,6) [note: 'Target nominal concentration of the component in the blend.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the blend ratio.']
  quality_control_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent quality control check.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the blend meets applicable regulatory requirements.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the blend ratio record must be retained per policy.']
  safety_data_sheet_ref varchar [note: 'Reference or link to the SDS associated with the blend.']
  scale_up_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'Factor used to scale the laboratory blend ratio to commercial production.']
  target_concentration decimal(12,6) [note: 'Desired concentration value used for SPC target tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(12,6) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit for the component concentration.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the blend ratio record for change control.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the record.']

  Note: 'Defines the precise blend ratios and concentration targets for each component in a formulation. Captures nominal ratio, lower control limit, upper control limit, target concentration (PPM, % w/w, % v/v, mol%), measurement basis (weight, volume, molar), and the analytical method used to verify the ratio. Supports SPC (Statistical Process Control) tolerance management and scale-up ratio adjustments.'
}

Table formulation.active_ingredient_spec {
  active_ingredient_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the active ingredient specification record.']
  primary_active_vendor_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the supplier entity.']
  active_ingredient_spec_code varchar [note: 'Internal catalog code used to reference the specification within ERP/MES systems.']
  active_ingredient_spec_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the active ingredient (e.g., Acetaminophen).']
  active_ingredient_spec_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the specification.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the specification was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the specification.']
  assay_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for assay values and content percentages.']
  batch_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number associated with the specification version.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service identifier for the ingredient.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent change to the specification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the specification record was created.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed specification document or data sheet.']
  echa_registration_number varchar [note: 'European Chemicals Agency registration number.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the specification becomes valid.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the specification is no longer valid (nullable).']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard class for the ingredient.']
  ghs_pictogram_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of GHS pictogram codes applicable to the ingredient. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GHS01|GHS02|GHS03|GHS04|GHS05|GHS06|GHS07|GHS08|GHS09 — promote to reference product]']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling or safety instructions for the ingredient.']
  hazard_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS hazard statement for the ingredient.']
  ingredient_type varchar [note: 'Category of the ingredient indicating its role in formulations.']
  max_content decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum allowed percentage of the active ingredient in the final product.']
  min_content decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum allowed percentage of the active ingredient in the final product.']
  particle_size_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum particle size specification.']
  particle_size_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum particle size specification.']
  particle_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for particle size measurements.']
  precautionary_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS precautionary statement for the ingredient.']
  purity_grade varchar [note: 'Required purity level for the ingredient.']
  reache_dossier_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the REACH dossier associated with the ingredient.']
  regulatory_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the regulatory threshold value.']
  regulatory_threshold_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum permissible level of the ingredient under regulatory limits (e.g., REACH).']
  restricted_use_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is subject to restricted‑use regulations.']
  storage_temperature_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum recommended storage temperature.']
  storage_temperature_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum recommended storage temperature.']
  storage_temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for storage temperature values.']
  target_assay decimal(10,4) [note: 'Desired assay value for the active ingredient.']
  test_method_code varchar [note: 'LIMS method code used for analytical testing of the ingredient.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the specification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the specification.']

  Note: 'Specification record for active ingredients (API or active chemical component) within a formulation. Defines minimum and maximum active content, target assay value, analytical test method (LIMS method code), regulatory threshold limits (e.g., REACH, EPA, FIFRA), GHS hazard classification, purity grade requirements, and particle size distribution where applicable. Serves as the authoritative active content tracking record for regulatory compliance and QC release.'
}

Table formulation.recipe_step {
  recipe_step_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for recipe_step']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Process step approvals are recorded against the responsible employee for GMP compliance and deviation tracking.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific equipment unit used.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent formulation process that this step belongs to.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material/ingredient used in the step.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SDS applicable to materials used in this step.']
  actual_agitation_rpm varchar [note: 'Measured agitation speed during execution.']
  actual_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured duration the step actually took.']
  actual_pressure_bar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured pressure achieved during execution.']
  actual_temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured temperature achieved during execution.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the step was approved.']
  batch_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the batch associated with this step.']
  ccp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the step is a critical control point for quality/safety.']
  control_parameter varchar [note: 'Key process parameter that is monitored or controlled in this step.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the step record was created.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the step deviated from target parameters.']
  deviation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any deviation from target parameters.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned duration for the step.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Category of equipment required to execute the step.']
  ingredient_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the ingredient required for the step.']
  ingredient_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the ingredient quantity.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the step is performed automatically by control systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or instructions for the step.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Type of operation performed in this step (e.g., charge, mix, heat, react, filter, blend).']
  parameter_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowed tolerance percentage for the control parameter.']
  step_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the step within the formulation process.']
  step_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the step.']
  target_agitation_rpm varchar [note: 'Desired agitation speed in revolutions per minute.']
  target_pressure_bar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Desired pressure to achieve during the step.']
  target_temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Desired temperature to achieve during the step.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the step record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the step record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the step definition for change control.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the step record.']

  Note: 'Ordered sequence of process steps required to manufacture a formulation. Each step captures step number, operation type (charge, mix, heat, cool, react, filter, blend, pH adjust), target process parameters (temperature, pressure, agitation speed, duration), equipment type required, and critical control point (CCP) flag. Supports MES integration and process simulation alignment for batch execution planning.'
}

Table formulation.formula_spec {
  formula_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the formula specification record.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Formula specifications belong to a specific formula version; linking provides versioned spec data and removes duplicated versioning fields.']
  active_ingredient varchar [note: 'Name of the primary active chemical component.']
  appearance varchar [note: 'Descriptive text of the formula\'s visual appearance (e.g., clear, opaque).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the latest revision was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the latest revision.']
  assay_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target assay percentage for the active ingredient.']
  boiling_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the formulation boils under standard pressure.']
  color varchar [note: 'Color description of the finished product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the formula specification record was first created.']
  density_kg_per_m3 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Mass per unit volume of the formulation.']
  flash_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature at which vapors ignite.']
  formula_spec_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the formula specification.']
  formula_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formula based on production method or purpose.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard classification codes.']
  hazard_statements varchar [note: 'Standardized hazard statements associated with the formula.']
  is_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the formula meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  lot_number_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern or format used to generate lot numbers for this formula.']
  max_hap_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Regulatory limit for hazardous air pollutants in the product.']
  max_tri_release_kg_per_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed release to the Toxics Release Inventory per year.']
  max_voc_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Regulatory limit for volatile organic compounds in the product.']
  melting_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the formulation solidifies.']
  moc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal MOC is required for changes to this formula.']
  moisture_content_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Moisture level in parts per million.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the formula specification.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Primary packaging configuration for the product.']
  ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acidity/alkalinity measurement of the formulation.']
  purity_grade varchar [note: 'Regulatory purity standard applicable to the active ingredient.']
  refractive_index decimal(5,3) [note: 'Optical property indicating light bending capability.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region whose regulatory regime applies to the formula.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the latest revision was approved.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision identifier for change control.']
  specific_gravity decimal(5,3) [note: 'Ratio of the formulation\'s density to that of water.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the formula specification.']
  viscosity_cp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Viscosity of the formulation measured in centipoise.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Quality specification record for a formula version, defining all physical, chemical, performance, and active ingredient properties the formulation must meet. Covers appearance, viscosity, density, pH, flash point, active content (assay targets, purity grades), regulatory threshold limits, and customer-specific acceptance criteria. Linked to COA generation, QC release decisions, and LIMS analytical methods. SSOT for \'what does good look like\' for a given formula version.'
}

Table formulation.compatibility_matrix {
  compatibility_matrix_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compatibility matrix record.']
  component_a_active_ingredient_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.active_ingredient_spec. Business justification: Compatibility matrix entries reference active ingredient specifications; new FK follows naming rule and replaces legacy column.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the second component in the compatibility pair.']
  compatibility_matrix_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compatibility record.']
  compatibility_rating varchar [note: 'Overall compatibility assessment for the component pair.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was initially created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this compatibility record is effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which this compatibility record remains effective (null if indefinite).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context or observations.']
  pressure_max_bar decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum pressure (bar) for which the compatibility assessment is valid.']
  pressure_min_bar decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum pressure (bar) for which the compatibility assessment is valid.']
  risk_type varchar [note: 'Primary safety risk associated with the interaction.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature (°C) at which the compatibility assessment is valid.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature (°C) at which the compatibility assessment is valid.']
  test_basis varchar [note: 'Source of evidence used to determine compatibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the compatibility assessment, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Chemical compatibility assessment matrix recording known interactions between formulation components, raw materials, packaging materials, and storage conditions. Captures component pair identifiers, compatibility rating (compatible, incompatible, conditionally compatible), reaction risk type (exothermic, corrosive, toxic gas generation), test basis (experimental, literature, computational), and applicable temperature/pressure range. Critical for PSM (Process Safety Management) and HAZOP compliance.'
}

Table formulation.formula_change_request {
  formula_change_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the formula change request.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: CUSTOMER‑INITIATED CHANGE REQUEST: Records the originating customer account for formula change requests, needed for traceability and SLA reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Change‑request cost impact is tracked against a cost center for financial approval and budgeting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or stakeholder who originated the change request.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Formulation change requests often mandate equipment modifications; linking to equipment supports MOC tracking and compliance reporting.']
  management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'FK to ehs.management_of_change.management_of_change_id — Formula change requests must link to the master MOC record in EHS for PSM/OSHA compliance. Without this FK, cannot trace formulation changes back to the formal MOC lifecycle.']
  primary_formula_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the component being replaced in the formulation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Change‑request financial impact is evaluated against the responsible profit center for decision making.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Change Request Origin Tracking ties each formula change request to the R&D project that initiated it, supporting governance and audit.']
  additional_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information or notes related to the change request.']
  approval_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent approval action.']
  approval_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the multi‑step approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the latest approval decision was recorded.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role of the individual performing the current approval step (e.g., Process Engineer, Quality Manager, Regulatory Officer).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the business need or benefit driving the change.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of formulation change being requested.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the record was first created in the data lake.']
  digital_signature varchar [note: 'Cryptographic signature of the approver confirming authenticity of the decision.']
  effective_implementation_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved change becomes operational in production.']
  equivalency_assessment varchar [note: 'Technical evaluation confirming functional equivalence between original and substitute components.']
  formula_change_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change request.']
  impacted_formula_versions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of formula version identifiers that would be affected by the change.']
  regulatory_reregistration_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the change triggers a regulatory re‑submission or re‑registration.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the change request, used in communications and tracking.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the change request was initially submitted.']
  risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of identified risks and mitigations associated with the change.']
  substitution_approval_status varchar [note: 'Overall approval status of the component substitution.']
  test_data_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to laboratory test results supporting the substitution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Management of Change (MOC) request, approval workflow, and substitution record for formulation changes. Captures change request number, change type (ingredient substitution, ratio adjustment, process parameter change, supplier change), business justification, risk assessment summary, impacted formula versions, regulatory re-registration requirement flag, approval workflow stages (approver role, decision, timestamp, digital signature), substitution details (original/substitute component, equivalency assessment, test data, approval status), and effective implementation date. SSOT for formulation change control governance including full approval audit trail and ingredient substitution management.'
}

Table formulation.moc_approval {
  moc_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the MOC approval record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who performed the approval.']
  management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MOC request to which this approval belongs.']
  approval_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the approval must be completed to keep the MOC schedule on track.']
  approval_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this approval step within the overall MOC workflow.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the approver within the organization.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments provided by the approver.']
  condition_notes varchar [note: 'Additional conditions or actions required when the decision is \'conditionally_approved\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval record was first created in the system.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the approval step.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  digital_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the stored digital signature for this approval.']
  is_final_approval boolean [note: 'True if this step represents the final approval required for the MOC request.']
  moc_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this approval record.']
  reason_for_rejection varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when the decision is \'rejected\'.']
  related_document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of supporting documentation attached to the approval (e.g., risk assessment, test report).']
  signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the digital signature was captured.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the approval record.']

  Note: 'Approval workflow record for a MOC (Management of Change) request. Tracks each approval stage, approver role (R&D, QA, EHS, Regulatory, Operations), approval decision (approved, rejected, conditionally approved), approval timestamp, comments, and digital signature reference. Provides full audit trail for regulatory compliance and GMP documentation requirements.'
}

Table formulation.scale_up_record {
  scale_up_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the scale‑up record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: CUSTOMER‑DRIVEN SCALE‑UP: Captures which customer commissioned a scale‑up, supporting project costing and invoicing.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Scale‑up studies require an employee sign‑off to validate data before pilot production, per internal SOPs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Scale‑up projects are capital‑budgeted; costs must be allocated to a cost center for CAPEX planning.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master formulation that is being scaled up.']
  formulation_process_simulation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Aspen HYSYS or other simulation model used for the scale‑up.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Capital project profit center requires scale‑up cost linkage for ROI calculation.']
  research_trial_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.trial_batch. Business justification: Scale‑up Planning uses data from R&D trial batch; linking enables comparison of pilot results with scale‑up performance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the formal scale‑up approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the scale‑up record received approval.']
  batch_size_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Target batch mass for the scale‑up run expressed in kilograms.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks related to the scale‑up.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the scale‑up record.']
  deviation_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any deviations observed during scale‑up.']
  energy_balance_mj decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total energy consumption or balance for the simulated scale‑up run, expressed in megajoules.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the scale‑up record.']
  phase_equilibrium_status varchar [note: 'Result of phase‑equilibrium analysis for the scale‑up (stable, unstable, or not applicable).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scale‑up record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scale‑up record.']
  scale_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the laboratory batch size to reach the target scale.']
  scale_up_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier assigned to the scale‑up event.']
  scale_up_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scale‑up record.']
  scale_up_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the scale‑up transition was executed.']
  simulation_conversion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Predicted conversion rate from reactants to product in the simulation.']
  simulation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the simulation (steady‑state or dynamic).']
  simulation_yield_prediction_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield percentage predicted by the simulation model.']
  source_formula_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the original laboratory‑scale formula.']
  target_scale varchar [note: 'Intended production scale for the formulation (lab, pilot, or commercial).']
  validated_against_actual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the simulation results have been validated with real production data.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the simulation was compared to actual production results.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Comments on the outcome of the validation exercise.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual product yield achieved at the target scale, expressed as a percent of theoretical maximum.']

  Note: 'Documents the scale-up transition of a formulation from R&D laboratory scale to pilot scale and commercial production scale, including associated process simulation data. Captures source formula version, target scale (lab/pilot/commercial), scale factor, batch size at each scale, process parameter adjustments, yield at each scale, observed deviations, scale-up approval status, simulation model references, simulation type (steady-state, dynamic), key simulation outputs (yield prediction, conversion rate, energy balance, phase equilibrium), and validated-against-actual flag. Bridges computational chemistry, physical formulation development, and production scale-up for process optimization confidence.'
}

Table formulation.validation_batch {
  validation_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the validation batch record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: R&D validation batch expenses are charged to a cost center for spend tracking and variance reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who executed the batch.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or production line used for the batch.']
  formula_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change control record associated with this batch.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific formula version being validated.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Validation batches are inspected; linking to the inspection lot enables traceability of validation outcomes.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: R&D profit center reporting needs validation batch linkage for revenue attribution.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: BATCH VALIDATION AT CUSTOMER SITE: Required for regulatory audit trails showing where validation batches were executed for a client.']
  actual_pressure_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured average pressure during processing in bar.']
  actual_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured average temperature during processing in degrees Celsius.']
  actual_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual yield achieved expressed as a percentage of theoretical maximum.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the validation batch.']
  batch_size_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Planned size of the validation batch expressed in kilograms.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch (e.g., pilot, scale‑up, full production, lab).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the batch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the validation batch record was first created.']
  data_capture_system varchar [note: 'System that captured the process data for the batch.']
  data_capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch data was ingested into the lakehouse.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any deviation from the approved formula occurred during execution.']
  impurity_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured impurity concentration expressed in parts per million.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number assigned to the batch for traceability.']
  material_batch_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the primary raw material batch used in the validation.']
  material_source varchar [note: 'Source identifier of the primary raw material used (e.g., vendor code).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes captured by the operator or quality engineer.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the batch.']
  pressure_setpoint_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target pressure setpoint for the process in bar.']
  process_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch processing completed.']
  process_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch processing started.']
  quality_issue_description varchar [note: 'Description of any quality issue observed during the batch.']
  quality_issue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if any quality issue was recorded for the batch.']
  regulatory_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory review for the batch.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether any safety incident occurred during the batch.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target yield percentage defined in the formula.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature setpoint for the process in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the validation batch record.']
  validation_batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the validation batch.']
  validation_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the validation batch indicating pass, fail, or conditional status.']

  Note: 'Record of a trial, pilot, or experimental batch produced to validate a new or modified formulation. Captures trial batch number, formula version under test, batch size, equipment used, actual process parameters, yield, trial outcome (pass/fail/conditional), and any deviations from the approved formula observed during execution. Supports R&D-to-production handoff, formula validation, and deviation tracking prior to commercial release.'
}

Table formulation.formula_deviation {
  formula_deviation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each formula deviation record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Deviation records must capture the approving employee to close the loop on corrective actions and regulatory reporting.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production or trial batch where the deviation occurred.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Link to the corrective and preventive action record associated with this deviation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quality deviation handling costs are charged to a cost center for quality cost tracking.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved formulation that the batch is based on.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific ingredient that is affected by the deviation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QA approval was recorded.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned steps to correct the immediate deviation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deviation record was first created in the system.']
  deviation_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between observed and expected values.']
  deviation_category varchar [note: 'Domain area impacted by the deviation.']
  deviation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the deviation event for tracking and reference.']
  deviation_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of why the formula deviated.']
  deviation_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the deviation amount (e.g., kg, %, ppm).']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final decision on how the affected batch will be handled.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deviation was observed on the shop floor or in the lab.']
  expected_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The value specified in the approved formula (e.g., weight, percent).']
  formula_deviation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deviation record.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Narrative assessment of the deviation\'s impact on product quality, safety, and compliance.']
  magnitude_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage representation of the deviation relative to the expected value.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the deviation.']
  observed_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual measured value recorded during production.']
  preventive_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned steps to prevent recurrence of similar deviations.']
  qa_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Quality Assurance has approved the deviation handling.']
  root_cause_classification varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the underlying cause of the deviation.']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the specific root cause identified.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the deviation based on impact and likelihood.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the deviation record.']

  Note: 'Records deviations from the approved formula specification during manufacturing or trial batches. Captures deviation type (ingredient substitution, ratio out-of-spec, process parameter exceedance), affected batch or trial reference, deviation magnitude, root cause classification, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) linkage, disposition decision (use-as-is, rework, reject), and QA approval. Supports OOS (Out of Specification) and OOT (Out of Trend) management.'
}

Table formulation.ip_record {
  ip_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IP record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: IP LICENSING OWNERSHIP: Links IP records to the customer account that holds the license, enabling royalty calculations and compliance tracking.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the base formulation associated with the IP.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formula version covered by the IP.']
  ip_sds_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Safety Data Sheet linked to the IP.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the IP record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when IP protection becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when IP protection expires or ends.']
  ip_approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the authority that approved the IP record.']
  ip_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the IP record received formal approval.']
  ip_cas_number varchar [note: 'Standard CAS registry number for the chemical component covered by the IP.']
  ip_change_rationale varchar [note: 'Reason for any changes made to the IP record.']
  ip_cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value or cost associated with the IP.']

  ip_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  ip_ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System classification applicable to the IP.']
  ip_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the IP record.']
  ip_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the IP record, such as patent number or trade secret code.']
  ip_owner_party_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party that owns the IP.']
  ip_owner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IP owner (internal, external, joint).']
  ip_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IP.']
  ip_regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to the IP.']
  ip_regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the IP.']
  ip_royalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Royalty amount per payment period.']
  ip_royalty_payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which royalty payments are made.']
  ip_royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Royalty rate expressed as a percentage of revenue.']
  ip_royalty_status varchar [note: 'Current status of royalty payments for the IP.']
  ip_type varchar [note: 'Category of IP protection.']
  ip_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the IP record.']
  ip_version_status varchar [note: 'Status of this IP version.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction for IP protection.']
  license_end_date date [note: 'Date when the license expires or terminates.']
  license_start_date date [note: 'Date when the license becomes effective.']
  licensee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity receiving a license for the IP.']
  licensing_restriction varchar [note: 'Type of licensing restriction applied to the IP.']
  patent_application_number varchar [note: 'Official patent application number assigned by the patent office.']
  patent_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the patent expires.']
  patent_filing_date date [note: 'Date the patent application was filed.']
  patent_grant_date date [note: 'Date the patent was granted.']
  patent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the patent.']
  trade_secret_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the trade secret content.']

  Note: 'Intellectual property record for proprietary chemical formulations and blends. Tracks IP type (trade secret, patent, know-how), patent application number, filing date, grant date, expiry date, jurisdiction, confidentiality classification, licensing restrictions, and the formula versions covered by the IP protection. Ensures proprietary blend data is governed and access-controlled appropriately.'
}

Table formulation.formulation_regulatory_submission {
  formulation_regulatory_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each regulatory submission record.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formulation to which this regulatory submission applies.']
  active_ingredient_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of the primary active ingredient in the formulation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory authority granted approval or issued a decision.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch linked to the regulatory submission, if applicable.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service registry number uniquely identifying the primary chemical substance.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining why the submission was modified or resubmitted.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the submission (e.g., registration, notification, amendment).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by regulatory affairs staff.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance outcome of the submission relative to regulatory requirements.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the electronic dossier or supporting documents stored externally.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the approved submission becomes effective for commercial use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Expiration date of the approval, if applicable; null for open‑ended authorizations.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Full GHS classification string, including hazard statements and pictograms.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class assigned to the formulation (e.g., Flammable, Toxic).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the submission within the regulatory workflow.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the chemical product associated with the formulation.']
  re_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow‑up submission is mandated due to changes or regulatory feedback.']
  re_submission_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition or event that triggers a required resubmission (e.g., formulation change, new hazard data).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the submission record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the submission record.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority receiving the submission, such as ECHA, EPA, FDA, or OSHA.']
  regulatory_classification_code varchar [note: 'Internal code linking the submission to a regulatory classification scheme used by the enterprise.']
  submission_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the submission within the regulatory lifecycle.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the submission was formally sent to the regulatory authority.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Unique dossier or reference number assigned by the regulatory body for tracking.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory submission, e.g., REACH registration, TSCA notification, EPA PMN, SDS filing, GHS classification.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the active ingredient content percentage or batch size.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the submission record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the submission record reflecting changes or resubmissions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the submission record.']

  Note: 'Tracks regulatory submissions required for a formulation, including REACH registration, TSCA notification, EPA pre-manufacture notice (PMN), SDS/MSDS filing, and GHS classification submissions. Captures submission type, regulatory body (ECHA, EPA, FDA), submission date, dossier reference number, submission status, approval date, and re-submission trigger conditions. Ensures formulations meet all applicable chemical regulations before commercial release.'
}

Table formulation.formulation_process_simulation {
  formulation_process_simulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for process_simulation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Simulation resource usage is budgeted to a cost center for R&D expense reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or engineer responsible for the simulation.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formulation version that the simulation supports.']
  actual_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield observed after validation against real production data.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the simulation results.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the simulation was approved or rejected.']
  avg_pressure_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average pressure across the simulation, in bar.']
  avg_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average temperature across the simulation, in degrees Celsius.']
  batch_size_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target batch size for which the simulation was performed, in kilograms.']
  conversion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Predicted conversion rate of reactants to products, expressed as a percentage.']
  cpu_usage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average CPU utilization during the simulation run.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the simulation record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Overall quality rating of the simulation input and output data (0‑1 scale).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the simulation execution finished.']
  energy_balance_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net energy consumption or generation for the simulated process, in megawatt‑hours.']
  formulation_process_simulation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the simulation.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard classification relevant to the simulated formulation.']
  input_data_set_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the input data set used for the simulation.']
  is_sensitivity_analysis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the simulation includes a sensitivity analysis.']
  memory_usage_gb decimal(6,2) [note: 'Peak memory consumption of the simulation, in gigabytes.']
  model_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying process model used in the simulation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the analyst about the simulation run.']
  output_data_set_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data set generated by the simulation.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the simulation adheres to applicable regulatory constraints (e.g., EPA, OSHA).']
  runtime_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total wall‑clock time the simulation consumed, in minutes.']
  sensitivity_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of parameters varied in the sensitivity analysis.']
  simulation_category varchar [note: 'Category of simulation such as pilot, full‑scale, or R&D.']
  simulation_code varchar [note: 'External code or number assigned to the simulation run.']
  simulation_software varchar [note: 'Software platform used to execute the simulation.']
  simulation_type varchar [note: 'Category of simulation: steady‑state, dynamic, or scale‑up.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the simulation execution began.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired product yield set for the simulation, expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the simulation record.']
  validated_against_actual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the simulation results have been validated against real production data.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Comments from the validation team regarding discrepancies or confirmations.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the simulation definition.']
  yield_prediction_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Predicted product yield expressed as a percentage.']

  Note: 'Record of a process simulation run associated with a formulation version. Captures simulation model reference, simulation software platform, simulation type (steady-state, dynamic, scale-up), key outputs (yield prediction, conversion rate, energy balance, phase equilibrium), simulation status, validated-against-actual flag, and the formula version the simulation supports. Bridges computational chemistry and physical formulation development for scale-up confidence and process optimization.'
}

Table formulation.formula_stability_test {
  formula_stability_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stability testing study record.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formulation that is being tested for stability.']
  stability_study_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.stability_study.stability_study_id — Formula stability tests must reference the master stability study in quality domain for unified stability program management and ICH compliance reporting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the study results were formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or role that approved the study results.']
  conclusion_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the stability study conclusion and any recommendations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stability study record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the stability study became effective for reporting purposes.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the stability study is considered expired or superseded.']
  formula_stability_test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stability study.']
  light_exposure varchar [note: 'Light exposure condition applied during storage (dark, ambient, or UV).']
  measurement_appearance varchar [note: 'Qualitative observation of appearance (e.g., clear, hazy, precipitate) at the test interval.']
  measurement_assay_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Assay result expressed as percent of active ingredient at the test interval.']
  measurement_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the formulation at the test interval.']
  measurement_viscosity_cp decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured viscosity of the formulation at the test interval, expressed in centipoise.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  protocol_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the test protocol, including sample preparation and analytical methods.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the test result for the interval.']
  result_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the analytical result was recorded.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Calculated shelf‑life in months based on the stability data.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target relative humidity percentage for the storage environment.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the stability study.']
  study_code varchar [note: 'Business code assigned to the stability study for tracking and reference.']
  study_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the stability testing study.']
  test_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after start of storage when the sample is tested.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the stability study record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the stability study record, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Stability testing study record for a formulation, tracking how the formulation\'s physical and chemical properties change over time under defined storage conditions (temperature, humidity, light exposure). Captures study protocol, storage conditions, test intervals, test parameters (viscosity, pH, assay, appearance), results at each time point, shelf life determination, and study conclusion. Supports product shelf life claims and regulatory submissions.'
}

Table formulation.formula_substitution {
  formula_substitution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the formula substitution record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Substitution approvals are tied to a qualified employee to ensure safety, compliance, and cost‑impact analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Substitution cost‑savings and impact are allocated to a cost center for financial analysis.']
  primary_formula_recipe_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the component being replaced.']
  affected_formula_version_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of formula version identifiers impacted by this substitution.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the substitution was formally approved.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number linking the substitution to a Management of Change (MOC) record.']
  cost_savings_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary savings per batch resulting from the substitution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the substitution record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the substitution may be applied in production.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) indicating the environmental benefit or risk of the substitution.']
  equivalency_assessment varchar [note: 'Summary of the technical assessment confirming equivalence.']
  equivalency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the technical equivalence of the substitute component.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the substitution is no longer valid.']
  formula_substitution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the substitution record.']
  hazard_classification_change varchar [note: 'Indicates if the substitution changes the overall hazard classification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the substitution.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Business reason for performing the substitution (e.g., supply shortage, cost reduction).']
  regulatory_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substitution triggers regulatory review.']
  substitution_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the substitution driver.']
  substitution_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the substitution in processes and documentation.']
  substitution_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the substitution.']
  test_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory test report supporting the substitution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the substitution record.']
  validation_test_date date [note: 'Date when the validation test was performed.']
  validation_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the validation testing performed on the substituted formulation.']

  Note: 'Records approved ingredient substitutions within a formulation, capturing the original component, the substitute component, substitution rationale (supply shortage, cost reduction, regulatory restriction), equivalency assessment outcome, test data supporting substitution, approval status, and the formula versions affected. Supports supply chain resilience and raw material sourcing flexibility without compromising product quality.'
}

Table formulation.mes_recipe {
  mes_recipe_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MES recipe record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: MES recipe changes must be authorized by a responsible employee to meet GMP and change‑control procedures.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: MES recipe execution costs are charged to a cost center for production cost accounting.']
