  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: MES recipe assignment to a specific reactor/equipment is required for production scheduling, traceability, and regulatory batch records.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Mes recipe is derived from an approved formula version; linking enables traceability from production recipe to its source version.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipe received regulatory or MOC approval.']
  batch_number_prefix varchar [note: 'Prefix used when generating batch numbers for runs of this recipe.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the recipe to its change control request.']
  control_strategy varchar [note: 'Control algorithm or strategy applied during recipe execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipe record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the recipe is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the recipe becomes valid for execution.']
  equipment_unit varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or unit where the recipe is executed.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System classification for the recipe\'s hazardous aspects.']
  hazardous_ingredient_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipe contains any hazardous ingredients.']
  hold_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration to hold a setpoint before proceeding to the next step, in minutes.']
  ingredient_material_code varchar [note: 'Material code of the ingredient used in the recipe.']
  ingredient_sequence varchar [note: 'Order in which ingredients are charged into the equipment.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this version is the current active recipe.']
  is_valid boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the recipe passes all validation checks for execution.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful sync with MES/DCS.']
  lot_number_prefix varchar [note: 'Prefix used when generating lot numbers for material produced with this recipe.']
  mes_recipe_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recipe.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the recipe.']
  ph_setpoint decimal(4,2) [note: 'Target pH value for the process.']
  ramp_rate_c_per_min decimal(6,2) [note: 'Rate at which temperature is increased or decreased, expressed in °C per minute.']
  recipe_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the recipe used in production planning.']
  recipe_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the recipe.']
  recipe_source varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating formula or version from which this recipe was derived.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the recipe.']
  setpoint_pressure_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target pressure setpoint for the process, in bar.']
  setpoint_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature setpoint for the process, in degrees Celsius.']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Current synchronization state between the recipe record and shop‑floor control systems.']
  target_batch_size_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Planned batch size for which the recipe is designed, expressed in kilograms.']
  target_yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected product yield expressed as a percentage of the target batch size.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recipe record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the recipe.']

  Note: 'Production-ready recipe record derived from an approved formula version for execution on manufacturing execution systems (MES), distributed control systems (DCS), or programmable logic controllers (PLC). Captures recipe code, target equipment unit, control recipe parameters, phase instructions, setpoints, material charge sequences, and synchronization status with plant automation. Bridges formulation master data and real-time batch execution on the shop floor per ISA-88 batch control standards.'
}

Table formulation.formula_contract {
  formula_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the FormulaContract association']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the chemical formulation']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is terminated']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes effective']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the contract (e.g., Active, Suspended, Terminated)']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier agreed for this account‑formula contract']

  Note: 'Represents a contractual agreement between a customer account and a chemical formulation. Each record captures the specific terms of the agreement, including effective dates, pricing tier, and contract status, linking one account to one formula.. Existence Justification: Customers (accounts) can hold contracts for multiple chemical formulas, and each formula can be sold to many customers under separate contracts. The contract itself carries attributes such as start/end dates, pricing tier, and status, which are managed by business users. Therefore the relationship is a true many‑to‑many operational entity.'
}

Table formulation.formula_waste {
  formula_waste_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the formula_waste association']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the formulation']
  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the waste stream']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Disposal method applied to this waste when generated by the specific formula']
  quantity decimal [note: 'Amount of waste generated for the formula in the unit defined by the waste stream']
  waste_origin_process varchar [note: 'Name of the production process step that creates the waste for this formula']

  Note: 'Represents the association between a chemical formulation and a waste stream. Each record captures the quantity of waste generated, the process that creates it, and the disposal method specific to that formula‑waste pairing.. Existence Justification: In chemical manufacturing, a single formulation can generate several distinct waste streams, and each waste‑stream type can be produced by many different formulations. Engineers actively record each formula‑waste pairing with quantities, the originating process, and disposal method, and the records are created, updated, and deleted as production changes.'
}

Table formulation.pricing_assignment {
  pricing_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the pricing_assignment association']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the formula version']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the pricing strategy']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the pricing strategy becomes active for the formula version']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the pricing strategy expires for the formula version']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification for assigning this pricing strategy to the formula version']

  Note: 'This association captures the assignment of a pricing strategy to a specific formula version. Each record links one formula_version to one pricing_strategy and stores the effective period and rationale for that assignment.. Existence Justification: The pricing team actively assigns pricing strategies to individual formula versions. A single formula version can have multiple pricing strategies over its lifecycle, and a pricing strategy can be applied to many different formula versions. The assignment is tracked with effective dates and a rationale, making the relationship a managed business entity.'
}

Table order.inquiry {
  inquiry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inquiry']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who submitted the inquiry.']
  inquiry_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the inquiry record.']
  inquiry_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative assigned to the inquiry.']
  inquiry_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the inquiry record.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: R&D Project Inquiry Tracking records which research project an RFQ originates from for feasibility analysis.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier to which the inquiry is sent.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price amounts.']
  delivery_date_required date [note: 'Date by which the customer expects the product to be delivered.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Address where the product is to be delivered.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Any discount the customer expects or negotiates on the gross price.']
  gross_price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Requested gross price before any discounts or adjustments.']
  inquiry_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the inquiry.']
  inquiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inquiry was initially created/submitted.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal comments or observations added by sales or support staff.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inquiry contains confidential or proprietary information.']
  net_price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final price after discounts, representing the amount the customer expects to pay.']
  priority_flag varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the inquiry for internal processing.']
  product_cas_number varchar [note: 'Standard CAS number identifying the chemical substance requested.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal product identifier or SKU requested in the inquiry.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the product specifications requested.']
  quantity_requested decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of product the customer wishes to purchase.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inquiry record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the inquiry record.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier is expected to respond to the inquiry.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the RFQ for customer and internal tracking.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the inquiry was received.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special handling, safety, or packaging instructions supplied by the customer.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated tax amount based on the net price, if applicable.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is tax‑exempt.']
  technical_spec_attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to a stored technical specification attachment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the requested quantity is expressed.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the quoted prices remain valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the quoted prices are valid.']

  Note: 'Request for Quotation record capturing a customer\'s formal inquiry for pricing, availability, and technical specifications before a sales order is placed. Stores RFQ number, customer account, inquiry date, validity period, requested products and quantities, target price, delivery requirements, technical specification attachments, and assigned sales representative. Represents the pre-order commercial negotiation stage in the order-to-cash workflow. Maps to SAP SD Inquiry (VA11) and Quotation (VA21) transactions.'
}

Table order.quotation {
  quotation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quotation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the quotation.']
  billing_address_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing address for invoicing.']
  quotation_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the quotation.']
  quotation_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the quotation.']
  quotation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative responsible for the quotation.']
  quotation_last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who last modified the quotation.']
  quotation_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping address for delivery.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Project‑Based Quotation Management ties custom quotes to the R&D project that defines the specification.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval state of the quotation within internal review process.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quotation was approved.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents (e.g., COA, COC) are attached to the quotation.']
  competitor_quote_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for a competitor\'s quote, if captured for win/loss analysis.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the quotation has been converted to a sales order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quotation record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts in the quotation.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall discount percentage applied to the quotation.']
  ehs_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Environmental Health and Safety review is required for the quoted products.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Delivery terms governing the shipment of goods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inquiry_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the original customer inquiry (RFQ) that led to this quotation.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates if the quotation contains confidential pricing or terms.']
  is_price_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quoted prices are locked for the validity period.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed lead time in days from order to delivery.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered for the quoted products.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes entered by sales or support staff.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed for the quotation (e.g., Net 30).']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate quoted prices.']
  quotation_date date [note: 'Date when the quotation was issued to the customer.']
  quotation_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the quotation as used in SAP Sales and Distribution.']
  quotation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quotation.']
  quote_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the quotation for revisions.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the quotation complies with relevant chemical regulations (e.g., REACH, TSCA).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the quotation.']
  technical_specifications varchar [note: 'Free-text description of technical grade specifications for the quoted products.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate discount applied to the quotation.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before discounts, taxes, and fees.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after discounts and before taxes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the quotation.']
  valid_until date [note: 'End date of the quotation\'s validity period.']
  win_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Reason recorded for winning or losing the quotation opportunity.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Start date of the quotation\'s validity period.']

  Note: 'Commercial negotiation record spanning the full pre-order lifecycle from initial customer inquiry (RFQ) through formal price quotation, negotiation, and acceptance or expiry. Captures inquiry/quotation number, stage (inquiry_received, qualification, draft_quote, formal_quote, negotiation, accepted, expired, rejected, converted_to_order), customer account, inquiry date, validity period, requested and quoted products with quantities, target and quoted prices, discount structure, payment terms, incoterms, MOQ, lead time commitment, technical grade specifications, delivery requirements, technical specification attachments, assigned sales representative, competitor quote reference, win/loss reason, and acceptance status. Tracks conversion rate from inquiry to quotation to confirmed sales order. Maps to SAP SD Inquiry (VA11) and Quotation (VA21) transactions.'
}

Table order.blanket_order {
  blanket_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the blanket order agreement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer owning the blanket order.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the product.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the blanket order agreement defining pricing model.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the blanket order was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the blanket order.']
  blanket_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blanket order.']
  call_off_frequency varchar [note: 'Expected frequency of call-off orders under the agreement.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance requirements applicable to the blanket order (e.g., REACH, GHS).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned to the blanket order.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the blanket order contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blanket order record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the price per unit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the blanket order becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the blanket order expires or ends.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates if the terms of the blanket order are confidential.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the blanket order grants exclusive supply rights.']
  minimum_call_off_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity required for each call-off.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes regarding the blanket order.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price agreed for the product grade.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier applicable based on volume thresholds.']
  product_grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or specification of the chemical product covered.']
  rebate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Rebate amount applied when threshold met.']
  rebate_threshold_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume threshold at which rebate applies.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Approval number from regulatory body for the product under this agreement.']
  remaining_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Remaining volume available for call-offs.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Renewal option for the blanket order after expiry.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special handling or safety instructions for the product.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination if the agreement is terminated.']
  total_call_offs varchar [note: 'Number of call-off orders released to date.']
  total_committed_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of product committed under the blanket order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the blanket order record.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the committed volume.']

  Note: 'Long-term volume commitment agreement between Chemical Mfg and a customer defining contracted volumes, pricing tiers, and delivery terms over a contract period (typically 12-36 months). Captures contract dates, total committed volume, product grades, agreed pricing tiers, volume rebate thresholds, call-off frequency, minimum call-off quantity, and remaining open quantity. Individual sales orders (call-offs) are released against this agreement. Common in chemical B2B for supply security and volume-based pricing. Maps to SAP SD scheduling agreements and contracts.'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {
  inventory_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each stock position record representing a material at a specific storage location, bin, or tank farm. Primary key for the stock_position data product.']
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for stock_position']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Needed for daily inventory balance reports linking each stock position to its master chemical product, enabling accurate valuation, SDS lookup, and REACH compliance.']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Safety and regulatory reporting links inventory stock to the registered compound record for hazard classification and SDS retrieval.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for cost accounting reports that allocate inventory value to cost centers per plant, enabling accurate product cost roll‑up.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Spare parts allocation for equipment repairs; maintenance work orders consume inventory items per equipment, required for parts consumption reporting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this stock position is held. Aligns with SAP organizational structure for plant-level inventory management.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for inventory valuation reports that pull the active price list to compute valuation_price per IFRS/GAAP.']
  primary_stock_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record identifying the specific raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material held in this stock position. Sourced from SAP MM material master.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for traceability of on‑hand stock to the originating purchase order for regulatory recall and inventory valuation reports.']
  stock_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to rawmaterial.material_master']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific warehouse storage location, bin, or tank farm where the material is physically stored. Managed within SAP EWM warehouse structure.']
  available_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material available for unrestricted use, calculated as on-hand quantity minus reserved, blocked, and quality inspection quantities. Drives ATP (Available-to-Promise) checks in SAP SD and production planning in SAP PP.']
  blocked_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material placed in blocked stock status due to quality failures, regulatory holds, damage, or quarantine. Blocked stock is not available for production or shipment until released by QA or EHS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this stock position record was first created in the source system (SAP EWM). Provides audit trail for record lineage and supports SOX compliance for inventory record integrity.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the material lot expires and is no longer suitable for use. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation logic, regulatory compliance, and preventing use of out-of-specification materials. Sourced from SAP batch classification.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the material was received into the warehouse via a goods receipt posting in SAP MM. Used as the FIFO reference date for lot rotation when manufacture date is unavailable.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material in this stock position is classified as a hazardous material requiring special storage, handling, and reporting under OSHA, DOT, EPA, and REACH regulations. Triggers hazmat storage compliance checks in EHS management.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material currently in transit between storage locations, plants, or external suppliers. Represents stock that has been shipped but not yet received at the destination, tracked via SAP EWM transfer orders.']
  last_count_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this stock position. Used to assess inventory accuracy and schedule next count per SOX and ISO 9001 audit requirements.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent inventory movement (goods issue, goods receipt, transfer order, or stock transfer) affecting this stock position. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory for write-off analysis.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned during production or receipt of the material. Critical for FIFO/FEFO lot rotation, traceability, Certificate of Analysis (COA) linkage, and regulatory compliance under GMP and REACH.']
  lot_rotation_method varchar [note: 'The inventory rotation strategy applied to this stock position for picking and consumption sequencing. FIFO (First In First Out) is used for most materials; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for materials with expiration dates per GMP requirements.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'The date on which the material lot was manufactured or produced. Used in conjunction with expiration_date to calculate shelf life and support FIFO/FEFO rotation. Sourced from SAP batch master or COA.']
  maximum_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable stock quantity for this material at this storage location, constrained by tank capacity, regulatory storage limits, or warehouse space. Enforced by SAP EWM capacity checks and EHS hazmat storage quantity limits under OSHA PSM.']
  on_hand_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total physical quantity of the material currently present at the storage location, including all stock types (unrestricted, quality inspection, blocked). Represents the gross inventory position before any reservations or holds are applied.']
  quality_inspection_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material currently under quality inspection in the LIMS or SAP QM module. This stock is not available for use until the inspection lot is closed and the material is released to unrestricted stock.']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason this stock position has been placed in quarantine or blocked status. Examples include failed QC test, regulatory hold, contamination suspicion, or pending CAPA investigation. Populated when stock_status is quarantine or stock_type is blocked.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum stock level at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this material at this storage location. Used by SAP MRP (Material Requirements Planning) to generate purchase requisitions or production orders automatically.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material reserved against open production orders, sales orders, or transfer orders in SAP. Reserved stock is committed but not yet physically moved, reducing the available quantity for new demand.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against supply disruptions, demand variability, or lead time uncertainty. Defined in SAP MRP parameters and used in available quantity calculations for production planning.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this stock position record was captured or last refreshed from the source system (SAP EWM/S4HANA). Represents the as-of time for the inventory snapshot, critical for real-time inventory reporting and reconciliation.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this stock position represents a special stock type such as consignment inventory, project stock, sales-order-specific stock, or vendor-managed inventory (VMI). Drives distinct accounting and ownership rules in SAP MM.']
  stock_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the material type held in this stock position. Drives inventory valuation rules, storage compliance requirements, and reporting segmentation across raw materials, intermediates, finished goods, and packaging materials.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock position record. Quarantine status is applied when material is pending investigation or regulatory hold. Expired indicates the material has passed its expiration date. Recalled indicates a product recall event has been triggered.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'SAP stock type classification indicating the usability state of the inventory. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality_inspection stock is pending QC release; blocked stock is not available for use; returns stock is from customer returns; in_transit stock is between locations.']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'The specific bin, rack position, or tank identifier within the storage location where the material is physically stored. Used by SAP EWM for directed putaway and picking operations in warehouse management.']
  storage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical storage infrastructure type where the material is held. Drives hazardous material storage compliance rules under OSHA PSM, NFPA, and DOT regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk_tank|drum_rack|pallet_rack|cold_storage|hazmat_cage|silo|ibc_bay — promote to reference product]']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which all quantity fields for this stock position are expressed. Chemical manufacturing uses weight (KG, MT), volume (L, GAL, M3), and count (EA, DRUM, IBC) units depending on material type. Aligns with SAP MM base UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|TON|EA|BAG|DRUM|IBC|M3|FT3 — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this stock position record was last modified in the source system. Used for change data capture (CDC) processing in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail maintenance.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'SAP valuation class code that determines the general ledger (GL) accounts to which inventory postings are made for this material. Links inventory movements to the correct COGS and balance sheet accounts per FASB and SOX requirements.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the valuation_price is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR). Required for multi-currency inventory valuation reporting across global plant locations.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Reference unit price used to calculate the inventory valuation of this stock position, expressed in the valuation currency. Carried as a reference attribute for inventory reporting; authoritative valuation is owned by the Finance domain (SAP FI/CO). May reflect standard cost or moving average price per SAP valuation class.']
  warehouse_number varchar [note: 'The SAP EWM warehouse number identifying the physical warehouse complex managing this stock position. Used for multi-warehouse inventory segregation and reporting.']

  Note: 'Real-time stock position master record for each material (raw material, intermediate, finished good, packaging) at each storage location, bin, or tank farm. Tracks on-hand quantity, available quantity, reserved quantity, blocked quantity, unit of measure, batch/lot number, stock category, and stock type (unrestricted, quality inspection, blocked, returns). Serves as the primary SSOT for current inventory levels across all SAP EWM-managed warehouses and tank farms. Supports FIFO/FEFO lot rotation logic and hazardous material storage compliance. Valuation carried as reference attribute for inventory reporting; authoritative valuation owned by finance domain.'
}

Table inventory.warehouse_location {
  warehouse_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for warehouse_location']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for safety audit reports; each warehouse must have a designated manager employee for OSHA compliance.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier for the aisle within the warehouse where this storage location is physically situated. Used for warehouse navigation, pick path optimization, and physical inventory counting.']
  area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical floor area of this storage location in square meters. Used for capacity planning, fire load calculations, and regulatory reporting of storage area dimensions per EPA and OSHA requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse location master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.']
  electrical_area_classification varchar [note: 'NEC/NFPA 70 electrical area classification for this storage location, indicating the presence and likelihood of flammable vapors or combustible dust. Determines requirements for explosion-proof electrical equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unclassified|class_i_div1|class_i_div2|class_ii_div1|class_ii_div2|zone_0|zone_1|zone_2 — promote to reference product]']
  epa_regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Applicable EPA regulatory citation(s) governing storage requirements at this location (e.g., \'40 CFR Part 264 - Hazardous Waste Storage\', \'40 CFR Part 68 - RMP\'). Used for environmental compliance documentation, permit management, and regulatory audit support.']
  fire_zone_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the fire protection zone in which this storage location resides. Fire zones define the boundaries of fire suppression systems (sprinkler zones, deluge systems), fire detection circuits, and emergency evacuation areas. Referenced in PSM and HAZOP documentation.']
  floor_load_capacity_kg_m2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable floor load in kilograms per square meter for this storage location. Ensures structural integrity of the warehouse floor when storing heavy drums, IBCs, or bulk containers. Referenced in facility engineering and OSHA compliance inspections.']
  ghs_hazard_class_compatibility varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of GHS hazard classes that are approved for storage at this location (e.g., \'Flammable Liquid|Oxidizer|Corrosive\'). Governs which chemical materials may be placed here based on GHS classification. Used to enforce chemical segregation rules and prevent incompatible co-storage.']
  height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Clear storage height in meters for this location. Used to determine maximum pallet stack height, forklift clearance requirements, and sprinkler system design (in-rack vs. ceiling sprinklers per NFPA 13).']
  incompatible_hazard_classes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of GHS hazard classes that must NOT be co-stored at this location due to chemical incompatibility or regulatory segregation requirements (e.g., \'Oxidizer|Explosive\'). Enforces NFPA and OSHA chemical segregation rules to prevent dangerous reactions.']
  is_hazmat_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is approved and certified for the storage of hazardous materials per OSHA, EPA, and DOT regulations. True = approved for hazmat storage; False = not approved. Drives putaway rule enforcement in SAP EWM.']
  is_quarantine_zone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is designated as a quarantine zone for materials under quality hold, regulatory hold, or investigation. True = quarantine zone; False = standard storage. Materials in quarantine zones are blocked from use in production or shipment per GMP and ISO 9001 requirements.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location has active temperature control (refrigeration, heating, or climate control). True = temperature controlled; False = ambient storage. Drives material assignment rules for temperature-sensitive chemicals and cold chain compliance.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection or safety audit conducted at this storage location. Used to track inspection frequency compliance per OSHA PSM, EPA RMP, and internal EHS management requirements. Triggers re-inspection workflows when overdue.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse location master record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with SAP EWM, and audit trail compliance.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location within the warehouse network. Corresponds to SAP EWM storage bin or storage section code used in warehouse operations, goods movements, and transfer orders.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the storage location (e.g., \'Tank Farm A - Bay 3\', \'Hazmat Drum Storage Zone 2\', \'Cold Storage Room 1\'). Used in operational reports, safety documentation, and warehouse management interfaces.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the storage location. \'quarantine\' indicates the location is reserved for materials under quality hold. \'blocked\' indicates the location is temporarily unavailable for putaway or picking. \'under_maintenance\' indicates the location is undergoing inspection or repair.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical storage location type within the warehouse network. Determines applicable storage rules, capacity constraints, and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk_tank|drum_storage|cold_storage|hazmat_zone|quarantine_bay|finished_goods|intermediate_storage|open_yard — promote to reference product]']
  max_humidity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable relative humidity percentage for this storage location. Prevents moisture-induced degradation, corrosion, or microbial growth in stored materials. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).']
  max_quantity_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity in kilograms of hazardous material permitted in this storage zone per EPA, OSHA PSM, and NFPA regulations. Used to enforce Tier II reporting thresholds, RMP covered quantity limits, and NFPA maximum allowable quantities (MAQ) per control area.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Prevents thermal degradation, spontaneous reaction, or ignition of stored chemicals. Enforced against material SDS storage condition requirements.']
  max_volume_liters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity in liters for this storage location. Particularly relevant for bulk tank farms and liquid chemical storage areas. Used to enforce maximum quantity limits per EPA and OSHA regulations.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable weight in kilograms that this storage location can safely hold. Enforced during putaway to prevent structural overloading. Critical for rack integrity compliance and OSHA load rating requirements.']
  min_humidity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable relative humidity percentage for this storage location. Relevant for hygroscopic chemicals, moisture-sensitive materials, and packaging integrity. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).']
  min_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Enforces cold chain and temperature-sensitive chemical storage requirements. Materials requiring higher minimum temperatures must not be stored here.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the next physical inspection or safety audit of this storage location must be completed. Drives EHS compliance calendar and corrective action workflows in Intelex EHS Management.']
  nfpa_fire_diamond_flammability varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 flammability hazard rating (red diamond) for the storage location. Scale: 0 (will not burn) to 4 (extremely flammable). Drives fire suppression system design, electrical classification, and hot work permit requirements.']
  nfpa_fire_diamond_health varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 health hazard rating (blue diamond) for the storage location, indicating the degree of health hazard posed by materials stored here. Scale: 0 (no hazard) to 4 (deadly). Posted on location signage for emergency responder awareness.']
  nfpa_fire_diamond_reactivity varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 instability/reactivity hazard rating (yellow diamond) for the storage location. Scale: 0 (stable) to 4 (may detonate). Informs emergency response procedures and incompatible material segregation requirements.']
  nfpa_fire_diamond_special varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 special hazard symbol (white diamond) for the storage location. Common values: \'W\' (water reactive), \'OX\' (oxidizer), \'SA\' (simple asphyxiant). Empty if no special hazard applies. Used in emergency response planning and SDS alignment.']
  osha_regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Applicable OSHA regulatory citation(s) governing safety requirements at this storage location (e.g., \'29 CFR 1910.106 - Flammable Liquids\', \'29 CFR 1910.119 - PSM\'). Used for workplace safety compliance documentation and OSHA inspection readiness.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility to which this warehouse location belongs. Aligns with SAP MM/EWM plant-warehouse structure for inventory valuation and materials management.']
  rack varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier for the rack or bay within the aisle where this storage location is physically situated. Used in conjunction with aisle and level to precisely identify the bin coordinate.']
  rotation_strategy varchar [note: 'Inventory lot rotation strategy enforced at this storage location. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for most materials; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is required for materials with expiration dates to prevent use of expired stock. Drives SAP EWM picking strategy configuration.']
  storage_condition_code varchar [note: 'SAP MM/EWM storage condition code assigned to this location, used to match materials requiring specific storage conditions (e.g., flammable, corrosive, refrigerated, dry). Enforces material-location compatibility during putaway strategy execution.']
  storage_section_code varchar [note: 'SAP EWM storage section code grouping this location within the warehouse. Storage sections define areas with similar storage conditions (e.g., flammable liquids section, refrigerated section) and drive putaway and picking strategies.']
  ventilation_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the ventilation system installed at this storage location. Determines suitability for storing volatile organic compounds (VOCs), flammable vapors, and hazardous air pollutants (HAPs). Explosion-proof ventilation is required for Class I flammable liquid storage per NFPA 30.']
  warehouse_location_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or tier identifier within the rack or shelving structure (e.g., \'01\' for ground level, \'02\' for second tier). Used for ergonomic storage assignment and weight-bearing capacity management.']
  warehouse_number varchar [note: 'SAP EWM warehouse number identifying the logical warehouse complex to which this location belongs. Used for warehouse-level inventory management, transfer order processing, and EWM configuration.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical and logical storage locations within the warehouse network, including bulk tank farms, drum storage areas, cold storage rooms, hazmat segregation zones, quarantine bays, and finished goods warehouses. Captures location code, storage type, capacity (volume/weight), temperature range, humidity range, ventilation class, hazard class compatibility, NFPA fire diamond rating, incompatible hazard class segregation rules, maximum storage quantity per zone, fire zone, EPA/OSHA regulatory citations, and SAP EWM storage section mapping. Governs where specific chemical materials may be stored based on GHS/NFPA compatibility rules and required storage conditions.'
}

Table inventory.lot {
  lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lot']
  supply_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the lot (batch) master record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for all downstream joins and traceability chains.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Lot traceability requires association with the chemical product for quality release, regulatory reporting, and batch‑level SDS reference.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GMP quality system requires linking each lot to the chemist/technician who created it for batch release documentation.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Scale‑up reporting ties each manufactured lot to the experimental formulation used, enabling cost and performance analysis.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Traceability report & regulatory compliance require linking each produced lot to the specific formula version used for batch investigation and quality control.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order or process order under which this lot was manufactured. Null for externally procured lots. Maps to SAP Production Order (AUFNR) in PP.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the chemical substance, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material associated with this lot. Maps to SAP Material Number (MATNR).']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or receiving facility where this lot was produced or first received. Maps to SAP Plant (WERKS) in MM/PP.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Needed to link each production lot to its supplying purchase order, enabling lot‑level traceability for compliance audits and quality investigations.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Regulatory batch traceability requires linking each production lot to the originating R&D project for audit and compliance.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external supplier or vendor from whom this lot was received, applicable when lot origin is external procurement. Null for internally produced lots. Maps to SAP Vendor (LIFNR).']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse storage location or tank farm bin where this lot is currently physically stored. Maps to SAP Storage Location (LGORT) and SAP EWM storage bin.']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'The document reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) issued for this lot, confirming that the material meets all specified quality parameters. Provided to customers with shipments and required for regulatory submissions. Stored in LabWare LIMS and SAP QM.']
  coc_number varchar [note: 'The document reference number of the Certificate of Compliance (COC) issued for this lot, attesting conformance to customer-specified or regulatory requirements beyond standard QC testing (e.g., REACH compliance, RoHS, food-contact suitability). Distinct from COA which covers analytical results.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this lot was manufactured or, for natural raw materials, where it was extracted. Required for customs declarations, import/export compliance, trade preference certificates, and REACH/TSCA regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this lot master record was first created in the source system (SAP MM Batch Master or LabWare LIMS), recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GMP record-keeping requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the inventory_valuation_price (e.g., USD, EUR). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and FASB/IFRS compliance.']
  current_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The remaining unreserved quantity of material currently available in this lot, updated with each inventory movement (goods issue, transfer, adjustment). Supports real-time stock position reporting and FIFO/FEFO allocation.']
  days_to_expiry_threshold varchar [note: 'The number of days before expiration at which this lot should trigger an inventory alert for proactive disposition, customer notification, or markdown action. Configured per material type and customer contract requirements. Used by FEFO rotation and expiry management workflows.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current quality disposition of the lot governing whether it may be used, shipped, or must be held. Released: QC-approved for use/sale. Quarantined: held pending QC decision. Rejected: failed QC and must be destroyed or reworked. Expired: past expiration date. Under Review: Out-of-Specification (OOS) or Out-of-Trend (OOT) investigation in progress. Recalled: subject to active recall action. Maps to SAP QM Usage Decision (VRBWG).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which this lot must not be used, sold, or shipped. Calculated from manufacture date plus approved shelf-life period or assigned by QA based on stability data. Central to FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation logic. Maps to SAP Batch Expiration Date (VFDAT).']
  gmp_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this lot was manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) conditions as required for pharmaceutical-grade, food-contact, or regulated chemical applications. Drives customer-specific COA declarations and regulatory submissions.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date this lot was physically received into the warehouse or plant, recorded at goods receipt posting in SAP MM. For internally produced lots, this is the date the finished goods were transferred to the warehouse. Maps to SAP MIGO Goods Receipt Posting Date (BUDAT).']
  grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade classification assigned to this lot based on QC test results and intended end-use application. Prime: meets full specification. Off-grade: meets reduced specification for alternative use. Technical/Pharmaceutical/Food/Agricultural grades reflect regulatory and customer-specific quality tiers. Drives pricing and customer allocation.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is classified as a hazardous material requiring special storage, handling, labelling, and transport compliance under DOT, OSHA, and GHS regulations. Triggers hazmat storage location validation in SAP EWM.']
  initial_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total quantity of material in this lot at the time of creation (production completion or goods receipt). Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material. Used as the denominator for yield calculations and lot consumption tracking.']
  inventory_valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The per-unit valuation price applied to this lot for inventory accounting purposes, expressed in the company\'s functional currency. Used for COGS calculation, inventory write-down assessment, and financial reporting. Maps to SAP Moving Average Price or Standard Price (VERPR/STPRS) in FI/CO.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this lot master record in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, audit trail compliance, and detecting stale records in the Silver Layer.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric batch/lot identifier assigned at production or goods receipt. Used on Certificate of Analysis (COA), Certificate of Compliance (COC), shipping documents, and regulatory submissions. Maps to SAP Batch Number (CHARG) in MM/QM.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'The date on which production of this lot was completed or, for externally received lots, the date of manufacture as declared by the supplier. Basis for shelf-life and expiration date calculation. Maps to SAP Batch Manufacturing Date (HSDAT).']
  moisture_content_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'The measured moisture content of this lot expressed as a percentage by weight, as determined by QC testing (e.g., Karl Fischer titration, loss on drying). Critical for solid and powder chemical products where moisture affects stability, reactivity, and product performance.']
  nfpa_fire_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 flammability hazard rating (0-4) for the material in this lot. 0 = will not burn; 4 = will rapidly vaporize at normal conditions. Used for storage location assignment, fire suppression system design, and emergency response planning.']
  origin varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this lot was created through internal manufacturing, received from an external supplier, produced via rework/reprocessing of a non-conforming lot, or originated from a customer return. Drives traceability and regulatory reporting workflows.']
  ph_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'The measured pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of this lot as determined by QC laboratory testing. Critical quality parameter for chemical products; drives corrosivity classification, storage material compatibility, and regulatory classification under GHS/DOT. Sourced from LabWare LIMS COA results.']
  purity_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'The measured purity of the primary chemical substance in this lot expressed as a percentage by weight or volume, as determined by QC analytical testing. Key quality attribute for pricing, formulation accuracy, and regulatory compliance. Sourced from LabWare LIMS COA.']
  qc_release_date date [note: 'The date on which the Quality Assurance function formally released this lot from quarantine to unrestricted use following successful completion of all required QC testing. Populated from LabWare LIMS usage decision timestamp. Critical for lead-time and cycle-time analytics.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that this lot is currently under quarantine and must not be issued, shipped, or consumed until released by Quality Assurance. Complements disposition_status for rapid system-level blocking in SAP EWM and WMS pick operations.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is subject to an active product recall or market withdrawal action. When True, triggers immediate inventory blocking in SAP EWM, customer notification workflows, and regulatory reporting to FDA/EPA/ECHA as applicable.']
  restricted_substance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot contains substances subject to regulatory restriction or authorization under REACH SVHC (Substances of Very High Concern), TSCA, or RoHS. Triggers compliance review before shipment to regulated markets.']
  retest_date date [note: 'The date by which this lot must be retested by the QC laboratory to confirm continued conformance to specification before further use or shipment. Applicable to raw materials and intermediates with defined retest intervals. Maps to SAP Batch Next Inspection Date (PRÜFDATUM).']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The total approved shelf life of this lot expressed in days, as defined by the material specification or stability study. Used to validate expiration date assignment and to support shelf-life remaining calculations for customer order fulfillment. Maps to SAP Material Master Total Shelf Life (MHDRZ).']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'The original lot or batch number assigned by the external supplier, as printed on the supplier COA and shipping documents. Maintained alongside the internal lot_number for full traceability back to the supplier\'s manufacturing records. Null for internally produced lots.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which lot quantities are expressed (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for litres, MT for metric tonnes). Aligns with SAP Base Unit of Measure (MEINS) on the material master. Required for all quantity-based calculations and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|G|M3|BBL|EA|BAG — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  voc_content_ppm decimal(10,4) [note: 'The measured or declared Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) content of this lot expressed in Parts Per Million (PPM) by weight. Required for EPA air emissions reporting, state VOC regulations, and customer environmental product declarations. Sourced from QC analytical results in LabWare LIMS.']

  Note: 'Lot (batch) master record representing a discrete quantity of a chemical material produced or received under uniform conditions. Captures lot number, material reference, manufacturing date, expiration date, retest date, days-to-expiry threshold, COA reference, GMP status, quarantine flag, lot origin (internal production vs. external receipt), current disposition (released, quarantined, rejected, expired), shelf-life remaining calculation basis, and country-of-origin for regulatory reporting. Central to FIFO/FEFO rotation, full traceability, recall management, and regulatory compliance (FDA, REACH, TSCA). Integrates with LabWare LIMS for QC release status.'
}

Table inventory.stock_movement {
  stock_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory movement event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the stock_movement data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for posting inventory movements to the correct cost center in the Cost Center Allocation report.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID (USNAM) of the person who posted the inventory movement in SAP S/4HANA MM. Critical for audit trail, SOX compliance, and accountability tracking. Represents the system user, which may be a human operator or an automated interface (e.g., MES integration, SCADA feed).']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Stock movement must be linked to the lot it moves; replace string lot_number with FK lot_id for traceability.']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.outbound_delivery. Business justification: Required for the Delivery Execution report to trace which stock movements belong to each outbound delivery for compliance and audit.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit center profitability analysis of inventory movements for internal management reporting.']
  transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.transfer_order. Business justification: Link stock movement to its originating transfer order for end‑to‑end tracking; replace free‑text field with FK.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Stock movement must reference the source warehouse location entity for accurate location tracking and audit reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the stock movement record was first captured and persisted in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FIFO/FEFO sequencing validation. Corresponds to SAP CPUDT + CPUTM fields combined.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the movement_amount valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Corresponds to SAP WAERS field. Required for multi-currency chemical manufacturing operations with global procurement and sales.']
  destination_storage_location varchar [note: 'The SAP storage location code (Lagerort) to which the material is being moved or received. Represents the target warehouse area, tank farm zone, production staging area, or quarantine location. Null for pure outbound goods issues with no destination location within the plant.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the physical or external source document (e.g., vendor delivery note, production order confirmation) that triggered the inventory movement. May differ from posting_date when documents are processed with a lag. Corresponds to SAP BLDAT field.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration or shelf-life end date of the lot/batch being moved. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation enforcement, quarantine triggering for expired materials, and regulatory compliance. Corresponds to SAP VFDAT (shelf life expiration date) on the batch master. Critical for chemical raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods with defined shelf lives.']
  fifo_fefo_validation_result varchar [note: 'The result of the FIFO (First In First Out) or FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation validation check performed at the time of goods issue. Passed = correct lot selected per rotation policy; Failed = incorrect lot selected, violating rotation rules; Not Applicable = movement type does not require lot rotation validation (e.g., goods receipt); Overridden = FIFO/FEFO rule was manually overridden with documented justification. Critical for chemical product quality, shelf-life management, and GMP compliance.']
  goods_receipt_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt movement was posted to GR blocked stock (Wareneingang gesperrt), meaning the material has been physically received but not yet released for use pending inspection or documentation review. True = posted to blocked GR stock; False = posted directly to unrestricted or inspection stock. Corresponds to SAP movement type 103/105 scenario.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material being moved is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, or transport compliance. True = hazardous material subject to EHS regulations (GHS, DOT, OSHA, EPA); False = non-hazardous material. Triggers hazmat storage compliance checks and EHS reporting requirements.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known SAP material document number (Materialbelegs-nummer) assigned by SAP S/4HANA MM upon posting the inventory movement. Serves as the primary business identifier linking back to the source system transaction. Equivalent to MBLNR in SAP.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the material document was posted in SAP S/4HANA MM. Combined with material_document_number forms the unique SAP document key (MBLNR + MJAHR).']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (Materialnummer - MATNR) uniquely identifying the chemical substance, raw material, intermediate, finished product, or packaging material being moved. Links to the material master record which contains CAS number, GHS classification, and SDS references.']
  movement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The inventory valuation amount (in local currency) associated with this stock movement, representing the financial impact on inventory accounts. Calculated as quantity multiplied by the moving average price or standard price at time of posting. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) reporting, inventory valuation, and FI/CO integration. Corresponds to SAP DMBTR field.']
  movement_category varchar [note: 'High-level business classification of the inventory movement event. Goods Receipt (GR) covers inbound receipts from vendors or production; Goods Issue (GI) covers consumption or outbound shipments; Stock Transfer covers location-to-location moves; Return covers vendor or customer returns; Scrapping covers material write-offs; Sampling covers quality lab withdrawals; Revaluation covers price or valuation adjustments. Subsumes formerly separate goods_receipt and goods_issue records as movement subtypes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goods_receipt|goods_issue|stock_transfer|return|scrapping|sampling|revaluation — promote to reference product]']
  movement_direction varchar [note: 'Directional classification of the inventory movement relative to the plant or warehouse: inbound (stock entering the facility, e.g., goods receipt), outbound (stock leaving the facility, e.g., goods issue to customer or production), or internal (stock moving within the facility, e.g., storage location transfer). Used for net stock position calculations and directional reporting.']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'A coded reason explaining why the inventory movement occurred, beyond what the movement type conveys. Examples include: production consumption, quality rejection, customer return, vendor return, cycle count adjustment, process loss, sampling withdrawal, spillage/waste, regulatory disposal. Corresponds to SAP GRUND field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production_consumption|quality_rejection|customer_return|vendor_return|cycle_count_adjustment|process_loss|sampling|spillage|regulatory_disposal — promote to reference product]']
  movement_reason_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context or explanation for the inventory movement reason, supplementing the movement_reason_code. Used for exception documentation, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) linkage, and audit trail narratives. Corresponds to SAP text fields associated with GRUND.']
  movement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock movement record. Posted = successfully committed to inventory books; Reversed = cancelled by a subsequent reversal posting; Blocked = movement is on hold pending approval or investigation; Pending Approval = movement requires workflow approval before final posting (e.g., high-value scrapping). Supports workflow management and audit compliance.']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code (Bewegungsart) that classifies the inventory transaction. Standard codes include 101 (Goods Receipt for PO), 122 (Return to Vendor), 201 (Goods Issue to Cost Center), 261 (Goods Issue for Production Order), 301 (Transfer Posting), 551 (Scrapping), 601 (Goods Issue for Delivery), 701 (Inventory Difference). Replaces the former movement_type reference table as an embedded code per product design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 101|122|201|261|301|551|601|701|... — promote to reference product]']
  order_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used in the reference order document (purchase order, sales order, or production order) for this movement. Corresponds to SAP ERFME field. Enables reconciliation between order quantities and inventory quantities when UOM conversion applies.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code (Werk) identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility where the inventory movement occurred. In chemical manufacturing, plants correspond to production sites, tank farms, or distribution centers. Serves as the primary organizational unit for inventory management.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which the inventory movement was officially posted to the books in SAP S/4HANA MM (Buchungsdatum - BUDAT). This is the date used for inventory valuation, financial period assignment, and regulatory reporting. Distinct from the document creation timestamp.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material involved in the inventory movement, expressed in the base unit of measure. Positive values represent inbound or internal movements; negative values represent outbound or consumption movements per SAP convention. Supports up to 4 decimal places for precision chemical measurements (e.g., kg, L, mol).']
  quantity_in_order_uom decimal(18,4) [note: 'The movement quantity expressed in the order unit of measure (e.g., purchase order UOM or sales order UOM), which may differ from the base unit of measure. For example, a material ordered in drums (DR) but managed in kilograms (KG). Corresponds to SAP ERFMG field.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material involved in this movement is under quarantine status at the time of the movement. True = material is quarantined (e.g., pending QC release, OOS investigation, regulatory hold, contamination suspicion); False = material is not under quarantine. Supports hazardous material storage compliance and GMP quality hold management.']
  reference_document_item varchar [note: 'The line item number within the reference document (e.g., PO line item, production order component line) associated with this inventory movement. Combined with reference_document_number provides precise traceability to the originating document line. Corresponds to SAP EBELP (PO item) field.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The number of the originating reference document (e.g., Purchase Order number, Production Order number, Sales Order number, Delivery number) that triggered this inventory movement. Provides the complete audit trail linking inventory movements back to procurement, production, or sales transactions. Corresponds to SAP EBELN (PO) or AUFNR (order) depending on context.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of the originating business document that triggered this inventory movement. Purchase Order (PO) for vendor receipts; Production Order or Process Order for manufacturing consumption/receipt; Sales Order for customer-specific stock; Delivery for outbound shipments; Transfer Order for warehouse transfers; Reservation for planned withdrawals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase_order|production_order|sales_order|delivery|transfer_order|process_order|reservation — promote to reference product]']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock movement record is a reversal (cancellation) of a previously posted movement. True = this document is a reversal posting that negates a prior movement; False = original movement. Essential for net inventory position calculations and audit trail integrity.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The material document number of the original movement that this record reverses, when reversal_flag is True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linking between original and reversal postings. Corresponds to SAP STBLG field.']
  source_plant_code varchar [note: 'The SAP plant code of the sending plant for inter-plant stock transfer movements (STO - Stock Transfer Orders). Populated only for cross-plant transfers. Corresponds to SAP UMWRK field.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP special stock indicator (SOBKZ) identifying non-standard stock ownership or custody arrangements. E = Sales Order Stock (make-to-order), K = Consignment Stock at Customer, Q = Project Stock, V = Consignment Stock from Vendor, W = Returnable Transport Packaging. Empty for standard own stock. Critical for chemical toll manufacturing and consignment inventory scenarios.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The SAP stock type category indicating the usability status of the inventory being moved. Unrestricted stock is available for use; Quality Inspection stock is under QC hold pending COA approval; Blocked stock is restricted from use (e.g., OOS - Out of Specification); Restricted stock has conditional use limitations; Returns stock is customer-returned material awaiting disposition. Corresponds to SAP SOBKZ / INSMK fields.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure for the movement quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, EA for each, DR for drums, IBC for intermediate bulk containers). Corresponds to SAP MEINS (base UOM) field. Critical for chemical inventory accuracy and regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the stock movement record was last modified in the source system or lakehouse silver layer. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'SAP valuation type (Bewertungsart - BWTAR) used when a material is managed with split valuation — for example, differentiating between domestic-sourced and imported raw materials, or between different quality grades of the same chemical. Enables separate inventory valuation for the same material number.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every inventory movement event including goods receipts (GR), goods issues (GI), stock transfers, returns, scrapping, sampling, and revaluations. Captures movement type code (replacing the former movement_type reference table), material, lot number, quantity, unit of measure, source location, destination location, movement direction (inbound/outbound/internal), movement reason, reference document (PO, production order, sales order, delivery), posting date, posting user, and FIFO/FEFO lot selection validation result. Provides the complete audit trail for all inventory changes. Subsumes formerly separate goods_receipt and goods_issue records as movement subtypes.'
}

Table inventory.transfer_order {
  transfer_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the warehouse transfer order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns inter‑warehouse transfer orders to cost centers for budgeting and expense tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse operator or forklift driver assigned to execute this transfer order. Used for labor tracking and accountability.']
  handling_unit_id varchar [note: 'SAP EWM handling unit identifier (pallet, IBC, drum, container) being physically moved. Links the transfer order to the physical packaging unit for scan-based confirmation and traceability.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: Inter‑plant transfer orders are often created to fulfill a particular sales order line; linking enables the Transfer Fulfillment tracking report.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging) being physically moved by this transfer order. Aligns with SAP MM material master.']
  transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.transfer_price. Business justification: Needed for interplant transfer pricing calculations used in profitability analysis and tax compliance.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse complex within which this transfer order is executed. Scopes the source and destination bins to a single warehouse facility.']
  warehouse_task_id varchar [note: 'SAP EWM warehouse task identifier linked to this transfer order. A transfer order may be decomposed into one or more warehouse tasks for execution by operators or automated systems (e.g., conveyor, AGV).']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operator physically began executing the transfer order (e.g., first scan of source bin or handling unit). Enables measurement of queue wait time and operator response time.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for cancellation of the transfer order (e.g., material not found at source bin, bin blocked, production order cancelled, duplicate order). Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer order was cancelled in SAP EWM. Populated only when transfer_order_status = \'cancelled\'. Used for exception reporting and warehouse efficiency analysis.']
  confirmation_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the operator or system to confirm execution of the transfer order. \'rf_scan\' = RF barcode/RFID scanner; \'manual\' = paper-based or keyboard entry; \'automated_conveyor\' = conveyor system auto-confirmation; \'voice_picking\' = voice-directed warehouse system; \'agv\' = automated guided vehicle.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity confirmed as physically moved by the operator upon transfer order completion. May differ from transfer_quantity due to partial picks, short picks, or discrepancies discovered during execution.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer order was fully confirmed in SAP EWM, triggering the inventory goods movement posting. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer order was first created in SAP EWM. Represents the record audit creation time and the start of the transfer order lifecycle.']
  destination_bin varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the specific storage bin, rack location, or tank to which the material is being moved. Represents the physical destination within the warehouse complex.']
  destination_storage_section varchar [note: 'SAP EWM storage section within the destination storage type, providing logical grouping of target bins.']
  destination_storage_type varchar [note: 'SAP EWM storage type code for the target location. Used to validate compatibility with material hazard classification and storage requirements (e.g., flammable, corrosive, oxidizer segregation per OSHA PSM and NFPA codes).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or shelf-life expiry date of the material lot being transferred. Used to enforce FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation rules and prevent movement of expired material to active storage. Critical for GMP compliance.']
  goods_movement_document varchar [note: 'SAP MM material document number generated upon posting of the inventory goods movement triggered by transfer order confirmation. Links the physical warehouse movement to the financial inventory posting in SAP FI/CO.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross weight in kilograms of the material quantity being transferred, including packaging (drums, IBCs, pallets). Used for load planning, equipment capacity validation, and DOT hazmat transport documentation.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS/DOT hazard classification of the material being transferred (e.g., Flammable Liquid Class 3, Corrosive, Oxidizer, Toxic). Drives storage segregation rules and emergency response procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flammable_liquid|corrosive|oxidizer|toxic|explosive|compressed_gas|environmental_hazard|none — promote to reference product]']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material being transferred is classified as hazardous under DOT, OSHA, or GHS regulations. When True, additional handling protocols, PPE requirements, and segregation rules apply. Supports EHS compliance reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the transfer order record in SAP EWM (e.g., quantity change, operator reassignment, status update). Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
