  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inbound delivery record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes to delivery status and attributes.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight of the actual chemical materials excluding packaging and container, measured in kilograms. Used for inventory valuation and goods receipt.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special instructions, exceptions, incidents, or observations related to this delivery. Used for communication between logistics, receiving, and quality teams.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming materials require quality inspection before release to unrestricted inventory. Triggers QM inspection lot creation.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for rejecting the delivery if materials do not meet quality standards, documentation is incomplete, or delivery conditions are violated. Free-text field for detailed explanation.']
  required_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in Celsius that must not be exceeded during transport to prevent material degradation or safety hazards.']
  required_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in Celsius that must be maintained during transport to preserve material integrity. Applies to temperature-sensitive chemicals.']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date for the delivery to arrive at the destination plant. Used for production planning and resource allocation.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the delivery to arrive at the destination plant, including time zone. Precise timing critical for dock scheduling and unloading crew coordination.']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security seal was found intact upon arrival at the destination. Broken seals trigger investigation and potential rejection.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the transport container or tanker. Used to verify container integrity and detect tampering during transit.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery requires temperature-controlled transport to maintain material stability and quality. Critical for temperature-sensitive chemicals.']
  transport_equipment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific transport equipment used (tanker truck number, rail car number, container number). Used for tracking and safety audits.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Method of transportation used for the delivery. Critical for chemical logistics planning and hazmat compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: road_tanker|rail_car|sea_container|pipeline|bulk_truck|iso_tank|flexitank — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials being transported. Required for DOT compliance and emergency response.']
  volume_liters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of liquid or bulk materials in the delivery, measured in liters. Critical for tank capacity planning and material balance.']

  Note: 'Inbound logistics delivery record tracking the physical movement of raw materials and chemicals from vendor to plant, coordinated with Oracle Transportation Management. Captures delivery number, vendor, carrier, origin location, destination plant and storage location, scheduled arrival date/time, actual arrival date/time, transport mode (road tanker, rail, sea container, pipeline), hazmat transport document number (DOT), temperature-controlled flag, seal number, and delivery status. Links ASN to goods receipt.'
}

Table supply.invoice_verification {
  invoice_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice verification record. Primary key for three-way match verification in SAP MM MIRO.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Invoice posting must allocate expense to the appropriate cost center for reporting and variance analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: GL account is required to post invoice amounts to the correct ledger account per accounting standards.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Invoice validation against project budgets requires linking each invoice verification to its research project.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the chemical supplier who issued the invoice. Links to vendor master data.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Baseline date for payment terms calculation in SAP. Typically the invoice date or goods receipt date depending on payment terms configuration.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the invoice verification is blocked from payment. Common blocks include price tolerance exceeded, quantity short, or quality hold pending Certificate of Analysis (COA) review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price_tolerance_exceeded|quantity_short|quality_hold_pending_coa|duplicate_invoice|missing_gr|missing_po|tax_code_mismatch|vendor_blocked|manual_review_required|unblocked — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  coa_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Certificate of Analysis has been received from the vendor. Critical for chemical procurement where COA review is required before payment release.']
  coa_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Certificate of Analysis review by Quality Control. Invoices may be blocked pending COA approval for chemical raw materials.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity receiving the invoice. Required for financial posting and legal compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice verification record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Critical for multi-currency chemical procurement.']
  document_date date [note: 'Document date of the invoice verification record. Typically matches the vendor invoice date but may differ for backdated postings.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the invoice verification document was posted.']
  freight_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Freight or transportation charge included in the vendor invoice. Common surcharge in chemical procurement for bulk material shipments.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the chemical materials were physically received and recorded in SAP MM.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Goods receipt document number confirming physical receipt of chemical materials. Third element of three-way match (PO, GR, Invoice).']
  hazmat_handling_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Special handling fee for hazardous chemical materials. Charged by vendors for DOT-regulated hazmat transportation and storage compliance.']
  index_escalator_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Price adjustment based on commodity index escalation clauses common in chemical supply contracts. Reflects market-driven price volatility.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount invoiced by the vendor before tax. Base amount for three-way match price variance calculation.']
  invoiced_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of chemical material invoiced by the vendor. Compared against PO quantity and GR quantity for variance detection.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master number for the chemical raw material being invoiced. Enables material-level spend analysis.']
  payment_block_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the invoice is blocked for payment. True if blocked, False if released for payment processing.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date by which payment is due to the vendor based on invoice date and payment terms. Critical for cash flow management and vendor relationship.']
  payment_release_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who released the invoice for payment. Audit trail for payment authorization.']
  payment_release_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice was released for payment after successful three-way match verification and block resolution.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms code from the purchase order or vendor master (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Determines payment due date and early payment discount eligibility.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the chemical materials were received. Links to plant master data.']
  po_line_number varchar [note: 'Line item number within the purchase order being verified. Enables line-level reconciliation for multi-material POs.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number against which the vendor invoice is being verified. Core reference for three-way match.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice verification document was posted to the financial ledger in SAP FI.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current posting status of the invoice verification document in SAP FI. Indicates whether the document has been financially posted to the general ledger.']
  price_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between invoiced price and PO price. Common in chemical procurement due to index-linked pricing, freight surcharges, and hazmat handling fees.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between invoiced quantity and goods receipt quantity. Typical with bulk liquid chemical deliveries measured by weight at receipt.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount on the vendor invoice (VAT, GST, sales tax). Calculated based on tax jurisdiction and material tax classification.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP tax code determining the tax rate and tax account assignment. Varies by jurisdiction, material type, and vendor location.']
  total_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount including tax. Gross amount payable to the vendor upon verification approval.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the invoiced quantity (e.g., KG, L, MT, GAL). Critical for bulk chemical deliveries measured by weight or volume.']
  vendor_invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor issued the invoice. Used for payment terms calculation and aging analysis.']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number provided by the chemical supplier on their invoice document. Critical for reconciliation and payment processing.']
  verification_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP document number assigned to the invoice verification transaction. Unique business identifier for the three-way match record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the three-way match verification process. Tracks the invoice through verification workflow from receipt to approval.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who performed the invoice verification. Audit trail for accountability and compliance.']
  verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice verification was completed. Tracks verification cycle time for process efficiency analysis.']

  Note: 'Three-way match verification records reconciling vendor invoices against purchase orders and goods receipts for chemical raw material purchases. Captures verification document number, vendor invoice number and date, PO and GR references, invoiced amount, tax amount, currency, price variance (common with index-linked chemical pricing), quantity variance (typical with bulk liquid deliveries measured by weight), blocking reasons (price tolerance exceeded, quantity short, quality hold pending COA review), payment release status, and posting status. Critical for chemical procurement where pricing often includes freight surcharges, hazmat handling fees, and index-based escalators. Integrates with SAP MM MIRO and FI accounts payable.'
}

Table supply.material_info_record {
  material_info_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the material info record. Primary key for this purchasing master data entity.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material being sourced. Links to the material master data defining the chemical product, raw material, or finished good covered by this purchasing agreement.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this material will be delivered. Defines the destination location for this sourcing arrangement.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved supplier for this material. Links to vendor master data defining the chemical supplier authorized to provide this material.']
  acknowledgement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must send a formal acknowledgement confirming receipt and acceptance of purchase orders. When true, procurement tracks acknowledgement status.']
  agreement_item_number varchar [note: 'Specific line item within the referenced contract that covers this material. Five-digit numeric identifier linking to the contract line item.']
  blocked_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this source is temporarily blocked from procurement. When true, no new purchase orders can be created using this material-vendor-plant combination.']
  certificate_of_analysis_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must provide a Certificate of Analysis with each shipment. Critical for chemical materials to verify composition, purity, and compliance with specifications.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master purchasing contract or agreement governing this sourcing relationship. Links to long-term supply agreements with negotiated terms and pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this info record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for sourcing relationship establishment.']
  fixed_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the mandatory source for the material. When true, Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and purchase requisitions must use this vendor exclusively.']
  goods_receipt_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal goods receipt transaction must be posted in SAP MM before invoice processing. Standard requirement for physical materials.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Standard three-letter code (e.g., DDP, FOB, CIF) governing shipping responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Specific location or port referenced in the Incoterms agreement. Defines the point where risk and cost transfer occurs between vendor and buyer.']
  info_record_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchasing info record type. Defines the procurement model: standard purchase, subcontracting arrangement, consignment stock, or pipeline supply for bulk chemicals.']
  info_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the purchasing info record. Unique alphanumeric code assigned by SAP MM system (ME11/ME12) to identify this material-vendor-plant sourcing relationship.']
  info_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchasing info record. Determines whether this source can be used for new purchase orders and MRP planning.']
  invoice_receipt_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoice verification is required for purchases from this source. When true, three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) is enforced.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who most recently updated this info record. Used for change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent change to this info record. Tracks when pricing, terms, or other sourcing parameters were last updated.']
  last_purchase_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order created for this material-vendor-plant combination. Used to track sourcing activity and identify inactive sources.']
  last_purchase_order_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Most recent actual price paid for this material from this vendor. Used for price variance analysis and to track pricing trends over time.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original manufacturer\'s part number when the vendor is a distributor. Ensures correct material identification and traceability to the chemical producer.']
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase order from this vendor. May be limited by vendor production capacity, storage constraints, or hazmat shipping regulations.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from this vendor for this material. Enforced during purchase order creation to ensure compliance with vendor minimum order requirements.']
  mrp_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this source is considered during MRP runs for automatic source determination. When true, MRP will propose this vendor when generating planned orders or purchase requisitions.']
  net_price_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the standard price is a net price (no further discounts apply) or a gross price (subject to additional discounts or surcharges).']
  order_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for ordering this material from this vendor. Examples: KG (kilograms), L (liters), MT (metric tons), GAL (gallons). Must align with vendor packaging and shipping capabilities.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage by which the vendor may exceed the ordered quantity without requiring approval. For example, 5.00 allows delivery of up to 105% of ordered quantity.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt at the plant. Used by MRP for scheduling and by procurement for delivery date calculation.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard price. Defines the currency in which pricing is negotiated and invoiced.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Quantity to which the standard price applies. For example, if price_unit is 100 and standard_price is 50.00, the price is 50.00 per 100 units.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer or procurement team responsible for this material-vendor combination. Three-character code identifying the specific purchasing group handling this sourcing relationship.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Four-character code identifying the purchasing group that negotiated and manages this sourcing relationship.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming materials from this vendor must undergo quality control inspection before release to production. When true, goods are placed in quality inspection stock upon receipt.']
  reach_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must provide REACH compliance documentation for materials supplied to European plants. Ensures regulatory compliance for chemical substance registration.']
  safety_data_sheet_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must provide a current Safety Data Sheet (SDS) with shipments. Mandatory for hazardous chemicals to ensure safe handling and compliance with OSHA and GHS requirements.']
  source_list_record boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this info record is part of the approved vendor list (AVL). When true, this vendor is authorized to supply this material to this plant during the validity period.']
  standard_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Recommended or typical order quantity for this material-vendor combination. Used as default value in purchase requisitions and for optimizing order economics.']
  standard_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Default unit price for this material from this vendor. Used as the baseline price for purchase order creation and cost estimation. Expressed in the currency specified in price_currency_code.']
  supplier_material_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s internal part number or catalog code for this material. Used for cross-referencing between buyer and supplier systems and for accurate order communication.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code determining applicable sales tax, VAT, or other tax treatment for purchases from this vendor. Two-character code linked to tax calculation rules.']
  under_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage by which the vendor may deliver less than the ordered quantity without triggering a shortage alert. For example, 2.00 allows delivery of as low as 98% of ordered quantity.']
  unlimited_over_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is allowed to deliver any quantity above the purchase order amount without restriction. Typically used for bulk chemicals where exact quantities are difficult to control.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this info record expires and can no longer be used for new purchase orders. Defines the end of the approved sourcing period. Null indicates indefinite validity.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this info record becomes active and can be used for purchase order creation. Defines the beginning of the approved sourcing period.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the procurement specialist who created this info record in SAP MM. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Purchasing master data establishing approved sourcing parameters and default procurement conditions for specific material-vendor-plant combinations. Captures info record number, material, vendor, plant, purchasing organization, standard price, planned delivery time, MOQ/max order quantity, delivery tolerances, last PO price, and source status. Also serves as the approved source list (AVL): defines which vendors are authorized to supply specific materials to specific plants during defined validity periods, with fixed source flags, MRP relevance indicators, and agreement references. Single source of truth for which vendors can supply which materials to which plants under what conditions. Drives automatic source determination and default PO values. Integrates with SAP MM ME11/ME12 and source list functionality.'
}

Table supply.delivery_schedule {
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery schedule line. Primary key for the supply delivery schedule entity.']
  primary_delivery_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the raw material, chemical compound, or finished product being scheduled for delivery. Links to material master data.']
  scheduling_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent scheduling agreement contract that governs this delivery schedule line. Links to the long-term supply arrangement with the chemical vendor.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical supplier responsible for delivering materials according to this schedule line. Links to vendor master data.']
  actual_delivered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of material delivered and received. Used to track delivery accuracy and calculate over-delivery or under-delivery variances.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the material was delivered and goods receipt was posted. Used for vendor performance measurement and on-time delivery KPI calculation.']
  batch_managed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material requires batch or lot number tracking for quality control, traceability, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) management.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique numerical identifier assigned by Chemical Abstracts Service to every chemical substance. Used for regulatory reporting, safety data sheet (SDS) management, and chemical inventory tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery schedule line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cumulative_received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material received to date against this scheduling agreement. Used to track fulfillment progress and calculate outstanding delivery obligations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price per unit (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency procurement operations.']
  delivery_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery schedule line indicating the commitment level. Forecast deliveries are planning estimates, firm deliveries are binding commitments, just-in-time deliveries support lean manufacturing, blanket deliveries cover long-term agreements, and trade-off deliveries allow quantity adjustments.']
  delivery_tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage variance (plus or minus) from the scheduled quantity that the vendor is allowed to deliver without requiring approval. Supports flexible chemical supply chain management.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the goods receipt posted against this schedule line. Links delivery schedule to warehouse management and inventory transactions.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the scheduled material is classified as hazardous and requires special handling, transportation, and storage procedures per DOT and OSHA regulations.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms code defining the responsibilities and risks between buyer and seller for this delivery (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP). Critical for chemical logistics and customs compliance.']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Specific location or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., Houston Port, Vendor Warehouse). Defines the point where risk and cost transfer occurs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery schedule line record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials for procurement analysis, spend management, and supplier performance reporting (e.g., solvents, polymers, catalysts).']
  net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net value of this schedule line calculated as scheduled quantity multiplied by price per unit. Used for budget tracking and financial planning.']
  outstanding_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Remaining quantity yet to be delivered under this schedule line. Calculated as scheduled quantity minus cumulative received quantity.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the scheduled material delivery will be received. Supports multi-site chemical manufacturing operations.']
  price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed unit price for the material as defined in the scheduling agreement. Used for cost planning and invoice verification.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the procurement team or buyer responsible for managing this scheduling agreement and delivery schedule. Used for workload distribution and accountability.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming material must undergo quality control (QC) inspection and laboratory testing before release to production. Supports Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when this schedule line was officially released to the vendor for fulfillment. Marks the transition from planning to execution.']
  release_strategy varchar [note: 'Method by which schedule lines are released to the vendor for fulfillment. Immediate releases are sent directly, forecast-based uses demand planning, consumption-based triggers on usage, and manual requires buyer approval.']
  schedule_line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the scheduling agreement that uniquely identifies this delivery schedule entry. Used for ordering and referencing specific schedule lines.']
  schedule_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery schedule line. Tracks progression from initial scheduling through vendor confirmation, partial fulfillment, completion, or cancellation.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date when the vendor is expected to deliver the material quantity to the receiving plant. Used for just-in-time manufacturing planning and production scheduling.']
  scheduled_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned quantity of material to be delivered on the scheduled delivery date. Supports continuous chemical manufacturing process planning and inventory optimization.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation planned for this delivery. Critical for hazardous material transportation planning and Department of Transportation (DOT) compliance.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing specific requirements for material handling, storage, or transportation (e.g., temperature control, moisture sensitivity, light protection). Critical for chemical safety and quality preservation.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Specific storage location code within the plant where the delivered material will be stored. Critical for hazardous material segregation and inventory management.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials used in international transportation (e.g., UN1090 for acetone). Required for shipping documentation and emergency response.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the scheduled quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, GAL for gallons). Critical for chemical quantity calculations and conversions.']
  vendor_confirmation_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor confirmed their ability to meet the scheduled delivery date and quantity. Critical for supply chain reliability tracking.']
  vendor_confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity that the vendor has confirmed they can deliver on the scheduled date. May differ from scheduled quantity if vendor capacity constraints exist.']

  Note: 'Scheduling agreement delivery schedule lines defining planned delivery dates and quantities for long-term supply arrangements with chemical vendors. Captures schedule line number, scheduling agreement reference, material, plant, scheduled delivery date, scheduled quantity, cumulative received quantity, delivery category (forecast vs. firm), and schedule line status. Supports just-in-time raw material delivery planning for continuous chemical manufacturing processes.'
}

Table supply.vendor_document {
  vendor_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor document record. Primary key.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific chemical material or product that this document pertains to. May be null for vendor-level certificates.']
  superseded_by_document_vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer vendor document that supersedes this document. Null if this is the current version.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical supplier who provided this document. Links to the vendor master data.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or jurisdiction for which this document is valid (e.g., USA, EU, APAC). Pipe-separated list if multiple regions apply.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status of the document for use in procurement and operations.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who approved the document for operational use.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was formally approved for use by the authorized approver.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'The CAS registry number identifying the chemical substance covered by this document. Format: XXXXXX-XX-X.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor document record was first created in the system.']
  document_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 or MD5 hash of the document file for integrity verification and duplicate detection.']
  document_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language of the document content.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or control number assigned to the document by the vendor or issuing authority.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current validity status of the vendor document in the compliance repository.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor document. SDS = Safety Data Sheet, MSDS = Material Safety Data Sheet, COA = Certificate of Analysis, COC = Certificate of Compliance, REACH = Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals dossier, TSCA = Toxic Substances Control Act certification, GMP = Good Manufacturing Practice certificate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SDS|MSDS|COA|COC|REACH_DOSSIER|TSCA_CERTIFICATION|ISO_9001_CERTIFICATE|ISO_14001_CERTIFICATE|GMP_CERTIFICATE|RESPONSIBLE_CARE_ATTESTATION|HAZMAT_DECLARATION|TECHNICAL_DATA_SHEET|ALLERGEN_STATEMENT|HALAL_CERTIFICATE|KOSHER_CERTIFICATE|CONFLICT_MINERALS_DECLARATION — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version or revision number of the document as assigned by the issuing authority or vendor.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the document becomes valid and enforceable for compliance purposes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the document expires and is no longer valid for regulatory or compliance purposes. Null for documents without expiration.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'The digital file format or MIME type of the stored document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|XML|HTML|TXT|TIFF|JPG|PNG — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the digital file as stored in the document management system or data lake.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'The storage location or URI path where the document file is stored in the lakehouse or document repository.']
  file_size_kb decimal(12,2) [note: 'The size of the document file in kilobytes.']
  ghs_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document (typically SDS/MSDS) complies with GHS classification and labeling standards.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was issued or published by the vendor or certifying authority.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'The name of the organization, certification body, or regulatory agency that issued or certified the document.']
  issuing_authority_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the issuing authority or certification body.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor document record was last updated or modified.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The material code or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that this document covers. Denormalized for reporting convenience.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review or renewal of the document to ensure continued compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or compliance notes related to the document.']
  reach_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document demonstrates compliance with EU REACH regulation for chemical substance registration and authorization.']
  received_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who received and registered the document in the system.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was received from the vendor and entered into the system.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code if the document was rejected during review or approval process.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who performed the last compliance review of the document.']
  reviewed_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was last reviewed by EHS, procurement, or quality assurance personnel for compliance verification.']
  tsca_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document certifies compliance with US EPA TSCA requirements for chemical substances.']

  Note: 'Repository of compliance and regulatory documents received from chemical suppliers including SDS/MSDS, COA, COC, REACH registration dossiers, TSCA certifications, ISO certificates, GMP certificates, and Responsible Care attestations. Captures document type, vendor, material or CAS number reference, document version, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, document file reference, and document validity status. Ensures regulatory document currency for EHS and procurement compliance.'
}

Table supply.toll_manufacturing_order {
  toll_manufacturing_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the toll manufacturing order.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Contract manufacturing orders must be linked to the internal manufacturing order they fulfill for cost allocation and traceability.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical material to be produced under the toll order.']
  rework_toll_manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on toll_manufacturing_order (rework_toll_manufacturing_order_id)']
  toll_manufacturer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third‑party manufacturer performing the toll production.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor supplying the toll manufacturing service.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the product was actually received from the toll manufacturer.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the toll manufacturing order.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the order.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order received final approval.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch generated by the toll manufacturer.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service registry number uniquely identifying the chemical substance.']
  certificate_of_analysis_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a COA was supplied with the toll order.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the overarching contract governing this toll order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency used for pricing.']
  delivery_location_code varchar [note: 'Plant or site code where the finished product will be received.']
  delivery_schedule_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date for the delivery window.']
  delivery_schedule_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the delivery window.']
  ghs_hazard_category varchar [note: 'GHS classification category for the material.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes and fees.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the material is classified as hazardous.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Customer‑facing material number or SKU for the product.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after taxes and any discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or special instructions related to the toll order.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the toll manufacturing order was created in the source system.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the toll manufacturing order.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the toll manufacturer (e.g., Net 30).']
  price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price for each unit of material produced.']
  quality_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific testing or certification requirements for the toll order.']
  quality_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed quality criteria the produced material must meet.']
  quantity_requested decimal(18,3) [note: 'Amount of material to be produced by the toll manufacturer.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer expects the finished product.']
  safety_data_sheet_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the SDS provided for the material.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage bin identifier for the received product.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applied.']
  toll_manufacturing_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the toll manufacturing order.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations identification number for hazardous substances.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the requested quantity.']

  Note: 'Toll manufacturing (contract manufacturing) order record tracking outsourced chemical production to third-party manufacturers. Captures toll manufacturer, material, quantity, quality requirements, pricing terms, and delivery schedule.'
}

Table supply.toll_manufacturer {
  toll_manufacturer_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the toll manufacturer record.']
  parent_toll_manufacturer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on toll_manufacturer (parent_toll_manufacturer_id)']
  capacity_annual_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of product the manufacturer can produce per year, expressed in metric tons.']
  capacity_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the annual capacity field.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer record is no longer effective (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer record became effective for business use.']
  environmental_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the manufacturer’s environmental compliance performance.']
  global_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the manufacturer can serve global supply locations.']
  manufacturer_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to reference the toll manufacturer.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the toll manufacturing organization.']
  manufacturer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the toll manufacturer relationship type.']
  manufacturing_process_type varchar [note: 'Primary process mode the manufacturer uses for production.']
  max_batch_size_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Largest single batch the manufacturer can produce, expressed in kilograms.']
  max_continuous_rate_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum continuous production rate, expressed in metric tons per hour.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the manufacturer.']
  number_of_sites varchar [note: 'Count of distinct production sites operated by the manufacturer.']
  primary_product_category varchar [note: 'Main category of chemicals the manufacturer is qualified to produce.']
  regulatory_approvals varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of key regulatory approvals held (e.g., REACH, TSCA, FDA).']
  toll_manufacturer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the toll manufacturer record.']

  Note: 'Master record for approved toll (contract) manufacturers qualified to produce chemical products on behalf of Chemical Mfg. Captures site capabilities, certifications, capacity, and qualification status.'
}

Table supply.supply_agreement {
  supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplyAgreement association']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the material master']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the vendor for the material (e.g., REACH compliant, ISO certified)']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Packaging type used by the vendor for this material (e.g., Drum, Bulk, Bag)']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was qualified for the material']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the vendor for the material (e.g., Qualified, Pending, Disqualified)']
  sds_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Safety Data Sheet used for the material from this vendor']
  supply_risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the vendor‑material supply relationship']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual and qualification relationship between a vendor and a raw material. Each record captures qualification status, qualification date, supply risk tier, compliance status, SDS version, and packaging type for a specific vendor‑material pairing.. Existence Justification: In chemical manufacturing a vendor can supply many raw materials and a raw material can be sourced from multiple vendors. The business actively manages each vendor‑material pairing with qualification status, dates, risk tier, compliance and packaging details. Therefore the relationship is an operational entity that is created, updated, and deleted by procurement teams.'
}

Table logistics.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shipment']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Direct account link on shipment enables traceability of which customer a shipment belongs to, supporting delivery performance dashboards.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for the shipment.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Regulatory shipment manifest must link each shipment to the primary chemical product for SDS, GHS labeling and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation report requires each shipment charged to a cost center for accurate freight expense tracking.']
  driver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Driver assignment for each shipment is needed for DOT compliance and safety incident reporting; logistics tracks driver via employee_id.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Shipment traceability mandates recording the exact formula version used to produce the shipped batch, supporting recalls and quality audits.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Shipment execution report ties each outbound shipment to the originating manufacturing order for traceability and regulatory compliance.']
  origin_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Loading planning needs the precise warehouse location of origin for inventory allocation and temperature control compliance.']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'FK to order.outbound_delivery.outbound_delivery_id — Shipment-to-order fulfillment linkage is required for delivery confirmation and proof-of-delivery workflows. Without this FK, logistics cannot confirm which sales order deliveries have shipped.']
  packaging_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_config. Business justification: Linking shipment to packaging configuration ensures correct DOT, IATA and hazmat labeling per product packaging for regulatory compliance.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Each shipment fulfills a specific sales order; adding a FK ties the physical shipment to its originating order for end‑to‑end logistics tracking.']
  production_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_plan. Business justification: Production‑to‑Shipment Traceability Report requires linking each shipment to the Production Plan that created the product.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis links each shipment to the profit center that owns the revenue stream.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Needed to associate each shipment with its originating purchase order for inbound receipt verification and logistics cost allocation.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: R&D material delivery tracking report requires linking each shipment to the research project that requested the material.']
  transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.transfer_order. Business justification: Transfer orders are fulfilled via shipments; linking them enables end‑to‑end tracking of inter‑plant movements.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real date and time when the shipment was received at destination.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real date and time when the shipment left the origin facility.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the advanced shipping notice sent to the receiver.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the bill of lading document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for freight amounts.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the customs declaration associated with the shipment.']
  destination_facility_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the facility where the shipment is destined.']
  dot_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status with DOT 49 CFR shipping paper requirements.']
  equipment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trailer, container, or railcar used for the shipment.']
  estimated_transit_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from departure to arrival.']
  export_control_status varchar [note: 'Export control clearance status for the shipment.']
  freight_amount_discount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Discounts applied to the gross freight charge.']
  freight_amount_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight charge before discounts or adjustments.']
  freight_amount_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net freight charge after discounts.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Payment responsibility for freight charges.']
  hazardous_materials_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazardous material classification code (1‑9). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Total count of individual packages or units in the shipment.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Standard classification of packaging (e.g., pallet, drum, barrel).']
  regulatory_documentation_status varchar [note: 'Status of required regulatory paperwork for the shipment.']
  scheduled_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for shipment arrival at destination.']
  scheduled_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for shipment departure from origin.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'External shipment reference number used in logistics and customer communications.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipment.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Category of shipment based on mode of transport.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional instructions for handling, e.g., fragile, keep upright.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment must be temperature‑controlled.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature for the shipment.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature for the shipment.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of the shipment in kilograms.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for real‑time status lookup.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations identification number for hazardous substances.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment record.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every outbound or inbound freight movement of chemical products. Captures shipment type (LTL/FTL/intermodal), origin and destination facilities, scheduled and actual departure/arrival dates, total weight, volume, hazmat class, UN number, DOT 49 CFR compliance status, carrier assignment, freight terms (prepaid/collect), shipment status, and Oracle TMS shipment reference. Serves as the primary operational entity for all logistics execution activities. Supports DOT 49 CFR §172.200 shipping paper requirements and §177.817 driver document requirements.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_line {
  shipment_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment line record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch_record. Business justification: Batch traceability report requires linking each shipped line to its batch record for quality recall and compliance.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Each shipment line needs the specific chemical product to generate product‑specific hazard documentation, traceability and batch control.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Batch traceability matrix ties each shipped line to the experimental formulation it fulfills, needed for QA and scale‑up decisions.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: Shipment line items correspond to order line items; linking provides detailed fulfillment and billing reconciliation.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent shipment header.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Regulatory traceability (GHS/REACH) requires each shipment line to reference the exact inventory lot shipped.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or product being shipped.']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planned_order. Business justification: Shipment‑to‑Planned‑Order Traceability links each shipped line back to its originating Planned Order.']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Enables traceability from shipped items back to the original quoted line for compliance, billing reconciliation, and margin analysis.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Pick‑by‑position processes need the exact stock position for each line to optimize warehouse operations.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the carrier responsible for transporting this line.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Certificate of Analysis associated with the material.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  customs_declaration_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if a customs declaration is required.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Customs document number for international shipments.']
  dot_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if DOT labeling is required for this line.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the material, if applicable.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Freight charge amount for this line.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Freight class for LTL/FTL billing purposes.']
  ghs_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if GHS labeling is required for this line.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight including packaging in kilograms.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line contains hazardous material.']
  is_freight_chargeable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether freight charges apply to this line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the line within the shipment.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the shipment line.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Manufacturing date of the material.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the shipped items (excluding packaging) in kilograms.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Number of packages of this type in the line.']
  package_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the package in centimeters.']
  package_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the package in centimeters.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for the line item.']
  package_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of the package in centimeters.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'Packing group indicating degree of danger.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material shipped in the line.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Seal or closure identifier for verification.']
  seal_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package seal has been verified.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code for the shipped product.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight of the empty packaging in kilograms.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if temperature control is required for the shipment.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in Celsius for temperature‑controlled shipment.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in Celsius for temperature‑controlled shipment.']
  un_hazard_class varchar [note: 'UN hazard class for hazardous material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment line record.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each product, material, or package included in a shipment. Records the finished chemical SKU or raw material, ordered and shipped quantities, unit of measure, lot number, batch number, hazmat indicator, UN hazard class, packing group, net and gross weight, COA reference, package count, package type (drum/IBC/tote/ISO tank), package dimensions, tare weight, GHS/DOT label requirements per 49 CFR §172.400, seal or closure verification, and packing list documentation for customs clearance. Supports multi-product chemical shipments, regulatory manifest line-item traceability, receiving verification, and international customs documentation requirements.'
}

Table logistics.bill_of_lading {
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated surrogate primary key for the Bill of Lading record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Bill of lading should reference carrier entity; carrier_scac is redundant once carrier_id FK is added.']
  destination_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the location where the shipment is destined.']
  origin_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the location where the shipment originates.']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: A bill of lading is generated for a single shipment; adding parent_logistics_shipment_id creates the required child‑to‑parent link.']
  primary_bill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party that is sending the shipment.']
  shipment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planned transportation route.']
  bill_of_lading_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the Bill of Lading.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier printed on the legal transport document.']
  bol_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the document is a master BOL or a house BOL.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of compliance indicators (e.g., REACH|TSCA|GHS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Bill of Lading record was first created in the system.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customs filing associated with the shipment.']
  declared_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for the shipment for liability and customs purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the declared value.']
  dot_certification_statement varchar [note: 'Legal statement required by DOT 49 CFR confirming compliance.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the emergency contact listed on the Bill of Lading.']
  equipment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier (e.g., container number) of the equipment.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment used to move the shipment.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification for export control compliance.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged by the carrier for transportation.']
  freight_charge_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the freight charge.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Freight classification used for rate calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 50|55|60|65|70|77.5|85|92.5|100|110|125|150|175|200|250|300|400|500 — promote to reference product]']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory class of the hazardous material, per DOT.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  hazmat_un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations identification number for the hazardous material.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial term governing responsibility and cost allocation.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Bill of Lading was issued to the carrier.']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Total count of individual packages or units in the shipment.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Standard classification of the packaging used.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference such as purchase order or sales order number linked to the shipment.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the security seal applied to the transport unit.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free‑text instructions for handling the shipment (e.g., keep upright, no stacking).']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shipment must be kept within a temperature range.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the required temperature range in Celsius.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the required temperature range in Celsius.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the Bill of Lading record.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Legal transport document issued for each chemical shipment, serving as the contract of carriage between shipper and carrier. Captures BOL number, shipper and consignee details, carrier SCAC code, freight class, declared value, hazmat emergency contact, special handling instructions, seal numbers, and DOT 49 CFR certification statement. Required for all regulated chemical transport under DOT and IATA rules.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the carrier master record.']
  approved_hazard_classes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of GHS/UN hazard classes the carrier is authorized to transport.']
  average_load_capacity_tons decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical maximum payload per vehicle expressed in metric tons.']
  benchmark_comparison varchar [note: 'Result of comparing carrier performance against internal benchmarks.']
  carrier_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the carrier\'s capabilities and services.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the freight carrier.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport the carrier provides.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the carrier service contract (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the carrier service contract.']
  corrective_action_flag boolean [note: 'True if any corrective actions were issued for the carrier during the measurement period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier record was first created.']
  ctpat_certified boolean [note: 'True if the carrier is certified under the C‑TPAT program.']
  damage_claim_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of shipments resulting in damage claims.']
  dot_carrier_number varchar [note: 'Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) registration number for the carrier.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Total number of vehicles or units operated by the carrier.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier holds a valid hazmat certification for regulated chemical shipments.']
  hazmat_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of hazardous‑material incidents recorded for the carrier in the measurement period.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier\'s insurance certificate.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier\'s liability insurance coverage expires.']
  insurance_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier providing liability coverage.']
  invoice_accuracy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of carrier invoices that match agreed rates and terms.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or performance audit of the carrier.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier record.']
  mc_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier\'s operating authority.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the reporting window for carrier KPIs.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'First day of the reporting window for carrier KPIs.']
  oracle_tms_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Carrier identifier used within Oracle Transportation Management.']
  performance_on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries made on schedule during the measurement period.']
  performance_on_time_pickup_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of pickups completed within the agreed time window during the measurement period.']
  preferred_lanes varchar [note: 'Typical origin‑to‑destination lane pairs where the carrier is preferred.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email address used for carrier communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for carrier communications.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with DOT, EPA, and other applicable regulations.']
  safety_rating_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Composite safety score (0‑5) derived from audit results and incident history.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character code assigned to the carrier by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association.']
  tender_acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of carrier tenders accepted versus offered.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier relationship was terminated, if applicable.']
  tier_rating varchar [note: 'Internal tier classification based on performance and strategic importance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the carrier record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all approved freight carriers used to transport chemical products, including qualification status, contractual terms, and periodic performance scorecards. Stores carrier name, SCAC code, DOT carrier number, MC number, carrier type (LTL/FTL/rail/intermodal/tanker/ISO tank), hazmat certification status, insurance certificate expiry, approved chemical hazard classes, preferred lanes, carrier tier rating, CTPAT certification status, Oracle TMS carrier ID, and performance KPIs (on-time pickup/delivery rates, damage and claim rates, hazmat compliance incidents, tender acceptance rate, invoice accuracy, measurement period, corrective action flags, benchmark comparison). Supports carrier qualification reviews, contract renewal decisions, tier classification, and compliance vetting per DOT 49 CFR §172.800 security plan requirements for regulated chemical transport.'
}

Table logistics.freight_order {
  freight_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the freight order.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Freight order reporting and cost allocation per customer account requires direct account reference; enables financial reconciliation and compliance reporting.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier selected to execute the shipment.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Freight orders must reference the chemical product to calculate hazardous‑material surcharges and produce compliance reports.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight order budgeting is recorded against the responsible cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  destination_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the location where the shipment is to be delivered.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Internal order tracking ties each freight procurement to an internal order for approval workflow and spend control.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Freight Order Planning uses material master data to determine carrier selection, hazmat handling, and temperature requirements; linking to material_master provides the necessary material specification']
  origin_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the location where the shipment originates.']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: A freight order is tendered for a specific shipment; linking to shipment provides the parent reference.']
  primary_freight_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipper (internal customer) that owns the freight order.']
  production_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_plan. Business justification: Freight Order Generation Process creates freight orders only after a Production Plan is firmed.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for generating freight orders from purchase orders; enables carrier tendering and freight cost tracking per PO.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Freight cost allocation to R&D projects is required for project budgeting and cost‑to‑complete analysis.']
  accessorial_charges decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional charges (e.g., liftgate, detention) applied to the freight order.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the electronic shipping notice sent to the receiver.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Document number issued by the carrier confirming receipt of the shipment.']
  carrier_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Current acceptance response from the carrier.']
  carrier_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used by the carrier for this freight order.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance assessment of the freight order with DOT/EPA regulations.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier contract governing rates and terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'Latest time the carrier must complete delivery at the destination.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Earliest time the carrier may deliver to the destination.']
  dot_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing applicable DOT transportation regulations for the shipment.']
  epa_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing applicable EPA environmental regulations for the shipment.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of trailer or equipment required for the shipment.']
  freight_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the freight order.']
  freight_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base rate charged by the carrier before accessorials.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials subject to DOT regulations.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT classification of hazardous material (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this freight order record.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment load based on size, hazard, and temperature requirements.']
  number_of_pallets varchar [note: 'Count of pallets included in the freight order.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the freight order for tracking and communication.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the freight order was created in the TMS.']
  pickup_window_end timestamp [note: 'Latest time the carrier must complete loading at the origin.']
  pickup_window_start timestamp [note: 'Earliest time the carrier may begin loading at the origin.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Customer purchase order linked to this freight shipment.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the freight order meets all required regulatory checks.']
  temperature_requirement_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature control for temperature-sensitive chemicals.']
  tender_method varchar [note: 'Method used to tender the freight order to carriers.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount payable for the freight order (rate + accessorials).']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific trailer assigned to the freight order.']
  volume_cubic_m decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the freight order in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate weight of the freight order in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Operational freight order tendered to a carrier for execution of a shipment or consolidated load. Captures freight order number, tendering method (spot/contract/load board), carrier acceptance status, agreed freight rate, accessorial charges, pickup and delivery windows, load type, temperature requirements for temperature-sensitive chemicals (e.g., epoxy resins, catalysts), and TMS order reference. Represents the contractual execution unit between Chemical Mfg and the carrier per DOT 49 CFR §172.204 shipper certification requirements. Supports multi-stop and pool-point distribution common in chemical distribution networks.'
}

Table logistics.delivery_confirmation {
  delivery_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the delivery confirmation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Associating delivery confirmations with the customer account is required for receipt acknowledgment reporting and SLA compliance.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Delivery confirmation includes carrier name/scac; carrier_id FK links to carrier master data.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Delivery confirmation ties actual receipt to the cost center responsible for the inbound logistics spend.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Proof‑of‑delivery is required for invoice settlement; linking POD to its invoice supports audit and payment release.']
  lab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.lab_experiment. Business justification: Proof‑of‑delivery for samples ties confirmation records to the specific lab experiment that consumes them.']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Delivery confirmation records proof of delivery for a shipment; linking to shipment ties the POD to its shipment.']
  receiver_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or receiving party that accepted the shipment.']
  condition_on_arrival varchar [note: 'Observed condition of the goods at the time of receipt.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the proof‑of‑delivery document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery confirmation record was first created in the system.']
  delivered_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Quantity of product actually delivered to the receiver.']
  delivery_address varchar [note: 'Physical address where the shipment was delivered.']
  delivery_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the delivery confirmation.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment was physically received by the customer.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing any shortages, damages, or other issues noted on receipt.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the type of exception, if any, associated with the delivery.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials subject to DOT 49 CFR.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage measured at the delivery location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier of the product batch delivered.']
  pod_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic proof‑of‑delivery document.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the delivered quantity.']
  received_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who signed for the delivery.']
  receiver_signature varchar [note: 'Electronic representation of the signature captured from the receiver as proof of delivery.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery met all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature recorded at the delivery location, in degrees Celsius.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
