  total_paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative sum of all payments posted against this open item.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the quantity field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the open‑item record.']
  write_off_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value written off for this item.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the write‑off was recorded.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'True when the remaining amount has been written off as uncollectible.']

  Note: 'Open accounts receivable item representing an outstanding customer obligation not yet fully cleared. Tracks invoice reference, due date, overdue days, dunning level, disputed amount, and partial payment status. Serves as the operational AR aging ledger for collections management and SOX-compliant financial reporting in SAP FI.'
}

Table billing.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the billing dispute record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who raised the dispute.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a linked case or ticket in the customer service system.']
  dispute_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who initially created the dispute record.']
  dispute_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or team responsible for resolving the dispute.']
  dispute_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who performed the most recent update to the dispute record.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the invoice that is under dispute.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Dispute analysis: linking disputes to the material master allows root‑cause analysis of quality or specification issues raised by customers.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Maintenance Invoice Dispute: disputes are linked to the originating work order to trace the source of the charge.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any credit or debit applied during dispute resolution (e.g., discount, penalty).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dispute was initially logged by the customer or internal staff.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency for the disputed amounts.']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text entered by the customer or agent describing the issue.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the dispute case, used in communications and tracking.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the dispute within the resolution workflow.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount that the customer claims is incorrect.']
  disputed_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount originally billed on the disputed invoice.']
  escalated_to_department varchar [note: 'Department to which the dispute was escalated, if applicable.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute has been escalated beyond the initial owner.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments; the amount to be paid or refunded.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the dispute for handling urgency.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason why the customer is contesting the invoice.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to settle the dispute (e.g., issue credit, provide rebate).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the dispute was closed or otherwise resolved.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or details recorded by the resolver.']

  Note: 'Formal record of a customer-raised dispute against an invoice or billing document. Captures dispute case number, disputed invoice reference, dispute reason (pricing error, quantity discrepancy, quality claim, duplicate billing), disputed amount, dispute status, resolution owner, and resolution date. Supports CAPA-driven dispute resolution workflows and customer satisfaction tracking.'
}

Table billing.billing_rebate_agreement {
  billing_rebate_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the rebate agreement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer party to which the rebate agreement applies.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying sales contract linked to the rebate agreement.']
  sales_rebate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_rebate_agreement. Business justification: Links billing‑side rebate contracts to sales‑side rebate agreements for unified rebate accounting.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Rebate calculations follow a defined pricing strategy; linking enables accurate rebate accrual reporting.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method used to accrue rebate liability (periodic, continuous, or on invoice).']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the rebate agreement, used in contracts and invoicing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rebate agreement received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who approved the rebate agreement.']
  billing_rebate_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the rebate agreement purpose and scope.']
  billing_rebate_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rebate agreement.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Metric used to calculate the rebate amount (revenue, volume, or product group).']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory frameworks (e.g., REACH, GHS, EPA) that the rebate must satisfy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REACH|GHS|EPA|OSHA|ISO_9001|ISO_14001|ISO_45001|TSCA|FASB|SOX — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rebate agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for rebate amounts.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rebate agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rebate agreement expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rebate is exclusive to the customer (true) or can be combined with other incentives (false).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent update to the rebate agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal comments or observations related to the agreement.']
  product_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the product group(s) to which the rebate applies.']
  rebate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the rebate calculated for the current period.']
  rebate_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount payable under the rebate agreement for a given period.']
  rebate_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage or factor applied to the calculation basis to determine rebate value.']
  rebate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rebate (e.g., volume, growth, loyalty, market share).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country or region code where the rebate is valid.']
  revenue_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum revenue amount required to trigger the rebate.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often rebate amounts are settled with the customer.']
  tiered_scale_description varchar [note: 'Textual or JSON representation of tiered rebate rates based on thresholds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rebate agreement record.']
  volume_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum volume (e.g., metric tons) required to trigger the rebate.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the rebate agreement record.']

  Note: 'Master record of a customer rebate or volume incentive agreement tied to a commercial contract. Captures rebate agreement number, customer account, rebate type (volume, growth, loyalty, market-share), calculation basis (revenue, volume, product group), rebate rate or tiered scale, validity period, accrual method, and settlement frequency. Common in chemical manufacturing for large-volume industrial customers and distributors with annual volume commitments.'
}

Table billing.rebate_settlement {
  rebate_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each rebate settlement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the rebate.']
  billing_rebate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rebate agreement that this settlement fulfills.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the settlement.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rebate settlements are posted to a specific GL account; linking ensures accurate financial reporting of rebate expenses.']
  sales_rebate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_rebate_agreement. Business justification: Associates each rebate settlement with its originating sales rebate agreement for audit trails.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Settlement of rebates must reference the pricing strategy that generated the rebate for audit and compliance.']
  accrued_rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total rebate amount accrued during the settlement period before any adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the settlement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the settlement was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the settlement amounts.']
  external_settlement_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from an external system (e.g., bank, ERP) for reconciliation.']
  gross_rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Rebate amount before tax or other deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the settlement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to transfer the rebate payment.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference (e.g., check number, transaction ID) for the payment.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the period for which the rebate is calculated.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the period for which the rebate is calculated.']
  rebate_settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the settlement.']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for statutory reporting of rebate settlements (e.g., SOX, EPA).']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement has been reversed.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a settlement was reversed, if applicable.']
  settled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the customer after tax and adjustments.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Means by which the rebate is settled (e.g., credit memo, cash).']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable settlement document number assigned by finance.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the settlement was executed.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the rebate settlement, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the settlement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a rebate payout or credit settlement issued to a customer upon fulfillment of rebate agreement conditions. Captures settlement document number, rebate agreement reference, settlement period, accrued rebate amount, settled amount, settlement method (credit memo, payment), and settlement date. Supports period-end rebate accounting and SOX revenue recognition compliance.'
}

Table billing.revenue_recognition_event {
  revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue recognition event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the revenue event.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Revenue must be recognized when a manufacturing order is completed (ASC 606/IFRS 15); linking enables accurate revenue timing.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance: revenue events tied to material deliveries need the material identifier to allocate revenue correctly.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Revenue recognition is driven by closed opportunities; linking enables ASC 606/IFRS15 reporting.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific performance obligation being recognized.']
  period_id varchar [note: 'Financial period identifier (e.g., FY2023Q2) for the revenue event.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: ASC 606 revenue recognition for R&D contracts requires linking each recognition event to the underlying project for compliance reporting.']
  revenue_recognition_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the accounting journal entry generated for this revenue event.']
  revenue_recognition_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue recognition rule applied (e.g., straight-line, milestone).']
  reversal_of_event_revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [note: 'If this event is a reversal, references the original revenue recognition event.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Revenue recognition rules (ASC 606/IFRS 15) are derived from the pricing strategy applied to the contract.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Revenue Recognition for Maintenance Services: revenue events are linked to the work order delivering the service.']
  compliance_flag_asc606 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with ASC 606 revenue recognition criteria.']
  compliance_flag_ifrs15 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with IFRS 15 revenue recognition criteria.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the revenue event for internal reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue recognition record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the revenue amounts.']
  deferred_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of revenue deferred (unearned) associated with this event.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount or rebate amount applied to the recognized revenue.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the revenue recognition posting event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition event was triggered (e.g., delivery, billing, milestone).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if currency conversion was required.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the revenue event is intercompany (between legal entities).']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the recognized revenue amount is estimated (e.g., for long-term contracts).']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates if this event is a reversal of a previously posted revenue recognition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the revenue recognition event.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Financial posting date for the revenue recognition entry.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'End date of the revenue recognition period (for over-time recognitions).']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the revenue recognition period (for over-time recognitions).']
  recognition_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether revenue is recognized at a single point or over a period.']
  recognized_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of revenue recognized in this event.']
  revenue_amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Recognized revenue amount in the local currency of the transaction.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Category of revenue (e.g., product sales, service, licensing).']
  revenue_line_type varchar [note: 'Type of revenue line item.']
  revenue_recognition_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the revenue recognition event.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Accounting standard used for revenue recognition.']
  source_event_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source transaction (e.g., delivery note, invoice) that caused this revenue recognition.']
  source_event_type varchar [note: 'Source event that triggered the revenue recognition.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component included in the recognized revenue amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to the revenue transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue recognition record.']

  Note: 'Record of a revenue recognition posting event triggered by delivery, billing, or contractual milestone completion. Captures recognition event type (point-in-time, over-time), recognized revenue amount, deferred revenue amount, performance obligation reference, recognition date, and ASC 606 / IFRS 15 compliance flags. Integrates with SAP FI/CO revenue accounting and reporting (RAR) for SOX-compliant financial close.'
}

Table billing.intercompany_billing {
  intercompany_billing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each intercompany billing record.']
  interplant_supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.interplant_supply_plan. Business justification: Needed to tie intercompany billing records to the interplant supply plan that scheduled the material transfer, supporting transfer‑pricing compliance and inventory reconciliation.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Intercompany billing documents are conceptually similar to invoices; linking them via invoice_id enables traceability and eliminates the free-text invoice_number column.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Transfer‑pricing: internal billing for raw‑material transfers must reference the material master to satisfy tax and regulatory reporting.']
  receiving_party_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the receiving legal entity in the master party table.']
  sending_party_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the sending legal entity in the master party table.']
  transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.transfer_price. Business justification: Inter‑company billing is based on a pre‑agreed transfer price; tax and compliance reports require the transfer_price reference.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Intercompany Billing for Cross‑Plant Maintenance Work Order: internal invoices reference the work order that generated the cost.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before tax, discount, and net adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax and discount, the amount payable.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the transaction for reporting.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the billing record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency used for the transaction.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount granted on the gross amount.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the invoice must be paid.']
  ic_elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is eliminated in consolidated financial statements.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the billing record.']
  material_service_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the material or service transferred between entities.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment for the invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms defining when payment is due.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Financial period date on which the invoice is posted.']
  price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of the transferred material or service before taxes and discounts.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal profit center linked to the transaction.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric amount of the material or service transferred.']
  rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any rebate applied to the invoice.']
  rebate_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a rebate has been settled on this invoice.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity that is receiving the goods or services.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the billing record.']
  regulatory_transfer_pricing_doc varchar [note: 'Reference to the supporting transfer pricing documentation required for regulatory compliance.']
  sending_company_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity that is sending the goods or services.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applicable to the transaction.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intercompany invoice.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the intercompany invoice was issued.']
  transfer_price_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the intercompany transfer price.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the quantity field.']

  Note: 'Billing document for intercompany transactions between legal entities within the chemical manufacturing group. Covers transfer of chemical intermediates, toll manufacturing charges, catalyst loans, shared service allocations, and technology licensing fees. Captures sending and receiving company codes, intercompany invoice number, transfer price methodology (cost-plus, market-based, comparable uncontrolled price), material or service description, quantity, amount, currency, and IC elimination flag for consolidated financial reporting. Supports arm\'s-length transfer pricing documentation required by OECD guidelines and local tax authorities.'
}

Table billing.dunning_notice {
  dunning_notice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dunning notice.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to whom the notice is issued.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to settlement transaction if payment reconciled.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Dunning Notice for Overdue Maintenance Invoice: dunning records are tied to the work order whose invoice is overdue.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that generated the notice.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the notice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notice record was created in the system.']
  credit_limit_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the notice triggered a credit limit review.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for amounts.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has raised a dispute on the notice.']
  due_date date [note: 'Payment due date associated with the notice.']
  dunning_date date [note: 'Date the dunning notice was generated and sent.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Escalation level of the notice (1-4).']
  dunning_notice_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the notice.']
  dunning_number varchar [note: 'System-generated sequential number for the dunning notice.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the notice was escalated to next level.']
  interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated interest charge applied.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable interest rate applied to overdue amount.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the notice was generated automatically.']
  last_reminder_sent_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent reminder sent to the customer.']
  legal_escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the notice has been escalated to legal action.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or actions taken.']
  oldest_overdue_item_date date [note: 'Date of the earliest overdue invoice item included in the notice.']
  payment_received_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount received against this notice.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date when payment was received for this notice.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional penalty fees applied.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current response status from the customer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the notice record.']
  total_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of overdue amount, interest, and penalties.']
  total_overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount overdue at time of notice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the notice record.']

  Note: 'Formal overdue payment reminder issued to a customer at escalating dunning levels per SAP FI dunning program configuration. Captures dunning notice number, customer account, dunning level (1-4), total overdue amount, oldest overdue item date, dunning date, communication channel (email, postal, EDI), interest/penalty charges applied, and response status. Supports automated collections workflow, credit limit review triggers, and legal escalation for chronic non-payment. Critical for managing extended payment cycles common in chemical manufacturing B2B relationships where Net 60-90 terms are standard.'
}

Table billing.payment_term {
  payment_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the payment term.']
  applicability_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment code (e.g., INDUSTRIAL, RETAIL) for which this payment term is valid.']
  applicability_product_group varchar [note: 'Product group identifier (e.g., CHEMICALS, POLYMERS) to which the term applies.']
  applicability_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code) where the payment term can be used.']
  baseline_date_rule varchar [note: 'Rule indicating which document date is used as the starting point for due‑date calculation.']
  cash_flow_impact_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the term\'s impact on cash‑flow forecasting.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason recorded for the most recent change to the payment term.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework that governs handling of the payment term.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the payment term record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment term record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code applicable to monetary amounts in the payment term.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the early‑payment discount is a percentage or a fixed monetary amount.']
  dunning_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive dunning notices.']
  dunning_level_start varchar [note: 'Initial dunning level triggered after the due date passes.']
  early_payment_discount_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the baseline date during which the early‑payment discount applies.']
  early_payment_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered for payment made within the qualifying early‑payment window.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the payment term becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the payment term expires; null if open‑ended.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment term in an external source system (e.g., legacy ERP).']
  fixed_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount discount offered for early payment when discount_type is fixed_amount.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the term applies to intercompany or consolidated billing scenarios.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term is the default for new customers or contracts.']
  legal_clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract clause or statutory provision governing this payment term.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cap on the total discount value that can be applied under this term.']
  net_due_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days after the baseline date when full payment is due if no discount is taken.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks, special conditions, or legal caveats.']
  payment_method_allowed varchar [note: 'Payment methods that may be used when this term is applied.']
  payment_term_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the payment term and its business purpose.']
  payment_term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment term.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'True if usage of this payment term must be approved by a manager or finance officer.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'True if taxes are considered included in the net due amount.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the payment term (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the payment term record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment term record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the payment term definition for change‑management tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining standard payment terms offered to customers in chemical manufacturing commercial agreements. Captures payment term code, description, baseline date calculation rule, early payment discount percentage and qualifying days, net due days, and applicability rules by customer segment, region, or product group. Examples include Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30, letter of credit terms, and consignment settlement terms. Referenced by invoices and sales orders to calculate due dates and drive AR aging, dunning triggers, and cash flow forecasting in SAP FI/SD.'
}

Table billing.billing_schedule {
  billing_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing schedule record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to be billed.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or service covered by the billing schedule.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying sales contract linked to this billing schedule.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Schedule creation is owned by a finance employee; audit logs require the creator employee for governance.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Contractual billing: scheduled billing for raw‑material supply contracts requires linking each schedule to the specific material being supplied.']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_term. Business justification: Billing schedules reference payment terms; adding a payment_term_id FK formalizes this relationship and allows joins to retrieve term details.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Derives billing frequency and amounts from the governing price agreement, used in contract billing execution.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Project‑based billing schedules are tied to R&D project milestones for accurate invoicing and schedule tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Billing Schedule tied to Maintenance Work Order: schedule defines periodic billing for recurring maintenance contracts per work order.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent amendment was applied.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of contract amendments affecting this schedule.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the schedule was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the schedule.']
  billing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned monetary amount to be invoiced for each billing event.']
  billing_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code of the billing amount.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Regular interval at which billing events are generated.']
  billing_interval varchar [note: 'Number of frequency units between successive billings (e.g., every 2 months).']
  billing_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billing schedule.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment collection process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing schedule record was created.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied to the billing amount.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the billing amount before tax.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the invoice must be paid.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the billing schedule becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the billing schedule expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date of the final planned billing event.']
  fixed_amount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the billing amount is a fixed value (true) or variable (false).']
  is_milestone_based boolean [note: 'True if billing is driven by achievement of defined milestones.']
  last_billing_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent billing was executed.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the milestone tied to billing.']
  milestone_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of the total contract value associated with this milestone.']
  next_billing_date date [note: 'Date of the upcoming billing occurrence.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions related to the billing schedule.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition governing when payment is due.']
  percentage_of_contract decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of the total contract value represented by this schedule (e.g., 10.00 for 10%).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the billed amount has been reconciled with received payments.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue for the scheduled billing.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the billing schedule for easy identification.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is based on regular periods or specific milestones.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date of the first planned billing event.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is already included in the billing amount.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) for the billing amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the billing schedule.']
  variable_amount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the billing amount can vary based on usage or other factors.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the schedule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a periodic or milestone-based billing plan for long-term chemical supply contracts, toll manufacturing agreements, or take-or-pay arrangements. Captures billing plan type (periodic, milestone), billing dates, planned billing amounts, percentage of contract value, and billing status per period. Managed in SAP SD billing plan (FPLA/FPLP) for contract-driven revenue scheduling.'
}

Table billing.withholding_tax {
  withholding_tax_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each withholding tax record.']
  adjusted_withholding_tax_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on withholding_tax (adjusted_withholding_tax_id)']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tax filing responsibility is assigned to a finance employee; linking enables compliance tracking and reporting.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer invoice to which this withholding tax applies.']
  reversal_of_tax_withholding_tax_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original withholding tax record that is being reversed.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Withholding Tax applied to Maintenance Work Order Invoice: tax records reference the work order that generated the taxable invoice.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the withholding tax record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the withholding tax record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the withholding tax becomes effective for reporting purposes.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when the tax amount is recorded in a foreign currency.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the withholding tax filing was submitted to the authority.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the tax authority for the filing of the withholding tax.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Status of the tax filing with the jurisdiction.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the tax jurisdiction.']
  remittance_date date [note: 'Date on which the tax amount was remitted to the authority.']
  remittance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tax remittance to the tax authority.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a reversal of a previously posted withholding tax.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the withholding tax record (e.g., SAP FI/CO).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated tax amount to be withheld from the invoice payment.']
  tax_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount on which the withholding tax is calculated.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is exempt from withholding tax.']
  tax_period_end date [note: 'End date of the tax period for which the withholding applies.']
  tax_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the tax period for which the withholding applies.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15%).']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Category of tax being withheld, such as income, sales, royalty, service, or generic withholding.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the withholding tax record.']
  withholding_tax_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the withholding tax record.']

  Note: 'Withholding tax calculation and reporting record for customer invoices in jurisdictions requiring tax withholding at source. Captures tax type, rate, base amount, and remittance status.'
}

Table billing.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']
  parent_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent party in hierarchical relationships.']
  ultimate_parent_id varchar [note: 'Top-level parent party identifier for consolidated reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reported annual revenue of the party.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Primary bank account number for payments to the party.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number associated with the party\'s bank account.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the party\'s primary address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the party\'s primary address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was first created.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount extended to the party.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the party.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'DUNS number for the party.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the party became effective in the system.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the party\'s record is no longer effective (null if still active).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the party.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code (e.g., NAICS) for the party.']
  is_blacklisted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the party is blacklisted for compliance reasons.']
  is_vat_registered boolean [note: 'Indicates if the party is registered for VAT.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contact with the party.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the party as registered.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total number of employees for the party (if applicable).']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party based on business relationship.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms (e.g., Net 30) applicable to the party.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the party.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the party\'s primary address.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code used for transactions with the party.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for primary communications with the party.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Government registration number for the party (e.g., business registration).']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Internal risk score (0-100) for the party.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Standard Industrial Classification code for the party.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the party data originated.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the party\'s primary address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is exempt from sales tax.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., EIN, VAT) for the party.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Commonly used trade or brand name of the party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the party record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'VAT registration number for the party.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by party_id.'
}

Table billing.settlement {
  settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for settlement']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that this settlement settles.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (customer, vendor, etc.) associated with the settlement.']
  payment_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment receipt linked to this settlement.']
  reversed_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on settlement (reversed_settlement_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Overall approval state of the settlement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User who approved the settlement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement was approved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for bulk settlement processing.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the settlement was processed.']
  country varchar [note: 'Country associated with the settlement transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the settlement purpose or details.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount applied to the gross amount before tax.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement is under dispute.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for the settlement dispute.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the settlement amount is expected to be paid.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the settlement\'s effective period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the settlement\'s effective period (null if open‑ended).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement event occurred.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied when settlement currency differs from reporting currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Date on which the exchange rate was sourced.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, and adjustments.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the settlement was entered manually rather than automatically.']
  method varchar [note: 'Payment method used for the settlement.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after taxes, discounts, and adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes related to the settlement.']
  number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the settlement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing payment timing and conditions.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (e.g., North America, EMEA) for reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the settlement record (e.g., SAP FI, external ERP).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current settlement status.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the settlement.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to the settlement.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate percentage used to calculate tax_amount.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of settlement (e.g., invoice, credit memo).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the settlement record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for settlement. Referenced by settlement_id.'
}

Table billing.performance_obligation {
  performance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance_obligation']
  parent_performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance_obligation (parent_performance_obligation_id)']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often billing occurs for this obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance obligation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the deliverable, scope, and conditions.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the total amount, if any.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the performance obligation expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the performance obligation becomes binding.']
  is_recognized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether revenue for the obligation has been fully recognized.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  metric_actual decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual measured value of the performance metric to date.']
  metric_target decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target value for the performance metric defined in the contract.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the performance obligation.']
  obligation_number varchar [note: 'External contract or agreement number that identifies the performance obligation to customers and auditors.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the performance obligation indicating the nature of the deliverable.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms (e.g., Net 30, 2% 10 Net 30).']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure fulfillment of the obligation.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue for the obligation was recognized.']
  revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue recognized to date for this performance obligation.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue for this obligation per IFRS 15 / ASC 606.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the performance obligation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for the obligation.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used for calculating tax on the obligation.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual monetary value associated with the performance obligation before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the performance obligation record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for performance_obligation. Referenced by performance_obligation_id.'
}

Table billing.revenue_recognition_rule {

  applicable_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment (e.g., wholesale, retail) for which the rule is valid; null if universal.']
  applicable_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category to which this rule applies; null if universal.']
  cost_account_code varchar [note: 'Chart of accounts code for cost of goods sold related to revenue under this rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires or is superseded; null if indefinite.']
  fiscal_year_alignment varchar [note: 'Alignment of the rule\'s timing with the company\'s fiscal year.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether revenue recognition is performed automatically by the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any additional remarks or exceptions.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue under this rule.']
  recognition_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue to recognize per period when applicable.']
  recognition_timing varchar [note: 'Frequency at which revenue is recognized under the rule.']
  revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'Chart of accounts code for revenue postings under this rule.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule\'s purpose and application.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the revenue recognition rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule defining the recognition methodology.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'Business event that triggers revenue recognition according to the rule.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that last updated the rule record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the rule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for revenue_recognition_rule. Referenced by revenue_recognition_rule_id.'
}

Table ehs.safety_incident {
  safety_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the safety incident record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Carrier Safety Performance Tracking links safety incidents to the carrier involved, enabling carrier compliance metrics and OSHA reporting.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Safety incident reports must link to the CAS registry to retrieve official hazard classifications for the reported chemical.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Incident reports must link to the specific chemical product for root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting (OSHA, EPA).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating incident-related costs to the responsible cost center for budgeting and regulatory cost reporting.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: INCIDENT MANAGEMENT: Safety incident reports must record the equipment involved for root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula. Business justification: INCIDENT REPORTING: Safety incident reports must reference the specific formula being produced to assess product‑specific hazards and corrective actions.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location (plant, site, area) where the incident occurred.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Transport Safety Incident Reporting requires linking each safety incident to the shipment during which it occurred for root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Required for incident investigation to trace the specific lot that caused the safety incident, enabling recall and root‑cause analysis.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Safety Incident Reporting requires linking each incident to the manufacturing order that caused it for root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Incident tracking uses the material master record to access detailed specifications, storage, and compliance data for the affected raw material.']
  primary_safety_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initially reported the incident.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Regulatory incident reports must reference the specific sales order that caused the safety incident for traceability and liability analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Root‑cause analysis ties each safety incident to the specific PO that delivered the offending material, supporting supplier audits and compliance reporting.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Incident reporting for R&D projects requires linking each safety incident to its project for risk tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  tertiary_safety_investigation_lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee leading the incident investigation.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle. Business justification: Vehicle Accident Investigation requires associating safety incidents with the specific vehicle to analyze fleet safety and maintenance needs.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Regulatory incident reports require identifying the responsible supplier of the hazardous material, enabling supplier performance tracking and corrective actions.']
  attachments_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting documents or media are attached to the incident record.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of the corrective action.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action plan is needed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was initially created.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the area where the incident happened.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident resulted in an environmental release or impact.']
  exposure_concentration decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of the chemical exposure.']
  exposure_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'True if measured concentration exceeds the regulatory threshold.']
  exposure_measurement_type varchar [note: 'Method used to measure chemical exposure.']
  exposure_threshold decimal(12,4) [note: 'Regulatory threshold (PEL/TLV) for the measured chemical.']
  exposure_units varchar [note: 'Units of the exposure concentration measurement.']
  first_aid_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether first‑aid was administered on site.']
  follow_up_actions varchar [note: 'Planned or completed actions to prevent recurrence.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the incident for external reference and tracking.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident (e.g., injury, near‑miss, chemical exposure).']
  injury_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of injury or health effect resulting from the incident.']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was formally closed.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the incident investigation.']
  lost_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of full days of work lost due to the incident.']
  lost_time_hours varchar [note: 'Additional hours of work lost beyond full days.']
  medical_treatment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether professional medical treatment was required.']
  number_of_affected varchar [note: 'Count of individuals impacted (injured, ill, or exposed) by the incident.']
  occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident actually occurred.']
  osha_recordable_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident meets OSHA recordable criteria.']
  property_damage_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of property or equipment damage caused by the incident.']
  psm_incident_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident is a PSM‑related event (e.g., loss of containment).']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident must be reported to OSHA or other regulators.']
  report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was first reported into the system.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the primary cause of the incident. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment|human_error|procedure|material|environment|management|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the root cause analysis findings.']
  safety_incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incident record.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the incident based on impact and regulatory criteria.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the incident record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all workplace safety incidents, near-misses, first-aid events, and occupational chemical exposure monitoring at Chemical Mfg facilities. Captures incident type (injury, illness, near-miss, property damage, process safety, chemical exposure), severity classification (OSHA recordable, lost-time, first-aid), incident date/time, location, affected personnel, initial description, root cause category, and regulatory reportability flags. For chemical exposure events: captures substance (CAS number), exposure measurement type (personal air sampling, area monitoring, biological monitoring), measured concentration, OSHA PEL/ACGIH TLV threshold, exceedance flag, and required follow-up. Integrates with Intelex EHS Management. Supports OSHA 300/300A recordkeeping, PSM incident reporting, and OSHA 1910.1000 air contaminants compliance.'
}

Table ehs.incident_investigation {
  incident_investigation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the incident investigation record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tracks investigation expenses against the cost center responsible for the incident, enabling accurate financial reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the primary investigator leading the effort.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the incident occurred.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organizational department responsible for the area of the incident.']
  production_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_deviation. Business justification: Investigations of safety incidents are often triggered by a specific production deviation; linking provides traceability for CAPA.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Investigations are performed per R&D project; linking enables project‑level corrective actions and audit trails.']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the safety incident or near‑miss that triggered this investigation.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: CORRECTIVE ACTION: Investigations often create a work order to implement corrective actions; linking enables traceability.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for closing the investigation (e.g., resolved, withdrawn).']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the investigation was marked complete.']
  contributing_factors varchar [note: 'Identified factors that contributed to the incident but are not direct causes.']
  corrective_action_recommendations varchar [note: 'Proposed actions to prevent recurrence of the incident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the estimated loss amount.']
  direct_cause varchar [note: 'Immediate cause that directly led to the incident.']
  estimated_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of loss or cost associated with the incident.']
  follow_up_actions varchar [note: 'Planned or completed actions that follow the investigation recommendations.']
  impact_area varchar [note: 'Business domain most affected by the incident.']
  incident_investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigation.']
  investigation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the context, scope, and background of the investigation.']
  investigation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the investigation for tracking and reporting.']
  investigation_title varchar [note: 'Brief title summarizing the focus of the investigation.']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigation based on the nature of the event.']
  investigator_team_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of employee IDs participating in the investigation.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigation has been formally closed.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the investigation findings.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the root‑cause analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the investigation based on risk and impact.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory submission was made, if applicable.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory filing related to the investigation.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating derived from severity, likelihood, and exposure.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Fundamental underlying cause(s) identified through analysis.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the incident impact.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the investigation was formally started.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the investigation record.']

  Note: 'Detailed root cause investigation record linked to a safety incident or near-miss. Captures investigation team members, RCI (Root Cause Investigation) methodology used (5-Why, Fault Tree, HAZOP-based), contributing factors, direct and root causes, corrective action recommendations, investigation completion date, and regulatory submission status. Supports PSM incident investigation requirements under OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 and ISO 45001 audit trails.'
}

Table ehs.capa_record {
  capa_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CAPA record.']
  capa_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Coordinated corrective actions: EHS CAPA records often reference the same quality CAPA record to ensure aligned safety and quality remediation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Charges CAPA corrective action costs to the appropriate cost center for cost control and compliance budgeting.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant, facility, or site where the CAPA originates.']
  management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MOC record linked to this CAPA, if applicable.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  primary_capa_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party (person or team) responsible for executing the CAPA.']
  related_incident_safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the safety or quality incident that generated this CAPA.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: CAPA EXECUTION: Each CAPA may be executed via one or more work orders; linking provides execution tracking.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record documents a corrective action or a preventive action.']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the CAPA record.']
  capa_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CAPA record.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the CAPA was closed (e.g., completed, withdrawn, not applicable).']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the CAPA was closed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the CAPA with the referenced regulation.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed steps to correct the identified non‑conformance.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred for the CAPA.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected financial cost to implement the CAPA.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department that owns the CAPA (e.g., Production, Quality, Maintenance).']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the CAPA must be completed.']
  effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Result of the effectiveness review after CAPA closure.']
  effectiveness_review_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness of the CAPA was evaluated.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the CAPA was formally initiated.']
  is_external boolean [note: 'True if external consultants or contractors are involved in the CAPA.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the CAPA record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the owner or reviewers.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed steps to prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the CAPA.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the CAPA record.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or standard that prompted the CAPA (e.g., OSHA, EPA).']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Comments entered during the effectiveness review.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause that triggered the CAPA.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the original issue that triggered the CAPA.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the CAPA was effective.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the CAPA record.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record tracking remediation actions arising from safety incidents, near-misses, audit findings, inspection citations, or regulatory observations at Chemical Mfg facilities. Captures action description, action type (corrective vs. preventive), responsible owner, priority, due date, completion date, verification method, effectiveness review outcome, and linkage to originating incident, inspection finding, or MOC. Supports ISO 45001 continual improvement (Clause 10.2), ISO 14001 nonconformity management, OSHA PSM follow-up, and Responsible Care management system requirements.'
}

Table ehs.hazop_study {
  hazop_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the HAZOP study record.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: HAZOP studies reference the CAS registry to ensure the correct chemical hazard profile is evaluated during process safety analysis.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: HAZOP studies are conducted per chemical process; linking to the product enables traceability of risk assessments.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the primary engineer or safety professional leading the study.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula. Business justification: HAZOP ANALYSIS: Each Hazop study is initiated for a particular formulation to evaluate process safety risks of that product.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: HAZOP SCOPE: HAZOP studies are scoped to a functional location; linking enables location‑based risk management.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Funds Hazop studies through an internal order, linking safety analysis to the capital project financial tracking.']
  action_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the recommended actions.']
  action_owner varchar [note: 'Individual or department responsible for implementing the action.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recommended action.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the study was formally approved.']
  control_measures varchar [note: 'Engineering or administrative controls in place to mitigate identified hazards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the study record was first created.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to the full HAZOP report or supporting documents.']
  exposure_limit_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Permissible exposure limit for the chemical expressed in parts per million.']
  hazard_classifications varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classes and pictograms applicable to the chemical.']
  hazop_study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the study.']
  inventory_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maximum intended inventory.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the most recent review of the study.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent review.']
  max_intended_inventory decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the chemical intended to be stored in the unit (numeric value).']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Specific analytical approach applied (Guideword‑based HAZOP, What‑If, Checklist, LOPA).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations captured during the study.']
  overall_risk_rank varchar [note: 'Aggregated risk ranking for the study based on likelihood and severity.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'Physical state of the chemical at operating conditions.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site where the study was performed.']
  process_unit_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the process unit (e.g., reactor, distillation column) examined in the study.']
  process_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the process unit covered by the study.']
  recommended_actions varchar [note: 'Summary of corrective or preventive actions proposed by the study.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory program(s) governing the study (e.g., OSHA PSM, EPA RMP).']
  revalidation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revalidation or periodic review of the study.']
  risk_likelihood varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑5) of the likelihood of identified hazards.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (likelihood × severity) for each node or overall.']
  risk_severity varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑5) of the severity of potential consequences.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the functional and physical scope of the HAZOP study.']
  study_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference code assigned to the study (e.g., HZP‑2024‑001).']
  study_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the HAZOP study activities were completed.']
  study_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the HAZOP study activities commenced.']
  study_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the HAZOP study.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the study (HAZOP, What‑If, LOPA, Checklist).']
  team_members varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of participants (engineers, operators, safety specialists).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the study record.']

  Note: 'Process safety master record encompassing Process Safety Information (PSI), Hazard and Operability (HAZOP) studies, Process Hazard Analyses (PHA), and granular study nodes for chemical process units at Chemical Mfg plants. PSI content: chemical identity (CAS number), maximum intended inventory, physical/chemical properties, reactivity hazards, corrosivity data, thermal stability, P&ID references, equipment design specifications, safe operating limits. Study content: study scope, process unit reference, team composition, methodology (HAZOP, What-If, Checklist, LOPA), study/revalidation dates, overall risk ranking. Node-level detail: node identifier, design intent, guide words (No, More, Less, Reverse, Other Than), deviations, causes, consequences, safeguards, risk rankings (likelihood × severity), recommended actions. Required under OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119(d) and (e) and EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68. Integrates with Intelex EHS Management.'
}

Table ehs.hazop_node {
  hazop_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the HAZOP node.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Each HAZOP node references the chemical product to evaluate deviations and safeguards.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Individual or team responsible for implementing the recommended action.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of equipment associated with the node, if applicable.']
  hazop_study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent HAZOP study to which this node belongs.']
  action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of the recommended action.']
  cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis for the identified deviation.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the node with relevant regulations.']
  consequence varchar [note: 'Possible outcome or impact if the deviation occurs.']
  control_strategy varchar [note: 'Description of the control strategy applied at this node.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the HAZOP node record was first created.']
  design_intent varchar [note: 'Description of the intended operation or purpose of the process at this node.']
  existing_safeguard varchar [note: 'Current safeguards or controls that mitigate the deviation.']
  exposure_limit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable exposure limit for the identified hazard.']
  exposure_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the exposure limit (e.g., ppm, mg/m³).']
  guide_words varchar [note: 'Guide words applied to the node (e.g., No, More, Less, Reverse, Other Than).']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Category of hazard associated with the node.']
  hazop_node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the node within the HAZOP workflow.']
  identified_deviation varchar [note: 'Specific deviation from the design intent identified during the HAZOP.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is considered critical to safety or operation.']
  likelihood varchar [note: 'Qualitative likelihood rating for the deviation.']
  mitigation_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing mitigation actions.']
  mitigation_owner varchar [note: 'Person or team responsible for mitigation actions.']
  mitigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the mitigation actions for the node.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the node within the HAZOP study.']
  node_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the HAZOP node, e.g., "Reactor Feed Section".']
  node_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or plant area where the node is situated.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the node based on its function in the process.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Proposed corrective or preventive action to address the deviation.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory standard(s) (e.g., OSHA, EPA) applicable to the node.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the node was last reviewed.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the latest review of the node.']
