  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the simulation model, incremented on each revision.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall product yield from the simulated process, expressed as a percentage.']

  Note: 'Record of process simulation models and scale-up feasibility assessments for R&D compounds and formulations. Captures simulation ID, simulation name, associated synthesis procedure or formulation, simulation type (steady-state, dynamic, heat integration), process units modeled, key outputs (conversion rate, selectivity, energy consumption, yield), thermodynamic model used (NRTL, UNIFAC, Peng-Robinson, etc.), validation status against lab data, scale factor evaluated, identified scale-up challenges (heat transfer, mass transfer, mixing, safety), HAZOP/PHA findings, recommended process modifications, capital cost estimate, and feasibility outcome (feasible, feasible with modifications, not feasible). Enables data-driven scale-up decisions and supports go/no-go stage-gate reviews. Integrates with process simulation tools (Aspen HYSYS, Aspen Plus, CHEMCAD) for model import/export.'
}

Table research.analytical_method {
  analytical_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the analytical method record.']
  lab_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the instrument on which the method is performed.']
  accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Closeness of measured value to the true value, expressed as a percentage.']
  analytical_method_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the method.']
  applicable_matrix varchar [note: 'Sample matrix or material type the method is validated for (e.g., water, soil, polymer).']
  associated_cas_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of CAS numbers relevant to the analytes covered by the method.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the method record was first created.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent data quality assessment.']
  detection_limit decimal(12,6) [note: 'Lowest concentration of analyte that can be reliably detected by the method.']
  detection_limit_units varchar [note: 'Units for detection limit (e.g., ppm, mg/L).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the method became officially effective for use.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the method is considered obsolete (if applicable).']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type or model of the instrument on which the method is performed.']
  is_compendial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the method is a recognized compendial method.']
  labware_method_code varchar [note: 'Code used by LabWare LIMS to reference this method.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last periodic review of the method.']
  linearity_range_max decimal(12,6) [note: 'Maximum concentration where the method response remains linear.']
  linearity_range_min decimal(12,6) [note: 'Minimum concentration where the method response is linear.']
  linearity_units varchar [note: 'Units for the linearity range (e.g., µg/mL).']
  method_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to reference the method within LIMS and other systems.']
  method_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the analytical procedure.']
  method_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the analytical method.']
  method_type varchar [note: 'Technology category of the method (e.g., HPLC, GC).']
  method_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the method (e.g., v1.2).']
  precision_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Repeatability expressed as %RSD across replicate injections.']
  regulatory_compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compendial standard the method complies with.']
  sample_preparation_notes varchar [note: 'Procedural notes for preparing samples prior to analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the method record.']
  validation_author varchar [note: 'Name of the scientist or engineer who approved the validation.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the method was formally validated.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation stage of the method.']

  Note: 'Master record for validated analytical methods used in R&D laboratory testing and characterization. Stores method ID, method name, method type (HPLC, GC, NMR, FTIR, ICP-MS, titration, rheology, particle size), applicable matrices, detection limits, linearity range, precision and accuracy parameters, validation status (development, validated, compendial), regulatory compliance reference (USP, ASTM, EPA method), instrument type, and LabWare LIMS method code. Serves as the SSOT for R&D analytical methodology.'
}

Table research.milestone {
  milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for milestone']
  approving_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Milestone gate decisions require an approving authority; linking to employee enables audit trail and gate approval reports.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent R&D project.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the milestone was actually finished; may be null if not yet completed.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial budget earmarked for activities associated with the milestone.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone meets applicable regulatory or internal compliance criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Free‑text justification for the gate decision outcome.']
  gate_decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the stage‑gate review: go, no‑go, conditional, or not applicable.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the milestone is deemed critical for project delivery.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone (e.g., "Formulation Prototype Complete").']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Operational status reflecting progress of the milestone.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the milestone indicating its purpose in the R&D stage‑gate workflow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary comments or observations captured by the project team.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Target date on which the milestone is scheduled to be achieved.']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'True if the milestone triggers a regulatory filing (e.g., FDA, REACH).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating assigned during project planning.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence indicating the position of the milestone in the project timeline.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Tracks stage-gate milestones and deliverables within R&D projects. Captures milestone ID, milestone name, milestone type (stage-gate review, go/no-go decision, prototype delivery, regulatory submission, technology transfer sign-off), planned date, actual completion date, milestone status (not started, in progress, completed, delayed, cancelled), gate decision outcome (go, no-go, conditional), decision rationale, and approving authority. Enables R&D portfolio governance and innovation pipeline visibility.'
}

Table research.sample {
  sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sample']
  associated_experiment_lab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the experiment or trial batch linked to the sample.']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Samples are physical instances of a compound; adding compound_registry_id links each sample to its master compound.']
  custodian_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sample custody is tracked for compliance; assigning a custodian employee supports chain‑of‑custody and disposal audits.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with the sample.']
  trial_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trial or production batch associated with the sample.']
  analysis_results varchar [note: 'Free‑text field capturing key analytical findings for the sample.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service registry number for the compound represented by the sample.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample record was first created in the system.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the sample was disposed.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of the sample when no longer needed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the sample is considered expired and must not be used.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class of the sample.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the sample is classified as hazardous under GHS.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample status last changed.']
  light_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample is sensitive to light exposure.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number assigned to the sample for traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations about the sample.']
  ppe_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether special PPE is required when handling the sample.']
  quality_control_status varchar [note: 'Result of quality control testing performed on the sample.']
  quantity decimal(20,6) [note: 'Amount of material in the sample.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sample meets applicable regulatory requirements.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the sample must be retained according to regulatory or project policy.']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'External reference code assigned to the sample, used in lab records and reports.']
  sample_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the sample.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the sample.']
  source_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source entity (e.g., supplier batch number, field collection ID).']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Origin of the sample.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage of the storage environment.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Location code of the storage area (e.g., freezer, cabinet).']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of any special storage conditions (e.g., inert atmosphere, refrigeration).']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Temperature condition of the storage environment in degrees Celsius.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the sample quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sample record.']

  Note: 'Tracks physical samples generated, received, tested, or distributed by the R&D laboratory throughout their lifecycle. Captures sample ID, sample type (synthesized compound, experimental formulation, reference standard, retained sample, raw material evaluation), source (internal synthesis, external supplier, field collection, customer return), associated experiment or trial batch, quantity and unit of measure, storage location and conditions (temperature, humidity, light sensitivity), chain of custody log with custodian transfers, retention period, expiration date, current status (available, in-test, consumed, expired, disposed), and disposal record with method. Managed in LabWare LIMS sample management module. Supports GLP/GMP sample traceability requirements.'
}

Table research.structure_activity_record {
  structure_activity_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the structure-activity relationship record.']
  assay_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the assay or test method used to generate the activity data.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who performed or oversaw the assay.']
  lab_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the instrument or equipment used for the measurement.']
  primary_structure_compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the chemical compound (linked to the compound master data).']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated Safety Data Sheet (SDS) record.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the measured activity (e.g., efficacy, toxicity, solubility).']
  activity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the activity value (e.g., mg/L, µM, %).']
  activity_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Numeric result of the activity measurement.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the experimental batch associated with the measurement.']
  concentration_mg_per_ml decimal(12,4) [note: 'Concentration of the compound in the assay solution.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative confidence rating of the measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SAR record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the data quality for the measurement.']
  is_control boolean [note: 'True if the record represents a control sample; otherwise False.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the material used in the assay.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the activity measurement was recorded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the measurement.']
  ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the assay solution.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SAR record.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) applicable to the compound or activity.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the assay result.']
  solvent varchar [note: 'Name of the solvent used in the assay.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the activity record.']
  temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the assay was conducted, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  test_condition_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any additional test conditions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SAR record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the SAR record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Records structure-activity relationship (SAR) data linking molecular structural features to observed performance outcomes in chemical and agrochemical applications. Captures structural descriptors (functional groups, substituents, molecular fragments), activity type (efficacy, toxicity, solubility, reactivity, thermal stability, UV resistance), measured values with units, test system or assay used, and confidence level. Supports computational chemistry, lead optimization, and formulation design decisions in specialty chemical and crop protection R&D.'
}

Table research.licensing_agreement {
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for licensing_agreement']
  amended_licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on licensing_agreement (amended_licensing_agreement_id)']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Licensing agreements reference specific chemical compounds; using a foreign key to compound_registry centralizes compound information and removes duplicated name and CAS fields.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the licensing agreement by the legal department.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the licensing agreement indicating the nature of rights granted.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the agreement has been amended.']
  audit_rights varchar [note: 'Rights reserved by the licensor to audit compliance with the agreement.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Level of confidentiality required for the licensed information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the licensing agreement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the licensing agreement terminates, if applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the licensing agreement becomes binding.']
  exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the license is exclusive to the licensee.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Jurisdiction whose laws govern the agreement.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to the agreement.']
  license_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the rights granted (e.g., manufacturing, distribution, research).']
  licensee_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the licensee\'s point of contact.']
  licensee_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the licensee\'s point of contact.']
  licensee_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party receiving the license.']
  licensor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the licensor\'s point of contact.']
  licensor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the licensor\'s point of contact.']
  licensor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party granting the license.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often royalty payments are due.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Specific conditions governing payment (e.g., net 30, invoicing details).']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that a renewal notice must be given.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Whether the agreement can be renewed and how.']
  reporting_requirements varchar [note: 'Obligations for the licensee to report usage, sales, or other metrics.']
  royalty_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for royalty payments.']
  royalty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage of revenue payable as royalty to the licensor.']
  signed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who signed the agreement on behalf of the licensor.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was signed by authorized parties.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the licensing agreement.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to notify termination of the agreement.']
  territory varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the license rights are granted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the licensing agreement record.']
  usage_limit_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of the compound that may be used under the agreement.']
  usage_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the usage limit.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for licensing_agreement. Referenced by licensing_agreement_id.'
}

Table research.assay {
  assay_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assay']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Assays are performed on specific chemical compounds; linking to the authoritative compound registry eliminates redundancy of storing CAS numbers and enables consistent compound data across the domain.']
  replicate_assay_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on assay (replicate_assay_id)']
  accuracy decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical accuracy (bias) of the assay expressed as a percentage.']
  application varchar [note: 'Typical application area (e.g., environmental monitoring, pharmaceutical QC).']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the assay was formally approved for use.']
  assay_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the assay within the R&D portfolio.']
  assay_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the assay.']
  assay_quality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay has passed internal quality checks.']
  assay_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the assay record must be retained for compliance.']
  assay_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status, such as deprecation rationale.']
  assay_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the assay category.']
  assay_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer version of the assay definition.']
  code varchar [note: 'External code or catalogue number used to reference the assay in lab systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assay record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or laboratory where assay data originates (e.g., LabWare LIMS).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the assay purpose, principle, and scope.']
  detection_limit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lowest concentration reliably detectable by the assay.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the assay definition is considered obsolete.']
  instrument varchar [note: 'Primary analytical instrument used to perform the assay.']
  is_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay is designated as a control or reference assay.']
  limit_of_quantitation decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lowest concentration at which the assay can reliably quantify the analyte.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantitative assay results (e.g., mg/L, ppm).']
  method varchar [note: 'Laboratory technique or analytical method employed (e.g., HPLC, GC‑MS, ELISA).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the assay.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the person or group responsible for the assay.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the assay.']
  precision decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical precision (e.g., %RSD) of the assay at the target concentration.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed experimental protocol or SOP used for the assay.']
  quantification_range varchar [note: 'Concentration range over which the assay provides accurate quantification.']
  regulatory_approval varchar [note: 'Regulatory body or standard that has approved the assay (e.g., EPA Method 525).']
  result_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assay yields quantitative measurements or qualitative outcomes.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety level associated with handling the assay reagents or samples.']
  sample_matrix varchar [note: 'Typical sample type or matrix the assay is validated for (e.g., water, soil, serum).']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure governing the assay.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assay definition.']
  technology varchar [note: 'Underlying technology platform (e.g., mass spectrometry, immunoassay).']
  type varchar [note: 'Category describing the scientific discipline or technique of the assay.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assay record.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the assay after method verification.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the assay definition, reflecting revisions.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for assay. Referenced by assay_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for transfer_package_document. Referenced by transfer_package_document_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for transfer_package. Referenced by package_id.'

Table maintenance.equipment {
  equipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each maintainable plant asset in the equipment master registry. Primary key for the equipment data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Asset ownership tracking; required for customer asset registers and billing reports linking equipment to the owning account.']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Required for Service Contract Management: links each equipment to the owning customer\'s site for billing, compliance, and maintenance scheduling.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Equipment must be assigned to a functional location for hierarchy and maintenance planning.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Equipment spec sheets require exact material grade for construction; regulatory compliance (PSM, ASME) and corrosion tracking depend on this link.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this equipment is physically installed and operated.']
  sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Assigns a sales rep to each sold equipment; used in after‑sales support KPIs and regional sales performance dashboards.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Maps equipment to the sales territory where it is installed; supports regional service planning and territory performance metrics.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Needed for equipment procurement, warranty, recall, and regulatory compliance tracking of the supplier of each equipment.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA fixed asset number linked to this equipment for financial accounting, depreciation calculation, and CapEx tracking. Bridges the maintenance and finance domains.']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Physical asset tag or nameplate number affixed to the equipment in the field. Used for physical identification during inspections, maintenance activities, and fixed asset accounting reconciliation.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment (typically instrumentation) requires periodic calibration to maintain measurement accuracy and regulatory compliance. True=calibration program applies; False=not applicable.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the equipment capacity value (e.g., m3, m3/hr, kW, kg/hr, bar). Follows SI unit conventions for chemical process engineering.']
  capacity_value decimal(14,4) [note: 'Principal rated capacity or throughput of the equipment (e.g., volumetric capacity for vessels in m³, flow rate for pumps in m³/hr, heat duty for heat exchangers in kW). The unit is specified in capacity_unit.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service following installation, pre-commissioning checks, and startup testing. Distinct from installation date.']
  construction_year varchar [note: 'Year in which the equipment was manufactured or constructed. Used for age-based risk assessment, remaining useful life estimation, and regulatory inspection scheduling.']
  corrosion_allowance_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Designed corrosion allowance in millimeters added to the minimum required wall thickness to account for expected corrosion over the equipment\'s design life. Used in fitness-for-service assessments and remaining life calculations.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center to which maintenance costs for this equipment are charged. Used for maintenance cost allocation, OpEx reporting, and budget variance analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.']
  criticality_class varchar [note: 'Equipment criticality ranking used to prioritize maintenance resources and inspection frequency. A=Safety-critical/production-critical (highest priority, PSM-covered); B=Important but has redundancy or workaround; C=Non-critical/low impact on safety or production.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was permanently removed from service and decommissioned. Null for active equipment. Required for fixed asset retirement accounting and regulatory closure documentation.']
  design_pressure_kpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) for which the equipment was designed, expressed in kilopascals (kPa). Critical nameplate data for pressure vessel inspection, relief valve sizing, and PSM mechanical integrity documentation.']
  design_temperature_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable design temperature for which the equipment was designed, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used for material of construction verification, thermal stress analysis, and PSM mechanical integrity records.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'SAP PM equipment category code classifying the asset type: M=Mechanical, E=Electrical, I=Instrumentation, P=Piping, V=Vessel/Reactor. Drives maintenance planning strategy and inspection requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: M|E|I|P|V|C|R — promote to reference product]']
  equipment_class varchar [note: 'Detailed equipment classification within the category (e.g., Centrifugal Pump, Reciprocating Compressor, Shell-and-Tube Heat Exchanger, Pressure Vessel, Control Valve, Thermocouple). Aligns with ISO 14224 equipment taxonomy for reliability data collection.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name or short description of the equipment asset (e.g., \'Reactor Feed Pump\', \'Distillation Column Overhead Condenser\'). Corresponds to SAP PM equipment description field.']
  equipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric equipment number assigned in SAP PM (EQUNR). Serves as the primary business identifier used across maintenance work orders, inspection records, and P&ID cross-references.']
  equipment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the equipment asset. Active=in normal service; Inactive=installed but not in service; Decommissioned=permanently removed from service; Standby=available as backup; Under_Maintenance=currently undergoing planned or corrective maintenance.']
  hazardous_area_classification varchar [note: 'Electrical area classification of the location where this equipment is installed, per NEC/IEC standards. Determines requirements for explosion-proof or intrinsically safe electrical equipment. Zone 0/1/2 per IEC 60079; Division 1/2 per NEC Article 500.']
  inspection_interval_months varchar [note: 'Required inspection frequency in months as determined by regulatory requirements, risk-based inspection (RBI) analysis, or manufacturer recommendations. Used to auto-calculate next_inspection_date.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was physically installed at its current functional location. Marks the start of the equipment\'s operational lifecycle at this site for maintenance history and warranty tracking.']
  insulation_type varchar [note: 'Type of thermal or acoustic insulation applied to the equipment (e.g., mineral wool, calcium silicate, cellular glass, none). Relevant for corrosion under insulation (CUI) inspection programs and energy efficiency assessments.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed inspection for this equipment. Used to calculate inspection intervals, assess compliance with regulatory inspection frequency requirements, and support risk-based inspection (RBI) programs.']
  maintenance_planner_group varchar [note: 'SAP PM planner group code responsible for planning and scheduling maintenance activities for this equipment. Determines which maintenance team owns the equipment\'s work order planning.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Assigned maintenance strategy for this equipment asset: Time_Based=fixed interval preventive maintenance; Condition_Based=maintenance triggered by condition monitoring thresholds; Predictive=data-driven prediction of failure; Run_To_Failure=no planned maintenance; Reliability_Centered=RCM-derived strategy.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM). Used for spare parts sourcing, warranty claims, and technical support escalation.']
  moc_status varchar [note: 'Current Management of Change (MOC) workflow status for any pending or recent modification to this equipment. Not_Required=no active MOC; Pending=MOC initiated and under review; Approved=MOC approved, change authorized; Closed=MOC completed and documented.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model or part number for this equipment type. Used for spare parts procurement, technical documentation retrieval, and like-for-like replacement identification.']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'Date by which the next calibration of this instrument or measurement device must be completed to maintain traceability and compliance. Null if calibration_required is False.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the next mandatory regulatory or internal inspection due for this equipment. Drives inspection scheduling for PSM mechanical integrity, API 510/570/653 inspections, and insurance surveys.']
  operating_pressure_kpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Normal operating pressure of the equipment under standard process conditions, expressed in kilopascals (kPa). Used for process monitoring, deviation detection, and SPC control limit setting.']
  operating_temperature_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Normal operating temperature of the equipment under standard process conditions, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used for process monitoring, heat balance calculations, and corrosion rate estimation.']
  pi_tag_reference varchar [note: 'Aveva PI System historian tag name or tag pattern associated with this equipment for real-time process data retrieval (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow readings). Enables linkage between equipment master data and real-time operational data.']
  pid_tag varchar [note: 'The P&ID tag number identifying this equipment on process engineering drawings (e.g., P-101A, E-201, V-301). Used for cross-referencing with Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams, HAZOP studies, and PSM documentation.']
  psm_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment is subject to OSHA Process Safety Management (PSM) mechanical integrity requirements under 29 CFR 1910.119. True=PSM-covered; requires documented inspection, testing, and quality assurance program.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for the equipment unit. Required for warranty tracking, OEM service records, and regulatory inspection documentation.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dry operating weight of the equipment in kilograms. Used for structural load calculations, rigging and lifting plans, and transportation logistics for equipment removal or replacement.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'SAP PM work center responsible for executing maintenance activities on this equipment (e.g., MECH-01, ELEC-02, INST-03). Drives labor scheduling, capacity planning, and cost center assignment.']

  Note: 'Equipment master data registry for all maintainable plant assets including reactors, vessels, pumps, compressors, heat exchangers, instrumentation, and piping systems. SSOT for equipment identity, technical specifications (design pressure/temperature, materials of construction, nameplate data), installation history, criticality classification (A/B/C), PSM mechanical integrity status, and regulatory inspection requirements. Supports equipment taxonomy, BOM structure, P&ID tag cross-reference, and full maintenance history linkage.'
}

Table maintenance.functional_location {
  functional_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the functional location record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the functional_location data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Enables charging maintenance expenses of a functional location to its assigned cost center, used in plant cost‑center performance reports.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Functional location belongs to a plant; plant_code is redundant after FK.']
  abc_indicator varchar [note: 'Criticality classification of the functional location using the SAP PM ABC indicator. \'A\' = critical (production-critical, safety-critical), \'B\' = important (significant impact), \'C\' = non-critical (minimal impact). Drives maintenance prioritization, spare parts stocking, and OEE improvement focus. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-ABCKZ.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Financial fixed asset number from SAP FI/CO Asset Accounting linked to this functional location. Enables reconciliation between the maintenance asset hierarchy and the financial asset register for CapEx tracking, depreciation, and SOX compliance.']
  building_or_area_code varchar [note: 'Physical building, bay, or plant area code where the functional location is situated. Used for maintenance crew routing, emergency response, and facility management. Complements the hierarchical floc_code with a physical location reference.']
  catalog_profile_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM catalog profile assigned to this functional location, defining the damage codes, cause codes, and activity codes available for maintenance notifications and work orders. Ensures standardized failure coding for reliability analysis and CAPA workflows. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-RBNR.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the functional location was formally commissioned and handed over to operations. Marks the start of the operational lifecycle and the beginning of preventive maintenance scheduling. May differ from installation_date for complex systems.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code (Buchungskreis) representing the legal entity that owns this functional location. Required for financial reporting, asset accounting, and SOX compliance. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-BUKRS.']
  construction_material varchar [note: 'Primary material of construction for the functional location (e.g., \'Carbon Steel A516-70\', \'316L Stainless Steel\', \'Hastelloy C-276\', \'FRP\'). Critical for corrosion management, inspection planning, and chemical compatibility assessment under REACH and PSM requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this functional location record was first created in the source system (SAP PM). Provides audit trail for asset master data governance and SOX compliance.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the functional location was formally decommissioned and removed from active service. Null for active locations. Required for asset lifecycle reporting, regulatory closure documentation, and EPA/OSHA decommissioning notifications.']
  floc_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the functional location within the plant hierarchy, as maintained in SAP PM (IFLOT-TPLNR). Follows the plant-unit-area-system-subsystem naming convention (e.g., CH01-RX-001-P-001). This is the business identifier used on work orders, P&IDs, and maintenance records.']
  floor_or_elevation varchar [note: 'Floor level or elevation reference (e.g., \'Grade Level\', \'EL +12.5m\', \'Mezzanine Level 2\') for the functional location within a building or structure. Supports maintenance crew navigation and emergency response planning.']
  functional_location_category varchar [note: 'Broad equipment category grouping for the functional location. Used for maintenance strategy segmentation, PSM mechanical integrity scope definition, and EHS compliance reporting. Aligns with ISO 14224 taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process_equipment|utility_equipment|instrumentation|electrical|civil_structural|safety_system|environmental_control — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  functional_location_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the functional location, describing its physical or logical purpose within the plant (e.g., \'Reactor Feed Pump — Unit 3 Ethylene Plant\'). Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-PLTXT.']
  geo_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the functional location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables spatial analytics, emergency response planning, and integration with plant GIS systems for asset location visualization.']
  geo_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the functional location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables spatial analytics, emergency response planning, and integration with plant GIS systems for asset location visualization.']
  hazardous_area_classification varchar [note: 'Electrical area classification for the functional location per NEC/IEC standards, indicating the presence and likelihood of flammable gases or vapors. Drives equipment selection, inspection requirements, and EHS compliance. Critical for OSHA and NFPA compliance.']
  inspection_class varchar [note: 'Inspection methodology classification for this functional location. \'RBI\' (Risk-Based Inspection) applies to pressure equipment per API 580/581; \'fixed_interval\' for time-based statutory inspections; \'condition_based\' for CBM programs; \'run_to_failure\' for non-critical assets. Drives inspection planning and regulatory compliance.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date on which the functional location (equipment or system) was physically installed and commissioned at the plant. Used for asset age calculations, depreciation scheduling, and mechanical integrity baseline establishment.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this functional location record in the source system (SAP PM). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and master data governance audit requirements.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed inspection for this functional location. Used to calculate inspection overdue status, support audit readiness, and demonstrate PSM mechanical integrity compliance to regulators.']
  linear_asset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this functional location represents a linear asset (e.g., pipeline, cable tray, conveyor) as opposed to a point asset. Linear assets require linear reference measurement for inspection and maintenance location identification.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the functional location. Drives maintenance scheduling eligibility, work order creation, and PSM mechanical integrity scope. \'Decommissioned\' locations are retained for historical traceability.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level classification of the functional location within the plant topology. Defines whether this node represents a plant, process unit, area, system, or sub-system. Drives hierarchy navigation and maintenance planning scope.']
  maintenance_strategy_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM maintenance strategy assigned to this functional location, defining the preventive maintenance cycle (e.g., time-based, condition-based, predictive). Drives automatic generation of maintenance orders and inspection plans. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-MSTRAT.']
  moc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any modification to this functional location requires a formal Management of Change (MOC) workflow per OSHA PSM requirements. Typically true for PSM-covered locations and safety-critical systems. Drives MOC initiation in the change management process.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled statutory or preventive inspection for this functional location. Used for maintenance planning, regulatory compliance tracking, and PSM mechanical integrity program management. Derived from inspection intervals but stored for operational scheduling.']
  object_type varchar [note: 'SAP PM object type code classifying the functional location by equipment category (e.g., reactor, pump, heat exchanger, vessel, compressor, instrumentation). Drives maintenance strategy assignment and spare parts planning. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-EQART. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reactor|pump|heat_exchanger|vessel|compressor|instrumentation|piping|column|tank|filter — promote to reference product]']
  parent_floc_code varchar [note: 'The floc_code of the immediate parent node in the functional location hierarchy. Enables recursive traversal of the plant topology from plant down to sub-system level. Null for top-level plant nodes. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-TPLMA.']
  pi_tag_reference varchar [note: 'Primary process historian tag identifier in the Aveva PI System associated with this functional location. Enables linkage between the SAP PM asset hierarchy and real-time process data for condition-based maintenance, OEE analysis, and predictive analytics.']
  pid_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or drawing identifier of the P&ID document on which this functional location appears. Critical for PSM mechanical integrity, MOC workflows, and HAZOP studies. Enables traceability between the physical asset hierarchy and engineering drawings.']
  planner_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM planner group responsible for planning and scheduling maintenance activities at this functional location. Used for workload distribution, capacity planning, and maintenance KPI reporting. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-INGRP.']
  pressure_rating_bar decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) for the functional location in bar units. Used for mechanical integrity assessments, inspection planning, and PSM compliance. Applies primarily to pressure vessels, reactors, and piping systems.']
  process_unit_name varchar [note: 'Name of the chemical process unit or production unit to which this functional location belongs (e.g., \'Ethylene Cracker Unit 1\', \'Sulfuric Acid Plant\', \'Polymerization Train B\'). Provides operational context for maintenance planning and P&ID alignment.']
  psm_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this functional location is within the scope of OSHA Process Safety Management (PSM) regulations (29 CFR 1910.119). PSM-covered locations require mechanical integrity programs, PHA/HAZOP studies, and MOC documentation.']
  sort_field varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric sort field used for grouping and filtering functional locations in maintenance reports and work order lists. Typically encodes plant area, equipment class, or operational unit for reporting convenience. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-EQFNR.']
  temperature_rating_c decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable design temperature for the functional location in degrees Celsius. Used for material selection validation, mechanical integrity assessments, and process safety compliance.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP work center responsible for performing maintenance activities at this functional location. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost allocation. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-GEWRK.']

  Note: 'Hierarchical functional location structure representing the physical and logical plant topology — plant, unit, area, system, and sub-system levels. Provides the spatial context for equipment installation and maintenance activities. Aligns with SAP PM Functional Location (IFLOT) and supports P&ID-based asset hierarchy for chemical process units.'
}

Table maintenance.work_order {
  work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the maintenance work order. Primary key for the work order entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Work orders are billed to a customer account; linking provides the Accounting & Invoicing report and compliance with contract billing.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Needed for Production Work Order report to track which chemical product is being manufactured in each work order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating labor and material costs of each work order to the proper cost center for financial reporting and budgeting.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Work Order Creation Report requires tracking which employee created each work order for audit and compliance.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment, asset, reactor, vessel, pump, or instrumentation device that is the subject of this maintenance work order.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: Freight Order Maintenance Request creates a work order to service carrier equipment or resolve issues tied to a specific freight order.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location (plant area, process unit, or facility section) where the maintenance work is to be performed. Functional locations represent the hierarchical structure of the plant.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Shipment‑Delay Work Order process requires linking a work order to the affected shipment for root‑cause analysis and delay reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Maintenance work orders often replace raw materials; linking enables material cost tracking, inventory deduction, and safety‑data‑sheet reference.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Service work orders are generated from sales opportunities; needed for service revenue attribution and opportunity closure analysis.']
  planner_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.planner_group. Business justification: Work order is planned by a planner group; code string replaced by FK.']
  pm_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance plan that generated this work order, if applicable. Null for corrective or breakdown work orders not originating from a scheduled maintenance plan.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Links installation work orders to the original sales quote; enables cost reconciliation and quote‑to‑service reporting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Links external maintenance contractor (vendor) to work order for cost allocation, contract compliance, and safety reporting.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual total duration of the maintenance work in hours, calculated from start to completion timestamps. Used for performance analysis and future work estimation.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when maintenance work execution was completed, recorded when all work activities are finished and equipment is ready for return to service.']
  actual_labor_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual total cost of internal labor based on confirmed hours and standard or actual labor rates. Used for maintenance cost accounting and variance analysis.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual total labor hours consumed across all crafts and operations, confirmed through time confirmations. Used for cost accounting and productivity analysis.']
  actual_material_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual total cost of spare parts, materials, and consumables consumed during work order execution, based on goods issue transactions and purchase order receipts.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when maintenance work execution began, recorded by the maintenance crew or supervisor when work commences on site.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes recorded by the maintenance crew upon work completion, documenting work performed, parts replaced, test results, and any deviations from planned scope.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken to address the root cause and prevent recurrence. Part of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this work order record was first created in the maintenance management system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all cost amounts on this work order (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for global manufacturing sites.']
  downtime_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual duration in hours that the equipment or process unit was offline due to this maintenance work. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation and production loss analysis.']
  downtime_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance work requires taking the equipment or process unit offline. True for intrusive maintenance; false for online maintenance activities.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned total duration of the maintenance work in hours, used for scheduling and resource allocation. Includes all craft labor time across all operations.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned total cost of internal labor based on estimated hours and standard labor rates by craft. Used for maintenance budget planning.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned total labor hours across all crafts and operations for this work order. Used for workforce planning and cost estimation.']
  estimated_material_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned total cost of spare parts, materials, and consumables required for this work order. Used for budget planning and work order approval.']
  external_service_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of external contractor services, third-party vendors, or specialized service providers engaged for this work order. Includes service labor, equipment rental, and contractor-supplied materials.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Description of the equipment failure mode or degradation mechanism that necessitated this corrective maintenance work. Used for reliability analysis and Root Cause Investigation (RCI).']
  lockout_tagout_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lockout/tagout procedures are required for this work order to control hazardous energy sources. True for work on equipment with electrical, mechanical, hydraulic, or chemical energy hazards.']
  long_text varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions, technical notes, safety precautions, and procedural guidance for executing the maintenance work. May include references to Standard Operating Procedures (SOP), Process and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&ID), or special tools required.']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Management of Change (MOC) authorization if this work order involves modifications to process equipment, instrumentation, or control systems that require formal change management per PSM requirements.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this work order record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this work order record was last modified in the maintenance management system.']
  notification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating maintenance notification or trouble report that triggered creation of this work order. Links work order to the initial problem report or service request.']
  permit_to_work_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the safety permit authorizing this maintenance work, required for work in hazardous areas, confined spaces, hot work, or energized equipment per Process Safety Management (PSM) requirements.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the work order indicating urgency and impact on production operations. Critical priority indicates immediate safety or production impact requiring emergency response.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the equipment failure or maintenance need, determined through Root Cause Investigation (RCI). Used to drive Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) programs.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date when maintenance work is scheduled to be completed. Used for outage planning and production restart coordination.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when maintenance work is scheduled to begin. Used for capacity planning and coordination with production schedules.']
  total_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual cost of the work order including internal labor, materials, and external services. Used for maintenance cost reporting and asset lifecycle cost analysis.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the maintenance work center (craft shop or service group) that will execute the work. Work centers represent organizational units such as mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, or contractor crews.']
  work_order_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the maintenance work to be performed, summarizing the scope and nature of the activity for quick identification and communication.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the work order, typically system-generated and used for tracking and communication across maintenance teams and external service providers.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order tracking progression from creation through execution to closure. Released status indicates work is approved and ready for execution; completed indicates work is finished but administrative closure pending. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|in_progress|on_hold|completed|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance work order indicating the nature of the maintenance activity: planned maintenance, corrective repair, preventive maintenance schedule, predictive maintenance based on condition monitoring, emergency breakdown response, or capital project work.']

  Note: 'Core maintenance work order record capturing all planned, corrective, preventive, predictive, and externally-contracted maintenance activities. Tracks work order type, priority, functional location, equipment, planner group, work center, scheduled/actual dates, labor hours, material consumption summary, internal/external cost totals, permit-to-work reference, and completion status. Supports both internal craft execution and external service provider work. Primary transactional entity for maintenance execution, cost tracking, and PM compliance.'
}

Table maintenance.work_order_operation {
  work_order_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work order operation. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each operation may be billed to a specific cost center; required for detailed operation‑level cost tracking and internal charge‑back.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment, asset, or technical object on which the operation is performed (e.g., pump, reactor, vessel, instrument).']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location (process unit, area, or system) where the operation is performed. Provides hierarchical location context.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Operation‑level details need the specific material used for accurate labor/material costing and SDS compliance.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to maintenance.plant']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Associates each operation’s consumed spare parts with the purchase order that supplied them, enabling traceability, inventory reconciliation, and audit of maintenance costs.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external vendor or service provider performing the operation, applicable only when external_operation_flag is true.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (maintenance crew, shop, or organizational unit) responsible for executing this operation.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent maintenance work order that contains this operation. Links to the work_order product.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity being performed (e.g., mechanical repair, electrical work, instrumentation calibration, inspection). Used for cost allocation and capacity planning.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when work on the operation was completed, as recorded in the final time confirmation.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when work on the operation actually began, as recorded in the first time confirmation.']
  actual_work_duration decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours spent on the operation as recorded through time confirmations. Used for performance analysis and variance reporting.']
  actual_work_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for actual work duration. H=hours, MIN=minutes, D=days.']
  calculation_key varchar [note: 'Key that determines how operation duration is calculated based on work quantity and standard values. Used for automatic time calculation in preventive maintenance.']
  confirmation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time confirmation is required for this operation. True if the operation must be confirmed with actual hours and completion status.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'Control key that determines the operation type and scheduling parameters (e.g., PM01 for internal processing, PM02 for external service, PM03 for inspection). Defines whether the operation requires confirmation, external processing, or automatic goods issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operation record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  earliest_finish_date date [note: 'Earliest date the operation can be completed based on earliest start date and planned duration.']
  earliest_start_date date [note: 'Earliest date the operation can begin based on scheduling constraints, predecessor operations, and resource availability.']
  external_operation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation is performed by an external service provider rather than internal maintenance staff. True for subcontracted work.']
  final_confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operation has received its final confirmation and no further time bookings are expected. True when operation is complete.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operation record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes.']
  latest_finish_date date [note: 'Latest date the operation must be completed to meet the work order required end date.']
  latest_start_date date [note: 'Latest date the operation must start to meet the work order required end date without delaying subsequent operations.']
  number_of_capacities varchar [note: 'Number of people or capacity units required to perform the operation simultaneously. Used for resource leveling and scheduling.']
  operation_long_text varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the operation including step-by-step instructions, safety precautions, and technical specifications required to complete the task.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Sequential operation number within the work order, typically a 4-digit numeric code (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030) that defines the execution sequence.']
  operation_short_text varchar [note: 'Brief description of the operation task, summarizing the work to be performed (e.g., \'Replace pump seal\', \'Calibrate pressure transmitter\').']
  planned_work_duration decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned labor hours required to complete the operation, excluding setup time. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.']
  planned_work_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for planned work duration. H=hours, MIN=minutes, D=days.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Urgency level for the operation. 1=Very High (immediate), 2=High (same day), 3=Medium (this week), 4=Low (this month), 5=Very Low (planned). Inherited from work order but can be overridden at operation level.']
  setup_time decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time required to prepare tools, equipment, and work area before the actual maintenance work can begin. Measured in the same unit as work duration.']
  standard_text_key varchar [note: 'Reference to standard text templates containing predefined operation instructions, safety procedures, or technical specifications that apply to this operation.']
  system_status varchar [note: 'Current system-controlled status of the operation in its lifecycle. System status is automatically set by SAP PM based on business transactions and controls which actions are allowed.']
  teardown_time decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time required to clean up, restore the work area, and return tools after operation completion. Measured in the same unit as work duration.']
  user_status varchar [note: 'User-defined status that provides additional business-specific status information beyond system status. Configurable per business requirements.']
  work_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quantity of work to be performed, expressed in operation-specific units (e.g., number of valves to inspect, meters of pipe to replace, number of calibrations).']
  work_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for work quantity (e.g., EA=each, M=meter, KG=kilogram). ISO unit codes.']

  Note: 'Individual operation (task step) within a maintenance work order, representing discrete work activities assigned to specific work centers and crafts. Captures operation number, description, work center, control key, planned and actual labor hours, setup time, execution factor, and confirmation status. Aligns with SAP PM Order Operation (AFVC) and supports detailed labor planning and execution tracking.'
}

Table maintenance.notification {
  notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for notification']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Customer‑impact notifications must be tied to the responsible account for SLA tracking and regulatory incident reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Notifications trigger maintenance actions that generate costs; linking to cost center ensures proper cost capture in the notification‑to‑work‑order flow.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator, inspector, or technician who created the notification. Supports accountability and follow-up communication.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment, reactor, vessel, pump, or instrumentation asset that is the subject of this notification.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location (plant area, process unit, or system) where the equipment or asset is installed.']
  notification_activity_code_maintenance_catalog_catalog_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the catalog code describing the corrective activity or repair action required (e.g., replace seal, recalibrate instrument, clean filter).']
  notification_catalog_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the catalog code describing the type of damage or defect observed (e.g., corrosion, wear, leakage, electrical failure). Supports Pareto analysis of failure modes.']
  notification_cause_code_maintenance_catalog_catalog_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the catalog code identifying the root cause of the failure (e.g., material fatigue, operator error, design flaw, inadequate maintenance). Critical for Root Cause Investigation (RCI) and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflows.']
  planner_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.planner_group. Business justification: Notification is planned by a planner group; replace string with FK.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Notification is tied to a plant; replace plant code with FK.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order created to address this notification. Establishes the notification-to-work-order linkage for tracking resolution.']
  breakdown_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the breakdown or malfunction in hours, calculated from breakdown start to end timestamps. Key metric for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and reliability reporting.']
  breakdown_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the equipment was restored to operational status. Used to calculate Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) and total downtime.']
  breakdown_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this notification represents a complete equipment breakdown (true) or a partial malfunction/defect (false). Critical for MTBF and reliability analysis.']
  breakdown_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the equipment breakdown or malfunction began. Used to calculate downtime duration and Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF).']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to the CAPA workflow document if a formal corrective action plan was created.']
  capa_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the notification requires a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflow to prevent recurrence.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the notification was closed and the issue was resolved. Used for cycle time analysis and notification aging reports.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the notification was completed. Supports time-to-resolve KPI calculation.']
  ehs_incident_number varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to an EHS incident report if the notification is related to a safety event, environmental release, or occupational health issue.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of how the equipment failed or the nature of the defect. Supports Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) analysis and failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA).']
  long_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the defect, malfunction, or activity request, including symptoms, observations, and any immediate actions taken by the reporter.']
  malfunction_start_date date [note: 'Date when the defect or malfunction was first observed, which may differ from the reported date if discovered retrospectively.']
  malfunction_start_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day when the malfunction started, in HH:MM format. Used in conjunction with malfunction_start_date for precise event timing.']
  moc_number varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to the MOC workflow document if a formal change review was triggered by this notification.']
  moc_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the corrective action requires a formal Management of Change (MOC) review due to process equipment modification or operating parameter change. Critical for PSM compliance.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the maintenance notification, typically a 10-12 digit system-generated number used for tracking and communication.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the notification: outstanding (newly created), in_progress (under investigation or work order created), completed (resolved), cancelled (no action required).']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the notification: M1 (Malfunction/Breakdown), M2 (Activity Request), M3 (Inspection Finding), M4 (Other). Determines workflow and priority handling.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency and impact: critical (immediate safety or production risk), high (significant impact), medium (moderate impact), low (minor issue).']
  production_impact_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the malfunction or breakdown caused a production stoppage or throughput reduction (true) or had no production impact (false).']
  production_loss_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Estimated quantity of product not produced due to the equipment breakdown, measured in the primary unit of measure for the affected production line (e.g., metric tons, liters, kilograms).']
  production_loss_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the production loss quantity: MT (metric tons), KG (kilograms), L (liters), GAL (gallons), LB (pounds).']
  rci_number varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to the RCI or RCA investigation document if a formal root cause analysis was initiated.']
  rci_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the failure severity or recurrence pattern requires a formal Root Cause Investigation (RCI) or Root Cause Analysis (RCA).']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date when the notification was created in the system. Used for response time analysis and notification aging reports.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the notification was created. Supports time-to-respond KPI calculation and audit trail.']
  required_end_date date [note: 'Target date by which the issue must be resolved to avoid production impact or regulatory non-compliance.']
  required_start_date date [note: 'Target date by which corrective action or work order execution should begin, based on priority and production schedule impact.']
  safety_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the notification involves a safety hazard, near-miss, or condition that could lead to injury or environmental release. Triggers Process Safety Management (PSM) review.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the notification issue, typically 40-80 characters, used for list views and quick identification.']
  work_order_created_date date [note: 'Date when the work order was created in response to this notification. Used to calculate notification-to-work-order response time.']

  Note: 'Maintenance notification record capturing all unplanned maintenance demand including equipment defects, malfunctions, breakdowns, activity requests, and condition-based alerts raised by operators, inspectors, or automated monitoring systems. Tracks notification type (M1 malfunction/breakdown, M2 activity request, M3 inspection finding), breakdown indicator with start/end timestamps and duration, reported damage/cause/activity codes (via catalog_code reference), failure mode classification, production impact and loss quantity, safety implications, time to restore, and notification-to-work-order linkage. Serves as the SSOT for all failure events including full breakdowns — consolidating failure recording into a single entry point. Supports MTBF/MTTR calculation, reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis, RCI/RCA investigations, CAPA workflows, PSM incident reporting, and Pareto analysis of failure modes.'
}

Table maintenance.pm_plan {
  pm_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pm_plan']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Preventive maintenance plans are often part of a service contract with a specific customer account; needed for contract compliance dashboards.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center that bears the financial burden of maintenance activities generated by this plan. Used for maintenance cost allocation and budgeting.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment master record (reactor, vessel, pump, compressor, heat exchanger, instrumentation) covered by this PM plan.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location (plant area, process unit, production line) where the PM plan applies. Alternative to equipment_id for location-based maintenance.']
  maintenance_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance strategy defining cycle packages and intervals (e.g., 3-month inspection, 6-month service, 12-month overhaul, 5-year major turnaround). Used for strategy_plan type.']
  planner_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance planner group responsible for scheduling, coordinating, and managing the PM plan execution.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where the PM plan is executed. Supports multi-site maintenance management.']
  rbi_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RBI assessment that optimized the inspection interval for this PM plan based on probability of failure and consequence of failure analysis. Supports API 580/581 RBI methodology.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Preventive maintenance plans are aligned with pricing strategies to ensure maintenance cost recovery is reflected in product pricing.']
  task_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the task list (general maintenance task list, equipment task list, functional location task list) defining the standardized work procedures, operations, materials, and labor for this PM plan.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center (mechanical shop, electrical shop, instrumentation shop, contractor crew) responsible for executing the PM activities.']
  auto_generate_work_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the system automatically generates work orders when maintenance calls are created, or whether manual planner review and approval is required before work order creation.']
  call_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance that the system generates maintenance call objects (schedule lines) before the planned date. Enables proactive work order creation and resource planning.']
  counter_reading_at_last_maintenance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Performance counter value (operating hours, production cycles, runtime hours) recorded at the time of last maintenance completion. Used for counter-based scheduling.']
  counter_threshold_for_next_maintenance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Performance counter value that triggers the next maintenance call for counter-based plans (e.g., next PM at 10,000 operating hours).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PM plan master record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_length decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value defining the interval between maintenance activities in the specified cycle_unit (e.g., 90 days, 500 operating hours, 1000 production cycles).']
  cycle_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance cycle interval: day, week, month, year (time-based), operating_hour, runtime_hour (performance-based), production_cycle, batch_count (counter-based). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: day|week|month|year|operating_hour|production_cycle|batch_count|runtime_hour — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  end_date date [note: 'Effective end date when the PM plan expires and stops generating maintenance calls. Nullable for indefinite plans. Used for equipment decommissioning or plan retirement.']
  inspection_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulatory inspection requirement driving this PM plan (API 510 pressure vessel inspection, API 570 piping inspection, API 653 tank inspection, ASME boiler inspection, PSM mechanical integrity inspection). Links to compliance frameworks.']
