  insurance_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM plan is mandated by insurance carrier requirements for boilers, pressure vessels, or other insured equipment. Compliance affects insurance coverage.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the last preventive maintenance activity was completed under this plan. Used to calculate the next scheduled maintenance date based on cycle length.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to this PM plan require formal MOC approval per PSM regulations. True for safety-critical equipment and process safety-related maintenance.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the maintenance planner or engineer who last modified the PM plan master record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PM plan master record was last modified in the system.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Calculated date for the next preventive maintenance activity based on cycle length, last maintenance date, and scheduling parameters. Drives work order generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes capturing planner comments, historical context, lessons learned, or special considerations for plan execution. Supports knowledge retention.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the PM plan, externally visible and used in work order generation and scheduling communications.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PM plan: active (generating calls), inactive (not generating calls), suspended (temporarily paused), completed (end date reached), cancelled (permanently terminated).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PM plan structure: single_cycle (one maintenance cycle), strategy_plan (multiple cycles with different intervals), multiple_counter (based on operating hours or production volume), time_based (calendar-driven), performance_based (triggered by performance degradation), condition_based (triggered by sensor readings or inspection results).']
  pm_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the PM plan purpose, scope, and maintenance objectives. Includes equipment context, maintenance rationale, and special instructions.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level for maintenance activities generated by this plan. Critical for safety-critical equipment (PSM), high for production-critical assets, medium for supporting equipment, low for non-critical assets.']
  scheduling_indicator varchar [note: 'Defines how the PM plan generates maintenance calls: time_based (calendar intervals), performance_based (operating hours, cycles, production volume), condition_based (sensor thresholds, inspection findings), fixed_date (specific calendar dates), annual (yearly recurrence), seasonal (tied to production seasons or weather).']
  scheduling_tolerance_after_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the planned date that maintenance can be performed without resetting the cycle counter. Provides flexibility for delayed execution.']
  scheduling_tolerance_before_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the planned date that maintenance can be performed without resetting the cycle counter. Provides flexibility for early execution.']
  seasonal_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PM plan schedule is adjusted for seasonal production patterns (e.g., agricultural chemical production peaks, winter shutdown periods, summer turnaround windows).']
  seasonal_adjustment_months varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of month numbers (1-12) when seasonal adjustments apply to the PM schedule. Used in conjunction with seasonal_adjustment_flag.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when the PM plan becomes active and begins generating maintenance calls. Aligns with equipment commissioning or plan activation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the maintenance planner or engineer who created the PM plan master record.']

  Note: 'Preventive maintenance plan master record defining the scheduling strategy, cycle packages, and parameters for time-based, counter-based, or condition-based PM activities on equipment or functional locations. Captures plan type (single cycle, strategy, multiple counter), strategy cycles and packages (e.g., 3M/6M/12M/5Y), scheduling indicator, call horizon, tolerance window, task list references for work content definition, cycle set definitions, and seasonal scheduling adjustments. Governs automated generation of schedule calls and resulting work orders. References task_list for standardized work procedures. Supports PSM mechanical integrity inspection programs, API/ASME code-required inspection intervals (API 510, 570, 653), RBI risk-based inspection optimization, and insurance carrier PM requirements.'
}

Table maintenance.schedule_call {
  schedule_call_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for schedule_call']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment or functional location that this scheduled maintenance call is for (reactor, vessel, pump, instrumentation, etc.).']
  planner_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.planner_group. Business justification: Schedule call is assigned to a planner group; replace string with FK.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Schedule call occurs at a plant; replace plant code string with FK.']
  pm_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent preventive maintenance plan that generated this schedule call item.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (maintenance crew, technician group) assigned to execute this maintenance call.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order that was generated from this schedule call item to execute the maintenance activity.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when the maintenance work was completed. Null if not yet completed.']
  call_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule call item was automatically generated by the maintenance planning system.']
  call_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance that this call was generated before the scheduled date, based on the maintenance plan call horizon configuration.']
  call_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the scheduled maintenance call, typically a sequential number within the maintenance plan.']
  call_object_type varchar [note: 'Type of technical object that this maintenance call is scheduled for.']
  call_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheduled maintenance call item.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description if this scheduled maintenance call was cancelled (equipment decommissioned, plan revised, consolidated with other work, etc.).']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance requirement that mandates this maintenance activity (PSM, EPA, OSHA, ISO certification, etc.).']
  counter_reading_at_call decimal(18,3) [note: 'The equipment counter reading (operating hours, cycles, production volume) at the time this maintenance call was generated, for performance-based maintenance scheduling.']
  counter_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the counter reading (hours, cycles, kilometers, batches, etc.).']
  cycle_number varchar [note: 'Sequential cycle number within the maintenance plan, indicating which iteration of the recurring maintenance this call represents.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of days this maintenance call is overdue beyond the tolerance window. Null or zero if not overdue.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated time in hours required to complete the scheduled maintenance activity.']
  is_overdue boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this scheduled maintenance call is past its due date and tolerance window.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule call record was last updated (status change, date adjustment, work order assignment, etc.).']
  maintenance_activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity to be performed (inspection, lubrication, calibration, overhaul, cleaning, etc.).']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated MOC workflow if this maintenance involves process equipment changes.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a Management of Change workflow is required for this maintenance activity due to process equipment modifications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this scheduled maintenance call, including special instructions, coordination requirements, or planning remarks.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'The planned date by which the maintenance work should be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The planned start date for executing the maintenance work, may differ from scheduled date due to planning adjustments.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this scheduled maintenance call, indicating urgency and business criticality.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this maintenance call is for PSM-critical equipment requiring mechanical integrity compliance per OSHA PSM regulations.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date when this preventive maintenance call is scheduled to be performed, calculated from the maintenance plan cycle.']
  shutdown_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether equipment or process shutdown is required to perform this maintenance activity.']
  tolerance_days_early varchar [note: 'Number of days before the scheduled date that the maintenance can be performed and still be considered on-time.']
  tolerance_days_late varchar [note: 'Number of days after the scheduled date that the maintenance can be performed before being considered overdue.']

  Note: 'Individual scheduled maintenance call item generated from a preventive maintenance plan, representing a specific due date and call object for a PM activity. Tracks call date, planned date, completion date, call status, counter reading at call, and the resulting work order reference. Aligns with SAP PM Maintenance Schedule (MPOS) and enables PM compliance tracking and overdue analysis.'
}

Table maintenance.measurement_point {
  measurement_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the measurement point record. Primary key.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment or functional location on which this measurement point is defined. Links to the equipment master record.']
  market_price_index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.market_price_index. Business justification: Raw‑material measurement points are tied to market price indices for dynamic cost adjustments in pricing calculations.']
  planner_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.planner_group. Business justification: Measurement point is managed by a planner group; replace string with FK.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center responsible for monitoring and maintaining this measurement point. Links to work center master data.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement point was activated and monitoring began. Used for historical analysis and compliance documentation.']
  calibration_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval in days between calibration activities for this measurement point. Supports compliance with ISO 9001 and GMP requirements.']
  calibration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point requires periodic calibration to maintain accuracy and compliance with quality and safety standards.']
  characteristic_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical characteristic being measured (e.g., vibration, temperature, pressure, flow rate, pH, operating hours). Aligns with plant instrumentation standards.']
  characteristic_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the characteristic being measured at this point.']
  counter_overflow_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum value at which a counter-type measurement point resets to zero. Used for cumulative counters such as operating hours or cycle counts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement point record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  criticality_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the measurement points importance to safety, environmental compliance, product quality, or equipment reliability. Drives prioritization of monitoring and maintenance activities.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or method by which measurement readings are captured: manual entry, Distributed Control System (DCS), Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA), Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), or process historian.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement point was deactivated or decommissioned. Null for active measurement points.']
  decimal_places varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places to which measurement values should be recorded and displayed, ensuring precision consistency.']
  historian_tag varchar [note: 'Tag identifier in the process historian system (e.g., Aveva PI System) that stores time-series data for this measurement point. Enables integration with real-time process data.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement point or associated instrument was last calibrated. Used to track calibration compliance and schedule next calibration.']
  last_reading_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent measurement reading was recorded for this point. Used to track compliance with reading schedules.']
  last_reading_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The most recent measurement value recorded at this point. Provides current state for condition monitoring and trend analysis.']
  lower_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower critical threshold that triggers an alarm and may require immediate maintenance action or equipment shutdown per Process Safety Management (PSM) requirements.']
  lower_warning_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower threshold value that triggers a warning alert when measurement falls below this level. Part of condition-based maintenance strategy.']
  measurement_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the measurement point. Active points are monitored per schedule; inactive or decommissioned points are no longer in use.']
  measurement_position varchar [note: 'Specific physical location or position on the equipment where the measurement is taken (e.g., bearing housing north side, inlet flange, discharge line). References Piping and Instrumentation Diagram (P&ID) tag.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to this measurement point (limits, frequency, sensor type) require formal Management of Change (MOC) approval per PSM requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement point record was last modified. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration activity based on calibration frequency. Critical for maintaining measurement accuracy and regulatory compliance.']
  next_reading_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next measurement reading based on the reading frequency. Used for work order generation and compliance tracking.']
  oee_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point contributes to Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculations, such as operating hours, cycle counts, or production rates.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the measurement point is located. Supports multi-site maintenance management.']
  point_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the measurement point type: counter (cumulative readings such as operating hours) or measurement (instantaneous readings such as temperature or pressure).']
  point_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the measurement point, including its physical location on the equipment and what is being measured.']
  point_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the measurement point, typically alphanumeric code used in operations and maintenance documentation.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is critical to Process Safety Management (PSM) mechanical integrity requirements and must be monitored per OSHA PSM regulations.']
  reading_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval in days between successive readings of this measurement point. Supports preventive and condition-based maintenance scheduling.']
  sensor_type varchar [note: 'Type of sensor or instrument used to capture the measurement (e.g., thermocouple, pressure transmitter, vibration sensor, flow meter, pH probe).']
  tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique tag identifier from the Piping and Instrumentation Diagram (P&ID) for the instrument or sensor associated with this measurement point. Aligns with ISA standards.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The ideal or expected value for this measurement point under normal operating conditions. Used for variance analysis and condition monitoring.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the measurement is recorded (e.g., degC, bar, ppm, hours, rpm, m3/h). Must align with ISO and industry standards.']
  upper_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper critical threshold that triggers an alarm and may require immediate maintenance action or equipment shutdown per Process Safety Management (PSM) requirements.']
  upper_warning_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper threshold value that triggers a warning alert when measurement exceeds this level. Part of condition-based maintenance strategy.']

  Note: 'Measurement point master record defining a specific location on equipment where counter readings or measurement values are recorded (e.g., vibration, temperature, pressure, operating hours). Captures measurement point category, characteristic, unit of measure, target value, upper/lower limits, and counter overflow value. Aligns with SAP PM Measurement Point (MPLA/IMRG) and supports condition-based maintenance and OEE tracking.'
}

Table maintenance.measurement_reading {
  measurement_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the measurement reading record. Primary key for the measurement reading entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that captured the measurement reading. For manual readings, this is the employee ID; for automatic readings, this is the sensor or SCADA system identifier.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment asset on which the measurement was taken. Links to equipment master data for reactors, vessels, pumps, instrumentation, and other process equipment.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location in the plant hierarchy where the measurement was taken. Supports spatial organization of measurement data.']
  measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the measurement point on the equipment where this reading was captured. Links to the measurement point master data defining the characteristic being measured.']
  notification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance notification that was automatically generated when this reading violated threshold limits. Supports condition-based maintenance triggering.']
  pm_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance plan that scheduled this measurement reading. Links measurement data to planned maintenance strategies.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order that triggered or resulted from this measurement reading. Links measurement data to maintenance execution.']
  annotation varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the reader or reviewer regarding the measurement reading. Used to document unusual conditions, context, or corrective actions taken.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved or validated this measurement reading. Used for quality assurance and compliance documentation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this measurement reading was approved or validated. Supports audit trail and regulatory compliance requirements.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'The date when the measurement instrument or sensor is next due for calibration. Used to assess the reliability of the reading and trigger calibration work orders.']
  counter_overflow_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that is true when a counter reading has rolled over or reset. Used to maintain accurate cumulative totals across counter resets.']
  counter_reading_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that is true when this reading represents a cumulative counter value (such as operating hours, cycle count, production volume) rather than an instantaneous measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this measurement reading record was first created in the system. Represents the system capture time, which may differ from the actual reading timestamp.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The system or method from which the measurement reading originated. Identifies whether the reading came from SCADA, DCS, Aveva PI System, LIMS, manual field entry, mobile application, or sensor network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SCADA|DCS|PI_System|LIMS|Manual_Entry|Mobile_App|Sensor_Network — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deviation_from_target decimal(18,6) [note: 'The calculated difference between the measured value and the target value. Positive values indicate readings above target, negative values indicate readings below target. Used for trend analysis and condition monitoring.']
  deviation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage deviation of the measured value from the target value. Calculated as (measured_value - target_value) / target_value * 100. Enables normalized comparison across different measurement points.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'The date when the measurement instrument or sensor was last calibrated. Provides context for reading accuracy and quality assurance.']
  lower_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower critical threshold value that triggers an alarm when the measured value falls below this limit. Indicates a serious condition requiring immediate attention.']
  lower_warning_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower threshold value that triggers a warning alert when the measured value falls below this limit. Part of condition monitoring and predictive maintenance logic.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual numeric value captured by the measurement reading. Represents the observed quantity such as temperature, pressure, flow rate, vibration, pH, or counter reading.']
  measurement_characteristic varchar [note: 'The physical or operational characteristic being measured. Examples include temperature, pressure, vibration, flow rate, pH, conductivity, viscosity, level, speed, power consumption.']
  measurement_document_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the measurement document. Used for traceability and cross-system reference.']
  measurement_position varchar [note: 'The specific position or location on the equipment where the measurement was taken. Examples include inlet, outlet, bearing housing, motor winding, seal area, valve stem.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this measurement reading record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data quality tracking.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The Aveva PI System tag name associated with this measurement reading. Enables integration and traceability between the maintenance system and the real-time process historian.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or facility where the measurement reading was captured. Supports multi-site maintenance operations and reporting.']
  quality_code varchar [note: 'Data quality indicator for the measurement reading. Good indicates reliable data, bad indicates known error, uncertain indicates possible error, substituted indicates estimated value, questionable indicates requires review.']
  reading_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the measurement reading was taken. Used for day-level aggregation and reporting.']
  reading_status varchar [note: 'Current validation status of the measurement reading. Indicates whether the reading has been reviewed, approved, or flagged for quality issues.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement reading was actually captured in the field or by the sensor. Represents the real-world event time of the observation.']
  reading_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the reading was obtained. Actual indicates direct measurement, estimated indicates interpolated or approximated value, calculated indicates derived from other readings, manual indicates human-entered, automatic indicates sensor-captured.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The expected or target value for this measurement point. Used to calculate deviation and trigger alerts when actual readings fall outside acceptable range.']
  threshold_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that is true when the measured value falls outside the defined warning or alarm limits. Used to trigger maintenance notifications and condition-based maintenance workflows.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value is expressed. Examples include PSI (pressure), DEG_C (temperature), GPM (flow), HZ (frequency), PPM (concentration), PH (acidity).']
  upper_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper critical threshold value that triggers an alarm when the measured value exceeds this limit. Indicates a serious condition requiring immediate attention.']
  upper_warning_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper threshold value that triggers a warning alert when the measured value exceeds this limit. Part of condition monitoring and predictive maintenance logic.']

  Note: 'Individual measurement document recording an actual counter or measurement value captured at a measurement point on equipment. Tracks reading date and time, measured value, unit, reading type (actual, estimated), reader identification, and deviation from target. Aligns with SAP PM Measurement Document (IMRG) and supports condition monitoring, predictive maintenance triggers, and Aveva PI System integration.'
}

Table maintenance.calibration_record {
  calibration_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the calibration record. Primary key for the calibration record entity.']
  calibration_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or work instruction used for this calibration. Links to documented calibration method.']
  calibration_standard_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reference standard or master instrument used for calibration. Ensures traceability to national or international standards.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Calibration activities incur costs that must be posted to the responsible cost center for compliance and cost recovery.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment or instrument being calibrated (transmitters, analyzers, gauges, safety valves, laboratory instruments).']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location where the calibrated equipment is installed.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Calibration record is linked to a plant for traceability; replace plant code with FK.']
  primary_calibration_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the technician or calibration specialist who performed the calibration. Required for accountability and competency tracking.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality assurance reviewer who verified and approved the calibration record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to external calibration service provider or laboratory, if calibration was performed by third party.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order under which this calibration was performed, linking calibration to preventive maintenance schedule.']
  accuracy_specification varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or regulatory accuracy requirement for the instrument (e.g., ±0.5%, ±2 degC). Used to determine tolerance limits.']
  adjustment_performed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument required adjustment or recalibration to bring it within tolerance.']
  as_found_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Measured value of the instrument before calibration adjustment. Documents initial condition and drift since last calibration.']
  as_left_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Measured value of the instrument after calibration adjustment. Documents final calibrated state.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number issued for this calibration event. External business identifier for traceability and audit purposes.']
  calibration_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of calibration activity including labor, materials, and external service fees. Supports maintenance budget tracking.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the calibration was performed. Principal business event timestamp for this calibration record.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled calibration date based on calibration interval. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard calibration frequency in days (e.g., 365 for annual, 180 for semi-annual). Determines next due date calculation.']
  calibration_points_tested varchar [note: 'Number of measurement points tested during calibration (e.g., 5-point calibration across instrument range).']
  calibration_standard_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number of the reference standard used, providing traceability to NIST or equivalent national metrology institute.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calibration record: scheduled (planned but not started), in-progress (calibration underway), passed (within tolerance), failed (out of tolerance), conditional (marginal pass with restrictions), cancelled (calibration not performed), overdue (past due date). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in-progress|passed|failed|conditional|cancelled|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  calibration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of calibration method: in-house (performed by internal technicians), external (third-party calibration lab), vendor (OEM calibration service), field (on-site calibration), laboratory (off-site lab calibration).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this calibration record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  environmental_humidity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage during calibration. Environmental condition documentation required for measurement traceability.']
  environmental_temperature decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature during calibration in degrees Celsius. Environmental condition documentation required for measurement traceability.']
  gmp_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this calibration is subject to Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements for pharmaceutical or food-contact chemical production.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this calibration record was last updated. Audit trail for record lifecycle and change tracking.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantified uncertainty of the calibration measurement, expressed in the same unit as the measured value. Required for ISO 17025 compliance.']
  out_of_tolerance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the as-found condition was outside acceptable tolerance limits, triggering investigation and potential product impact assessment.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Calibration result: pass (within tolerance), fail (out of tolerance), conditional (marginal but acceptable with restrictions).']
  psm_critical_instrument_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this instrument is designated as PSM-critical under OSHA 1910.119 mechanical integrity requirements, requiring enhanced calibration documentation and frequency.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting calibration observations, issues encountered, corrective actions taken, or special conditions. Supports root cause investigation and continuous improvement.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the calibration record was reviewed and approved by quality assurance.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Expected or nominal value for the calibration point. Reference setpoint for comparison.']
  technician_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification or qualification number of the technician performing calibration, demonstrating competency for this calibration type.']
  tolerance_lower_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for calibration pass criteria. Defines lower bound of acceptable measurement range.']
  tolerance_upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for calibration pass criteria. Defines upper bound of acceptable measurement range.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit for calibration values (e.g., PSI, bar, degC, degF, pH, mA, mV, GPM, kg/h). Applies to as-found, as-left, target, and tolerance values.']
  vendor_lab_accreditation varchar [note: 'Accreditation body and certificate number for external calibration laboratory (e.g., A2LA, ANAB, UKAS). Ensures vendor competency.']

  Note: 'Calibration record for instrumentation and measurement devices (transmitters, analyzers, gauges, safety valves) documenting as-found and as-left conditions, calibration procedure, tolerance limits, pass/fail status, calibration interval, and next due date. Supports PSM mechanical integrity requirements, ISO 9001 instrument calibration compliance, and LIMS integration for laboratory instrument calibration.'
}

Table maintenance.asset_change_request {
  asset_change_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Management of Change (MOC) request record. Primary key for the asset change request entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: MOC requests are budgeted and expensed; assigning a cost center enables tracking of change‑related expenditures.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Primary equipment identifier for the asset being modified or affected by the change request.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the functional location within the plant hierarchy where the change will occur, used for asset management and maintenance planning.']
  management_of_change_id varchar [note: 'FK to ehs.management_of_change.management_of_change_id — Asset/equipment change requests must link to master MOC record for OSHA PSM 1910.119 compliance. Enables unified MOC tracking across all change types.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Asset change request (MOC) is associated with a plant; replace code with FK.']
  primary_asset_initiator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who originated and submitted the MOC request.']
  tertiary_asset_closed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who formally closed the MOC after verifying all requirements were met.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when all implementation activities were completed and the change was fully operational.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when implementation activities commenced.']
  affected_equipment_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or narrative description of all equipment, vessels, reactors, pumps, and instrumentation affected by the proposed change.']
  affected_pid_list varchar [note: 'List of P&ID drawing numbers that will require revision as a result of the proposed change.']
  approval_required_roles varchar [note: 'List of organizational roles or positions required to approve the MOC before implementation can proceed (e.g., Plant Manager, EHS Manager, Process Engineer, Maintenance Manager).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the approval workflow indicating whether all required approvals have been obtained.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Budget or cost center code to which the change implementation costs will be charged.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the change costs as capital expenditure, operating expenditure, or a combination of both.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the proposed change including scope, rationale, technical specifications, and expected outcomes.']
  change_justification varchar [note: 'Business and technical justification for the proposed change including safety improvements, efficiency gains, regulatory compliance, or operational necessity.']
  change_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the proposed change for quick identification and reference.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of change being requested: equipment modification, piping alteration, instrumentation upgrade, chemical substitution, procedure revision, or operating condition adjustment.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the MOC was formally closed after all implementation activities, PSSR, training, documentation updates, and post-implementation review were completed.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MOC request record was first created in the system.']
  environmental_impact_description varchar [note: 'Assessment of environmental impacts including emissions changes, waste generation, spill potential, and regulatory compliance implications.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost for implementing the change including materials, labor, engineering, downtime, and contractor expenses.']
  final_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final approval was granted and the MOC was authorized for implementation.']
  implementation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan describing how the change will be executed including sequence of activities, resource requirements, safety precautions, and coordination needs.']
  initiator_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area of the initiating employee, used for routing and accountability.']
  initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who initiated the MOC request for reference and communication purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MOC request record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  moc_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the MOC request, externally visible and used for tracking and reference across systems and documentation.']
  moc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MOC request from initiation through closure. Tracks workflow progression through review, approval, implementation, and PSSR completion stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|implementation|pssr_pending|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  operational_impact_description varchar [note: 'Description of impacts on production operations including downtime requirements, capacity changes, quality effects, and process performance.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for completing all implementation activities and closing out the MOC.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Target date for beginning implementation of the approved change.']
  post_implementation_review_date date [note: 'Date when the post-implementation review was conducted.']
  post_implementation_review_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings from the post-implementation review including effectiveness assessment, lessons learned, and any corrective actions required.']
  post_implementation_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal post-implementation review is required to verify the change achieved its objectives and did not introduce unintended consequences.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the change request based on safety impact, operational urgency, regulatory deadlines, or strategic importance.']
  psm_covered_process_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change affects a process covered under OSHA PSM regulations (29 CFR 1910.119), requiring enhanced review and documentation.']
  pssr_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the PSSR was completed and the equipment or process was cleared for startup.']
  pssr_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Pre-Startup Safety Review is required before the modified equipment or process can be returned to service, as mandated by OSHA PSM for covered processes.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when the MOC request is rejected, including specific concerns or deficiencies that must be addressed.']
  risk_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Type of risk assessment methodology applied: Process Hazard Analysis, Hazard and Operability Study, What-If Analysis, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis, Layer of Protection Analysis, or not required.']
  risk_assessment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal risk assessment (PHA, HAZOP, or similar) is required for this change based on its scope and potential impact.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level determined from the risk assessment, considering safety, environmental, and operational impacts.']
  safety_impact_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of potential safety impacts including hazards introduced, hazards eliminated, and mitigation measures required.']
  sop_update_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all required SOP revisions were completed and approved.']
  sop_update_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether existing SOPs must be revised to reflect the implemented change.']
  training_completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required employee training activities associated with the MOC implementation.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employee training is required as a result of the change to procedures, equipment operation, or safety protocols.']

  Note: 'Management of Change (MOC) request record for proposed changes to process equipment, piping, instrumentation, chemicals, procedures, or operating conditions. Captures change type, description, initiator, affected equipment and P&IDs, risk assessment, required approvals, action items with responsible parties and due dates, PSSR tasks, implementation status, and post-implementation review. Supports PSM MOC requirements (OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119) and integrates with EHS workflows. Tracks full MOC lifecycle from initiation through action item completion and closure.'
}

Table maintenance.breakdown_event {
  breakdown_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the breakdown event record. Primary key for the breakdown event entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Breakdown events incur repair and downtime costs; assigning a cost center is required for loss analysis and financial impact reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or operator who first reported the breakdown event to the maintenance system.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment asset that experienced the breakdown failure.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location where the breakdown occurred within the plant hierarchy.']
  planner_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.planner_group. Business justification: Breakdown event is managed by a planner group; replace string with FK.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Breakdown event occurs at a plant; replace plant code with FK.']
  pm_notification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PM notification that reported this breakdown event. Links to the maintenance notification system.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective work order created to address this breakdown event.']
  affected_production_unit varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production unit, process line, or manufacturing cell that was impacted by the equipment breakdown.']
  breakdown_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in hours from breakdown start to restoration. Used for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation and downtime analysis.']
  breakdown_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the equipment was restored to operational status following the breakdown event.']
  breakdown_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the breakdown event, typically auto-generated or manually assigned for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  breakdown_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the equipment breakdown or failure was first detected or occurred. Critical for MTBF and MTTR calculations.']
  breakdown_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breakdown event indicating progression from detection through resolution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the breakdown event record was first created in the maintenance management system.']
  damage_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of damage observed on the equipment, such as crack, deformation, erosion, or contamination.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the breakdown event was first detected, such as operator observation, automated alarm, scheduled inspection, or predictive analytics.']
  ehs_incident_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated EHS incident report if the breakdown event had safety or environmental implications.']
  environmental_release_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event resulted in an unplanned release of chemicals or hazardous materials to the environment.']
  estimated_repair_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost to repair or restore the equipment following the breakdown event, including labor, materials, and contractor costs.']
  failure_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the breakdown, such as wear, corrosion, operator error, design defect, or inadequate maintenance.']
  failure_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the root cause analysis findings for the breakdown event. Supports RCI (Root Cause Investigation) documentation.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Description of how the equipment failed, such as mechanical fracture, electrical short, seal leak, bearing seizure, or control system fault. Supports failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the breakdown event record was last updated or modified.']
  long_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the breakdown event, including symptoms, observations, and contextual information.']
  malfunction_start_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this breakdown event marks the initial onset of a malfunction or is a continuation of a previous issue.']
  moc_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the MOC workflow initiated to implement corrective actions for this breakdown event.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether corrective actions for this breakdown require a formal Management of Change process due to process safety implications.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority classification for the breakdown event response, determining urgency of corrective action and resource allocation.']
  production_impact_severity varchar [note: 'Categorical assessment of the severity of production impact caused by the breakdown, ranging from critical plant shutdown to minimal disruption.']
  production_loss_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantified amount of production output lost due to the breakdown event, measured in the unit of measure specified.']
  production_loss_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the production loss quantity, such as kilograms, liters, tons, or batch count.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event involved PSM-covered equipment requiring mechanical integrity documentation and regulatory reporting.']
  rci_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal root cause investigation conducted for this breakdown event.']
  rci_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event severity or recurrence pattern requires a formal root cause investigation.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the breakdown event was formally reported into the maintenance management system.']
  response_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in hours from breakdown detection to initiation of corrective action. Key metric for maintenance responsiveness.']
  restoration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to restore equipment to operational status, such as on-site repair, component replacement, temporary fix, or operational workaround.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event resulted in or was associated with a safety incident requiring EHS reporting.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief summary description of the breakdown event for quick identification and reporting purposes.']

  Note: 'Unplanned equipment breakdown or failure event record capturing the onset of failure, failure mode, failure cause, affected production unit, production loss quantity, safety implications, and time to restore. Tracks breakdown start and end timestamps, breakdown duration, malfunction start indicator, and linkage to corrective work order and PM notification. Supports MTBF/MTTR calculation, RCI investigations, and PSM incident reporting.'
}

Table maintenance.inspection_round {
  inspection_round_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection round record. Primary key for the inspection round entity.']
  checklist_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the structured checklist template used during this inspection round. Defines the inspection points, parameters, and acceptance criteria.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment, reactor, vessel, pump, or instrument inspected during this round.']
  follow_up_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary maintenance work order created to address findings from this inspection round. Links inspection findings to corrective maintenance actions.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location where the inspection round was performed. Represents the hierarchical location structure in the plant.']
  primary_inspection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or employee who performed the inspection round. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the predefined inspection route or path that defines the sequence of equipment and checkpoints to be inspected during this round.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center responsible for this inspection round. Identifies the organizational unit performing the inspection.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection round was completed. Used to calculate inspection duration and verify compliance with scheduled intervals.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspector actually began the inspection round. Captures the real-world start of the inspection activity.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection round results require formal approval by a supervisor, engineer, or regulatory authority before being finalized.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection round results were formally approved. Part of the audit trail for compliance documentation.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the inspection round was cancelled if status is cancelled. Captures reasons such as equipment unavailability, safety concerns, or rescheduling.']
  compliance_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory or statutory deadline by which this inspection must be completed to maintain compliance. Overdue inspections may result in regulatory violations or insurance coverage issues.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection round record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  deviations_found_count varchar [note: 'Number of deviations, anomalies, or out-of-specification conditions identified during the inspection round. Triggers follow-up actions and work orders.']
  highest_severity_level varchar [note: 'The most severe classification level among all deviations found during the inspection round. Critical findings require immediate action; high findings require urgent attention; medium and low findings are tracked for planned maintenance.']
  inspection_findings_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary text describing the overall findings, observations, and conclusions from the inspection round. Provides executive-level overview of inspection results.']
  inspection_interval_days varchar [note: 'Defined interval in days between successive inspection rounds for this equipment or route. Based on regulatory requirements, manufacturer recommendations, or risk-based inspection methodology.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection round. Tracks progression from scheduled through execution to completion or cancellation.']
  inspector_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for inspector comments, observations, and contextual information not captured in structured checklist fields. Provides qualitative insights and situational context.']
  inspector_qualification_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the qualification or certification level of the inspector performing the round. Ensures inspections are performed by appropriately trained personnel per OSHA PSM and API requirements.']
  insurance_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the insurance carrier for whom this inspection is performed or to whom results must be reported.']
  insurance_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection round is required by or performed for insurance carrier compliance. Insurance carriers often mandate periodic inspections of pressure vessels, boilers, and critical equipment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection round record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change for audit and data quality purposes.']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the MOC workflow initiated as a result of this inspection round findings. Links inspection to change management process.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether findings from this inspection round require a Management of Change (MOC) process before corrective actions can be implemented, per PSM requirements for process equipment modifications.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Calculated date for the next inspection round based on the inspection interval and completion date of this round. Used for forward planning and scheduling.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the inspection round was performed.']
  psm_mechanical_integrity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection round is part of the PSM mechanical integrity program required by OSHA for process equipment handling highly hazardous chemicals.']
  regulatory_requirement_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific regulatory or statutory requirement that mandates this inspection (e.g., OSHA PSM, API 510, API 570, API 653, insurance carrier requirement, local jurisdiction code).']
  round_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection round. Externally visible unique number used for tracking and reference in maintenance systems and compliance documentation.']
  round_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection round activity. Operator rounds are routine operational checks; predictive inspections use condition monitoring; statutory/regulatory inspections meet legal requirements; PSM mechanical integrity inspections support Process Safety Management programs; safety device inspections verify critical safety equipment.']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection round covers safety-critical equipment or systems that are subject to Process Safety Management (PSM) mechanical integrity requirements.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date when the inspection round is scheduled to be performed. Used for planning and resource allocation.']
  total_checkpoints_completed varchar [note: 'Actual number of inspection checkpoints completed during the round. Used to track inspection progress and completeness.']
  total_checkpoints_planned varchar [note: 'Total number of inspection checkpoints or measurement points planned for this round based on the route and checklist definition.']
  work_order_generated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more maintenance work orders were automatically generated as a result of deviations or findings from this inspection round.']

  Note: 'Operator or maintenance inspection round record capturing structured field inspection activities performed on equipment, instruments, safety devices, and process systems at defined intervals. Tracks round type (operator rounds, predictive inspection, statutory/regulatory inspection, PSM mechanical integrity inspection), route definition, inspection date/time, inspector qualification, checklist findings, measured values, deviations noted, severity classification, and follow-up action status with work order generation. Distinct from calibration_record (which covers instrument accuracy verification) and from PM schedule_call (which triggers full maintenance work orders). Supports PSM mechanical integrity programs, OSHA Process Safety compliance, API 510/570/653 inspection documentation, and insurance carrier inspection requirements.'
}

Table maintenance.permit_to_work {
  permit_to_work_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the permit to work record. Primary key for the permit to work entity.']
  alt5_permit_attendant_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the person assigned as confined space attendant who remains outside the space to monitor entrants and initiate rescue if needed.']
  alt5_permit_closed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authorized person who closed the permit after verifying work completion and area safety.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Permit issuance often includes fees and resource allocation; linking to cost center supports permit cost accounting and audit trails.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment or asset on which the permitted work will be performed.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location where the permitted work will be performed. Identifies the specific plant area or process unit.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Permit to work is issued for a specific plant; replace plant code with FK.']
  primary_permit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authorized person who issued and approved the permit. Must be a qualified individual with authority to authorize work in hazardous areas.']
  quaternary_permit_safety_officer_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Environment Health and Safety (EHS) officer who reviewed and approved the permit from a safety compliance perspective.']
  quinary_permit_fire_watch_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the person assigned as fire watch for hot work operations. Must be trained in fire prevention and emergency response.']
  tertiary_permit_area_authority_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operations supervisor or area owner who has authority over the work location and must approve work in their area.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center responsible for executing the permitted work.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order that this permit authorizes. Links permit to the underlying maintenance activity.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation if the permit was cancelled before work completion. Includes reason code and detailed justification.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit was formally closed after work completion and area inspection confirmed safe conditions restored.']
  closure_confirmation varchar [note: 'Documentation confirming that all work is complete, all tools and materials removed, all isolations restored, area cleaned, and safe conditions verified before permit closure.']
  confined_space_entry_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work involves entry into a permit-required confined space as defined by OSHA 1910.146. Triggers additional safety requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit record was first created in the system.']
  fire_watch_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a dedicated fire watch person must be present during hot work operations to monitor for ignition and respond to fire emergencies.']
  gas_test_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether atmospheric testing for oxygen, flammable gases, and toxic gases is required before and during the work.']
  gas_test_results varchar [note: 'Results of atmospheric testing including oxygen percentage, lower explosive limit (LEL) readings, and toxic gas concentrations. Recorded before work begins and at specified intervals.']
  hazard_identification varchar [note: 'Comprehensive identification of all hazards present in the work area including chemical, physical, biological, and process hazards. Critical for risk assessment and control measures.']
  isolation_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific isolation and lockout/tagout requirements for the work including electrical isolation, mechanical isolation, line blanking, and energy isolation procedures.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit was officially issued and approved by the issuing authority.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit record was last updated in the system.']
  lel_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured flammable gas concentration as percentage of lower explosive limit. Must be below threshold (typically <10% LEL) for hot work authorization.']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated Management of Change document if MOC review was required for this work.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work requires a Management of Change review because it involves modifications to process equipment, procedures, or chemicals.']
  oxygen_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured oxygen concentration in the work area atmosphere expressed as percentage. Must be within safe range (typically 19.5% to 23.5%) for entry.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique permit number assigned to this work authorization. Used for tracking and reference in safety documentation.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit to work. Tracks the permit from initial request through closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the permit based on the nature of hazardous work being authorized. Determines specific safety protocols and approval requirements.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Mandatory personal protective equipment required for the permitted work including respirators, protective clothing, gloves, eye protection, and fall protection.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level of the permitted work indicating urgency and scheduling precedence.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work is on PSM-covered equipment or processes that contain highly hazardous chemicals above threshold quantities.']
  rescue_plan varchar [note: 'Documented rescue and emergency response plan for confined space entry or other high-risk work. Includes rescue equipment, procedures, and emergency contacts.']
  shutdown_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work requires a process unit shutdown or can be performed during normal operations.']
  special_precautions varchar [note: 'Additional safety precautions, restrictions, or conditions specific to this permit beyond standard requirements. May include weather restrictions, simultaneous operations restrictions, or special monitoring requirements.']
  toxic_gas_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured concentration of toxic gases in parts per million. Must be below permissible exposure limits for the specific chemicals present.']
  valid_from_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit becomes effective and work is authorized to begin. Marks the start of the permits validity period.']
  valid_until_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit expires and work must cease. Permits typically have limited duration (e.g., 8 hours, 12 hours, or one shift) and must be renewed if work continues.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance or construction activity to be performed under this permit. Includes scope, methods, and expected outcomes.']

  Note: 'Permit to Work (PTW) record authorizing maintenance and construction activities in hazardous areas of a chemical plant. Captures permit type (hot work, confined space entry, electrical isolation, line breaking, excavation), issuing authority, work description, hazard identification, isolation requirements, PPE requirements, gas test results, validity period, and closure confirmation. Supports OSHA PSM, EHS compliance, and safe work practices.'
}

Table maintenance.catalog_code {
  catalog_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for catalog_code']
  api_689_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to API 689 (Collection and Exchange of Reliability and Maintenance Data for Equipment) standard. Used for alignment with American Petroleum Institute reliability standards.']
  capa_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this catalog code automatically triggers a CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) workflow for quality management and continuous improvement. Aligns with GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) and ISO 9001 requirements.']
  catalog_code_code varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric code within the catalog type and code group. This is the actual code value used in PM (Plant Maintenance) notifications and work orders for structured failure analysis. Example: CORR for corrosion, WEAR for wear, FRAC for fracture.']
  catalog_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the catalog code. Active codes are available for use in maintenance transactions. Deprecated codes are retained for historical reporting but not available for new entries.']
  catalog_type varchar [note: 'The type of catalog this code belongs to: damage codes (what failed), cause codes (why it failed), activity codes (what was done), object part codes (which component), or symptom codes (how failure manifested). Aligns with SAP PM catalog profile structure.']
  code_group varchar [note: 'Hierarchical grouping identifier for the catalog code. Enables multi-level classification and rollup reporting. For example, mechanical failures, electrical failures, instrumentation failures. Corresponds to SAP PM code group.']
  code_long_text varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the catalog code providing full definition, usage guidance, and examples. Used for training and reference documentation.']
  code_short_text varchar [note: 'Brief description of the catalog code, typically 20-40 characters. Used in dropdown lists and compact displays in maintenance transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this catalog code entry was created in the system. Used for audit trail and version control.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this catalog code is no longer valid for new transactions. Null for codes with no planned end date. Historical transactions retain the code for reporting continuity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this catalog code becomes valid and available for use in maintenance transactions. Supports versioned catalog management.']
  ehs_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failures coded with this catalog entry require EHS (Environment Health and Safety) incident reporting to regulatory authorities (EPA, OSHA, ECHA).']
  equipment_class varchar [note: 'The equipment class or category this catalog code is applicable to (e.g., rotating equipment, static equipment, instrumentation, electrical, piping). Enables filtering of relevant codes by equipment type.']
  estimated_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical equipment downtime duration in hours associated with this failure mode or activity code. Used for production impact planning and scheduling.']
  estimated_repair_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Typical repair cost in base currency associated with this failure mode or activity code. Used for maintenance budgeting and cost analysis.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The depth level in the catalog hierarchy. Level 1 is top-level, level 2 is sub-category, etc. Used for reporting aggregation and navigation.']
  iso_14224_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to ISO 14224 (Petroleum, petrochemical and natural gas industries - Collection and exchange of reliability and maintenance data for equipment) failure mode taxonomy. Enables benchmarking against industry standards.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code for the catalog code text. Supports multi-language catalog maintenance for global operations.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'The most recent date this catalog code was used in a maintenance transaction. Used to identify obsolete codes for catalog cleanup.']
  moc_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure mode or activity code automatically triggers a MOC (Management of Change) workflow for process equipment changes. Required for PSM mechanical integrity compliance.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified this catalog code entry. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this catalog code entry was last modified. Used for audit trail and version control.']
  parent_code_group varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent code group in the hierarchical catalog structure. Enables multi-level rollup and drill-down analysis. Null for top-level groups.']
  plant_section varchar [note: 'The plant section or process area this catalog code is primarily used in (e.g., reactor area, distillation, packaging, utilities). Supports location-specific failure analysis.']
  priority_indicator varchar [note: 'Default priority level associated with this catalog code. Used to auto-populate priority on PM notifications when this code is selected. Critical codes trigger immediate response.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this catalog code is associated with PSM (Process Safety Management) critical equipment or failure modes requiring special handling, documentation, and regulatory reporting per OSHA PSM requirements.']
  rci_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failures coded with this catalog entry mandate a formal RCI (Root Cause Investigation) or RCA (Root Cause Analysis) process. Typically set for critical or recurring failure modes.']
  sort_sequence varchar [note: 'Display order sequence number for this catalog code within its code group. Controls the presentation order in dropdown lists and reports.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this catalog code has been used in PM notifications and work orders. Used for Pareto analysis of failure modes and catalog optimization.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created this catalog code entry. Used for audit trail and data governance.']

  Note: 'Maintenance catalog code master defining the standardized taxonomy of damage codes, cause codes, activity codes, and object part codes used across PM notifications and work orders for structured failure analysis. Each catalog entry is a first-class reference entity with its own identity (catalog type + code group + code), versioned lifecycle, hierarchical grouping structure, and cross-references to industry failure mode standards (ISO 14224, API 689). Supports RCA/RCI investigations, Pareto analysis of failure modes, reliability reporting, and consistent coding across all maintenance transactions. Aligns with SAP PM Catalog Profile (T352/T353/T357).'
}

Table maintenance.task_list {
  task_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each maintenance task list template.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Task List Management process logs the employee who authored the task list for accountability and change control.']
  predecessor_task_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on task_list (predecessor_task_list_id)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the task list revision.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task list revision was approved.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for cost estimates.']
  craft_skill_required varchar [note: 'Skill or qualification code required for personnel performing the operation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task list record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the task list is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the task list is valid for use.']
  equipment_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of equipment for which the task list is applicable (e.g., reactor, pump).']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of labor cost for the task list.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total labor hours estimated for the entire task list.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the functional location scope when type is functional_location.']
  is_template boolean [note: 'True if the record represents a reusable template (always true for task_list).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the task list record.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent work order generation from this task list.']
  lockout_tagout_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lockout/tagout procedures are mandatory for the operation.']
  material_requirements varchar [note: 'List of materials and quantities needed for the operation.']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated MOC document, if applicable.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Management of Change (MOC) must be completed before execution.']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work to be performed in this step.']
  operation_step_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the operation within the task list.']
  planned_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated labor time required for the operation.']
  planned_hours_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for planned hours.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the task list for planning purposes.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'True if the task list is subject to PSM mechanical integrity requirements.']
  regulatory_requirement_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing applicable regulatory or compliance requirement (e.g., OSHA, EPA).']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for change control.']
  safety_precautions varchar [note: 'Safety instructions, PPE, and lockout/tagout notes for the operation.']
  task_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of maintenance activity the task list supports.']
  task_list_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code (e.g., PLKO) used in SAP PM to reference the task list.']
  task_list_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the purpose and scope of the task list.']
  task_list_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the task list used by planners and technicians.']
  task_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the task list.']
  task_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the task list: equipment‑specific, functional‑location‑specific, or general.']
  tool_requirements varchar [note: 'List of tools or equipment required to complete the operation.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the task list has been used to generate work orders.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center responsible for executing the operation.']

  Note: 'Reusable maintenance task list (job plan/procedure template) defining standardized sequences of operations, required materials, tools, safety precautions, and estimated labor for recurring maintenance activities. Serves as the master template from which work order operations are generated, ensuring consistent execution quality across crews and shifts. Captures task list type (equipment-specific, functional-location-specific, general), group/counter structure, operation steps with detailed descriptions, work center assignments, planned hours per operation, required craft qualifications, material/tool requirements with quantities, and safety/lockout-tagout instructions. Referenced by pm_plan for automated work order generation. Aligns with SAP PM Task List (IA01-IA03/PLKO) and supports PSM mechanical integrity procedure documentation, OSHA-required safe work procedures, craft standardization, and audit-ready maintenance procedure records.'
}

Table maintenance.equipment_allocation {
  equipment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the equipment_allocation association']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the equipment']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the shipment']
