  load_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment began loading the shipment']
  unload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment completed unloading the shipment']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of equipment to a shipment, capturing the load and unload timestamps for each equipment‑shipment pair. Each record links one shipment to one piece of equipment and stores attributes that belong only to the relationship.. Existence Justification: In the chemical plant, equipment such as trailers, containers, and loading devices is assigned to shipments for loading and unloading. A single shipment can involve multiple pieces of equipment, and each piece of equipment can be used on many shipments over time. The plant tracks load and unload timestamps for each equipment‑shipment pair, making the relationship an operational many‑to‑many entity.'
}

Table maintenance.calibration_procedure {

  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certificate issued after successful calibration.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval, in days, between required calibrations for equipment using this procedure.']
  calibration_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the calibration is performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration procedure record was created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the calibration procedure.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation or SOP for the procedure.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the calibration procedure becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the calibration procedure expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Category of equipment to which the procedure applies.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the procedure is mandatory for the equipment class.']
  last_calibrated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent calibration performed using this procedure.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the primary calibration value.']
  method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the calibration.']
  next_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next required calibration.']

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calibration procedure.']
  tolerance_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the tolerance value.']
  tolerance_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable deviation from the target value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calibration procedure record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the procedure definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for calibration_procedure. Referenced by calibration_procedure_id.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration standard record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context about the standard.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the calibration standard becomes valid for use.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the calibration standard must no longer be used (nullable).']
  instrument_category varchar [note: 'Category of equipment that uses this calibration standard.']
  is_primary_standard boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this standard is a primary reference (True) or a secondary reference (False).']
  last_calibrated_date date [note: 'Date when the standard was last used to calibrate equipment.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that produced the calibration standard.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the calibration standard (e.g., "Standard Temperature 100°C").']

  nominal_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'The target measurement value defined by the standard.']

  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the standard with respect to regulatory bodies.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the physical standard artifact, if applicable.']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Business code or catalogue number that uniquely identifies the standard within the organization.']
  standard_type varchar [note: 'Category of measurement the standard applies to.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the standard.']
  tolerance_minus decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable deviation below the nominal value.']
  tolerance_plus decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable deviation above the nominal value.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the nominal value and tolerances are expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calibration standard record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the calibration standard (e.g., "v2.1").']

  Note: 'Master reference table for calibration_standard. Referenced by calibration_standard_id.'
}

  city varchar [note: 'City where the plant is located.']
  code varchar [note: 'External business code or short identifier assigned to the plant.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier for budgeting and charge‑back.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the plant location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plant record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the plant was or will be retired from service (nullable).']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the plant, its purpose, and notable characteristics.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated emergency contact for the plant.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary environmental compliance permit.']

  is_certified_iso9001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds ISO 9001 quality certification.']
  is_certified_ohsas18001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant is certified to OHSAS 18001 occupational health and safety standard.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Most recent date a safety or regulatory inspection was performed.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the plant in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the plant in decimal degrees.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Approach used for maintaining plant equipment.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the plant manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the plant manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the plant manager.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average operating time between equipment failures.']
  mttr_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair equipment after a failure.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the plant used in reports and UI.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total staff employed at the plant.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of major equipment units (reactors, vessels, etc.) at the plant.']
  operating_company varchar [note: 'Legal entity that owns or operates the plant.']
  plant_area_sq_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total built‑up area of the plant in square metres.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the plant address.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the plant\'s regulatory compliance.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Overall safety performance rating of the plant.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the plant location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plant.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the plant based on its primary function.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plant record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plant. Referenced by plant_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for checklist_template. Referenced by checklist_template_id.'

Table maintenance.planner_group {
  planner_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planner_group']
  parent_planner_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planner_group (parent_planner_group_id)']
  primary_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the main plant or facility where the planner group operates.']
  average_experience_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean years of experience across all members of the planner group.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique business code assigned to the planner group for external reference.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code used for budgeting and charge‑back of the planner group\'s activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planner group record was initially created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the planner group\'s purpose and scope.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date when the planner group starts being valid for planning activities.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date when the planner group ceases to be valid; null if open‑ended.']
  is_shift_based boolean [note: 'True if the planner group operates on a shift schedule; otherwise false.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total count of individual planners assigned to the group.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the planner group used in maintenance planning.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern of work shifts assigned to the planner group.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the planner group.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planner group based on its functional focus.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the planner group record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the planner group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planner_group. Referenced by planner_group_id.'
}

Table maintenance.rbi_assessment {
  rbi_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rbi_assessment']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee identifier for the assessor.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset that the assessment evaluates.']
  prior_rbi_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rbi_assessment (prior_rbi_assessment_id)']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment was formally approved.']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code assigned to the assessment for tracking across systems.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the RBI assessment was performed.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method or standard used to conduct the RBI assessment.']
  assessment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the RBI assessment.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the assessment indicating its purpose or trigger.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the engineer or specialist who performed the RBI assessment.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations recorded by the assessor.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment record for data handling purposes.']
  consequence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the potential impact of a failure scenario.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  is_critical_equipment boolean [note: 'True if the assessed equipment is classified as critical to plant safety or production.']
  likelihood_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the probability of the failure scenario occurring.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next RBI reassessment of the same equipment.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Code or name of the plant/facility where the equipment resides.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that drives the assessment requirements.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric risk score derived from consequence and likelihood, expressed on a 0‑100 scale.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assessment.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of attached supporting documents (e.g., inspection reports, photos).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the assessment document.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for rbi_assessment. Referenced by rbi_assessment_id.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each cost center record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the manager accountable for the cost center.']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent cost center in the hierarchy.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenses recorded against the cost center for the fiscal year.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to allocate costs to the center.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned expense amount allocated to the cost center for the budget year.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the budget amounts apply.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category for cost allocation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used in accounting and reporting (e.g., "CC‑0012").']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context.']
  cost_center_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating position in multi‑level hierarchy.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center (e.g., "North Plant Operations").']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by organizational role.']
  cost_driver varchar [note: 'Primary driver used for allocating costs (e.g., labor hours, machine hours).']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the cost center operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for budgeting and reporting (e.g., "USD").']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center in external ERP or legacy systems.']
  group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for consolidated reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy (1 = top level).']
  is_capex boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center is used for capital expenditures.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Name of the department that owns the cost center.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier (e.g., "North America").']
  reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for statutory reporting and SOX audit trails.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the cost center is reviewed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the cost center record.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual spend (budget – actual).']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance calculated as (variance_amount / budget_amount) * 100.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective for accounting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center is retired or no longer valid (nullable).']

  Note: 'Master record for organizational cost centers used in management accounting and cost controlling. Represents a plant, department, or functional unit to which operating costs are allocated. Tracks hierarchy position, responsible manager, profit center assignment, currency, validity period, and budget ownership. Serves as the primary cost collection point for OpEx tracking, overhead allocation, and variance-to-budget reporting across chemical manufacturing facilities including production plants, R&D labs, and shared services.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the profit center master record.']

  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent profit center in the hierarchy (null for top‑level).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned total budget allocated to the profit center for the fiscal year.']
  capex_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Capital expenditure budget assigned to the profit center.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Controlling area to which the profit center belongs for cost accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profit center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for financial reporting of the profit center.']
  ebitda_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization) realized by the profit center.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the profit center becomes operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the profit center is retired or becomes inactive (null if open‑ended).']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the profit center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  is_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the profit center is included in statutory and management reporting.']
  opex_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Operating expenditure budget assigned to the profit center.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier (alphanumeric code) assigned to the profit center.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the profit center.']
  profit_center_group varchar [note: 'Grouping of profit centers for consolidated reporting.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the profit center used in reports and dashboards.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the profit center indicating its organizational role.']
  segment varchar [note: 'High‑level market segment to which the profit center belongs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the profit center record.']

  Note: 'Master record for profit centers representing business segments, product lines, or plant units for which profitability is measured independently. Tracks profit center hierarchy, controlling area, responsible manager, currency, segment classification, and validity dates. Supports EBITDA reporting, segment profitability analysis under FASB ASC 280, and statutory reporting for chemical product lines including specialty chemicals, performance materials, and agricultural solutions.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the GL account record.']
  account_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each GL account has an accountable accountant; linking provides ownership reporting and segregation of duties.']
  parent_account_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent account for hierarchical roll‑up.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping used for reporting and roll‑up (e.g., assets, liabilities, revenue, expense).']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the GL account.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'External account code used in financial postings and reporting.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Category indicating whether the account appears on the balance sheet, profit & loss statement, equity, or off‑balance sheet.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Controlling (CO) area identifier for cost accounting integration.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center linked to the account for cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GL account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code in which the account is denominated.']
  depreciation_key varchar [note: 'Key linking the account to a depreciation rule set.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the account becomes valid for posting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the account is retired; null if open‑ended.']
  financial_statement_line varchar [note: 'Line item name on the financial statement where the account is reported.']
  gl_account_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or usage of the account.']
  gl_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL account.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the account within the chart of accounts hierarchy.']
  is_budget_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is included in budgeting and variance analysis.']
  is_capitalized boolean [note: 'True if expenditures posted to this account are capitalized as assets.']
  is_consolidation_account boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account is used in legal entity consolidation.']
  is_currency_fixed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account’s currency is locked and cannot be changed.']
  is_depreciable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is used for depreciable assets.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'True if the account is designated for intercompany transactions.']
  is_legal_entity_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is tied to a specific legal entity.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'True if posting to the account is temporarily blocked.']
  is_reconciliation_account boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account is used for automatic reconciliation of sub‑ledger postings.']
  is_tax_relevant boolean [note: 'True if the account participates in tax reporting calculations.']
  posting_block varchar [note: 'Type of posting block applied to the account.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center to which the account is assigned for profitability reporting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment used for internal reporting and analysis.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Tax treatment applied to postings against this account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the GL account record.']

  Note: 'General ledger account master defining the chart of accounts structure for the chemical manufacturing enterprise. Includes account type (P&L, balance sheet), account group, reconciliation account flag, tax category, currency, controlling integration flag, and account description. Serves as the foundational classification for all financial postings, statutory reporting, trial balance generation, and SOX compliance across all legal entities.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the journal entry record.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business unit or division responsible for the entry.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the cost of the transaction is charged.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: SOX audit requires identifying the employee who created each journal entry for accountability.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Journal entries belong to a fiscal period; linking to the fiscal_period master enables period-based reporting and removes the redundant string field.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company code) that owns the transaction.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Profit center that receives the revenue or expense impact.']
  reversed_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original journal entry that is being reversed.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the journal entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction amount.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned to the journal entry for reference and audit.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the journal entry.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the journal entry indicating its business purpose (e.g., SA=Sales, AB=Asset, AR=Accounts Receivable).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the transaction currency to the corporate group currency.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the posting belongs.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value before tax, discounts, or adjustments.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the entry represents an intercompany transaction.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context for the posting.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle stage of the journal entry for compliance tracking.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount expressed in the legal entity\'s local currency.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount after tax and other adjustments.']
  party_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external party (customer, vendor, etc.) associated with the entry.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'Key that determines the posting type (debit/credit) and account type.']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Period code (e.g., "0010") indicating the posting window.']
  posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference text used by the posting program for traceability.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from an external system (e.g., purchase order, invoice).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this entry reverses a prior posting.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the journal entry.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining tax rate and jurisdiction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the business event that generated the journal entry occurred.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the journal entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the journal entry.']

  Note: 'Core general ledger posting document capturing all financial transactions across the chemical manufacturing enterprise. Includes document type, posting date, fiscal period, legal entity, reference number, currency, exchange rate, reversal indicator, and audit trail fields. Represents the authoritative record for all accounting events including accruals, reversals, intercompany eliminations, period-end adjustments, tax postings, and reclassifications. Subject to SOX controls, FASB retention requirements, and statutory audit trail obligations.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each journal entry line.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry line should reference the GL account via FK for accurate posting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry header.']
  amount_document_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the line in the document currency.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the line converted to the company’s local currency.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'Custom assignment field for additional cost allocation (e.g., order number, asset).']
  business_segment_code varchar [note: 'Business segment code for segment‑level reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center identifier for cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the document amount.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a debit or a credit.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on the source document for the line.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert from document currency to local currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) of the posting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year of the posting.']
  journal_entry_line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line.']
  line_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity associated with the line (e.g., units, liters).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the line within the journal entry.']
  line_text varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the journal entry line.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the line is posted to the ledger.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center identifier for profitability reporting.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates if this line is a reversal of a prior line.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for the line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the line for tax calculation.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the line.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Work breakdown structure element or project code associated with the line.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the line.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a general ledger journal entry representing one side of a double-entry posting. Captures GL account, cost center, profit center, project/WBS element, business segment, debit/credit indicator, amount in local and document currency, tax code, assignment field, and line item text. Enables granular cost allocation, plant-level cost accounting, and detailed financial audit trails required for SOX and FASB compliance in chemical manufacturing operations.'
}

Table finance.cost_element {
  cost_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cost element master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost element belongs to a cost center; replace code with FK for proper hierarchy.']
  cost_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each cost element is owned by a cost accountant; the FK supports cost allocation and audit trails.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cost element may be posted to a GL account; add FK for accurate accounting linkage.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the cost element to cost objects (e.g., direct, absorption, activity‑based).']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost element indicating its accounting purpose (e.g., primary cost, secondary overhead, revenue).']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the cost element in financial transactions and reporting.']
  cost_element_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cost element, including business rules and usage notes.']
  cost_element_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the cost element.']
  cost_element_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost element.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost element record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the default rate.']
  default_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Default monetary rate applied per unit of measure when the cost element is used.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost element is subject to tax.']
  overhead_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default overhead rate applied when the cost element is used for overhead absorption.']
  quantity_based_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost element is calculated on a per‑quantity basis.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost element must be tracked for regulatory reporting (e.g., REACH, EPA).']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) when the cost element is taxable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure associated with the cost element (e.g., kg, hour, liter).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the cost element record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost element record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the cost element definition for change management and audit.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the cost element record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the cost element becomes valid for posting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date after which the cost element is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']

  Note: 'Cost element master defining the nature of costs and revenues tracked in management accounting. Classifies costs as primary (directly linked to GL accounts for external reporting) or secondary (internal allocations, overhead absorption, activity-based costing). Includes cost element category, unit of measure, quantity-based costing flag, and validity period. Critical for standard costing of chemical formulations, overhead rate application, and COGS analysis across batch and continuous manufacturing processes.'
}

Table finance.internal_order {
  internal_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the internal order record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center responsible for the order\'s expenses.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user responsible for the internal order.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the order\'s activities occur.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred to date against the internal order.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal order received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the internal order.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fixed asset to which the order is settled, if applicable.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the internal order.']
  budget_month varchar [note: 'Fiscal month (1‑12) of the budget allocation.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the budget (e.g., initial, revised).']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the budget applies.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of funds that have been committed but not yet spent.']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the cost element category for accounting purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department owning the internal order.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an external document such as a purchase order or contract.']
  financial_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier used for financial reporting (e.g., FY2025Q1).']
  internal_order_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the purpose and scope of the internal order.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric identifier assigned to the internal order.']
  is_capitalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order\'s costs are capitalized as an asset.']
  is_external boolean [note: 'True if the order pertains to external projects or vendors.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the internal order.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the internal order was initially created in the system.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order indicating whether it is capital expenditure, operating expense, research & development, or maintenance.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Code of the project or initiative to which the internal order is linked.']
  project_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the manager overseeing the project linked to the internal order.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  remaining_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget remaining after actual and committed amounts are deducted.']
  settlement_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that determines how costs are settled (e.g., capitalized to an asset or expensed).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the internal order record.']

  Note: 'Cost collection object for tracking expenditures on specific initiatives including capital projects, plant turnarounds, R&D programs, EHS compliance investments, and maintenance campaigns in chemical manufacturing. Captures order type (CapEx, OpEx, R&D, maintenance), budget, actual costs, commitments, settlement rules, responsible cost center, and lifecycle status. Enables project-level cost monitoring, CapEx/OpEx classification per FASB ASC 360, and settlement to fixed assets upon project completion.'
}

Table finance.standard_cost {
  standard_cost_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each standard cost record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Standard cost is defined per cost center; FK replaces code for normalization.']
  cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_element. Business justification: Standard cost is associated with a cost element; FK enables detailed cost tracking.']
  cost_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Cost owners are responsible for cost element accuracy; linking enables cost accountability reporting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the cost applies.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the cost record.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the cost record.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost record received approval.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of the product, expressed in the specified currency.']
  cost_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the cost record (e.g., "Standard Cost for Polyethylene 2024 Q1").']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost record indicating its purpose (standard, planned, budget, or actual).']
  cost_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cost amount (e.g., kilogram, liter, unit).']
  cost_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for revisions of the standard cost.']
  costing_lot_size decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of product units for which the standard cost is calculated.']
  costing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the standard cost (e.g., FIFO, LIFO, weighted average, standard).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amount.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the cost record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the standard cost becomes valid for valuation.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date after which the standard cost is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the standard cost expires (mirrors effective_to).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the cost record.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Unique product or material identifier to which this standard cost applies.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the standard cost was officially released for use in valuation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the cost data (e.g., SAP FI/CO).']
  standard_cost_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the cost record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the cost record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the cost record.']

  Note: 'Standard cost estimate for chemical finished goods, intermediates, and formulations used for inventory valuation and COGS determination. Captures cost component breakdown (raw materials, direct labor, energy, manufacturing overhead, depreciation), standard price per unit, costing lot size, validity period, and release status. Supports product costing for complex multi-step chemical synthesis, make-vs-buy decisions, transfer pricing, and variance analysis against actual production costs in batch and continuous process environments.'
}

Table finance.budget_plan {
  budget_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each budget plan record.']
  budget_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget plan is prepared for a specific cost center; FK replaces code.']
  cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_element. Business justification: Budget plan allocates amounts by cost element; FK enables detailed reporting.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal order linked to the budget plan for project‑level cost tracking.']
  primary_budget_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the employee who approved the budget plan.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget plan also targets a profit center; FK provides direct linkage.']
  tertiary_budget_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the budget plan received final approval.']
  approval_outcome varchar [note: 'Enum indicating the final outcome of the budget plan approval process.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the approval workflow (e.g., Pending, Approved, Rejected).']
  budget_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget plan (e.g., Draft, Submitted, Approved, Closed).']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget plan indicating whether it is the original budget, a supplemental addition, or a return of funds.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget plan expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget plan becomes effective for financial posting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the budget applies (e.g., 2025).']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget plan is locked from further edits.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review or re‑forecast of the budget plan.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Standardized lifecycle state of the budget plan used for workflow routing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional comments, rationale, or special instructions.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier or code for the budget plan, often used in reporting and approvals.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the budget plan to support revisions and re‑approvals.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount budgeted for the specified cost element and period.']
  planning_period varchar [note: 'Period within the fiscal year for which the budget is planned (quarter, half‑year, or full year). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Q1|Q2|Q3|Q4|H1|H2|Annual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scenario varchar [note: 'Descriptive label for the budgeting scenario (e.g., Base, Optimistic, Pessimistic).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget plan record.']
  variance_allowed decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible variance (percentage) between actual spend and planned amount before alerts are raised.']

  Note: 'Annual and multi-year budget plan record for cost centers, profit centers, and internal orders. Captures budget version, fiscal year, planning period, budget type (original, supplement, return), planned amounts by cost element, currency, approval status, and planner identity. Supports financial planning, annual budgeting cycles, variance-to-budget reporting, and management reporting for chemical manufacturing operations including plant-level OpEx budgets and R&D investment planning.'
}

Table finance.capex_request {
  capex_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the capital expenditure request.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capex request is charged to a cost center; FK provides proper cost allocation.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the investment will be implemented.']
  primary_capex_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the CapEx request.']
  tertiary_capex_project_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for day‑to‑day execution of the project.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Real calendar date when work was completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Real calendar date when work actually began.']
  approval_workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of the internal approval workflow.']
  approved_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget amount formally approved for the project.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the budget was allocated.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Explanation of the strategic and financial rationale for the investment.']
  capex_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is tangible or intangible.']
  capex_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the request.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the project complies with internal and external regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to depreciate the capitalized asset.']
  environmental_impact_assessment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an environmental impact assessment is required.']
  estimated_total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total cost of the investment before approval.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the CapEx budget is allocated.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of capital funding for the project.']
  investment_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the capital project.']
  net_capex_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget less any offsets or rebates.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the request by the governance board.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope and purpose of the project.']
  project_end_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for project completion.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for project commencement.']
  project_title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title of the capital project.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if external regulatory sign‑off is needed for the project.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approvals.']
  request_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request was initially submitted.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the request.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment for the capital project.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the approved budget.']
  total_expected_savings decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cost savings or efficiency gains resulting from the investment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected economic life of the asset in years.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure request and approval record for plant investments, equipment acquisitions, and facility upgrades in chemical manufacturing. Captures project description, requesting plant/cost center, investment category (new capacity, replacement, EHS compliance, R&D), estimated total cost, approved budget, CapEx classification (tangible/intangible), business justification, approval workflow status, and expected useful life. Supports CapEx governance, FASB ASC 360 compliance, and capital planning.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the fixed asset record.']
  custodian_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Asset management requires a designated custodian employee for maintenance, audit, and compliance.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation recorded to date for the asset.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original cost incurred to acquire the asset.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was acquired.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the asset type.']
  asset_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the asset.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the asset used for reporting and identification.']
  asset_subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the asset category.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Unique asset tag or inventory number assigned to the asset.']
  capitalized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost has been capitalized on the balance sheet.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the asset.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the asset expense is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date depreciation is scheduled to end (or actual end if retired).']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation for the asset.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date depreciation began for the asset.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was disposed of or retired.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary proceeds received from disposing of the asset.']
  ehs_classification varchar [note: 'Environmental, Health, and Safety risk classification for the asset.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score representing the asset\'s environmental impact.']
  fixed_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the asset.']
  impairment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of loss recognized due to asset impairment.']
  impairment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the asset has been deemed impaired.']
  insurance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Insured value of the asset for risk management.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or safety inspection.']
  location_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the asset\'s primary location.']
  location_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the asset\'s primary location.']
  maintenance_contract_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is covered by a maintenance contract.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying amount of the asset (cost less accumulated depreciation).']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or site where the asset is installed.']
  regulatory_code varchar [note: 'CAS number or other regulatory identifier associated with the asset.']
  residual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated salvage value of the asset after disposal.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the asset.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the asset record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset in years for depreciation purposes.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the manufacturer\'s warranty expires.']

  Note: 'Master record for capitalized plant property and equipment including chemical reactors, distillation columns, storage tanks, control systems, buildings, and infrastructure. Captures asset class, acquisition date and cost, accumulated depreciation, net book value, useful life, depreciation method, plant/cost center assignment, and retirement status. Supports balance sheet reporting, depreciation scheduling, insurance valuation, and regulatory compliance under FASB ASC 360 for impairment testing.'
}

Table finance.asset_transaction {
  asset_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each asset transaction record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Asset transaction should reference the cost center responsible for the transaction.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fixed asset affected by the transaction.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Asset transaction needs to be linked to the GL account for proper posting.']
  reversal_transaction_asset_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that reverses this one, if applicable.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value before taxes, fees, or adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax/fee adjustments.']
  amount_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax or fee component associated with the transaction.']
  asset_acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was originally acquired or placed in service.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset (e.g., reactor, tank, column, infrastructure).']
  asset_life_years varchar [note: 'Planned useful life of the asset expressed in years.']
  asset_location_code varchar [note: 'Plant or site code where the asset is physically located.']
  asset_retirement_date date [note: 'Date the asset was retired or disposed of, if applicable.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the asset.']
  asset_sub_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific area or department within the plant where the asset resides.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Physical tag or barcode assigned to the asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the transaction currency.']
  depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount posted for depreciation in this transaction.']
  depreciation_area varchar [note: 'Depreciation area (e.g., main, special, tax) for the posting.']
  depreciation_book varchar [note: 'Depreciation book identifier used for accounting purposes.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculations cease (asset fully depreciated or retired).']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation for the asset.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculations begin for the asset.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the originating document (e.g., purchase order, invoice).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied when converting from transaction currency to reporting currency.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the posting.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was entered manually (True) or generated automatically (False).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Financial period date on which the transaction was posted to the general ledger.']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier used for the posting (e.g., 2023M04).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source ERP/system that generated the transaction (e.g., SAP FI/CO).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code used to determine tax treatment for the transaction.']
  transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or notes for the transaction.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the transaction (e.g., journal entry number).']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the transaction event occurred in the source system.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the asset value movement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset transaction record capturing all asset value movements including acquisitions, retirements, transfers, write-ups, write-downs, and periodic depreciation postings. Includes transaction type, posting date, amount, document reference, asset value date, depreciation area, and GL account assignment. Provides the complete financial history of plant assets (reactors, columns, tanks, infrastructure) for FASB ASC 360 compliance, audit trails, and SOX controls.'
}

Table finance.wbs_element {
  wbs_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the WBS element.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the expenses recorded against this WBS element.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for overall ownership of the WBS element.']
  parent_wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent WBS element in the hierarchy.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant to which the WBS element is assigned.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Higher‑level project to which this WBS element belongs.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred to date for the WBS element.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when work on the WBS element was completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date when work on the WBS element actually began.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the WBS element.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset for accounting purposes (e.g., building, equipment).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated to the WBS element.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the budget (e.g., baseline, revised).']
  capitalization_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether costs on this element are eligible for capitalization under ASC 360.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change request that modified this WBS element.']
  committed_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost that has been committed (e.g., purchase orders) but not yet incurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the WBS element record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  depreciation_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life in years for depreciation calculations.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to depreciate capitalized costs associated with this element.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of capitalized costs begins.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the WBS element becomes effective for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the WBS element is no longer effective (null if open‑ended).']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the WBS element.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code from external systems (e.g., ERP, PMIS) linked to this WBS element.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the element within the WBS hierarchy (root = 1).']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited string representing the full path from the root to this element (e.g., 001/005/012).']
  is_milestone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the WBS element represents a project milestone.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the WBS element was last reviewed for status or scope changes.']
  milestone_date date [note: 'Target date for the milestone, if applicable.']
  planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost forecast for the remainder of the element\'s lifecycle.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the WBS element for execution sequencing.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level for the WBS element.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the WBS element.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the WBS element record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the WBS element, used in project accounting and reporting.']
  wbs_element_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope and purpose of the WBS element.']
  wbs_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the WBS element.']
  wbs_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the WBS element.']
  wbs_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the element within the project hierarchy (e.g., project, phase, task).']

  Note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element for hierarchical project cost tracking of capital investments, plant turnaround shutdowns, capacity expansions, and R&D programs. Captures project hierarchy position, responsible cost center, plant assignment, budget, actual costs, commitments, and capitalization eligibility. Enables multi-year project cost control, milestone-based budget release, and FASB ASC 360 capitalization decisions for chemical manufacturing infrastructure investments.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bank account record was first created in the system.']

  signatory_secondary_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the secondary authorized signatory, if applicable.']

  treasury_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the treasury operating unit responsible for the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the bank account record.']

  Note: 'Master record for corporate bank accounts used in treasury operations and cash management for the chemical manufacturing enterprise. Captures bank identifier, account number, account type (operating, payroll, investment, escrow), currency, bank name, country, GL account assignment, signatory authorities, and account status. Supports cash position reporting, payment execution, bank statement reconciliation, and liquidity planning across multiple banking relationships and jurisdictions.'
}

Table finance.fx_exposure {
  fx_exposure_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FX exposure record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: FX exposure is reported per cost center; FK aligns exposure with cost accounting.']
  hedge_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific hedge contract (e.g., forward contract number).']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company) that owns the exposure.']
  netted_fx_exposure_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fx_exposure (netted_fx_exposure_id)']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FX risk monitoring is performed by a designated risk‑manager employee; the link supports exposure reporting and audit.']
  accounting_designation varchar [note: 'ASC 815 hedge accounting classification for the exposure.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the exposure for hedge accounting.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exposure was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the exposure.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit‑trail record capturing change history for this exposure.']
  base_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the notional amount is expressed.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the exposure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exposure record was first created in the system.']
  currency_pair varchar [note: 'Combined ISO 4217 codes of the base and quote currencies (e.g., USD/EUR).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the exposure becomes effective for accounting and risk purposes.']
  exchange_rate_at_valuation decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate used to convert the exposure to the reporting currency on the valuation date.']
  exposure_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the exposure purpose.']
  exposure_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the exposure for tracking and reporting.']
  exposure_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the exposure as arising from a transactional event or from translation of foreign‑currency balances.']
  fair_value decimal(20,4) [note: 'Current fair value of the hedge instrument in the reporting currency.']
  fx_exposure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exposure record.']
  hedge_cost decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the hedge instrument, expressed in reporting currency.']
  hedge_effectiveness decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0‑100) indicating how effectively the hedge offsets the exposure.']
  hedge_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type of derivative used to hedge the exposure.']
  hedge_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of the exposure that is covered by the hedge (0‑100%).']
  is_exposure_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the exposure is still open and subject to risk.']
  is_hedge_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the hedge is currently active.']
  last_valuation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the most recent valuation of the exposure was performed.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Date on which the hedge instrument expires or settles.']
  notional_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Principal amount of the exposure expressed in the base currency.']
  quote_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 code of the currency used to value the exposure.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exposure must be disclosed in regulatory filings.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the exposure is reported for financial statements.']
  risk_metric_delta decimal(12,4) [note: 'Sensitivity of the exposure value to a one‑unit change in the underlying rate.']
  risk_metric_vega decimal(12,4) [note: 'Sensitivity of the exposure value to volatility changes in the underlying instrument.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the exposure is expected to be settled or realized.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that supplied the exposure data (e.g., SAP FI/CO).']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying transaction that generated the exposure (e.g., purchase order).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the exposure record.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date on which the exposure and hedge were last valued.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the exposure record for concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Foreign exchange exposure and hedging record tracking currency risk positions arising from cross-border raw material purchases, international product sales, and intercompany transactions in chemical manufacturing. Captures exposure type (transactional, translational), currency pair, notional amount, hedge instrument (forward, option, swap), hedge effectiveness, fair value, maturity date, and FASB ASC 815 hedge accounting designation. Supports treasury risk management and financial reporting of derivative instruments.'
}

Table finance.fiscal_period {
  fiscal_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fiscal_period']
  prior_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fiscal_period (prior_fiscal_period_id)']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any adjustment applied to the period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fiscal period record was first created in the system.']
  days_in_period varchar [note: 'Total count of calendar days covered by the period.']
  description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description providing additional context about the period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter number (1‑4) within the fiscal year.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the period belongs.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'True if the period dates or length were adjusted for accounting reasons.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if this period is the active reporting period.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the fiscal period (e.g., FY2023Q1, 2023M01, 2023).']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the fiscal period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fiscal period, such as "Fiscal Q1 2023".']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the fiscal period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the period – month, quarter, or year.']
  status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the period for reporting purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the fiscal period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fiscal_period. Referenced by period_id.'
}

Table finance.hedge_instrument {
  hedge_instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hedge_instrument']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the counterparty to the hedge contract.']
  rolled_hedge_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on hedge_instrument (rolled_hedge_instrument_id)']
  underlying_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying asset or reference entity for the hedge instrument.']
  accounting_treatment varchar [note: 'Accounting classification for the hedge instrument under financial reporting standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hedge instrument record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the hedge instrument\'s notional amount.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional details about the hedge instrument.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the hedge instrument becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the hedge instrument ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  hedge_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of exposure covered by the hedge instrument.']
  instrument_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the hedge instrument based on underlying risk factor.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'External reference code or ticker for the hedge instrument.']
  instrument_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the hedge instrument.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of derivative instrument used for hedging.']
  last_valuation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent valuation performed on the hedge instrument.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Date when the hedge instrument expires or settles.']
  notional_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Principal amount on which the hedge instrument\'s cash flows are based.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating assigned to the hedge instrument.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the hedge instrument is settled.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hedge instrument.']
  strike_price decimal(20,4) [note: 'Fixed price agreed for options or caps/floors.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hedge instrument record.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Approach used to value the hedge instrument for accounting.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for hedge_instrument. Referenced by hedge_instrument_id.'
}

Table finance.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for legal_entity']
  parent_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent legal entity in a corporate hierarchy.']
  parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on legal_entity (parent_legal_entity_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the entity\'s registered street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the entity\'s registered street address (optional).']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue reported for the most recent fiscal year, in US dollars.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the entity\'s registered address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the entity\'s compliance with industry regulations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code where the entity is incorporated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the legal entity record was first created in the system.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned to the entity.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code of the entity\'s functional currency.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level assigned to the entity\'s data.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the entity ceased to exist (if applicable).']
  entity_category varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the entity\'s relationship to the organization.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity\'s legal structure.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'North American Industry Classification System code representing the entity\'s primary industry.']
  is_public_company boolean [note: 'True if the entity is publicly listed on a stock exchange.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the entity as per incorporation documents.']
  name varchar [note: 'Primary display name of the legal entity (e.g., company name).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any supplemental information about the entity.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total headcount employed by the entity.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the entity\'s registered address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the primary contact of the entity.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the entity.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the primary contact of the entity.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Government issued registration number (e.g., company registration).']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'SIC code representing the entity\'s industry.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the entity was legally established.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the entity\'s registered address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the legal entity.']
  stock_ticker varchar [note: 'Ticker symbol if the entity is publicly traded.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity is exempt from certain taxes.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Tax ID used for reporting to tax authorities.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Doing Business As name under which the entity operates commercially.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the legal entity record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the entity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for legal_entity. Referenced by legal_entity_id.'
}

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business unit became operational or effective.']
