  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the business unit record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for business_unit. Referenced by business_unit_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each employee.']
  job_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_role. Business justification: Employees belong to a job role; linking via employee.job_role_id enables normalization of role attributes and removes redundant job_role_code.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Each employee holds a specific authorized position; adding employee.position_id creates a direct relationship to position.']
  rough_cut_capacity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.rough_cut_capacity. Business justification: Rough‑cut capacity studies must record the employee author for governance and traceability.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Security clearance level for plant and system access.']
  address varchar [note: 'Full residential address used for emergency contact and tax purposes.']
  annual_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base yearly salary amount for salaried employees.']
  badge_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s access badge expires.']
  badge_number varchar [note: 'Physical access badge identifier.']
  benefits_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for company benefits.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Method of compensation for the employee.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date the contractor agreement expires.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date the contractor agreement becomes effective.']
  contractor_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external contractor organization.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which labor costs are charged.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Employee\'s birth date, used for age verification and benefits eligibility.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department identifier.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary work email address for the employee.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the employee\'s emergency contact.']
  employee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee.']
  gmp_qualification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is qualified under Good Manufacturing Practice.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee officially started employment.']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor\'s insurance coverage expires.']
  insurance_verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of contractor\'s insurance verification.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the job classification.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the employee\'s position.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname) of the employee.']
  osha_training_date date [note: 'Date of OSHA Process Safety Management training.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee.']
  ppe_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if personal protective equipment is required for the employee\'s role.']
  psm_training_date date [note: 'Date of PSM-specific training.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system.']
  safety_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'True if required safety training has been completed.']
  safety_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when safety training was completed.']
  salary_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade used for salary banding.']
  sap_personnel_number varchar [note: 'Employee number as stored in SAP HR.']
  site_access_authorization_level varchar [note: 'Level of authorization for site access.']
  social_security_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued identifier for payroll and tax reporting.']
  sponsoring_department varchar [note: 'Internal department that sponsors the contractor.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Core master record for ALL workers performing work at Chemical Mfg plant sites — full-time employees, part-time staff, contract/temporary workers, and service provider personnel. Single source of truth for worker identity regardless of employment relationship. Captures personal identity, employment status, hire date, job classification, plant assignment, cost center, employment type discriminator (full-time, part-time, contractor, temporary, service-provider), union membership, badge number, site access authorization level, SAP HR personnel number. For contractors and service providers: contractor company name, contract start/end dates, sponsoring department, safety induction completion status, OSHA training verification date, site-specific PPE requirements, and contractor insurance verification. Provides unified worker identity for all downstream systems including MES operator login, DCS access control, training compliance, safety qualification tracking, and OSHA recordkeeping. All qualification, training, and site access management flows through this single worker identity regardless of payroll relationship.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the position record.']
  budget decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated for this position (salary, benefits, overhead).']
  budgeted_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of headcount slots budgeted for this position.']
  creation_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the position record (e.g., SAP OM, manual entry, external HR system).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is retired or expires (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes active for staffing.']
  ehs_training_required boolean [note: 'True if the position requires mandatory Environment, Health & Safety training.']
  filled_flag boolean [note: 'True if the position is currently filled, otherwise False.']
  fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standardized FTE value representing the workload of the position.']
  grade_band varchar [note: 'Compensation grade band (e.g., G1, G2) that determines salary range and benefits.']
  is_safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is safety‑critical (e.g., PSM operator, EHS officer).']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional family grouping (e.g., Production, R&D, Quality).']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level within the job family indicating seniority.']
  last_filled_date date [note: 'Date when the position was most recently filled.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the position is based.']
  position_category varchar [note: 'High‑level functional category of the position. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|research|admin|logistics|sales|quality|maintenance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used to reference the position in HR and payroll systems.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment type for the position.']
  psm_operator_required boolean [note: 'True if the position must hold a Process Safety Management certification.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the position record.']
  reports_to_position_code varchar [note: 'Position code of the immediate supervisory role.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of mandatory certifications (e.g., OSHA PSM, GMP).']
  required_education varchar [note: 'Minimum education level required for the position (e.g., Bachelor\'s in Chemical Engineering).']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience needed.']
  salary_range_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range for the position.']
  salary_range_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range for the position.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern for the position.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official job title associated with the position (e.g., Process Engineer, Safety Officer).']

  Note: 'Authorized position master record within the organizational structure of Chemical Mfg. Defines budgeted headcount slots, job title, grade band, plant or department assignment, required qualifications, and whether the position is safety-critical (PSM operator, EHS officer). Supports workforce planning and org chart management. Linked to SAP OM Position object.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the organizational unit.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who manages the unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent unit in the hierarchy.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the unit\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the unit\'s street address (optional).']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the unit.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the unit\'s primary location.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Key compliance certifications held by the unit.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the unit\'s primary location.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the unit ceased operations, if applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the unit became operational.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the unit.']
  energy_consumption_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Annual energy consumption of the unit in megawatt-hours.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit identifier for environmental compliance.']
  facility_area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical area of the unit\'s facilities in square meters.']
  headcount_actual varchar [note: 'Current number of employees assigned to the unit.']
  headcount_planned varchar [note: 'Planned number of employees for the unit.']
  operating_hours_per_week varchar [note: 'Standard weekly operating hours for the unit.']
  org_hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path of the unit for reporting (e.g., /Company/Plant/Dept).']
  org_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the unit within the hierarchy (root=0).']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code for the unit, used in ERP and reporting.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the organizational unit (e.g., Production, Quality Control).']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the unit indicating its functional classification.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the unit.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the unit\'s address.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the organizational unit record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the organizational unit record.']
  region varchar [note: 'Region where the unit is located (e.g., North America).']
  safety_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit requires OSHA/PSM safety training for its staff.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Typical shift schedule used by the unit.']

  Note: 'Organizational unit master representing departments, cost centers, plants, and functional groups within Chemical Mfg (e.g., Reaction Processing, QC Laboratory, EHS, R&D, Logistics). Defines the hierarchical reporting structure used for workforce planning, labor cost allocation, and regulatory headcount reporting. Corresponds to SAP OM Organizational Unit object.'
}

Table workforce.job_role {
  job_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each job role record.']
  competency_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competency profile that defines required skills and certifications for the role.']
  required_training_course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.training_course. Business justification: Job roles may require a specific training course; required_training_course_id captures this relationship.']
  average_experience_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical years of experience required for incumbents of this role.']
  certification_required varchar [note: 'Professional certifications required for the role (e.g., Certified Process Engineer).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the job role record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Primary business department that owns the role (e.g., Production, Maintenance, Quality).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the role definition becomes effective for staffing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the role definition is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Fair Labor Standards Act classification for overtime eligibility.']
  gmp_qualification_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if the role must hold GMP‑qualified personnel status.']
  is_unionized boolean [note: 'True if the role is covered by a labor union agreement.']
  job_role_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of duties, responsibilities, and scope of the role.']
  job_role_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the role definition.']
  location_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope where the role is applicable.']
  pay_grade_max varchar [note: 'Highest pay‑grade level applicable to the role.']
  pay_grade_min varchar [note: 'Lowest pay‑grade level applicable to the role.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role is critical for PSM compliance (True/False).']
  regulatory_training_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if role holders must complete regulatory compliance training (e.g., REACH, GHS).']
  role_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the role in HR systems and payroll.']
  role_family varchar [note: 'Broad classification grouping similar roles (e.g., Operator, Engineer, Technician).']
  role_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the job role (e.g., Chemical Operator I).']
  safety_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if OSHA or other safety training is mandatory (True/False).']
  training_required varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of mandatory training modules (e.g., OSHA PSM, GMP).']
  typical_shift varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern associated with the role.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the job role record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the job role record.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of standardized job roles and job families used across Chemical Mfg plants (e.g., Chemical Operator I/II/III, Process Engineer, QC Analyst, EHS Specialist, Maintenance Technician, Shift Supervisor). Defines role family, pay grade range, FLSA classification, PSM-critical flag, GMP qualification requirement, and required competency profile. Used as the template for position creation.'
}

Table workforce.shift_schedule {
  shift_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the shift schedule record.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the schedule is applied.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes of scheduled breaks within the shift.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes related to regulatory or safety compliance for the schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the schedule becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the schedule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  employee_qualification_required varchar [note: 'Qualification or certification required for personnel assigned to this shift (e.g., "Operator", "Engineer").']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end time of the shift (HH:MM, 24‑hour clock).']
  is_flexible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule can be adjusted on short notice (True/False).']
  is_gmp_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is subject to GMP requirements (True/False).']
  is_psm_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule applies to PSM‑covered processes (True/False).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the schedule was last reviewed for compliance or operational relevance.']
  max_consecutive_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days an employee may work consecutively under this schedule.']
  min_rest_hours_between_shifts decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum required rest period (in hours) between the end of one shift and the start of the next.']
  overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overtime can be scheduled for this shift (True/False).']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who performed the last review.']
  rotation_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that constitute one full rotation of the schedule.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the schedule in operational systems and planning tools.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the shift schedule (e.g., "12‑Hour Continental").']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule pattern: rotating, fixed, or flexible.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version number incremented when the schedule definition changes.']
  shift_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the shift (e.g., day, swing, night).']
  shift_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of shifts based on functional area.']
  shift_pattern_details varchar [note: 'Detailed textual representation of the rotation pattern (e.g., "3 days on, 2 days off").']
  shift_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the shift schedule.']
  shift_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time of the shift (HH:MM, 24‑hour clock).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the schedule (e.g., "America/Chicago").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule record.']
  work_hours_per_day decimal(4,2) [note: 'Planned productive work hours for the shift.']

  Note: 'Master record defining shift rotation patterns and work schedules for plant operations at Chemical Mfg (e.g., 12-hour continental rotating shifts, 8-hour day/swing/night, DCS operator schedules). Captures shift name, start/end times, rotation cycle, plant applicability, and whether the schedule applies to PSM-covered processes. Used for labor planning, overtime calculation, and MES integration.'
}

Table workforce.shift_assignment {
  shift_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each shift assignment record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Assigns a shift to a specific product batch; needed for production scheduling and batch traceability reports.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the crew/team designated for the shift.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned to the shift.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or production unit where the shift occurs.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift_schedule. Business justification: Shift assignments should reference a defined schedule; shift_assignment.shift_schedule_id links to shift_schedule and removes the free‑form code.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production line associated with the shift.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Recorded actual end date and time when the employee completed work.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Recorded actual start date and time when the employee began work.']
  assignment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift assignment record was first created in the system.']
  assignment_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the shift assignment becomes effective.']
  assignment_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the shift assignment ends or expires; null if open‑ended.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier used by operations to reference the shift assignment.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the assignment (e.g., regular, temporary, overtime, training).']
  assignment_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift assignment record.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total scheduled break time within the shift, in minutes.']
  certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a specific certification is required for this shift.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when the shift assignment is modified after initial creation.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual labor cost incurred for the shift after completion.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected labor cost for the shift based on standard rates.']
  crew_designation varchar [note: 'Name or label of the crew/team assigned to the shift.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for labor cost values.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional information or comments about the assignment.']
  overtime_authorized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overtime was pre‑approved for this shift (True/False).']
  overtime_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why overtime was authorized for this shift.']
  plant_location_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the physical location of the plant.']
  relief_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'True if the assignment includes relief/coverage duties for another employee.']
  safety_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee has completed required safety training before the shift.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time of the shift.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time of the shift.']
  shift_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the shift in hours, calculated from scheduled times.']
  shift_pattern_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the shift pattern (e.g., "8‑hour rotating day/night").']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift assignment.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift based on time of day.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the shift assignment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record assigning a specific employee to a shift schedule for a defined period at a plant or production unit. Captures effective dates, assigned shift pattern, crew/team designation, relief coverage flag, and overtime authorization. Supports MES operator login validation, DCS access control, and labor cost allocation by shift.'
}

Table workforce.labor_time_entry {
  labor_time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key uniquely identifying the labor time entry record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Tracks labor hours per chemical product for cost accounting and product profitability analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique internal identifier for the employee who performed the labor.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Plant or facility location where the labor was performed.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the employee.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent timesheet record grouping multiple labor entries.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Reason for non‑working time if the entry represents an absence.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the labor activity performed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the labor entry.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User who approved the labor entry.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the entry was approved.']
  cost_object_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost object (e.g., production order, work order, cost center) charged to.']
  cost_object_type varchar [note: 'Category of the cost object linked to the entry.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact end time of the labor activity.']
  entered_by varchar [note: 'User who originally entered the labor record.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the entry was created in the system.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the employee\'s job role.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the labor (e.g., direct production, indirect support).']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated cost (total_hours × labor_rate) for the entry.']
  labor_cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code to which the labor cost is allocated.']
  labor_entry_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the entry record (e.g., manual entry, system import, mobile app).']
  labor_project_code varchar [note: 'Project identifier if labor is charged to a specific project.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly rate applicable to the labor entry before taxes.']
  labor_rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the labor rate.']
  labor_time_entry_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the entry is currently active or has been soft‑deleted.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the entry within its timesheet.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the labor entry.']
  overtime_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if overtime hours need separate managerial approval.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any overtime was recorded for this entry.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond regular schedule and eligible for overtime pay.']
  ppe_required boolean [note: 'Specifies whether PPE was mandatory for the activity.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the record was first persisted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked at standard rate.']
  safety_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if required safety training was completed before the work.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Designated work shift for the entry.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Competency tier of the employee for the performed activity.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact start time of the labor activity.']
  total_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours recorded for the entry (including regular and overtime).']
  work_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the labor was performed.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of actual hours worked by an employee for a given date, shift, and cost object (production order, maintenance work order, cost center). Captures regular hours, overtime hours, absence type, activity type (direct production, indirect, training, downtime), and approval status. Source data for payroll, COGS labor allocation, and OEE labor efficiency calculations. Integrates with SAP CATS (Cross-Application Time Sheet).'
}

Table workforce.absence_record {
  absence_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the absence record.']
  primary_absence_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee to whom the absence applies.']
  absence_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the absence record.']
  absence_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the absence request.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Category of the absence.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the absence request was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the absence record was initially created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the approved absence period.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the absence ended (used for partial‑day absences).']
  is_fmla_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for Family and Medical Leave Act coverage for this absence.']
  is_osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the absence is a recordable OSHA lost‑time incident.']
  leave_balance_after decimal(7,2) [note: 'Employee\'s leave balance (in days) after this absence is applied.']
  leave_balance_before decimal(7,2) [note: 'Employee\'s leave balance (in days) before this absence is applied.']
  medical_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required medical certification for the absence.']
  paid_leave_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the absence is paid under company policy.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description provided by the employee for the absence.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the employee submitted the absence request.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the absence request submission.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Date the employee is expected or actually returned to work after the absence.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the approved absence period.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the absence began (used for partial‑day absences).']
  total_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence expressed in days, including fractions.']
  total_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence expressed in hours.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the absence record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of employee absences including planned leave (vacation, FMLA, parental leave), unplanned absences (sick leave, injury leave), and OSHA recordable lost-time incidents. Captures absence type, start/end dates, duration in hours/days, FMLA eligibility flag, OSHA recordable flag, return-to-work date, and medical certification status. Critical for OSHA 300 log compliance and workforce availability planning.'
}

Table workforce.competency {
  competency_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each competency record.']
  competency_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the competency (e.g., technical, safety, regulatory, leadership, license, certification).']
  competency_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the competency in HR and compliance systems.']
  competency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the competency, including scope and applicability.']
  competency_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of related competencies (e.g., Process Operations, Safety Management).']
  competency_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the competency, skill, certification, or license.']
  competency_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competency definition.']
  competency_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of competency entity: skill, certification, or license.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the competency definition becomes effective for use.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the competency definition is retired or no longer valid.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the competency is mandatory for a specific job role or regulatory scope.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization or authority that issues the certification or license (e.g., OSHA, API, ISO).']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Standardized proficiency level label for the competency.']
  proficiency_scale decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric scale (e.g., 0.00‑5.00) indicating the level of proficiency required or achieved.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the competency record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the competency record.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or standard that mandates the competency (e.g., OSHA 1910.119, FDA 21 CFR 211).']
  renewal_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months before expiration when renewal actions should be initiated.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the competency must be renewed after the validity period expires.']
  subgroup varchar [note: 'More granular subgroup within a competency group (e.g., Reactor Operation, Confined Space Entry).']
  validity_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard validity period for the competency or certification, expressed in months.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the competency definition to support change management.']

  Note: 'Authoritative reference catalog of ALL competencies, skills, certifications, licenses, and technical capabilities tracked for Chemical Mfg workforce. Includes technical skills (DCS operation, HAZOP facilitation, GC/HPLC analysis, reactor operation), safety competencies (confined space entry, LOTO/LOTOTO procedures, hot work), regulatory qualifications (PSM operator authorization, GMP personnel qualification), and formal certifications/licenses (OSHA 30-Hour, Certified Industrial Hygienist (CIH), Certified Safety Professional (CSP), CDL for hazmat transport, Forklift Operator License, HAZWOPER 40-Hour, API Inspector Certification, Confined Space Rescue). Defines competency/certification name, category (technical, safety, regulatory, leadership, certification, license), proficiency scale or pass/fail indicator, certification/issuing body, standard validity period, renewal/recertification requirements, regulatory mandate reference (OSHA 1910.119, FDA 21 CFR 211, DOT 49 CFR), whether mandatory for PSM-covered roles or GMP-qualified positions, and whether it is a regulatory prerequisite for specific job roles. Single source of truth for the definition of what competencies and certifications exist — employee_competency records reference this catalog.'
}

Table workforce.employee_competency {
  employee_competency_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the employee competency record.']
  competency_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the competency definition from the competency catalog.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom the competency applies.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date the competency assessment or qualification was performed.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or authority that performed the assessment.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the issued certificate or license.']
  competency_validity_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the competency remains valid after issue.']
  current_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the competency record.']
  expiration_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how the competency expires (fixed date vs. rolling based on issue date).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certificate or license expires.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certificate or license was issued.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification or license.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the competency.']
  practical_evaluation_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the hands‑on practical assessment.']
  process_unit varchar [note: 'Specific plant unit or process the competency applies to (e.g., "HF Alkylation").']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Assessed level of competence for the employee.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the qualification event (e.g., initial, periodic).']
  record_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the competency record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the competency record.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulation or standard that mandates the competency (e.g., "29 CFR 1910.119").']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which renewal actions must be completed.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current renewal state of the competency.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the competency data (e.g., SAP HR, LIMS).']
  verification_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., URL or document ID) to the scanned verification document.']
  written_test_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score achieved on any written examination, expressed as a percentage.']

  Note: 'Unified transactional record linking employees to ALL assessed competencies, regulatory qualifications, process-specific authorizations, and formal certifications held. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for answering \'what is this worker qualified to do?\' Covers: technical competencies (DCS operation proficiency, HAZOP facilitation, GC/HPLC analysis), safety qualifications (confined space entry authorization, LOTO certification), OSHA PSM operator qualifications for specific covered processes (HF alkylation, chlorine handling, ammonia refrigeration — per 29 CFR 1910.119), GMP personnel qualifications (initial qualification, periodic requalification, hygiene assessment, documentation practice evaluation), and formal certifications/licenses (OSHA 30-Hour card, CIH, CSP, CDL, HAZWOPER 40-Hour, API Inspector, Forklift Operator). Captures: referenced competency from catalog, proficiency level or pass/fail, assessment/qualification date, assessor/qualified-by name, qualification type discriminator (PSM, GMP, certification, license, general skill), regulatory mandate reference, process unit (for PSM), written test score, practical evaluation result, issuing/certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, renewal/refresher due date, renewal status, verification document reference, and current qualification status (active, expired, suspended, revoked). Triggers automated renewal alerts, blocks MES/DCS login for lapsed qualifications, and supports single-query compliance reporting for OSHA PSM audits, GMP inspections, FDA reviews, ISO 9001 assessments, and insurance audits.'
}

Table workforce.training_course {
  training_course_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each training course record.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate participant competence.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Name of the certification granted upon passing, if applicable.']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'True if successful completion awards a formal certification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification status of the course.']
  cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with delivering the course (if any).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amount (e.g., USD).']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used to reference the course across systems.']
  course_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the course purpose or domain.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the course record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Accredited credit hours or CEUs awarded upon successful completion.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the training is delivered to participants.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned total instructional time for the course.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the course is no longer offered (nullable if indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the course becomes available for enrollment.']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary instructor or facilitator for the course.']
  is_external_provider boolean [note: 'True if the course is delivered by a third‑party vendor.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course is required for all applicable employees.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the course is delivered.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the course content was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  max_enrollment varchar [note: 'Maximum number of participants allowed in a single session.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.']
  pass_fail_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score (percentage) required to pass the assessment.']
  prerequisite_course_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of course codes that must be completed before enrolling.']
  regulatory_mandate varchar [note: 'Regulation or standard that requires the training (e.g., OSHA 1910.119).']
  requalification_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after which the course must be retaken to remain compliant.']
  review_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory content reviews.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the training course as shown in the catalog.']
  training_category varchar [note: 'Broad compliance or functional category the course belongs to.']
  training_course_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the course content, objectives, and scope.']
  training_course_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the course record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the course record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the course content (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Unified training management product covering the COMPLETE training lifecycle as a single source of truth: course catalog definition, scheduled/completed training sessions, and individual employee training completion records. COURSE CATALOG: Manages OSHA PSM initial and refresher training, GMP qualification courses, HAZMAT/DOT training, GHS/SDS awareness, confined space entry, LOTO/LOTOTO, process safety management, emergency response, and site-specific orientation courses. Captures course code, title, delivery method (classroom, hands-on, online, blended), regulatory mandate (OSHA 1910.119, EPA RMP, DOT 49 CFR, FDA 21 CFR 211), requalification interval, pass/fail threshold, and prerequisite courses. SESSIONS: Captures scheduled date, location (plant, training room, online platform), instructor/facilitator name, maximum enrollment, actual attendance roster, session status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled), and materials used. COMPLETION RECORDS: Captures individual employee results including completion date, assessment score, pass/fail result, certificate number issued, expiry/requalification date, trainer/assessor name, regulatory mandate satisfied, and CEU/contact hours earned. Supports training calendar management, compliance gap analysis, automated retraining notifications, and audit-ready reporting for OSHA PSM reviews, GMP inspections, and DOT compliance verification.'
}

Table workforce.headcount_plan {
  headcount_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each headcount planning record.']
  job_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_role. Business justification: Headcount plans target specific job roles; headcount_plan.job_role_id creates a proper FK and removes the redundant code.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Headcount plans are scoped to an org unit; linking via headcount_plan.org_unit_id replaces the code field with a proper FK.']
  actual_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of positions that have been filled as of the latest update.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the plan\'s approval process.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who granted final approval.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the plan received approval.']
  approved_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'FTE count that has been formally approved by governance.']
  budgeted_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of positions budgeted for the planning period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the headcount plan record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the headcount plan becomes operationally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the headcount plan ceases to be effective; null for open‑ended plans.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year associated with the headcount plan.']
  headcount_plan_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the headcount plan.']
  is_contractor_allowed boolean [note: 'True if the headcount can be satisfied with contractor labor.']
  is_filled boolean [note: 'True when the required headcount for the role is fully staffed.']
  is_gmp_required boolean [note: 'True if the role must meet Good Manufacturing Practice qualification.']
  is_psm_critical boolean [note: 'True if the position requires Process Safety Management certification.']
  last_review_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who performed the most recent review.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the headcount plan was last examined or updated by governance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, assumptions, or explanations related to the plan.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the objectives, scope, and assumptions of the headcount plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the headcount plan for easy identification.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan indicating whether it is strategic, operational, or tactical.']
  planning_category varchar [note: 'Business driver for the headcount request.']
  planning_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the period covered by the headcount plan.']
  planning_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the period covered by the headcount plan.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or location associated with the headcount plan.']
  target_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned total FTEs to be staffed for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the headcount plan.']

  Note: 'Workforce planning and talent acquisition product managing strategic headcount targets and the full staffing requisition lifecycle. Captures approved FTE targets by org unit, job role, and planning period for CapEx/OpEx workforce budgeting. Manages active staffing requisitions including requisition number, position title, org unit, plant, hiring manager, required start date, requisition type (new hire, backfill, contractor conversion), required certifications, PSM-critical flag, GMP qualification requirement, approval workflow status, recruiter assignment, candidate pipeline tracking, and fulfillment status (open, interviewing, offered, filled, cancelled). Supports plant staffing adequacy reviews for PSM compliance (minimum operator coverage per shift), drives the talent acquisition workflow from planning through requisition approval, recruiting, offer acceptance, and onboarding handoff. Single source of truth for all workforce demand planning and position fulfillment activity.'
}

Table workforce.employee_action {
  employee_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the employee action record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved the action.']
  primary_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom the action applies.']
  primary_employee_previous_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee\'s position before the action.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier or code for the personnel action (e.g., ACT‑2024‑00123).']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the personnel action.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Category of personnel change. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: return_from_leave — promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the action was approved or rejected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by HR during processing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the personnel action becomes effective for payroll and benefits.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment (date and time) the action takes effect.']
  hr_action_code varchar [note: 'SAP HR module code representing the specific action type.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the action is considered critical for operations or compliance.']
  is_gmp_required boolean [note: 'True if the role is subject to Good Manufacturing Practice qualification.']
  is_osh_training_required boolean [note: 'True if the employee must complete OSHA PSM or related safety training for the new role.']
  is_safety_critical boolean [note: 'True if the action impacts safety‑critical roles or equipment.']
  new_org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code of the org unit the employee belongs to after the action.']
  new_pay_grade varchar [note: 'Pay grade of the employee after the action.']
  previous_org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code of the org unit the employee belonged to before the action.']
  previous_pay_grade varchar [note: 'Pay grade of the employee prior to the action.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the personnel action was initiated.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the reason for the action.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee action record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee action record.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the personnel action request was entered into the system.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a personnel action event in the employee lifecycle at Chemical Mfg, including hire, transfer, promotion, demotion, role change, plant reassignment, leave of absence, return from leave, and termination. Captures action type, effective date, previous and new position/org unit/pay grade, reason code, approver, and SAP HR action code. Provides the complete employment history audit trail required for labor law compliance and OSHA recordkeeping.'
}

Table workforce.labor_agreement {
  labor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the collective bargaining agreement.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Labor agreements are often scoped to a particular org unit; adding org_unit_id creates this association.']
  agreement_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored digital copy of the agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the agreement by the union or company.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the labor agreement, e.g., collective bargaining agreement.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the agreement has been amended.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual/committee that approved the agreement.']
  benefits_included varchar [note: 'List of employee benefits covered (e.g., health, pension, paid leave).']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational scope covered by the agreement (e.g., specific plant, region, or company‑wide).']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the agreement document.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement expires or terminates (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes binding.']
  grievance_procedure varchar [note: 'Outline of the process for filing and resolving employee grievances.']
  hazard_classifications varchar [note: 'Any hazard‑related provisions (e.g., GHS, OSHA) included in the agreement.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement applies exclusively to the covered workforce.']
  is_mandatory_training boolean [note: 'True if the agreement requires specific safety or certification training.']
  job_classifications_covered varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of job families or classifications that the agreement applies to.']
  labor_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to the agreement.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement was last reviewed for compliance or renewal.']
  minimum_wage_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base hourly wage floor stipulated by the agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the agreement.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factor applied to base wage for overtime hours.']
  overtime_rule_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of overtime eligibility and calculation method.']
  ratification_status varchar [note: 'Whether the agreement has been formally ratified by the union and management.']
  ratified_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was officially ratified.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Mechanism for renewing the agreement after expiry.']
  seniority_rule_description varchar [note: 'Explanation of seniority‑based assignment or promotion rules.']
  seniority_years_required varchar [note: 'Minimum years of service required for certain benefits or assignments.']
  shift_differential_details varchar [note: 'Description of any shift‑differential pay rules (e.g., night shift premium).']
  shift_differential_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage premium for non‑standard shifts.']
  training_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of training or certification requirements mandated by the agreement.']
  union_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the union contact.']
  union_contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary contact person at the union for this agreement.']
  union_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the union contact.']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the labor union that negotiated the agreement.']

  Note: 'Master record of collective bargaining agreements (CBAs) and labor union contracts governing employment terms for unionized Chemical Mfg plant workers. Captures union name, agreement effective and expiry dates, covered job classifications, plant scope, key provisions (shift differentials, overtime rules, seniority-based assignment, grievance procedures), and ratification status. Drives shift scheduling rules, overtime eligibility, and labor cost modeling for unionized facilities.'
}

Table workforce.disciplinary_action {
  disciplinary_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the disciplinary action record.']
  primary_disciplinary_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee subject to the disciplinary action.']
  tertiary_disciplinary_hr_business_partner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR business partner responsible for handling the disciplinary case.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee acknowledged the disciplinary action.']
  acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has formally acknowledged receipt of the disciplinary action.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the disciplinary action for tracking and audit purposes.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Category of the disciplinary action taken against the employee.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Last date by which the employee may file an appeal.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal filed against the disciplinary action.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which any required corrective action must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action plan must be completed as part of the disciplinary process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disciplinary action record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount.']
  disciplinary_action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disciplinary action.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the disciplinary action becomes effective.']
  expungement_date date [note: 'Date on which the disciplinary record is scheduled to be removed from the employee\'s permanent record, if applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments related to the disciplinary action.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Resulting outcome after the disciplinary process is completed.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty associated with the disciplinary action, such as salary deduction or fine.']
  policy_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or code of the specific policy that was violated.']
  policy_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the action was triggered by a violation of a specific company policy.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for the disciplinary action (e.g., safety_violation, attendance, conduct).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details of why the disciplinary action was taken.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date scheduled for a post‑action review to assess effectiveness and compliance.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the disciplinary action based on impact and policy.']
  suspension_end_date date [note: 'End date of a suspension period.']
  suspension_start_date date [note: 'Start date of a suspension period, if the action type is suspension.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Effective date of employment termination, if the action type is termination.']
  training_completion_deadline date [note: 'Deadline for completing any required remedial training.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee must complete remedial training as part of the disciplinary action.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the disciplinary action record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a formal disciplinary action taken against a Chemical Mfg employee, including verbal warning, written warning, suspension, performance improvement plan (PIP), and termination for cause. Captures action type, effective date, reason (safety violation, GMP deviation, attendance, conduct), issuing supervisor, HR business partner, employee acknowledgment, appeal status, and expungement date. Supports progressive discipline management and OSHA recordkeeping for safety-related violations.'
}

Table workforce.competency_profile {
  competency_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competency_profile']
  superseded_competency_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competency_profile (superseded_competency_profile_id)']
  competency_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the competency (e.g., core, support, leadership).']
  competency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the overall competency level (0.00‑100.00).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the competency profile record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the competency profile and its purpose.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the competency profile expires or is retired (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the competency profile becomes effective.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the competency profile is mandatory for the associated job roles.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the competency profile was last reviewed.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next review of the competency profile.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Job role of the owner within the organization.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Target proficiency level for the competency.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the competency profile in HR systems.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the competency profile.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Category indicating the nature of the competency profile.']
  required_training_hours varchar [note: 'Total number of training hours required to achieve the competency.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competency profile.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the competency profile record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the competency profile for change management.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for competency_profile. Referenced by competency_profile_id.'
}

Table workforce.timesheet {
  timesheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for timesheet']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who approved the timesheet.']
  corrected_timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on timesheet (corrected_timesheet_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who logged the work.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical site where the work was performed.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the timesheet was approved.']
  bill_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly billing rate applied to billable hours.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form text entered by the employee or approver.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Accounting cost center to which labor costs are charged.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that created the record (e.g., Workday, SAP).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the work.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used by an external time‑tracking or payroll system.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recorded hours are billable to a customer or internal cost.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the labor performed.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond the regular schedule that are compensated at an overtime rate.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the timesheet.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the timesheet.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project or work order linked to the timesheet.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the timesheet record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the timesheet record.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked at the standard rate, excluding overtime.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work shift for the period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the timesheet.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the employee submitted the completed timesheet.']
  timesheet_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the timesheet, often used in reports and communications.']
  total_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate number of hours (regular + overtime) recorded for the period.']
  work_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work for labor accounting purposes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for timesheet. Referenced by timesheet_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for crew. Referenced by crew_id.'

Table planning.demand_forecast {
  demand_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each demand forecast version.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Customer‑specific demand forecast drives sales planning and supply allocation; key accounts require separate forecasts for accurate production scheduling.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: REQUIRED: Demand Forecast reports are generated per chemical product; linking enables product‑level demand planning and variance analysis.']
  forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.forecast_version. Business justification: Each demand forecast belongs to a specific forecast version; linking enables version control and eliminates redundant version fields.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Demand forecasting is performed per functional location; linking ties forecast to the exact plant unit for accurate planning.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Demand Forecasting uses the material master to tie forecast SKUs to the authoritative material record, ensuring accurate planning and regulatory reporting.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: S&OP demand forecast uses high‑value sales opportunities to project future demand, a core process linking sales pipeline to planning.']
  prior_demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on demand_forecast (prior_demand_forecast_id)']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: New‑product demand forecasts are created based on the R&D project that defines the product launch timeline.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: REQUIRED: SKU‑level forecasts drive packaging and logistics planning; FK ties forecast rows to the definitive SKU master.']
  adjusted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity after consensus or manual adjustments.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the forecast.']
  baseline_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Statistical or algorithmic baseline quantity forecast before adjustments.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the confidence interval for the forecast quantity.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the confidence interval for the forecast quantity.']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence percentage (0‑100) associated with the forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with the forecast.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand signal used for the forecast.']
  forecast_accuracy decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage accuracy of past forecasts compared to actuals.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of what is being forecasted.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate the forecast quantity.']
  forecast_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the forecast (e.g., "2025 Q3 Demand").']
  forecast_owner varchar [note: 'Department or team responsible for the forecast.']
  forecast_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the forecasted period.']
  forecast_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the forecasted period.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the forecast.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast is for demand or supply.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the forecast (e.g., "V1", "V2").']
  forecast_version_date date [note: 'Calendar date when this forecast version was released.']
  is_final boolean [note: 'True when the forecast version is the definitive one for planning.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast is locked for further edits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or rationale for the forecast.']
  planning_bucket varchar [note: 'Granularity of the forecast (e.g., weekly, monthly).']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Timeframe the forecast covers relative to the planning window.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for the forecast record.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the scenario applied to the forecast.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the forecast quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the forecast.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the forecast record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the forecast.']

  Note: 'Master demand forecast record capturing statistical and consensus-based demand projections for chemical products and SKUs across planning horizons (short, mid, long-term). Stores forecast version, method (statistical, ML, consensus), baseline quantity, adjusted quantity, forecast accuracy metrics, planning bucket (weekly/monthly), and lifecycle status. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for demand signals feeding S&OP and MRP processes.'
}

Table planning.forecast_version {
  forecast_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each forecast version snapshot.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system that created the forecast version.']
  superseded_forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast_version (superseded_forecast_version_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the forecast version.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast version was approved.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the forecast.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast version record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the data used to generate the forecast.']
  forecast_accuracy_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired forecast accuracy expressed as a percentage.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the forecast purpose.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Technique applied to produce the demand forecast.']
  forecast_run_date date [note: 'Date on which the demand forecast was generated.']
  forecast_version_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context for the forecast version.']
  forecast_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the forecast version.']
  is_final_version boolean [note: 'True if this version is the definitive version for the planning cycle.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the most recent update.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations about the forecast version.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last day of the planning horizon covered by this forecast version.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First day of the planning horizon covered by this forecast version.']
  planning_horizon_type varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the forecast horizon.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a new forecast version was created.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the forecasting scenario (e.g., Base, Optimistic, Pessimistic).']
  total_demand_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of forecasted demand across all items for the planning horizon.']
  total_demand_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value (in the selected currency) of the total forecasted demand.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the forecasted quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the forecast version record.']
  version_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this forecast version becomes effective for planning purposes.']
  version_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the forecast version is no longer valid for planning.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the version order within a forecast cycle.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that initially created the forecast version record.']

  Note: 'Version-controlled snapshot of a demand forecast cycle, capturing the forecast run date, planning horizon start/end, version number, approval status, and the planner or system that generated it. Enables comparison of forecast versions across S&OP cycles and supports forecast accuracy tracking over time. Each version contains the full set of demand_forecast line records for that planning cycle.'
}

Table planning.sop_cycle {
  sop_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the S&OP planning cycle.']
  prior_sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sop_cycle (prior_sop_cycle_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the S&OP cycle.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the executive who approved the cycle.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the cycle received final approval.']
  capacity_plan_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the capacity planning version referenced.']
  compliance_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cycle has passed regulatory compliance review.']
  consensus_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome agreed upon by cross‑functional participants (e.g., demand‑supply alignment).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the S&OP cycle record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the S&OP cycle ends.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or code for the planning cycle (e.g., "FY2025_Q1_SOP").']
  cycle_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) of the S&OP cycle.']
  cycle_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the S&OP cycle begins.']
  cycle_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the S&OP cycle belongs.']
  decision_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of key decisions made during the cycle.']
  demand_forecast_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the demand forecast version applied in this cycle.']
  facilitator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for facilitating the S&OP cycle.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cycle is locked from further edits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by participants.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the S&OP cycle (e.g., demand review, supply review).']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Time horizon that the cycle covers for demand and supply planning.']
  planning_scenario varchar [note: 'Scenario used for the cycle\'s forecasts (e.g., base case).']
  review_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the S&OP cycle is reviewed.']
  risk_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the risk assessment performed for the cycle.']
  sop_cycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the planning cycle.']
  supply_plan_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supply plan version used for this cycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the S&OP cycle record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the S&OP cycle record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) cycle master record representing a formal monthly or quarterly S&OP review cycle. Captures cycle ID, planning period, cycle phase (demand review, supply review, pre-S&OP, executive S&OP), status, facilitator, key decisions, and consensus outcome. Serves as the governance record for the integrated business planning process in chemical manufacturing.'
}

Table planning.sop_consensus_record {
  sop_consensus_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SOP consensus record.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business unit owning the consensus plan.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific product (SKU) covered by the consensus.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant associated with the consensus plan.']
  primary_sop_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the consensus record.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product family for which the consensus is recorded.']
  revised_sop_consensus_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sop_consensus_record (revised_sop_consensus_record_id)']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the S&OP planning cycle (e.g., Q3‑2026).']
  tertiary_sop_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the record.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consensus record was approved.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional notes or remarks.']
  consensus_plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the consensus plan for traceability.']
  constrained_supply_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Supply quantity after applying capacity, material, and other constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consensus record was first created in the system.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand forecast used in the consensus.']
  demand_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the demand quantity (e.g., kg, L, pcs).']
  forecast_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks ahead covered by the consensus forecast.']
  gap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between unconstrained demand and constrained supply (demand - supply).']
  gap_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for the demand‑supply gap.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is classified as critical for business continuity.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the consensus record.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Planned action to address the identified gap (e.g., overtime, subcontract, inventory draw).']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Scenario classification for the consensus plan.']
  sop_consensus_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consensus record.']
  sop_meeting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the S&OP meeting that produced the consensus was held.']
  supply_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of the constrained supply quantity.']
