  supply_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the supply quantity (should match demand UOM).']
  unconstrained_demand_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted demand quantity without any supply constraints.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the consensus record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the consensus record to support change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the consensus demand and supply plan agreed upon during an S&OP cycle review for a specific product family, plant, or business unit. Records unconstrained demand, constrained supply plan, gap quantity, resolution action, and approval sign-off. Provides the authoritative agreed plan that drives production scheduling and procurement.'
}

Table planning.mrp_run {
  mrp_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MRP run execution.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initiated the MRP run.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: MRP runs calculate material requirements using the specific formula version, ensuring accurate procurement for the intended composition.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the MRP run was executed.']
  prior_mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mrp_run (prior_mrp_run_id)']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data processing batch that loaded this MRP run record.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes associated with the MRP run.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates if the MRP run is marked as critical for production planning.']
  is_simulation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRP run was a simulation (true) or a live run (false).']
  mrp_run_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the MRP run.']
  planning_date date [note: 'The date for which the MRP planning is performed.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days ahead covered by the MRP planning horizon.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MRP run record was created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MRP run record.']
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total execution time of the MRP run in seconds.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'System-generated sequential identifier for the MRP run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MRP run started.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of MRP execution: regenerative, net change, or net change within planning horizon.']
  run_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who initiated the MRP run.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the MRP run data.']
  total_exception_messages varchar [note: 'Count of exception messages produced during the MRP run.']
  total_planned_orders varchar [note: 'Number of production orders generated by the MRP run.']
  total_planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity (in base unit) of all planned material requirements.']
  total_purchase_requisitions varchar [note: 'Number of purchase requisitions generated by the MRP run.']

  Note: 'Material Requirements Planning (MRP) run master record capturing each MRP execution event in SAP PP/MRP. Records run ID, run type (regenerative, net change, net change in planning horizon), plant scope, planning date, run status, total planned orders generated, total purchase requisitions generated, and exception message count. Serves as the audit header for all MRP-generated planning elements.'
}

Table planning.planned_order {
  planned_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the planned order.']
  pegged_planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planned_order (pegged_planned_order_id)']
  primary_planned_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique system identifier of the material to be produced or procured.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.purchase_order.purchase_order_id — MRP planned orders convert to purchase orders — without this FK, procurement cannot trace which POs originated from MRP requirements. Blocks MRP-to-procurement traceability.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the external vendor for purchase-type planned orders.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch to which the planned order belongs, if batch management is used.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Shows if the planned order has been converted to a production or purchase order, or cancelled.']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost for the planned order based on standard rates.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the planned order record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the planned order record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost estimate.']
  demand_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material required by dependent demand (e.g., sales orders).']
  firming_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the planned order has been firmed or released for execution.']
  last_changed_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the planned order record.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the planned order record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned lead time in days from order creation to receipt or production start.']
  lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum production or procurement lot size applicable to the material.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MRP controller responsible for the planning run.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the planned order.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the planned order.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the order is for internal production, external purchase, or stock transfer.']
  planned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material planned to be produced or procured.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant where the planned order will be executed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority code determining the order\'s execution precedence.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the material will be sourced internally, externally, or via consignment.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the order execution.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the order execution.']
  scheduling_status varchar [note: 'Current scheduling state of the planned order.']
  source_of_supply varchar [note: 'Code or description of the preferred source for the material.']
  supply_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material already available or scheduled from existing supply.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the planned quantity (e.g., kg, L, pcs).']

  Note: 'MRP-generated planned order representing a proposed production or procurement action to cover a material requirement. Captures planned order number, material, plant, order type (planned production order, planned purchase order, stock transfer), planned quantity, scheduled start date, scheduled finish date, firming status, and conversion status. Core planning element in SAP PP/MRP that drives production scheduling and procurement planning.'
}

Table planning.mrp_exception {
  mrp_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the MRP exception record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planner assigned to resolve the exception.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (product) to which the exception applies.']
  related_mrp_exception_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mrp_exception (related_mrp_exception_id)']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier of the material associated with the exception.']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the root cause of the exception (e.g., material shortage, lead time variance).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments added by the planner or system.']
  cost_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the cost impact.']
  deviation_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the planned date deviates from the target.']
  exception_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the exception (date part of exception_timestamp).']
  exception_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the exception for tracking.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was generated by the MRP run.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of the MRP exception indicating the nature of the planning anomaly.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception is considered critical for production continuity.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Indicates if this exception is a duplicate of another.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last reviewed the exception.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last review action.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the material affected by the exception.']
  mrp_exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exception.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the exception occurred.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the exception for planning response.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material impacted by the exception.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Suggested corrective action for the planner to address the exception.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Status of the exception resolution process.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was resolved.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the exception record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., kg, L, ton).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exception record.']

  Note: 'MRP exception message record generated during an MRP run flagging planning anomalies requiring planner action. Captures exception type (reschedule in, reschedule out, cancel, new order, shortage, excess stock), material, plant, exception date, days of deviation, recommended action, planner assignment, and resolution status. Critical for exception-based planning in chemical manufacturing where material lead times and minimum order quantities create complex planning constraints.'
}

Table planning.capacity_plan {
  capacity_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each capacity planning record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capacity planning costs are charged to cost centers; required for Capacity Cost Allocation report.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the capacity plan.']
  formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula. Business justification: Capacity planning evaluates equipment load using formula characteristics (viscosity, temperature), so linking to formula provides necessary data.']
  revised_capacity_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on capacity_plan (revised_capacity_plan_id)']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Capacity allocation is performed per sales territory to align manufacturing capacity with regional sales targets.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center, reactor, or line the capacity plan applies to.']
  available_capacity_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total available production capacity for the work center expressed in hours.']
  available_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total available production capacity for the work center expressed in kilograms (or tons).']
  bottleneck_flag boolean [note: 'True if the work center is identified as a bottleneck in the planning horizon.']
  capacity_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity plan.']
  capacity_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the capacity data (e.g., ERP, manual entry, simulation).']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for capacity values in this plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the capacity plan record was created.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the capacity plan was last reviewed or approved.']
  leveling_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether capacity leveling has been performed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the capacity plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External or business code used to reference the capacity plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the capacity plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planning methodology (e.g., Rough‑Cut Capacity Planning, Detailed Capacity Requirements Planning).']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last date of the planning horizon covered by the capacity plan.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First date of the planning horizon covered by the capacity plan.']
  planning_scenario varchar [note: 'Scenario used for the capacity plan (e.g., base, optimistic, pessimistic).']
  required_capacity_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregated capacity demand from planned orders expressed in hours.']
  required_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregated capacity demand from planned orders expressed in kilograms (or tons).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the capacity plan record.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of required to available capacity expressed as a percentage.']

  Note: 'Capacity planning master record defining the available and required capacity for a production work center, reactor, or processing line over a planning horizon. Captures work center, planning period, available capacity (hours/kg/tons), required capacity from planned orders, utilization percentage, bottleneck flag, and capacity leveling status. Supports rough-cut capacity planning (RCCP) and detailed capacity requirements planning (CRP) in chemical plant operations.'
}

Table planning.production_plan {
  production_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the production plan record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the finished chemical product or intermediate being planned.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Production Cost Allocation report linking each production plan to the cost center that funds it.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Scale‑up planning uses the exact experimental formulation record to generate batch recipes and cost estimates.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Production scheduling requires the exact formula version to generate batch recipes and ensure traceability of composition for each plan.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Production plans are executed at specific functional locations; FK enables location‑specific capacity and compliance tracking.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Production planning links each plan to the material master for bill‑of‑materials, hazard classification, and traceability.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  primary_production_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the plan.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Needed for Profit Center performance analysis; production plans contribute to profit center results.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Production scheduling is driven by confirmed sales quotes to ensure committed quantities are manufactured on time.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Production Launch Planning requires the originating R&D project ID to schedule start dates and allocate resources.']
  superseded_production_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_plan (superseded_production_plan_id)']
  capacity_utilization_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of plant capacity to be utilized by this plan.']
  comments varchar [note: 'User‑entered notes or remarks about the plan.']
  cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated production cost for the planned quantity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production plan record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost estimate.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand that drives the plan.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes effective for execution.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the plan expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  ehs_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan has a material environmental, health, or safety impact.']
  freeze_horizon_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is frozen for changes (true) or still editable (false).']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned lead time from order release to finished product availability.']
  lot_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'Standard production lot size for the product.']
  max_inventory_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Upper bound of inventory to avoid overstock.']
  min_inventory_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Lower bound of inventory that should be maintained.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the plan for reporting and analytics.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier or code for the production plan, used in S&OP and MRP processes.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan (e.g., Master Production Schedule, Material Requirements Planning, Capacity Planning, Scheduling).']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Authorized quantity of product to be produced within the planning horizon.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last date of the planning horizon covered by this plan.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First date of the planning horizon covered by this plan.']
  planning_period varchar [note: 'Granularity of the plan (e.g., daily, weekly).']
  production_method varchar [note: 'Process mode used to manufacture the product.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the plan complies with all applicable chemical regulations (EPA, REACH, etc.).']
  safety_stock decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer inventory quantity to protect against demand variability.']
  scheduling_priority varchar [note: 'Priority used by MRP/scheduling engines to allocate capacity.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the plan for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master Production Schedule (MPS) and production plan record defining the authorized production quantities for finished chemical products and key intermediates over a planning horizon. Captures product, plant, planning period, planned production quantity, UOM, production method, scheduling priority, freeze horizon flag, and plan status. Bridges the S&OP consensus plan and detailed MRP/scheduling execution in chemical manufacturing.'
}

Table planning.supply_plan {
  supply_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supply plan record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: REQUIRED: Supply plans must be aligned to specific chemical products to balance procurement, production, and regulatory compliance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supply procurement spend is allocated to cost centers for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  distributor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.distributor. Business justification: Supply planning incorporates distributor agreements and volume commitments, requiring a link to the distributor entity.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Supply planning of raw materials is based on the formula version of the product to be produced, linking ensures correct material quantities.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site for which the plan is created.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material or product family covered by the supply plan.']
  primary_supply_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party (e.g., planning department or manager) responsible for the plan.']
  superseded_supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on supply_plan (superseded_supply_plan_id)']
  tertiary_supply_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who most recently updated the plan.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the supply plan.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan satisfies all applicable regulatory constraints.']
  confirmed_supply_quantity decimal(20,5) [note: 'Quantity of supply that has been confirmed (e.g., purchase orders, production orders).']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Primary reason limiting the supply plan (e.g., capacity, material shortage).']
  cost_estimate decimal(20,5) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost associated with executing the supply plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supply plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost_estimate.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand that drives the supply plan.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes operationally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the plan expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended).']
  ehs_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score reflecting environmental, health, and safety impact of the plan.']
  last_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the plan was last approved.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average lead time in days required to obtain the supply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks entered by planners.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable alphanumeric identifier assigned to the supply plan.']
  plan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the moment the plan was generated or last refreshed.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is an aggregate (high‑level) or detailed (line‑item) plan.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the plan, incremented on each revision.']
  planned_supply_quantity decimal(20,5) [note: 'Total quantity of material expected to be supplied (production, procurement, transfers) during the horizon.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the planning horizon covered by the supply plan.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'First date of the planning horizon covered by the supply plan.']
  planning_methodology varchar [note: 'Algorithmic approach used to generate the supply plan.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the plan for execution sequencing.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the plan was revised.']
  supply_gap_quantity decimal(20,5) [note: 'Difference between planned and confirmed supply, indicating shortage.']
  supply_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supply plan.']
  supply_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the supply quantity (e.g., internal production, external procurement).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the supply quantities (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the supply plan record.']

  Note: 'Integrated supply plan record representing the constrained supply response plan for a material or product family across the planning horizon. Captures supply plan ID, material, plant, planning period, planned supply quantity (from production, procurement, and transfers), confirmed supply quantity, supply gap quantity, constraint type, and plan approval status. Serves as the supply-side counterpart to the demand forecast in S&OP balancing.'
}

Table planning.inventory_target {
  inventory_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each inventory target record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: REQUIRED: Inventory target calculations are performed per chemical product; FK allows accurate safety‑stock and reorder‑point reporting.']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Inventory targets are set per chemical compound; linking to the compound registry ensures correct safety and regulatory data.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory valuation and safety‑stock cost tracking require linking targets to the responsible cost center.']
  horizon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planning_horizon. Business justification: Inventory targets are defined per planning horizon; linking provides context and removes need for separate horizon attributes.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Inventory Target calculations require the material master to derive safety stock, reorder points, and compliance flags per material.']
  superseded_inventory_target_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inventory_target (superseded_inventory_target_id)']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch associated with the inventory target, if applicable.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'Category of material indicating its role in the production process.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Standard cost assigned to one unit of the material.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inventory target record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost values.']
  demand_forecast_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Projected demand quantity for the planning horizon used to set the target.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory target expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory target becomes effective.']
  ehs_category varchar [note: 'Environmental, Health, and Safety classification for the material.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the inventory target record.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent manual or automated review of the target.']
  lead_time_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average supplier lead time in days for the material.']
  lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Standard production lot size for the material.']
  max_order_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase order.']
  max_stock_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Upper limit for inventory to avoid overstocking.']
  min_order_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that can be ordered from the supplier.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the inventory is held.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material meets applicable regulatory requirements.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Frequency in days at which the inventory target is reviewed and possibly adjusted.']
  safety_stock_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Safety stock expressed as a percentage of average demand.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material kept as safety stock to protect against demand variability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that provided the inventory target data.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage location code within the plant.']
  target_days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned number of days the inventory should cover based on forecasted demand.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the material quantities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inventory target record.']

  Note: 'Inventory target and safety stock policy record defining the planned inventory levels for raw materials, intermediates, and finished chemical products. Captures material, plant, storage location, safety stock quantity, reorder point, maximum stock level, target days-of-supply, replenishment lead time, and review cycle. Drives MRP reorder point planning and inventory optimization for chemical feedstocks and finished goods.'
}

Table planning.demand_plan_adjustment {
  demand_plan_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each demand plan adjustment record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Key‑account forecast adjustments are recorded per account to reflect negotiated volume changes and support the Demand Adjustment Report.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved or rejected the adjustment.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical product to which the demand adjustment applies.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Adjustments to demand forecasts are recorded per material master to maintain audit trails and regulatory compliance.']
  primary_demand_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planner who created or submitted the adjustment.']
  reversal_demand_plan_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on demand_plan_adjustment (reversal_demand_plan_adjustment_id)']
  adjusted_forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity after the manual or consensus adjustment is applied.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the adjustment for tracking in S&OP processes.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was entered or applied.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment was manual, consensus‑driven, or system‑generated.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the adjustment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was approved or rejected.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the approver.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes related to the adjustment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  demand_plan_adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the adjustment record.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Optional end date for the adjustment\'s validity period.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the adjusted forecast quantity becomes effective.']
  forecast_horizon varchar [note: 'Planning horizon for which the forecast and adjustment apply.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the adjustment was entered manually (true) or generated by an algorithm (false).']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation supporting the adjustment decision.']
  original_forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Statistical forecast quantity before any manual adjustment.']
  planner_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the planner responsible for the adjustment.']
  quantity_delta decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between adjusted and original forecast quantities (adjusted - original).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why the forecast was adjusted.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing context for the reason code.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the adjustment originated.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the forecast quantities (e.g., kg, ton, liters).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the adjustment record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the adjustment record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the adjustment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing manual or consensus-driven adjustments made to a statistical demand forecast during the S&OP demand review process. Records original forecast quantity, adjusted quantity, adjustment delta, adjustment reason code, business justification, adjusting planner, adjustment date, and approval status. Provides full audit trail of human overrides to statistical forecasts for chemical product demand planning.'
}

Table planning.supply_demand_balance {
  supply_demand_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply-demand balance record.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing plant.']
  primary_supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the material (product) referenced in the balance.']
  prior_period_supply_demand_balance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on supply_demand_balance (prior_period_supply_demand_balance_id)']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard cost of the material per unit of measure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the balance record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for any monetary fields.']
  days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of days the projected balance will cover based on average demand.']
  excess_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if projected balance exceeds maximum inventory level.']
  maximum_inventory_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Upper inventory limit to avoid excess holding costs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments or remarks about the balance record.']
  opening_stock_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material on hand at the beginning of the planning period.']
  planned_dependent_demand_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity required as component for other finished goods.']
  planned_issues_total decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity expected to be issued (sales + dependent demand).']
  planned_procurement_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material expected to be received from external suppliers.']
  planned_production_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material scheduled for internal production during the period.']
  planned_receipts_total decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity expected to be received (production + procurement).']
  planned_sales_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material forecasted to be sold to customers.']
  planning_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the planning horizon for which the balance is calculated.']
  planning_period_start date [note: 'First day of the planning horizon for which the balance is calculated.']
  planning_scenario varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario used for the balance calculation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant code used in planning and execution systems.']
  projected_available_balance decimal(18,3) [note: 'Projected net stock after planned receipts and issues.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a procurement action.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory to protect against demand or supply variability.']
  shortage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if projected balance falls below safety stock.']
  supply_demand_balance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the balance record.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected monetary value of the projected available balance.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for all quantity fields (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the balance record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for revisions of the balance record.']

  Note: 'Supply-demand balance record representing the net position for a material at a plant for a specific planning period. Captures opening stock, planned receipts (production + procurement), planned issues (sales orders + dependent demand), projected available balance, days-of-supply, shortage flag, and excess flag. Core planning visibility record used in S&OP reviews and daily planning meetings in chemical manufacturing operations.'
}

Table planning.production_schedule {
  production_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the production schedule record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical product to be manufactured in this schedule.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planner responsible for creating or maintaining the schedule.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Production schedules need the exact formula version to set process parameters and track batch composition.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.manufacturing_order.manufacturing_order_id — Cannot link production plan to execution without this FK — planners need to see which scheduled items have been released as manufacturing orders. Critical for plan-vs-actual reporting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the schedule will be executed.']
  rescheduled_production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_schedule (rescheduled_production_schedule_id)']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or reactor where the production run will be executed.']
  batch_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned quantity of product to be produced in this run.']
  campaign_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run is part of a multi‑batch campaign (true) or a standalone batch (false).']
  capacity_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned utilization of the work center capacity expressed as a percentage.']
  changeover_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated minutes required to change over the equipment before this run starts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  freeze_status boolean [note: 'True if the schedule is locked from further changes.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier assigned to the batch for traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by planners or operators.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used by the optimizer to rank schedules (higher = more urgent).']
  production_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the schedule was released for execution.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version counter incremented each time the schedule is modified.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible schedule code used for reference in shop floor and planning systems.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the run is batch, continuous, or semi‑continuous.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time for the production run.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time for the production run.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Order of this schedule within a larger production campaign.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the batch size (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the last person who modified the schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the schedule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the planner who created the schedule.']

  Note: 'Detailed production schedule record defining the sequenced and time-phased production runs for chemical manufacturing work centers and reactors. Captures schedule ID, work center, product, batch size, scheduled start datetime, scheduled end datetime, sequence number, campaign flag, changeover time, scheduling status, and freeze status. Drives shop floor execution and feeds the MES/DCS systems in chemical plants.'
}

Table planning.changeover_matrix {
  changeover_matrix_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the changeover matrix record.']
  reverse_changeover_matrix_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on changeover_matrix (reverse_changeover_matrix_id)']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or reactor where the changeover occurs.']
  changeover_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with the changeover, including labor, materials, and waste.']
  changeover_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Duration of the changeover in hours.']
  changeover_matrix_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the changeover entry.']
  cleaning_type varchar [note: 'Type of cleaning required during changeover.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the changeover record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the changeover record is valid (null if indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the changeover record is valid.']
  from_product_code varchar [note: 'Code of the product that is being changed from (source product).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or special instructions for the changeover.']
  to_product_code varchar [note: 'Code of the product that is being changed to (target product).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update to the changeover record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the changeover record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the changeover record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining sequence-dependent changeover times and costs between chemical products on a shared work center or reactor. Captures from-product, to-product, work center, changeover duration (hours), cleaning requirement type (CIP, flush, purge), changeover cost, and validity period. Critical for production scheduling optimization in multi-product chemical plants where reactor cleaning between campaigns is a significant constraint.'
}

Table planning.parameter {
  parameter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the planning parameter record.']
  horizon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planning_horizon. Business justification: Planning parameters are scoped to a planning horizon; FK consolidates horizon information.']
  inherited_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on parameter (inherited_parameter_id)']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Planning parameters (lot sizing, safety stock) are defined per material master to reflect material‑specific characteristics.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'System-generated number indicating the change request that modified the record.']
  consumption_based_planning boolean [note: 'True if the material uses consumption-based planning; otherwise false.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning parameter record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the planning parameters are no longer valid (null if open-ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the planning parameters become valid.']
  in_house_production_time_days varchar [note: 'Time required to produce the material within the plant.']
  lot_size_maximum decimal(15,3) [note: 'Largest permissible production or procurement lot size for the material in the plant.']
  lot_size_minimum decimal(15,3) [note: 'Smallest permissible production or procurement lot size for the material in the plant.']
  lot_size_rounding decimal(15,3) [note: 'Rounding quantity applied to the calculated lot size to meet production or procurement constraints.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'Procedure that defines how lot sizes are calculated (e.g., EX = Fixed lot size, HB = Lot size based on historical consumption, VB = Lot size based on forecast, FO = Fixed order quantity, FOB = Fixed order quantity with minimum, ND = No lot sizing).']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Person or department responsible for the material\'s planning.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'Determines the planning method for the material (e.g., PD = MRP, VB = Manual reorder point, FO = Forecast, HB = Consumption based, EX = External, ND = No planning).']
  parameter_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the planning parameters.']
  parameter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planning parameter record.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Lead time for external procurement or inter-plant delivery.']
  planning_group varchar [note: 'Group used to aggregate materials for collective planning.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days into the future for which the material is planned.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant identifier where the material is planned and produced.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material is procured externally (E) or produced in-house (I).']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Stock level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Additional stock kept to protect against demand or supply variability.']
  safety_time_days varchar [note: 'Additional days added to the lead time to protect against uncertainties.']
  scheduling_margin_key varchar [note: 'Key that defines the safety margin applied during production scheduling.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for quantities defined in this record (e.g., KG, L, TON).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the planning parameter record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change tracking of the planning parameters.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the record.']

  Note: 'Material and plant-level MRP and planning parameter master record defining the control parameters governing how a material is planned. Captures MRP type (MRP, MPS, reorder point, consumption-based), lot sizing procedure, minimum lot size, maximum lot size, rounding value, planning horizon, safety time, in-house production time, planned delivery time, and scheduling margin keys. The authoritative planning configuration record for each material-plant combination in SAP PP/MM.'
}

Table planning.horizon {
  horizon_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each planning horizon definition.']
  parent_horizon_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on horizon (parent_horizon_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the horizon record was initially created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this horizon configuration expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this horizon configuration becomes effective for planning.']
  fence_type varchar [note: 'Type of planning fence applied (hard, soft, or none).']
  fence_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value (in days) defining the size of the planning fence.']
  horizon_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the horizon purpose and usage.']
  horizon_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the horizon definition.']
  horizon_type varchar [note: 'Category of the planning horizon (e.g., demand planning, MRP, freeze, firming fence, trading).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this horizon is the default configuration for its planning level.']
  length varchar [note: 'Numeric length of the horizon expressed in the selected unit.']
  length_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for horizon_length (days, weeks, or months).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the horizon.']
  planning_level varchar [note: 'Organizational or product level to which the horizon applies (e.g., plant, product family, material, site).']
  rolling_flag boolean [note: 'True if the horizon rolls forward automatically each planning cycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the horizon record.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining the planning time horizons and fence configurations used in demand and supply planning. Captures horizon type (demand planning horizon, MRP planning horizon, freeze horizon, firming fence, trading horizon), horizon length in days/weeks/months, rolling flag, and applicable planning level (plant, product family, material). Governs the time-phased planning windows used across S&OP, MRP, and production scheduling processes.'
}

Table planning.rough_cut_capacity {
  rough_cut_capacity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rough cut capacity record.']
  horizon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planning_horizon. Business justification: Rough‑cut capacity assessments are performed for a specific planning horizon; linking provides horizon context.']
  resource_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the resource (work center, equipment, or utility) for which capacity is evaluated.']
  revised_rough_cut_capacity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rough_cut_capacity (revised_rough_cut_capacity_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the capacity assessment.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User who approved the capacity assessment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment was approved.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Summary of changes made to the record for compliance and traceability.']
  available_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total capacity available for the resource in the planning period.']
  available_capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for available capacity.']
  capacity_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the available capacity figure.']
  constraint_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of any capacity constraint identified.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Category of constraint causing infeasibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the capacity assessment becomes effective for planning.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the capacity assessment expires or is superseded.']
  feasibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned load fits within available capacity (true = feasible).']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates if the record is locked from further edits.']
  load_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the required load quantity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations about the capacity assessment.']
  planning_period_end date [note: 'End date of the planning horizon for capacity assessment.']
  planning_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the planning horizon for capacity assessment.']
  required_load decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total load required by the Master Production Schedule for the resource during the planning period.']
  required_load_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for required load.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Type of resource being evaluated.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision number for audit tracking of the capacity record.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario (e.g., Base, Optimistic).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of capacity utilized (required_load / available_capacity * 100).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the rough cut capacity record for the scenario.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the record.']

  Note: 'Rough-Cut Capacity Planning (RCCP) record assessing high-level capacity feasibility of the Master Production Schedule against key resource profiles. Captures resource (work center, utility, raw material), planning period, required load from MPS, available capacity, utilization rate, feasibility flag, and constraint severity. Used in S&OP supply review to validate whether the production plan is achievable before committing to detailed MRP and scheduling.'
}

Table planning.campaign_plan {
  campaign_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production campaign plan.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Marketing campaigns are targeted to specific customer accounts; linking enables campaign ROI analysis and compliance tracking per account.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Campaign budgeting is managed through cost centers; needed for Campaign Cost Tracking report.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Campaign planning for batch production references the material master to ensure correct safety data sheet and regulatory compliance.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the plant hosting the campaign.']
  preceding_campaign_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign_plan (preceding_campaign_plan_id)']
  production_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.campaign. Business justification: Campaign Planning process requires linking each campaign plan to the actual production campaign record for execution tracking.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated Safety Data Sheet.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or reactor where the campaign is executed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the campaign plan.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign plan was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User who approved the campaign plan.']
  batch_size decimal(12,3) [note: 'Standard size of each batch within the campaign.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the campaign.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the campaign.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign.']
  campaign_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that initiates the campaign (e.g., inventory level, order backlog).']
  cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost for executing the campaign.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score representing the campaign\'s environmental impact.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign plan is locked from further edits.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the plant or site where the campaign is run.']
  minimum_campaign_length_days varchar [note: 'Shortest allowable duration for the campaign, in days.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the campaign.']
  number_of_batches varchar [note: 'Total number of batches planned in the campaign.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the campaign.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date of the campaign.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the campaign.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Family or group of products covered by the campaign.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the product being manufactured.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the campaign meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  schedule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used by the scheduler to order campaigns.']
  total_planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity to be produced across the campaign.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the planned quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Revision number of the campaign plan.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Production campaign planning record defining a multi-batch campaign for a chemical product or product family on a dedicated reactor or production line. Captures campaign ID, product family, work center, planned campaign start/end dates, total planned quantity, number of batches, minimum campaign length, campaign trigger (inventory level, order backlog), and campaign status. Supports campaign-based scheduling optimization common in specialty chemical and agrochemical manufacturing.'
}

Table planning.atp_check {
  atp_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the ATP check record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom the ATP check was performed.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: ATP (Available‑to‑Promise) checks need the material master to validate stock, hazard, and regulatory status for each material.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order associated with the ATP check.']
  recheck_atp_check_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on atp_check (recheck_atp_check_id)']
  allocation_rule varchar [note: 'Rule applied to allocate available stock to the request (e.g., FIFO, FEFO).']
  atp_check_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the ATP check.']
  atp_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used for the availability check: simple ATP, multi‑level ATP, or capable‑to‑promise (CTP).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier relevant for batch‑controlled materials.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier for the ATP check, used in communications and reporting.']
  check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ATP check was executed.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the system can commit to delivering the confirmed quantity.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(20,3) [note: 'Quantity that can be promised to the customer after ATP evaluation.']
  confirmed_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the confirmed quantity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ATP check record was first created in the system.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand triggering the ATP check.']
  is_backorder_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether backordering is permitted for this request.']
  is_batch_constrained boolean [note: 'True if batch‑level constraints affect availability.']
  is_capacity_constrained boolean [note: 'True if production capacity limits the confirmed quantity.']
  is_lot_constrained boolean [note: 'True if the material is subject to lot‑level availability constraints.']
  is_material_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is available in inventory for the request.']
  is_shortage_flag boolean [note: 'True when shortage_quantity is greater than zero.']
  is_simulation boolean [note: 'True if the ATP check was performed as a simulation rather than a live order.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned lead time in days used in the ATP calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the ATP check.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days ahead considered in the ATP calculation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where availability is evaluated.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the ATP check request.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer expects delivery of the requested quantity.']
  requested_quantity decimal(20,3) [note: 'Quantity requested by the customer or forecast, before ATP evaluation.']
  requested_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the requested quantity (e.g., KG, L, EA).']
  shortage_quantity decimal(20,3) [note: 'Quantity that cannot be fulfilled due to constraints.']
  shortage_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the shortage quantity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the ATP check (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, SAP PP).']
  supply_source varchar [note: 'Source of supply considered in the ATP calculation.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the ATP check record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ATP check record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the ATP check record for audit and re‑run purposes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the ATP check record.']

  Note: 'Available-to-Promise (ATP) check transactional record capturing the result of an ATP or capable-to-promise (CTP) availability check performed for a customer order or forecast requirement. Records check date/time, material, plant, requested quantity, requested delivery date, confirmed quantity, confirmed delivery date, ATP method used (simple ATP, multi-level ATP, CTP), and shortage quantity. Critical for order promising in chemical manufacturing where lead times and batch constraints affect delivery commitments.'
}

Table planning.allocation_rule {
  allocation_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the allocation rule.']
  fallback_allocation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_rule (fallback_allocation_rule_id)']
  primary_allocation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the rule.']
  tertiary_allocation_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the latest update.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which supply is allocated (e.g., historical share, contract priority, strategic customer tier).']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate allocations (proportional, fixed quantity, or priority‑based).']
  allocation_period_end date [note: 'Last date of the period for which the rule applies.']
  allocation_period_start date [note: 'First date of the period for which the rule applies.']
  allocation_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the intent and application of the allocation rule.']
  allocation_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  allocation_scope varchar [note: 'Scope level at which the rule is applied: per customer, geographic region, or sales channel.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the rule.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective for operational use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule exclusively governs allocation (true) or can be combined with other rules (false).']
  max_allocation_per_customer decimal(20,4) [note: 'Upper limit of quantity that any single customer can receive under this rule.']
  min_allocation_per_customer decimal(20,4) [note: 'Lower bound of quantity guaranteed to each eligible customer under this rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the rule.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority of the rule; lower numbers indicate higher priority when multiple rules apply.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the rule in external systems and documents.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the rule.']
  total_available_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the product available for allocation during the rule period.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity fields (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the rule.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rule to support change management.']
  version_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the rule becomes effective.']
  version_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the rule expires; null if still active.']

  Note: 'Product allocation rule master record defining how constrained supply of a chemical product is allocated across customers, regions, or sales channels during shortage situations. Captures allocation rule ID, product, allocation basis (historical share, contract priority, strategic customer tier), allocation period, total available quantity, allocation method, and approval status. Governs fair and strategic supply allocation during chemical supply shortages or plant outages.'
}

Table planning.customer_allocation {
  customer_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the customer allocation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer receiving the allocation.']
  allocation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Code identifying the allocation rule applied to this record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the chemical product being allocated.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Customer allocation of quantities is performed per material master to ensure correct product specification and compliance.']
  split_from_customer_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on customer_allocation (split_from_customer_allocation_id)']
  allocated_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of product allocated to the customer.']
  allocated_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the allocated quantity.']
  allocated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the allocated quantity expressed in the specified currency.']
  allocated_value_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the allocated monetary value.']
  allocation_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation expires or is scheduled to end.']
  allocation_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version of the allocation rule applied to this record.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'Source system or actor that created the allocation.']
  allocation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation becomes effective.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation origin or methodology.']
  allocation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the allocation rule instance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User or authority that approved the allocation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation was approved for execution.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier associated with the allocated product.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation complies with relevant chemical regulations.']
  consumed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the allocation that has been consumed by the customer.']
  consumed_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the consumed quantity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with the allocation.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the allocation became effective (may include time of day).']
  ehs_category varchar [note: 'Environmental, Health, and Safety classification of the allocated product.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the allocation expires.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation is locked from further changes.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the batch of product allocated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or business notes.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the allocation for fulfillment sequencing.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the allocation.']
  remaining_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the allocation that remains unconsumed.']
  remaining_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the remaining quantity.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for changes to the allocation record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or code describing why the allocation is in its current status.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that created the allocation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record assigning a specific allocated quantity of a constrained chemical product to a customer account or distribution channel for a defined period. Captures allocation ID, allocation rule reference, customer account, product, allocated quantity, consumed quantity, remaining quantity, allocation period start/end, and allocation status. Enables order management to enforce supply allocation limits during chemical product shortages.'
}

Table planning.forecast_accuracy_record {
  forecast_accuracy_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each forecast accuracy measurement record.']
  forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the accuracy record to the specific forecast version used.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Forecast accuracy reports tie actual vs. forecast quantities to the material master for performance analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the forecast was generated.']
  prior_period_forecast_accuracy_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast_accuracy_record (prior_period_forecast_accuracy_record_id)']
  accuracy_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of forecast accuracy based on predefined thresholds.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Real quantity sold or consumed during the measurement period.']
  bias decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average signed deviation indicating systematic over- or under-forecasting.']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence (percentage) associated with the forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values, if applicable.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the underlying data quality for the forecast inputs.']
  error_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Configured percentage threshold used to flag outlier forecast errors.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast accuracy measurement was recorded (typically end of the measurement period).']
  forecast_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks the forecast covers.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or technique used to generate the forecast.']
  forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity forecasted for the measurement period, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  forecast_source varchar [note: 'Originating system of the forecast (e.g., SAP, external model).']
  is_outlier boolean [note: 'True if the forecast error exceeds the defined error threshold.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific site or warehouse location.']
  mad decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average absolute deviation between forecast and actual values.']
  mape decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage error between forecast and actual quantities.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record measures demand forecast accuracy or consumption forecast accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the forecast accuracy measurement.']
  period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the measurement period.']
  period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the measurement period.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the accuracy record.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the forecast accuracy record is required for regulatory reporting (e.g., EPA, OSHA).']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level reason for significant forecast miss (e.g., demand_volatility, supply_disruption, data_quality).']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario (e.g., baseline, promotional, new_product).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit (e.g., kg, L, metric_ton) used for forecast and actual quantities.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  wmape decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted percentage error, accounting for volume importance.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Periodic forecast accuracy measurement record tracking the statistical accuracy of demand forecasts against actual sales or consumption for chemical products. Captures measurement period, product, plant, forecast quantity, actual quantity, forecast error (MAPE, WMAPE, bias, MAD), accuracy tier classification, and root cause category for significant misses. Drives continuous improvement of the demand planning process and statistical model selection.'
}

Table planning.constraint {
  constraint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the planning constraint record.']
  horizon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planning_horizon. Business justification: Constraints are defined for a specific planning horizon; adding this FK provides temporal context and eliminates the siloed status of planning_constraint.']
  parent_constraint_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on constraint (parent_constraint_id)']
  affected_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the material, work center, utility, or process that the constraint applies to.']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity impacted by the constraint.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the constraint with respect to applicable regulations.']
  constraint_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of the constraint type.']
  constraint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the planning constraint.']
  constraint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the constraint.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Category of the constraint such as raw material availability or reactor capacity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the constraint record was created.']
  duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of time‑based constraints expressed in hours.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the constraint expires; null if indefinite.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the constraint becomes effective.']
  impact_area varchar [note: 'Business area affected (e.g., production, supply chain, logistics).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the constraint is mandatory (true) or advisory (false).']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the constraint.']
  mitigation_action varchar [note: 'Planned action to mitigate or resolve the constraint.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments about the constraint.']
  origin varchar [note: 'Source of the constraint: internal policy, external requirement, or regulatory mandate.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the constraint.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the constraint (e.g., quantity, capacity, limit).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the regulatory rule or standard that imposes the constraint (e.g., EPA, REACH).']
  related_batch_lot varchar [note: 'Lot number or batch identifier associated with the constraint, if applicable.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the constraint is reviewed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the constraint.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the constraint originated (e.g., SAP PP, Honeywell DCS).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for constraint_quantity (e.g., kg, L, MW, hours).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the constraint.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the constraint record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the constraint record for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the constraint.']

  Note: 'Master record defining known planning constraints that limit production or supply capability for chemical manufacturing operations. Captures constraint ID, constraint type (raw material availability, reactor capacity, utility limitation, regulatory restriction, minimum batch size, shelf life), affected material or work center, constraint quantity or duration, validity period, and mitigation action. Feeds S&OP supply review and capacity planning processes as a structured constraint registry.'
}

Table planning.interplant_supply_plan {
  interplant_supply_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inter‑plant supply plan record.']
  primary_interplant_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the chemical material (e.g., CAS number or ERP material code).']
  return_interplant_supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on interplant_supply_plan (return_interplant_supply_plan_id)']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan was formally approved.']
