  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the plan\'s validity period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the plan\'s validity period; null if open‑ended.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is considered critical for production continuity.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required from dispatch to receipt, used for scheduling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions related to the plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible plan code used for tracking and communication across plants.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supply plan.']
  planned_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Quantity of material planned to be transferred between plants.']
  planned_transfer_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the material transfer is scheduled to occur.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the plan for execution sequencing.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned quantity.']
  receiving_plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant that will receive the material.']
  sending_plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant that will dispatch the material.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source ERP/system that generated the plan record.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Category of inter‑plant movement: stock transfer, tolling arrangement, or consignment.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for plan revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the plan.']

  Note: 'Inter-plant supply plan record defining planned stock transfers and tolling arrangements between chemical manufacturing plants to balance supply and demand across the production network. Captures sending plant, receiving plant, material, planned transfer quantity, planned transfer date, transfer type (stock transfer, tolling, consignment), lead time, and plan status. Supports multi-plant supply network optimization in chemical manufacturing groups with multiple production sites.'
}

Table planning.shelf_life_plan {
  shelf_life_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shelf life planning record.']
  horizon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planning_horizon. Business justification: Shelf life planning is horizon‑specific; FK ties plan to its horizon.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Shelf‑life planning requires the material master to apply material‑specific expiry rules and storage conditions.']
  superseded_shelf_life_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shelf_life_plan (superseded_shelf_life_plan_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the plan.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the plan.']
  at_risk_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of material approaching expiry and at risk of non-compliance.']
  at_risk_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for at-risk quantity.']
  batch_size decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard batch size for production runs related to the plan.']
  batch_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for batch size.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was created.']
  disposition_recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommended action for at-risk material.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the plan expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the material expires based on shelf life.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production lot associated with the plan.']
  minimum_remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Minimum remaining shelf life required by customer or market, expressed in days.']
  mrsl_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of product that must retain at least the minimum remaining shelf life.']
  mrsl_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for MRSL quantity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or remarks about the plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the plan, used in ERP and planning systems.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan based on business purpose.']
  planned_consumption_date date [note: 'Target date by which the material should be consumed to meet MRSL.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant associated with the plan.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date the material was produced, start of shelf life.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the plan for change tracking.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Safety stock amount to buffer against expiry risk.']
  safety_stock_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for safety stock.']
  shelf_life_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse location where the material is stored.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plan record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the plan.']

  Note: 'Shelf life and expiry planning record managing the planning constraints imposed by chemical product shelf life, minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) requirements, and expiry-driven demand. Captures material, lot, production date, expiry date, MRSL requirement by customer or market, planned consumption date, at-risk quantity (approaching expiry), and disposition recommendation (expedite sale, rework, disposal). Critical planning entity for specialty chemicals, agrochemicals, and performance materials with defined shelf lives.'
}

Table pricing.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price list record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Account‑specific negotiated price list assignment used in sales contracts and pricing approvals.']
  horizon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planning_horizon. Business justification: Price lists are created for a specific planning horizon to align pricing with budget cycles and forecast periods.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Price List Ownership Report requires linking each price list to the responsible org unit/plant for accountability and compliance.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: A price list is governed by a single pricing strategy; linking price_list to pricing_strategy enables direct lookup of strategy parameters and eliminates the need to infer strategy through multiple do']
  superseded_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_list (superseded_price_list_id)']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list was activated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the price list.']
  currency_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate to base currency at the time of price list creation.']
  customer_group_code varchar [note: 'Customer group identifier for price list targeting.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list was deactivated, if applicable.']
  default_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default margin percentage applied when no specific margin is defined.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Type of discount or surcharge applied in the price list.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'Code of the distribution channel for which the price list is valid.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the price list becomes effective.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the price list expires (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates if this price list is the default for its scope.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates if the price list applies globally across all sales orgs and regions.']
  material_group_code varchar [note: 'Material group identifier for price list applicability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes regarding the price list.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Owner or responsible department for the price list.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant identifier where the price list is applicable.']
  price_list_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level categorization of the price list.']
  price_list_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code identifying the price list.']
  price_list_description varchar [note: 'Longer textual description of the price list purpose and scope.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the price list.']
  price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list indicating its purpose.']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate prices in the list.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for price list applicability.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales organization to which the price list applies.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are included in the listed prices.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the price list.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the price list.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the price list.']

  Note: 'Master record for published standard price lists defining baseline list prices for chemical products, grades, and SKUs by currency, sales organization, and distribution channel. Manages price list versioning, validity periods, and activation status. Serves as the foundational pricing reference for all customer-facing price calculations in SAP SD condition technique. Distinct from sales.price_list which is the commercial/sales-facing view — this is the authoritative pricing master owned by the pricing domain.'
}

Table pricing.price_list_item {
  price_list_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price list line item.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Price list generation report requires each price list item to reference the master chemical product for cost, compliance, and margin calculations.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Formulation‑based pricing: pricing team uses experimental formulation data to calculate SKU cost and set list price.']
  forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.forecast_version. Business justification: Price list items are derived from a particular demand forecast version used in pricing simulations.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Price list items must reference the exact formula version to ensure compliance with versioned specifications and traceability.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Price List Management requires each price list item to reference the specific raw material it prices; linking enables accurate material‑wise pricing reports.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent price list that this line belongs to.']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Pricing team sets list item prices based on specific quality specifications; required for Specification‑Based Pricing report.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Site‑level pricing items for customers with multiple sites, required for site‑specific price calculations.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: SKU‑level pricing is defined per price‑list item; linking to the SKU master enables correct packaging, hazard, and unit‑of‑measure handling.']
  superseded_price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_list_item (superseded_price_list_item_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the price line.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the price.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the price complies with REACH, GHS, or other regulations.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes on regulatory compliance considerations.']
  cost_basis_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Underlying cost used for cost‑plus pricing calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price list item record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price amount.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment for which the price is defined.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the price becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the price expires (null for open‑ended).']
  energy_surcharge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Additional charge reflecting energy cost fluctuations.']
  is_default_price boolean [note: 'True if this line represents the default price for the product.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the latest update.']
  last_modified_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or comment for the most recent change.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the price list.']
  margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target profit margin expressed as a percentage.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered at this price.']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base price amount for one unit of the product.']
  price_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the product for pricing purposes.']
  price_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price amount was last changed.']
  price_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the pricing context or conditions.']
  price_list_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price line.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., SAP, manual entry).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing methodology.']
  price_uom_factor decimal(12,6) [note: 'Factor to convert between base UOM and pricing UOM.']
  price_validity_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the price\'s validity period (e.g., promotion, cost change).']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for price changes over time.']
  raw_material_index_factor decimal(12,6) [note: 'Multiplier based on raw‑material price index for dynamic pricing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region to which the price applies.']
  surcharge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge (e.g., energy, raw‑material index) is applied.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'True if taxes are already included in the price amount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit in which the price is expressed (e.g., kg, L, ton).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  volume_discount_tier varchar [note: 'Discount tier based on ordered volume.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Individual line-level price record within a price list, defining the unit price for a specific chemical product SKU, grade, or material in a given currency and unit of measure. Captures list price amount, pricing UOM, minimum order quantity, and validity dates. Supports multi-currency pricing and regional price differentiation for chemical products across global markets.'
}

Table pricing.condition {
  condition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing condition record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Carrier‑specific pricing conditions for freight surcharges used in product pricing calculations.']
  parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planning_parameter. Business justification: Many pricing conditions (e.g., safety stock, lot size) reference planning parameters defined in MRP settings.']
  parent_condition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on condition (parent_condition_id)']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Pricing conditions (discounts/surcharges) are applied when a product meets a particular quality specification, used in the Specification‑Based Discount policy.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Pricing condition belongs to a pricing strategy; adds strategic context']
  access_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number that determines the order of condition evaluation.']
  amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Default monetary amount or percentage associated with the condition.']
  applicable_customer_group varchar [note: 'Customer group code to which the condition applies.']
  applicable_material_group varchar [note: 'Material group code for which the condition is relevant.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region code (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3) where the condition is valid.']
  calculation_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the condition value.']
  condition_category varchar [note: 'Business area where the condition is applied.']
  condition_class varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the condition (price, discount, surcharge, tax, freight).']
  condition_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and usage of the condition.']
  condition_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for related conditions.']
  condition_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the pricing condition.']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the condition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the condition record was first created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  discount_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates if the condition represents a discount.']
  is_derived boolean [note: 'True if the condition value is derived from other conditions or formulas.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the condition is exclusive and prevents other conditions of the same class.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the condition is entered manually rather than generated by a system.']
  pricing_procedure varchar [note: 'Name of the pricing procedure to which this condition belongs.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority of the condition within the same access sequence.']
  scale_basis varchar [note: 'Basis for scaling the condition (e.g., per quantity, per value).']
  surcharge_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates if the condition represents a surcharge.']
  tax_indicator varchar [note: 'Flag indicating whether the condition is subject to tax.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that identifies the pricing condition type as defined in SAP SD.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applicable to the condition (e.g., KG, L, TON).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the condition record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the condition is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the condition becomes effective.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for SAP SD pricing condition types used in chemical manufacturing pricing — including base prices (PR00), surcharges (ZSU1), discounts (K004), freight conditions, and tax conditions. Defines condition class, calculation type, condition category, scale basis, and access sequence. The authoritative configuration record governing how each pricing element is calculated and applied in the condition technique.'
}

Table pricing.condition_record {
  condition_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the pricing condition record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to which the condition applies.']
  condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.condition. Business justification: Condition records belong to a master condition; adding condition_id FK enables lookup of condition metadata and prevents a silo.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (chemical product) the condition references.']
  product_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order header to which this condition belongs.']
  superseded_condition_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on condition_record (superseded_condition_record_id)']
  condition_application_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that determines how the condition is applied during pricing.']
  condition_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason for the most recent change to the condition.']
  condition_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary condition values.']
  condition_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the condition purpose or rule.']
  condition_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'True if the condition should be excluded from automatic pricing runs.']
  condition_manual_flag boolean [note: 'True if the condition was entered manually by a user.']
  condition_origin varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the condition was generated by the system or entered manually.']
  condition_price_group varchar [note: 'Price group identifier used for grouping similar conditions.']
  condition_priority varchar [note: 'Priority order used when multiple conditions apply; lower numbers have higher priority.']
  condition_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Scale quantity that triggers the condition (e.g., buy‑X‑get‑Y).']
  condition_record_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier for the condition record (e.g., 00012345).']
  condition_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the condition record.']
  condition_release_date date [note: 'Date when the condition was released for use.']
  condition_scale_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity threshold for scaled pricing (e.g., tiered discounts).']
  condition_scale_uom varchar [note: 'UOM for condition_scale_quantity.']
  condition_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that created the condition (e.g., SAP, external pricing engine).']
  condition_tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code associated with the condition, if applicable.']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'Category of the pricing condition (e.g., price, discount, surcharge).']
  condition_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for condition_quantity (e.g., KG, L, TON).']
  condition_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the condition – amount or percentage depending on condition_value_type.']
  condition_value_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether condition_value is a fixed amount or a percentage.']
  condition_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the condition record for change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the condition record was created in the system.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel code for the condition applicability.']
  line_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material on the sales order line to which the condition applies.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the condition line within the sales order.']
  pricing_procedure varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing procedure that applied this condition.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the condition is released for use or still in draft.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Sales organization code to which the condition belongs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the condition record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last date the condition remains valid; null for open‑ended.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First date the condition is valid for pricing.']

  Note: 'Transactional pricing condition record capturing a specific pricing condition value (price, discount, surcharge, freight) applicable to a defined combination of customer, material, sales organization, and distribution channel. Represents the resolved pricing condition instance used in order pricing. Tracks condition amount or percentage, scale quantity, validity period, and release status. Core to SAP SD condition technique execution for chemical product pricing.'
}

Table pricing.strategy {
  strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing strategy record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pricing strategy budgeting requires assignment to a finance cost center for expense allocation and reporting.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Pricing Strategy Governance is scoped to a specific business unit; linking to org_unit enables strategy approval and audit by the owning unit.']
  parent_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on strategy (parent_strategy_id)']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Pricing strategy is defined per R&D project to align margin targets with product development goals.']
  applicable_customer_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the customer segment (e.g., industrial, OEM, distributor) for which the strategy is valid.']
  applicable_geography_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the geography where the strategy applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|IND|BRA|RUS|JPN|DEU|FRA — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_product_line_code varchar [note: 'Code of the product line to which the pricing strategy is scoped.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the pricing strategy received formal approval.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who approved the pricing strategy.']
  cost_component_basis varchar [note: 'Primary cost elements used to calculate the price under cost‑plus methods.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who created the record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the pricing strategy record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the strategy.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the pricing strategy becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the pricing strategy expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  is_transfer_pricing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the strategy is used for inter‑company transfer pricing.']
  market_index_reference varchar [note: 'External market index (e.g., NYMEX WTI) used for market‑based pricing.']
  market_index_update_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the market index value is refreshed for pricing calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional comments, exceptions, or audit remarks.']
  pricing_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level authorized to approve changes to the strategy.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification and strategic intent behind the pricing approach.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews of the strategy.']
  strategy_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the pricing strategy in ERP and reporting systems.']
  strategy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing strategy.']
  strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing strategy.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing methodology applied by the strategy.']
  surcharge_energy_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional percentage added to price based on energy cost index fluctuations.']
  surcharge_raw_material_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional percentage added to price based on raw material cost index changes.']
  target_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired profit margin expressed as a percentage of net sales.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the pricing strategy.']
  volume_discount_tier_1_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount percentage applied when the minimum quantity of tier 1 is met.']
  volume_discount_tier_1_min_qty varchar [note: 'Minimum purchase quantity to qualify for the first discount tier.']
  volume_discount_tier_2_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount percentage applied for tier 2 volume thresholds.']
  volume_discount_tier_2_min_qty varchar [note: 'Minimum purchase quantity to qualify for the second discount tier.']
  volume_discount_tier_3_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount percentage applied for tier 3 volume thresholds.']
  volume_discount_tier_3_min_qty varchar [note: 'Minimum purchase quantity to qualify for the third discount tier.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the overarching pricing strategy applied to a product line, market segment, geography, or customer tier. Captures strategy type (cost-plus, market-based, value-based, competitive index), target margin band, pricing authority level, review frequency, and strategic rationale. Links pricing decisions to commercial strategy for specialty chemicals, commodity chemicals, and performance materials. Enables governance of pricing methodology across the portfolio.'
}

Table pricing.cost_plus_model {
  cost_plus_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each cost-plus pricing model record.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Cost‑plus model calculates cost using the BOM of a specific formula version; linking ensures accurate cost inputs.']
  freight_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_rate. Business justification: Cost‑plus pricing model incorporates freight rate per carrier to compute product cost.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Cost‑plus models are defined per plant; the org unit link supports cost allocation reporting and regulatory cost‑basis documentation.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Project‑specific cost‑plus model: finance builds cost‑plus pricing per R&D project for new product launches.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Cost‑plus model is defined per pricing strategy']
  superseded_cost_plus_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cost_plus_model (superseded_cost_plus_model_id)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved the model.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the model received formal approval.']
  ceiling_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum price that may be set under the model, often used for price caps.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the model complies with relevant pricing regulations (e.g., REACH, GHS).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center to which the model\'s costs are allocated.']
  cost_plus_model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose, assumptions, and scope of the model.']
  cost_plus_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing model.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the model record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency in which prices are expressed.']
  effective_from date [note: 'First date on which the pricing model is valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Last date on which the pricing model remains valid; null indicates open‑ended validity.']
  energy_index_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the base energy cost component.']
  fixed_cost_contribution decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of fixed manufacturing costs attributed to the product in the model.']
  floor_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable selling price derived from the cost‑plus calculation.']
  is_energy_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'True if the model includes an energy surcharge component.']
  is_transfer_pricing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the model is used for inter‑company transfer pricing calculations.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent model review.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who performed the latest review.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the model in pricing applications and documentation.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the pricing model.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the costing methodology applied by the model.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the model.']
  overhead_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of overhead costs allocated to the product within the model.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Higher‑level product grouping to which the model is associated.']
  raw_material_index_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier reflecting raw material price index adjustments.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier (e.g., NA, EU, APAC) where the model applies.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or guideline governing the model (e.g., "EPA 40 CFR Part 180").']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the costing scenario (e.g., "Base Case", "High Energy Price").']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base cost of the chemical product as calculated by standard costing rules.']
  target_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired profit margin expressed as a percentage of the standard cost.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the model record.']
  variable_cost_breakdown varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded detail of individual variable cost elements (e.g., energy surcharge, raw material index).']
  variable_cost_total decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregated variable cost components (e.g., energy, raw material index) for the product.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for change management.']

  Note: 'Cost-plus pricing model record defining the structured calculation of a chemical product\'s selling price from its standard cost base. Captures standard cost input, target margin percentage, overhead allocation rate, fixed cost contribution, variable cost components, and resulting floor price. Supports multiple costing scenarios (full absorption, marginal, contribution margin). Used by pricing analysts to set minimum acceptable prices and validate commercial pricing decisions against cost recovery requirements.'
}

Table pricing.market_price_index {
  market_price_index_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the market price index record.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Market price indices are published per chemical substance (CAS); linking to CAS registry provides authoritative identification for compliance and pricing calculations.']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Market price index tracks commodity price for a specific chemical; linking to compound registry provides CAS‑based identification.']
  parent_market_price_index_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on market_price_index (parent_market_price_index_id)']
  commodity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the chemical commodity or substance the index tracks.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence rating (0‑100) assigned by the data governance team.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the index record was first loaded into the lakehouse.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the overall data quality for the index record.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the index value is considered effective for pricing calculations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the index value is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended indices).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the index is updated by the source.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Country or region code to which the index applies.']
  index_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the index type.']
  index_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the index in external systems.']
  index_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the price index.']
  index_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the index release (e.g., v2023Q1).']
  market_price_index_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the index purpose and methodology.']
  market_price_index_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the index record.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the index is most relevant.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the price value.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price value type.']
  price_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric price value reported by the index source.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date the index value was published by the source.']
  source_publication varchar [note: 'Name of the external publication (e.g., ICIS, Platts) providing the index.']
  source_url varchar [note: 'Web address where the index data can be accessed or verified.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the price is expressed (e.g., USD per metric ton).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the index record.']

  Note: 'External market price index record tracking published commodity chemical price benchmarks from industry sources (ICIS, Platts, CMA, IHS Markit). Captures index name, commodity or chemical substance, publication date, index value, currency, unit of measure, source publication, and geographic region. Used as reference inputs for market-based pricing, raw material surcharge calculations, and competitive price positioning for commodity and semi-commodity chemical products.'
}

Table pricing.index_price_point {
  index_price_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price point record.']
  market_price_index_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the market index this price point belongs to.']
  prior_index_price_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on index_price_point (prior_index_price_point_id)']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence level (0‑100) assigned by the source to the price data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price point record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price value.']
  data_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the external organization supplying the price data (e.g., Bloomberg, Reuters).']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the reliability of the price data.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the price value remains effective; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the price value is considered effective.']
  index_price_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price point record.']
  index_type varchar [note: 'Category of the index indicating the commodity family.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the record was loaded into the lakehouse.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'True if the price value is an estimate rather than a directly observed market price.']
  market varchar [note: 'Broad market classification (e.g., US, EU, APAC) for the index.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the price point.']
  percent_change decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage change between the current price and the prior period price.']
  period_change decimal(18,6) [note: 'Absolute difference between the current price and the prior period price.']
  price_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the price observation (derived from price_timestamp).']
  price_source_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the external data provider for this price observation.']
  price_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the price value was observed or published.']
  price_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Published market price for the index expressed in the specified unit of measure.']
  prior_price_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Price value from the immediately preceding period used for change calculations.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region of the price index.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the price (e.g., Aveva PI, Bloomberg).']
  source_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the source system recorded the price.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the price is expressed (e.g., USD_per_barrel, EUR_per_tonne).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price point record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Time-series data point record capturing a specific published value for a market price index at a given date. Stores the actual index value, prior period value, period-over-period change, percentage change, and data quality flag. Enables trend analysis and formula-based pricing calculations that reference rolling averages or index movements for chemical feedstock-linked pricing contracts.'
}

Table pricing.surcharge {
  surcharge_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the surcharge record.']
  parent_surcharge_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on surcharge (parent_surcharge_id)']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: A surcharge is defined as part of a pricing strategy; linking surcharge to pricing_strategy provides the missing inbound relationship and enables hierarchy of pricing rules.']
  amount_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount applied when calculation_basis = fixed_amount.']
  applicable_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category (or line) to which the surcharge may be applied.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating geographic scope of the surcharge.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state of the surcharge.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who approved the surcharge.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the surcharge was approved.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the surcharge amount.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment for which the surcharge is applicable.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the surcharge becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the surcharge expires; null for open‑ended.']
  formula_expression varchar [note: 'Mathematical expression used when calculation_basis = formula (e.g., "base_price * 0.03 + 5").']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the surcharge amount is subject to sales tax.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity of product units for the surcharge to apply.']
  percentage_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage applied to the base price when calculation_basis = percentage.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the surcharge record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the surcharge record.']
  surcharge_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the surcharge in pricing contracts and ERP systems.']
  surcharge_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the surcharge purpose and calculation logic.']
  surcharge_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the surcharge (e.g., "Natural Gas Energy Surcharge").']
  surcharge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the surcharge.']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Category of the surcharge indicating the cost component it recovers.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the surcharge definition for change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a pricing surcharge applied to chemical product prices to recover specific cost components. Covers energy surcharges (natural gas, electricity index-linked), raw material index surcharges (ethylene, propylene, benzene derivatives), freight surcharges, hazmat handling surcharges, and small-order surcharges. Captures surcharge type, calculation basis (fixed amount, percentage, formula-linked), applicability rules, effective dates, and approval status. Critical for chemical manufacturers managing volatile feedstock and energy cost pass-through.'
}

Table pricing.surcharge_rate {
  surcharge_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each surcharge rate record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the surcharge rate.']
  superseded_surcharge_rate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on surcharge_rate (superseded_surcharge_rate_id)']
  surcharge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.surcharge. Business justification: Surcharge rates are specific to a surcharge definition']
  applicable_to_all_products boolean [note: 'True if the surcharge rate applies universally across product lines; false if limited to specific categories.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the surcharge rate was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the surcharge rate.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the surcharge (fixed amount, index‑based, or formula).']
  communication_date date [note: 'Date the approved surcharge rate was communicated to customers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the surcharge rate record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary surcharge rates.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the surcharge rate becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the surcharge rate expires; null if open‑ended.']
  index_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external index (e.g., Energy Index, Raw Material Index) used when calculation_method is index_based.']
  index_value_at_effective decimal(12,4) [note: 'Recorded value of the referenced index on the effective_from date.']
  is_percentage boolean [note: 'True if rate_value is expressed as a percentage; false if expressed as a fixed monetary amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the surcharge rate.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Category of chemical products affected by the surcharge.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'External code or identifier used in pricing contracts and systems.']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the surcharge rate.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the rate value (percent, currency amount, or basis points).']
  rate_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the surcharge (percentage or monetary amount).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code of the geographic region to which the surcharge applies.']
  surcharge_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the surcharge rate.']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Category of surcharge applied to chemical products (e.g., energy index, raw‑material cost).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the surcharge rate record.']
  volume_discount_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Discount rate applied to the surcharge when the volume threshold is met.']
  volume_discount_threshold decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum purchase volume (in metric tons) required to trigger a volume‑based discount on the surcharge.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the effective surcharge rate for a specific surcharge type during a defined validity period. Stores the calculated or approved surcharge amount or percentage, the triggering index value or cost basis, calculation date, approval workflow status, and communication date to customers. Enables periodic surcharge adjustments (monthly, quarterly) based on energy or raw material index movements for chemical product pricing.'
}

Table pricing.volume_discount_schedule {
  volume_discount_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the volume discount schedule record.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Volume discount schedules are scoped by pricing strategy']
  superseded_volume_discount_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on volume_discount_schedule (superseded_volume_discount_schedule_id)']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary discounts.']
  customer_scope_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer group or specific account the discount targets.']
  customer_scope_type varchar [note: 'Defines whether the discount is global, group‑based, or for a specific account.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the discount: percentage of list price, absolute monetary amount, or a fixed price per unit.']
  discount_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount as defined by discount_type.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the discount schedule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the discount schedule expires; null for open‑ended schedules.']
  is_cumulative boolean [note: 'Indicates if discounts accumulate across multiple orders within the validity period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal comments or remarks about the schedule.']
  product_scope_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product, product line, or hierarchy to which the discount applies.']
  product_scope_type varchar [note: 'Defines the granularity of products the discount applies to.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the discount schedule record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the discount schedule record.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the discount schedule in pricing and sales systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title for the discount schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the discount is applied per order quantity or based on cumulative annual volume.']
  tier_quantity_break decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity required to qualify for this discount tier.']
  tier_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the tier quantity breakpoint.']
  tier_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the tier within the schedule.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the discount schedule.']
  volume_discount_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or special conditions of the schedule.']
  volume_discount_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the discount schedule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the discount schedule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining tiered volume discount structures for chemical products. Captures discount scale tiers (quantity breakpoints), discount type (percentage, absolute amount, fixed price per tier), applicable product scope (product, product line, product hierarchy), customer scope (all customers, customer group, specific account), and validity period. Supports both order-quantity-based and cumulative annual volume discount programs common in chemical B2B commercial agreements.'
}

Table pricing.transfer_price {
  transfer_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transfer price record.']
  compound_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.compound_registry. Business justification: Internal transfer price for a material is based on the specific chemical compound recorded in the compound registry.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Internal cost center used for allocating the transfer price expense.']
  market_price_index_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external price index (e.g., commodity index) when index_price is used.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical material or intermediate being transferred.']
  sending_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that supplies the material.']
  superseded_transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on transfer_price (superseded_transfer_price_id)']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the intercompany transfer pricing agreement.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated monetary amount for the transfer price.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer price agreement was approved for use.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the transfer price (e.g., OECD guidelines).']
  cost_component_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base cost component used in cost‑plus calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer price amount.']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the supporting documentation required for OECD compliance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the transfer price becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the transfer price expires; null if open‑ended.']
  margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Profit margin percentage applied in cost‑plus pricing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special conditions for the transfer price.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used for price calculation (standard cost, market price, or index price).']
  price_index_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the selected price index at the effective date.']
  receiving_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that receives the material.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the region applicable to the pricing.']
  surcharge_energy_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Additional surcharge reflecting energy cost fluctuations.']
  surcharge_raw_material_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Additional surcharge reflecting raw material index changes.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is included in the transfer price amount.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage when tax is not included.']
  transfer_price_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the transfer price (e.g., cost-plus, resale minus, TNMM, CUP, market based).']
  transfer_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transfer price record.']
  transfer_price_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the price is for intercompany or external transactions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  volume_discount_tier varchar [note: 'Discount tier based on transaction volume.']

  Note: 'Intercompany transfer pricing record defining the price at which chemical products, intermediates, or raw materials are transferred between legal entities within the Chemical Mfg corporate group. Captures sending entity, receiving entity, material, transfer price method (cost-plus, resale minus, TNMM, CUP), transfer price amount, currency, validity period, and arm\'s-length documentation reference. Supports OECD transfer pricing compliance and intercompany billing reconciliation.'
}

Table pricing.transfer_price_study {
  transfer_price_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the transfer price study record.']
  prior_transfer_price_study_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on transfer_price_study (prior_transfer_price_study_id)']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Transfer price studies support a pricing strategy']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the study was formally approved by tax authority sign‑off.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the internal stakeholder who approved the study.']
  arm_length_range_lower decimal(15,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the arm\'s‑length price range derived from comparables.']
  arm_length_range_upper decimal(15,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the arm\'s‑length price range derived from comparables.']
  benchmark_methodology varchar [note: 'Specific benchmarking approach (e.g., TNMM, CUP) used to identify comparables.']
  comparable_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of external comparable transactions identified for the study.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification indicating how the study may be shared.']
  counterparty_legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related legal entity (e.g., the purchasing entity) covered by the study.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the study record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary amounts in the study.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system(s) that supplied the underlying data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, custom).']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., file path, URL, or document ID) to the full study documentation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the study\'s conclusions become effective for tax reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the study is no longer considered valid (nullable).']
  external_advisor_firm varchar [note: 'Organization employing the external advisor.']
  external_advisor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external tax or transfer‑pricing advisor who prepared or reviewed the study.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the study record.']
  lead_legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary legal entity (e.g., the selling entity) covered by the study.']
  method_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how the selected method is applied.']
  regulatory_reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) for which the study provides documentation.']
  risk_assessment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study identified a material transfer‑pricing risk (true) or not (false).']
  study_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the study for easy identification.']
  study_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by the analyst.']
  study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the study.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Category of the study indicating the analytical approach used.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the transfer pricing analysis applies.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'Methodology selected for the transfer pricing calculation.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the study document.']

  Note: 'Transfer pricing study and documentation record supporting OECD BEPS compliance for intercompany chemical product transactions. Captures study type (benchmarking, functional analysis, comparables search), tax year, legal entities covered, transfer pricing method selected, comparable transactions identified, arm\'s-length range established, and external advisor reference. Provides the audit trail required by tax authorities for intercompany pricing of chemical products across jurisdictions.'
}

Table pricing.proposal {
  proposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the pricing proposal record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or account to which the pricing proposal applies.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pricing proposals are evaluated against a cost center to track proposal costs in financial systems.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Pricing proposal cost calculations reference the experimental formulation that defines ingredient ratios and scale‑up feasibility.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Pricing proposals are generated for a specific formula version during product launch, linking proposal to formulation cost base.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue from approved proposals is posted to a specific GL account for financial consolidation.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Pricing proposals are generated for a specific sales opportunity to support win‑loss analysis.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Pricing proposal may be tied to a specific price list']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Proposals must be linked to a profit center to calculate expected margin and report profitability.']
  proposal_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who initially created the pricing proposal record.']
  proposal_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person responsible for approving or rejecting the proposal.']
  proposal_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who performed the most recent update to the record.']
  revised_proposal_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on proposal (revised_proposal_id)']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.sop_cycle. Business justification: Pricing proposals are reviewed and approved within the SOP (Sales & Operations Planning) cycle.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Pricing proposal should reference the strategy via FK; remove redundant string column']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the proposal was approved or rejected.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of when the record was first persisted for audit purposes.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.']
  competitive_price_reference varchar [note: 'External benchmark price or competitor reference used in the proposal analysis.']
  cost_basis_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Underlying cost of the product(s) that the proposal is built upon.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the pricing proposal was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of the proposal amounts.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Proposed monetary discount to be subtracted from the gross amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the proposed pricing becomes effective if approved.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the proposed pricing expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount before any discounts, surcharges, or taxes are applied.']
  margin_actual_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated margin based on cost and net amount; used for analysis after approval.']
  margin_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired profit margin percentage that the proposal aims to achieve.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Resulting amount after discount and before tax; the amount to be invoiced if approved.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Proposed price for a single unit of the product, expressed in the selected currency.']
  price_validity_end date [note: 'Last date on which the proposed price may be applied; null if indefinite.']
  price_validity_start date [note: 'First date on which the proposed price may be applied.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Classification code of the product(s) covered by the proposal.']
  proposal_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the proposal for tracking and communication.']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the proposal within the pricing governance workflow.']
  proposal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the proposal purpose (e.g., new product launch, price increase, discount request).']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the business reasoning behind the proposed pricing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code indicating the geographic market for which the proposal is intended.']
  surcharge_index_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric index (e.g., raw‑material price index) used to calculate the surcharge amount.']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Category of any additional surcharge applied to the proposal.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary tax calculated from the net amount and tax rate.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage of the net amount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the price per unit is expressed.']
  volume_discount_tier varchar [note: 'Discount tier based on purchase volume thresholds.']

  Note: 'Pricing proposal record capturing a formal internal pricing recommendation submitted for approval before communicating prices to customers or updating price lists. Captures proposed price or discount, product scope, customer or segment scope, pricing rationale, margin impact analysis, competitive context, and approval workflow status. Supports the pricing governance process for new product launches, annual price increases, and special pricing requests in chemical manufacturing commercial operations.'
}

Table pricing.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing approval record.']
  approval_delegated_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee to whom approval authority was delegated.']
  approval_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who performed the approval action.']
