  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pricing approvals record the responsible cost center for audit and cost‑center level control.']
  escalated_approval_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on approval (escalated_approval_id)']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Pricing approval must be tied to the exact formula version so margin targets align with the formulation\'s cost structure.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Pricing approval adjustments are recorded against a GL account to reflect accounting impact.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Pricing approvals are tied to the opportunity they affect; needed for audit and compliance reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Approvals need profit‑center association for profit‑center level pricing governance.']
  proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_proposal. Business justification: Pricing approval records the proposal it approves']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Pricing approvals for new products are tied to the originating R&D project to ensure compliance and cost recovery.']
  requester_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who submitted the pricing proposal.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level required to approve the pricing request.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the approval request, often used in communications and audit trails.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pricing approval.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the approval decision (approve/reject/escalate) was recorded.']
  approved_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final price amount authorized by the approver.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the product line.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form text entered by the approver explaining the decision.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of internal compliance validation for the pricing proposal.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the compliance check was performed.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Any contractual or operational conditions tied to the approved price.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the approval record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  delegation_expiration timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the delegated approval authority expires.']
  delegation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval authority was delegated to another employee.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the proposed price, if any.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the approved price expires; null for open‑ended pricing.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the approved price becomes effective for sales orders.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the approved price is treated as confidential information.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price values.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the price.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'SKU or catalogue code of the product.']
  proposed_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price amount requested in the proposal before any approval adjustments.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for requesting special or non‑standard pricing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country or region code where the pricing is applicable.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if external regulatory sign‑off is needed for this pricing.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the required regulatory approval.']
  regulatory_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when regulatory approval was granted or denied.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the pricing proposal was submitted for approval.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Additional charge (e.g., energy index, raw‑material surcharge) added to the proposed price.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the approval record.']

  Note: 'Approval workflow record for pricing proposals requiring authorization above standard pricing authority thresholds. Captures approver identity, approval level (regional manager, VP commercial, CFO), approval decision (approved, rejected, escalated), approval date, conditions or comments, and delegation records. Enforces pricing governance controls for special pricing, below-floor pricing, and large-volume contract pricing in chemical manufacturing.'
}

Table pricing.special_price_request {
  special_price_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the special price request record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom the special price is being requested.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved or rejected the request.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical product or material subject to the special pricing request.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Special price requests arise from a sales opportunity; linking enables request tracking per opportunity.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price list from which the standard price is derived.']
  primary_special_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative who originated the special price request.']
  proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_proposal. Business justification: Special price request may be based on a pricing proposal']
  resubmitted_special_price_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on special_price_request (resubmitted_special_price_request_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state of the special price request.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was approved or rejected.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the sales channel through which the request originated.']
  competitive_pressure varchar [note: 'Information about competitor pricing or market conditions influencing the request.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier associated with the request for accounting allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the pricing amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proposed discount expressed as a percentage of the standard price.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Start date when the approved special price becomes valid.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'End date when the special price expires; null if open‑ended.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request contains confidential pricing information.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation why the special price is needed (e.g., strategic win, market pressure).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations added by the sales rep or approver.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Pricing strategy applied to calculate the requested price.']
  product_grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or specification of the product (e.g., purity level, viscosity class).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the sales region applicable to the request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|CHN|DEU|FRA|GBR|IND|BRA|JPN — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable request number assigned by the pricing system for tracking.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the sales representative submitted the special price request.']
  requested_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price the sales rep proposes for the product, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  special_price_request_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the special price request record.']
  standard_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current price from the applicable price list before any special discount.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount of any applicable surcharge added to the requested price.']
  surcharge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge (e.g., energy index) is applied to the request.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the request record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the request record.']
  volume_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity the customer commits to purchase under the special price, in the unit defined by volume_uom.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume commitment (e.g., kilogram, ton).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the request record.']

  Note: 'Special pricing request (SPR) record capturing a sales representative\'s request for a customer-specific or deal-specific price deviation from the standard price list. Captures requesting sales rep, customer account, product and grade, requested price, standard list price, requested discount percentage, deal justification, competitive pressure details, volume commitment, and approval status. Core to the chemical industry\'s deal desk and special pricing management process.'
}

Table pricing.formula_price {
  formula_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing formula record.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Formula price calculations use experimental formulation parameters (ratios, ingredients) to derive price components.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Formula‑based price definitions are tied to a pricing strategy']
  superseded_formula_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on formula_price (superseded_formula_price_id)']
  applicable_plant_code varchar [note: 'Production plant to which the formula applies.']
  applicable_region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the formula is valid.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the formula.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formula was approved or rejected.']
  base_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Static component of the price before index adjustments.']
  ceiling_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Highest allowable price for the formula.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing the formula. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REACH|GHS|EPA|OSHA|ISO9001|ISO14001|ISO45001 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formula record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the formula becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the formula expires; null for open‑ended.']
  floor_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lowest allowable price for the formula.']
  formula_expression varchar [note: 'Textual representation of the calculation logic.']
  formula_price_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the formula in contracts and systems.']
  formula_price_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the pricing logic.']
  formula_price_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing formula.']
  formula_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the formula.']
  formula_price_type varchar [note: 'Category of pricing calculation method.']
  index_1_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the first external market index used in the formula.']
  index_1_coefficient decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the primary index value.']
  index_1_lag_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the index value is lagged.']
  index_2_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of an optional second market index.']
  index_2_coefficient decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier for the secondary index.']
  index_2_lag_days varchar [note: 'Lag in days for the secondary index.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this formula is the default for its product line.']
  minimum_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity required for the formula to apply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions.']
  reset_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the formula is recalculated (e.g., monthly).']
  surcharge_coefficient decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier for the selected surcharge type.']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Category of any additional surcharge applied.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the price is expressed (e.g., USD_per_ton).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the formula.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the formula for change management.']
  volume_discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when order volume exceeds thresholds.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the formula.']

  Note: 'Formula-based pricing record defining a price calculation formula that links a chemical product\'s selling price to one or more external market indices, feedstock costs, or energy indices. Captures formula expression (e.g., base price + ethylene index × coefficient + energy surcharge), index references, weighting factors, lag period, floor price, ceiling price, and reset frequency. Common in long-term chemical supply contracts where prices float with feedstock markets.'
}

Table pricing.formula_price_calculation {
  formula_price_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each formula price calculation record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical product whose price is being calculated.']
  experimental_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Each formula price calculation records which experimental formulation was used for traceability and audit.']
  formula_price_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master formula price record that this calculation applies to.']
  prior_formula_price_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on formula_price_calculation (prior_formula_price_calculation_id)']
  calculated_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Resulting price after applying formula and indexes.']
  calculation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the price calculation event.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price calculation.']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price calculation was executed.']
  ceiling_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable price for the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calculation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price values.']
  effective_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final price applied after floor/ceiling checks and any surcharges.']
  floor_ceiling_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calculated price violated floor or ceiling limits.']
  floor_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable price for the product.']
  index_energy decimal(12,4) [note: 'Index value for energy cost used in the calculation.']
  index_raw_material decimal(12,4) [note: 'Index value for raw material cost used in the calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the calculation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the price calculation is applied.']
  price_change_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute difference between calculated price and prior price.']
  price_change_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage change between calculated price and prior price.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the price change (e.g., index movement, contract clause).']
  price_valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the calculated price becomes effective.']
  price_valid_to date [note: 'Date until which the calculated price remains effective; null if open‑ended.']
  prior_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price from the previous calculation period for comparison.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code relevant to the pricing calculation.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the calculation record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calculation record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the calculation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the periodic execution of a formula-based price calculation for a specific formula price record. Stores calculation date, input index values used, calculated price result, prior period price, price change amount and percentage, floor/ceiling breach flag, and resulting effective price. Provides the audit trail for formula price resets and supports customer notification of price changes under index-linked chemical supply contracts.'
}

Table pricing.channel_price {
  channel_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each channel price record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the chemical product to which the channel price relates.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the price list to which this channel price belongs.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Channel‑specific pricing is derived from a particular price list line item; adding price_list_item_id to channel_price creates the correct child‑to‑parent relationship without forming a cycle.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing strategy that governs this channel price.']
  superseded_channel_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on channel_price (superseded_channel_price_id)']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the channel price was approved.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User who approved the channel price for activation.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales channel.']
  channel_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel price record.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution channel for which the price applies (e.g., direct, distributor, e‑commerce, export, OEM).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the channel price record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which price_amount is expressed.']
  customer_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the customer segment (e.g., industrial, OEM, distributor) for which the price is defined.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base price for the channel.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the channel price becomes valid.']
  effective_to date [note: 'End date when the channel price expires (null for open‑ended).']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region to which the price applies.']
  is_transfer_pricing boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the price is subject to transfer pricing rules.']
  margin_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired gross margin percentage the channel price aims to achieve.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the channel price.']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base price amount charged to the channel before discounts or surcharges.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price value (e.g., internal calculation, external market feed, system generated).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Category of the price component (list price, discount, surcharge, margin).']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the channel price record.']
  surcharge_energy_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Monetary surcharge derived from an energy cost index, applied to the channel price.']
  surcharge_raw_material_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Monetary surcharge based on a raw material cost index, applied to the channel price.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the channel price record.']
  volume_discount_tier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the volume discount tier applicable to the channel price.']

  Note: 'Channel-specific pricing record defining differentiated prices for chemical products sold through distinct distribution channels — direct industrial sales, distributor channel, e-commerce, export/international, and OEM supply. Captures channel type, product or product line scope, channel price or discount from list, channel margin target, and validity period. Enables channel pricing strategy management to prevent channel conflict and optimize margin across the chemical distribution network.'
}

Table pricing.regional_price {
  regional_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for each regional price record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the chemical product to which this regional price applies.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Regional price adjustments are scoped by pricing strategy']
  superseded_regional_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on regional_price (superseded_regional_price_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price record was initially created.']
  differential_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the price differential is an absolute amount or a percentage.']
  differential_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the price differential (absolute amount or percent).']
  ex_works_flag boolean [note: 'True if the price is quoted on an ex‑works basis (excluding freight).']
  freight_included_flag boolean [note: 'True if freight costs are included in the price.']
  import_duty_included_flag boolean [note: 'True if applicable import duties are baked into the price.']
  min_order_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for this regional price.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the price record.']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the price expressed in the specified currency.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the price (e.g., cost‑plus, market‑based).']
  price_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the price (e.g., standard, promotional, clearance).']
  price_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the price record in pricing procedures and contracts.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code for the price amount.']
  price_effective_from date [note: 'First calendar date on which the price becomes valid.']
  price_effective_to date [note: 'Last calendar date on which the price remains valid (null for open‑ended).']
  price_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the price record (e.g., "EU West Chemical A Price").']
  price_override_flag boolean [note: 'True if this price overrides a default or global price.']
  price_region_hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the region\'s position in the geographic hierarchy (e.g., continent > country > sub‑region).']
  price_regulatory_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the specific regulatory rule or exemption applied to the price.']
  price_regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price complies with applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, GHS).']
  price_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the price data.']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price record.']
  price_tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'True if taxes are already included in the price amount.']
  price_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) when tax is not included.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a base list price, a discount, or a surcharge.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for which the price applies (e.g., per kilogram).']
  price_uom_conversion_factor decimal(12,6) [note: 'Factor to convert the price to alternative units of measure.']
  price_validity_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a price is active, expired, or pending (e.g., contract renewal, market shift).']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the price record for change management.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country or region code to which the price adjustment applies.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the geographic region (e.g., "Western Europe").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the price record.']
  volume_discount_tier varchar [note: 'Tier identifier for volume‑based discount structures (e.g., 1, 2, 3).']

  Note: 'Regional or geographic pricing record defining price adjustments or differentials applied to chemical products in specific geographic markets or sales regions. Captures region or country, product scope, price differential (absolute or percentage from global list), local currency price, freight-included vs. ex-works basis, import duty considerations, and validity period. Supports geographic price differentiation for chemical products across domestic and international markets.'
}

Table pricing.price_change_event {
  price_change_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the immutable price change event record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (if the price change is customer‑specific).']
  condition_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing condition record impacted by the change.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who executed the price change.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: OOS inspection lots trigger price change events to adjust batch pricing; required for OOS‑Driven Price Adjustment workflow.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical material or product whose price is changed.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price list to which the change applies.']
  reversal_price_change_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_change_event (reversal_price_change_event_id)']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.supply_plan. Business justification: Price changes are often triggered by adjustments in the supply plan to reflect material availability.']
  approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the approval document or workflow instance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the price change approval process.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change was approved (or rejected).']
  change_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between new_price and old_price (new_price - old_price).']
  change_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change calculated as (change_amount / old_price) * 100.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why the price was changed.']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the business rationale behind the price change.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change event record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which prices are expressed.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the new price becomes legally effective.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Serialized JSON payload containing the full set of price change details for downstream processing.']
  event_source_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that generated the price change event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the price change event was recorded in the system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event; for this product it is always a price change.']
  initiating_user_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the user who performed the price change.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'True when the price change has passed all required approvals.']
  is_manual_change boolean [note: 'True if the price change was entered manually; false if system‑generated.']
  new_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price amount after the change, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form comments or audit notes related to the price change.']
  old_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price amount before the change, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  price_change_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the change applies to a price‑list item, a condition record, or a customer‑specific price.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the price change record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price change event record.']

  Note: 'Immutable event record capturing every price change applied to a price list item, condition record, or customer-specific price. Records old price, new price, change amount, change percentage, effective date, change reason code (annual increase, feedstock cost change, competitive response, contract renewal), initiating user, and approval reference. Provides the complete audit trail for pricing decisions and supports regulatory and commercial dispute resolution for chemical product pricing.'
}

Table pricing.competitor_price {
  competitor_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the competitor price observation record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the competing product or grade.']
  competitor_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the competing company providing the price.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Competitor price records are used to benchmark against internal price lists; linking each competitor_price to the relevant price_list provides a direct relationship and eliminates the siloed status of']
  prior_competitor_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competitor_price (prior_competitor_price_id)']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Confidence rating assigned to the price observation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the observed price.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the market where the price applies.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or purity level of the competing product.']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the competitor price was observed or recorded.']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Observed price value for the competing product.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the price is quoted (e.g., list price, net price, delivered price).']
  price_index_reference varchar [note: 'Reference name of any market index used in the price calculation (e.g., raw material index).']
  price_index_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Value of the referenced market index at the time of observation.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Incoterm defining the delivery terms associated with the price.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price (e.g., kg, ton, liter).']
  price_valid_from date [note: 'Start date of the price validity period, if applicable.']
  price_valid_until date [note: 'End date of the price validity period, if applicable.']
  product_cas_number varchar [note: 'Standard CAS registry number identifying the chemical substance.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the competing product or grade.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competitor price record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  source_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the specific source or report.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Category of source providing the price intelligence.']

  Note: 'Competitor market price intelligence record capturing observed or reported prices for competing chemical products in the market. Stores competitor identity, competing product or grade, observed price, price basis (list, net, delivered), source of intelligence (customer feedback, distributor report, public tender, market research), observation date, geographic market, and confidence level. Feeds competitive pricing analysis and market-based pricing decisions for chemical product portfolio management.'
}

Table pricing.price_simulation {
  price_simulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each price simulation record.']
  baseline_price_simulation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_simulation (baseline_price_simulation_id)']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: The price simulation tool runs scenarios on a specific chemical product to forecast revenue, margin, and regulatory impact.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing analyst or commercial manager who created or owns the simulation.']
  formula_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Price simulations are run against a particular formula version to forecast pricing impact of formulation changes.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Price simulation scenarios are run against a pricing strategy']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the simulation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the simulated price.']
  customer_scope_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or customer segment affected by the simulation.']
  customer_scope_type varchar [note: 'Defines whether the simulation targets an individual customer, segment, or all customers.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date from which the simulated pricing would be applicable if approved.']
  effective_to date [note: 'End date after which the simulated pricing would no longer be applicable.']
  estimated_margin_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected change in gross margin (currency) resulting from the simulation.']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected change in revenue (currency) attributable to the simulation.']
  estimated_volume_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected change in sales volume (units) resulting from the simulated price change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental information, comments, or audit trail remarks related to the simulation.']
  price_simulation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the simulation record.']
  product_scope_code varchar [note: 'Identifier (e.g., SKU or product line code) of the product(s) covered by the simulation.']
  product_scope_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the simulation applies to a single SKU, product line, or entire portfolio.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Category of what‑if analysis performed (e.g., price increase impact, margin optimization, volume‑price trade‑off).']
  simulated_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied in the simulation scenario.']
  simulated_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected gross margin percent derived from simulated price and cost.']
  simulated_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Projected unit price resulting from the simulation (before discounts).']
  simulation_code varchar [note: 'Business-friendly code used to reference the simulation in reports and communications.']
  simulation_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing assumptions, methodology, and any special notes for the simulation.']
  simulation_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the simulation scenario for easy identification by analysts.']
  simulation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the simulation was executed or last refreshed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the simulation record.']

  Note: 'Price simulation record capturing a what-if pricing scenario analysis performed by pricing analysts or commercial managers. Stores simulation name, scenario type (price increase impact, margin optimization, volume-price trade-off), product scope, customer scope, simulated price or discount, estimated volume impact, estimated revenue impact, estimated margin impact, and simulation status. Supports pricing decision-making for annual price reviews, contract negotiations, and new product launches in chemical manufacturing.'
}

Table pricing.authority_matrix {
  authority_matrix_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each pricing authority matrix record.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Pricing approval authority is often limited to a specific org unit; the FK enables the Pricing Authority Matrix report per unit.']
  parent_authority_matrix_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on authority_matrix (parent_authority_matrix_id)']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Authority matrix defines limits per pricing strategy']
  applicable_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for which the authority thresholds apply.']
  approval_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Textual representation of the hierarchical chain for escalations beyond this authority.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authority matrix entry was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the authority matrix entry.']
  authority_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the authority role within pricing systems.']
  authority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating hierarchy of the authority (higher number = higher authority).']
  authority_matrix_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the authority matrix record.']
  authority_role varchar [note: 'Name of the role or position that holds pricing authority (e.g., Regional Sales Manager, Global Pricing Director).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authority matrix record was first created.']
  customer_tier_scope varchar [note: 'Customer segmentation (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2) for which this authority level is valid.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the authority matrix becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the authority matrix expires (null for open‑ended).']
  escalation_path varchar [note: 'Textual description of the escalation process when a request exceeds this authority\'s limits.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Region or country codes where the authority matrix is effective.']
  is_below_margin_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role may approve pricing below the defined margin floor.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this matrix is the default for its scope when no specific matrix matches.']
  is_transfer_pricing_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role can approve transfer‑pricing decisions without further escalation.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum absolute discount amount (currency) this role may approve.']
  max_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum discount percentage this role may approve on standard pricing.']
  min_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum profit margin percentage that must be maintained for approvals by this role.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding special conditions or rationale.']
  price_floor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum margin floor percentage that must be respected for approvals by this role.']
  product_line_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list or code indicating which product lines the authority applies to.']
  surcharge_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role may approve surcharges (e.g., energy, raw‑material index) without escalation.']
  surcharge_type_allowed varchar [note: 'Enumerated list of surcharge categories the authority can approve.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authority matrix record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the authority matrix record for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the pricing authority levels and approval thresholds for pricing decisions across the Chemical Mfg organization. Captures role or position, maximum discount authority (percentage or absolute), minimum margin floor authority, product line scope, customer tier scope, and escalation path. Governs who can approve standard pricing, special price requests, below-floor pricing, and transfer pricing decisions without escalation.'
}

Table pricing.currency_exchange_rate {
  currency_exchange_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each currency exchange rate record.']
  prior_currency_exchange_rate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on currency_exchange_rate (prior_currency_exchange_rate_id)']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Auto-generated FK linking siloed currency_exchange_rate to strategy']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exchange rate record was first created in the system.']
  currency_base varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 code of the source (base) currency in the pair.']
  currency_exchange_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate record.']
  currency_pair varchar [note: 'Concatenated ISO 4217 codes representing the source and target currencies (e.g., USD/EUR).']
  currency_target varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 code of the target (quote) currency in the pair.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the exchange rate becomes valid for pricing calculations.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date on which the exchange rate ceases to be valid (nullable for open‑ended rates).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the rate (e.g., special market conditions).']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the rate, often used in finance or pricing systems.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate (e.g., spot, average, planning, hedge, mid, custom).']
  rate_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric exchange rate value representing how many units of the target currency equal one unit of the base currency.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rate data (e.g., ECB, internal treasury, SAP FI).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exchange rate record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Currency exchange rate record used in multi-currency pricing for chemical products sold across international markets. Captures currency pair, exchange rate type (spot, average, planning, hedge rate), rate value, valid-from date, and source (ECB, internal treasury, SAP FI). Distinct from any finance domain FX exposure records — this is the pricing-specific rate master used to convert list prices and condition records into local currency for customer invoicing and price list publication.'
}

Table pricing.price_floor {
  price_floor_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price floor record.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Price floor definitions are linked to a pricing strategy']
  superseded_price_floor_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_floor (superseded_price_floor_id)']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the floor was approved.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the executive who approved the floor.']
  calculation_methodology varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how the floor amount is derived.']
  cost_basis varchar [note: 'Primary cost component used for the floor calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price floor record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the floor amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the floor becomes enforceable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the floor is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  floor_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable selling price amount before discounts or surcharges.']
  floor_type varchar [note: 'Category of floor calculation basis (e.g., variable cost, contribution margin, full cost).']
  margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired contribution margin percent that the floor aims to protect.']
  market_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the market, region, or sales channel the floor applies to.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the floor.']
  price_floor_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used in pricing systems to reference the floor.']
  price_floor_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the price floor (e.g., "Base Chemical Floor").']
  price_floor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price floor.']
  product_grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or specification of the chemical product (e.g., "Technical Grade").']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the chemical product to which the floor applies.']
  raw_material_index_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier based on raw material price index used in floor calculation.']
  regulatory_reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulation or standard (e.g., EPA, REACH) that mandates the floor\'s existence or documentation.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the floor price is scheduled for review.']
  surcharge_energy_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor reflecting energy cost adjustments applied to the floor.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the price floor record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Price floor record defining the minimum acceptable selling price for a chemical product, grade, or SKU below which sales are not authorized without executive approval. Captures floor price basis (variable cost floor, contribution margin floor, full cost floor), floor price amount, currency, applicable market or channel, calculation methodology, review date, and approving authority. Protects margin integrity and prevents below-cost selling in chemical manufacturing commercial operations.'
}

Table pricing.calendar {
  calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each pricing calendar record.']
  calendar_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the pricing manager responsible for this calendar.']
  calendar_pricing_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Pricing calendars are plant‑specific; linking to org_unit allows schedule management and compliance tracking per facility.']
  prior_pricing_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on calendar (prior_pricing_calendar_id)']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_strategy. Business justification: Pricing calendar is associated with a pricing strategy for review cycles']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the pricing calendar.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing calendar received final approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the pricing calendar.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the pricing calendar in business processes.']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the pricing calendar (e.g., "2025 Annual Review").']
  calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pricing calendar.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing calendar record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values in the calendar.']
  customer_notification_deadline date [note: 'Latest date to notify customers of upcoming price changes.']
  customer_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the customer segment (e.g., industrial, OEM, distributor).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the pricing calendar becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the pricing calendar expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended).']
  energy_index_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base price based on an energy cost index.']
  is_energy_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'True when energy‑based surcharges are part of the pricing strategy.']
  is_transfer_pricing boolean [note: 'True if the calendar includes transfer‑pricing rules for inter‑company transactions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the calendar.']
  price_change_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the new pricing becomes legally binding for customers.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the product line or segment covered by the calendar.']
  raw_material_index_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier derived from a raw‑material cost index used in cost‑plus calculations.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code representing the geographic region the calendar applies to.']
  review_cycle_type varchar [note: 'Defines the frequency of the pricing review cycle.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive reviews when not using a named cycle type.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date when the scheduled pricing review period begins.']
  surcharge_notification_deadline date [note: 'Deadline for communicating surcharge adjustments to affected parties.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the pricing calendar.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the pricing calendar for change control.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the pricing calendar.']

  Note: 'Pricing calendar master record defining the scheduled pricing review cycles, price change effective dates, surcharge notification deadlines, and annual price increase timelines for chemical product portfolios. Captures review cycle type (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly index reset), product line or segment scope, review start date, customer notification deadline, effective date, and responsible pricing manager. Coordinates pricing operations across commercial, finance, and customer service teams.'
}
